EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG) 1
CHAPTER 1: About Kinaxis
Customer Support 2
Kinaxis enables our customers to improve and accelerate analysis and decision-making across 
their supply chain operations. 
We help leaders across multiple industries, including A&D, Automotive, High Tech, Industrial, and 
Life Sciences to create a foundation for concurrent planning, continuous performance 
monitoring, and coordinated responses to plan variances across multiple areas of the business. 
Our single-product offering supports a full spectrum of supply chain related business processes, 
including S&OP, supply planning, capacity planning, demand planning, inventory management, 
MPS, and order fulfillment.
Our customers have immensely complex supply chain networks and volatile business 
environments. Yet, they have been able to replace disparate planning and performance 
management tools and realize significant operations performance breakthroughs in planning 
cycles, supply chain response times, and decision accuracy. They can easily model varying supply 
chain conditions to make both long-term and real-time demand and supply balancing decisions 
quickly, collaboratively, and in line with the shared business objectives of multiple stakeholders.
For more information, visit www.kinaxis.com or the Kinaxis Knowledge Network at 
http://knowledge.kinaxis.com/. Kinaxis trades on the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX:KXS).

===== PAGE 12 =====
2 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER1:AboutKinaxis
Customer Support
The Kinaxis Customer Support team understands demand and supply chain planning, 
monitoring, and response, and is experienced in applying Kinaxis Maestro™ to real-world 
business scenarios. Areas of expertise include:
 l Maestro functionality
 l Data extraction and mapping issues
 l Technical software compatibility
 l Coordination of integration
 l Product implementation
If you are a key support contact, you can submit support cases. To submit a support case, sign in 
to the Kinaxis Knowledge Network. You can also contact Kinaxis Customer Support by phoning 
1-866-463-7877 or by sending a message to support@kinaxis.com.
Kinaxis Knowledge Network
The Kinaxis Knowledge Network is the place to find knowledge about Maestro. In one site, you 
can:
 l Search all knowledge about Maestro, including product documentation, knowledge base 
articles, and threaded discussions about common questions.
 l Review your company's Customer Support requests (and, if you are a key support contact, 
create new Support requests).
 l Engage with a community of Maestro experts in the Discussion Groups.
Go to https://knowledge.kinaxis.com and log in today. You can also access the Kinaxis 
Knowledge Network from Maestro by clicking Kinaxis Knowledge Network on the Help menu.

About this guide
This application configuration guide is for the Enterprise Scheduling application.
It includes information about:
 l The overall architecture and design of the application.
 l Initial configuration of the application in Maestro. 
 l Information about all the PDRs included with the application and their purpose.
Licensing and availability
The Enterprise Scheduling application requires a license and is delivered as an Independently 
Built and Released Product (IBRP).

===== PAGE 14 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG) 4
Overview of Enterprise Scheduling
The Enterprise Scheduling application provides users with the ability to improve their productivity 
by creating production schedules that optimize available resource use while considering actual 
due dates, the relationships between individual work orders, and site-specific requirements.
Maestro uses dispatch rules and other defined constraints in the Scheduling algorithm to 
calculate a preliminary schedule. Users can review and directly modify the schedule through an 
interactive Gantt chart. In addition, they can adjust the underlying data parameters for the 
schedule using the Enterprise Scheduling Workbench and Parameters workbooks.
Depending on which version of Enterprise Scheduling you have installed, different versions of On 
Demand Maestro might be compatible. For more information about version compatibility, see 
Version compatibility.

===== PAGE 15 =====
5 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER2:IntroductiontoEnterpriseScheduling
Install the Enterprise Scheduling application
Enterprise Scheduling consists of two components:
 l Application Package (KPK): contains all of the predefined resources and configurations 
required for the application. See Enterprise Scheduling resources.
 l Application container: the container that contains and manages the Scheduling 
algorithms. This container is deployed by Kinaxis Support.
For instructions on how to import and install application packages, see the Maestro 
Administration Guide.
Installation steps for Enterprise Scheduling:
 1. Apply the Enterprise Scheduling license.
 2. Import the Enterprise Scheduling KPK into Maestro.
When defining which package content to import, for the data model namespaces only 
import the Scheduling namespace. Clear the checkboxes for Core and Mfg.
 1. Contact Kinaxis Support to have the application extension container deployed. 
 2. Import the data required to populate the Enterprise Scheduling tables. See Enterprise 
Scheduling data model.
 3. If required, configure individual data points. See Data configuration.
 4. If the customer requires customization of Enterprise Scheduling logic, modify extension 
points on the Scheduling algorithm. See Application extensions.

===== PAGE 16 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Newandchangedin2026 6
New and changed in 2026
The following service updates introduced changes to the Enterprise Scheduling application:
 l ES2606
 l ES2605
 l ES2603
 l ES2602
 l ES2601
ES2606
Display operation details in the Gantt chart
A new Gantt property is available to display text directly next to operations on the Gantt. See 
Event Description.
Enhanced precedence rule behavior with locked and frozen operations
The precedence rule that determines when successor operations can be scheduled in relation to 
their predecessors has been enhanced to support locked or frozen predecessor or successor 
operations. See Precedence rule and locked or frozen operations.
ES2605
Include operation descriptions
You can now display descriptive text beside operation segments when the Gantt displays in 
operation-based mode. See Event Description.
ES2603
Model maintenances using operation properties
To support improved precision in the generated schedule, maintenances can now be modeled 
through property relationships. For example, instead of scheduling a cleaning after 50 units of a 
part, you can use the part's color property to specify that the cleaning only takes place after 50 
units of a red part is completed. See Schedule maintenance actions.

===== PAGE 17 =====
7 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER2:IntroductiontoEnterpriseScheduling
Improved scheduling of maintenances for campaigns
In cases where a campaign doesn't have a defined maximum or the maximum is not reached, the 
Scheduling algorithm now automatically and consistently schedules maintenance operations at 
the end of campaigns. See Schedule maintenance actions.
New types of precedence relationships
Scheduling now supports start-to-finish and finish-to-finish precedence relationships which 
allows operations to be constrained based on the start or completion of downstream operations, 
with optional maximum wait duration constraints on successor completion. This enables 
accurate modeling of real-world scenarios such as starting production only when downstream 
packaging setup is complete (start-tofinish) or ensuring that a mixing or holding operation 
completes exactly when packaging finishes to avoid immediate storage (finish-to-finish). See 
Define precedence relationships.
Updated extension point
The extension point OverrideGetPotentialBatchesForCampaign was updated to include the 
AreaGroup parameter.
ES2602
Improvement for overlapping downtimes
When downtimes with different configurations overlap in the schedule, the downtime with more 
constraints is applied to the overlapping period. For example, if a downtime that allows 
operation pausing is scheduled to overlap with a downtime that doesn't allow pausing, during 
the overlapping time the downtime that doesn't allow operation pausing is used because it is 
more constrained than the other downtime. See Operation pausing and overlapping.
Maintenance operations included in historical horizon
The historical horizon for an area group now excludes maintenance operations that start before 
the historical horizon start date or within the historical horizon duration.
Making part source data available to extensions
Part sources are now defined before pegging calculations are applied to ensure that part source 
data is available to extension points.
New metric to track negative Historical Actuals Horizon values
A new metric has been added to the Enterprise Scheduling Input Data Verification workbook to 
identify negative values input for the Historical Actuals Horizon. See Review Data Issues.

===== PAGE 18 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Newandchangedin2026 8
ES2601
Precedence relationships for scheduled receipt operations
Scheduled receipt operations can now inherit precedence constraints and their settings, such as 
dependency type and wait duration, from their associated route operations when no 
predecessor is defined at the scheduled receipt level. A new field on the 
ScheduledReceiptOperation table, UseRouteOperationPrecedence, enables this behavior while 
still allowing for explicit overrides from defined scheduled receipt operation predecessor 
records. See Create scheduled receipt operations.
Updated Parameter control
The Parameter control in the Enterprise Scheduling Parameters workbook has been updated to 
display more logical worksheet groupings and allow for navigation outside a parameter group 
through drill links.
Configure BOM effectivity date enforcement
The Constrain By Bom Effectivity column was added to the Route Operation worksheet in the 
Enterprise Scheduling Parameters workbook to help users easily configure how BOM effectivity 
dates are enforced. See Define routes.

===== PAGE 19 =====
9 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER2:IntroductiontoEnterpriseScheduling
New and changed in 2025
The following service updates introduced changes to the Enterprise Scheduling application:
 l ES2512
 l ES2511
 l ES2510
 l ES2509
 l ES2506
 l ES2505
 l ES2503
 l ES2502
 l ES2501
ES2512
New columns to link part sources to resources
New columns were added to worksheets in the Enterprise Scheduling Parameters workbook to 
support the linking of part sources to resources.
Resources worksheet
 l Constraint Name
 l Constraint Site
Part Sources worksheet
 l First Effective Date
 l Last Effective Date
Route Operation worksheet
 l Part Source Effectivity Type
For more information on these fields, see the Maestro Data Model and Algorithm Guide (Java 
client).

===== PAGE 20 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Newandchangedin2025 10
ES2511
Assign secondary resources to scheduled receipt operations
You can now explicitly assign secondary resources to scheduled receipt operations by mapping 
them in the Scheduled Receipt Operation Segment Resource Assignment worksheet in the 
Enterprise Scheduling Demand workbook. This lets you specify which operation segment uses a 
specific secondary resource, the quantity consumed, and the relevant secondary resource 
requirement that sets constraints.  See Create scheduled receipt operations.
Enforcing BOM effectivity dates
You can now control if operations are strictly scheduled within BOM effective dates by 
configuring the ConstrainByBomEffectivity field for each route operation and setting the 
pegging source to one of the following:
 l SupplyPlanning
 l SchedulingUpFront
 l SchedulingUpFront AND SupplyPlanning
See Enforcing BOM effective date for operations.
Schedule operations based on resource capacity
When size data for a scheduled receipt operation is not configured or missing, you can still 
schedule operations by using their corresponding route operation sizes whenever the 
ScheduledReceiptOperation.Size < 0. See Size constraints.
Script enhancements for planned order commands
Enhancements to the underlying scripts for the Firm Planned Order and Firm Selected Planned 
Order commands make sure that scheduled receipt operation predecessor records are created 
when either command runs from the Enterprise Scheduling Workbench or Enterprise Scheduling 
Demand workbooks.

===== PAGE 21 =====
11 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER2:IntroductiontoEnterpriseScheduling
ES2510
Increased control of your schedule
To provide more granular controller over your scheduling, a new worksheet in the Enterprise 
Scheduling Demand workbook: Scheduled Receipt Operation To Operation Specification, lets 
you map scheduled receipt operations to operation specifications. For example, you can directly 
configure resource preferences, wait times, and overrides for scheduled receipt operations.
If a scheduled receipt operation has no mappings to any operation specifications, then route 
operation specifications are used for that scheduled receipt operation. This is identified by a new 
column, Use Route Operation Specification, in the Scheduled Receipt Operations worksheet in 
the Enterprise Scheduling Demand workbook.
See Create scheduled receipt operations.
Balance secondary resource assignments
A new global dispatch rule is available to resolve scheduling conflicts when a secondary resource 
is shared by multiple primary resources. This rule is applied at the area group level and is used by 
the Scheduling algorithm to determine how the secondary resources are assigned.
See Define dispatch rules.
New extension points for Backward Scheduling
New extension points have bee added to support customizations for batches of operations that 
are backward scheduled:
 l OverrideGetBackwardScheduledPredecessors: This extension allows for the 
augmentation or replacement of the list of predecessor operations that are backward 
scheduled.
 l OverrideSelectBestBackwardScheduledBatch: This extension implements custom logic 
to determine which batch out of multiple that are feasible for backward scheduling should 
be selected.
 l OverrideTargetTimeForBackwardScheduledTriggeringBatch: When predecessors that 
are backward scheduled exist, this extension allows for the calculated start time of a 
triggering batch to be overridden.
 l OverrideBackwardScheduledPredecessorTargetTime: This extension overrides the 
calculated target start or end times for predecessor batches that are backward scheduled.
See Extension points.

===== PAGE 22 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Newandchangedin2025 12
ES2509
Pause or overlap operations over non-working periods
You can configure operation segments or maintenance actions to pause or overlap over 
downtimes or off-shift periods. You can also configure operation segments to pause or overlap 
with maintenance actions. See Operation pausing and overlapping.
ES2506
Define replenishment for secondary resources
To enhance the efficiency of your schedule, you can configure secondary resources to be 
continuously replenished to a set level over the schedule. A replenishment rule is defined for a 
specified quantity and period of time and then mapped to a specific secondary resource to be 
implemented. See Define secondary resource replenishment.
Backward scheduling concurrent batches
If no changeovers are scheduled between batches, you can fully utilize resource capacity in 
backwards scheduling by scheduling operations concurrently. The backward scheduled 
operations are scheduled as early as possible based on their predecessor end times. See 
Backward scheduling.
ES2505
Schedule maintenance actions during idle time
You can now set the maintenance type to IdleDuration to schedule maintenance operations 
when no working operation segment types are present. 
See Schedule maintenance actions.

===== PAGE 23 =====
13 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER2:IntroductiontoEnterpriseScheduling
ES2503
Additional constraints respected in backwards scheduling
In the Enterprise Scheduling application, backward scheduling aligns upstream operations in the 
schedule closer to bottle necked operations while accounting for additional constraints. In 
addition to accounting for minimum wait durations between operations, backward scheduling 
now also considers the following constraints when calculating a schedule:
 l Maximum wait durations between operations
 l Maximum due date pull ahead that determines when an operation is eligible for scheduling
 l Maximum wait duration for preference time that the algorithms will wait for an operation to 
be scheduled on the resource with the highest preference
See Backward scheduling.
Enhancement to Scheduling In Process mode
The Enterprise Scheduling algorithm now considers component availability types in fulfilling 
BOM requirements when the pegging source for the schedule is set to SchedulingInProgress. 
Depending on the value set for the ComponentConsumptionType.ComponentAvailabilityType 
and if component orders are included in the data, component orders are added to allocatable 
inventory either at its completion time or on its due date.
See Component availability types and scheduling.
Define an efficiency period on a resource
Users are now able to define an efficiency value for a specific date and time for a specified 
resource. In situations where the rate calculation must be temporarily overridden, defining an 
efficiency value on a downtime will apply to operations scheduled for that downtime period. 
Depending on shift interval overlap and efficiency values, operation segments are either 
multiplied by the Downtime.Efficiency value or use the value specified for ShiftInterval.Efficiency.
See Efficiency factors in scheduling.
ES2502
Calculate operation segment durations differently
Using the new OperationSegementType.DurationCalculationModeOverride field, you can assign 
different calculation modes to each operation segment type. See Define routes.

===== PAGE 24 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Newandchangedin2025 14
Backward scheduling and maximum due date pull ahead
When backward scheduling is enabled, the maximum due date pull ahead that determines when 
operations can be scheduled is respected. 
See Backward scheduling and maximum due date pull ahead.
ES2501
New worksheet to map route operations to properties
A new worksheet, Route Operation To Property, is now available in the Enterprise Scheduling 
Parameters workbook to map properties to route operations. This allows for properties that 
change values along a route to be included in the schedule, for example the changing diameter 
of a metal coil during production.
See Define routes.
Updated columns for Maintenance Operations
The Maintenance Operations worksheet in Enterprise Scheduling Parameters has updated 
headers for the following columns:
 l Resource is now Input Primary Resource
 l Start Date Time is now Input Start Date Time
 l End Date Time is now Input Start Date Time
 l The Unconstrained column has been removed from the worksheet and underlying table.
 l A new read only column, Is Bypass, has been added to identify maintenance operations that 
have been set to unassigned and held by users. 
See Schedule maintenance actions.
Improved control over property types that display to users
All PropertyType records that have a processing rule of None are the only values that display in 
the Selected Property control in the Enterprise Scheduling Workbench workbook.
See Define properties.
Increased flexibility in defining components 
A new field, ComponentAvailabilityType, replaces the Unconstrained field to provide increased 
flexibility on defining when components become consumable to satisfy BOM requirements. 
See Component consumption types.

===== PAGE 25 =====
15 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER2:IntroductiontoEnterpriseScheduling
New and changed in 2024
The following service updates introduced changes to the Enterprise Scheduling application:
 l ES2412
 l ES2411
 l ES2410
 l ES2409
 l ES2408
 l ES2407
    l ES2406
 l ES2405
 l ES2404
 l ES2403
 l ES2402
 l ES2401
ES2412
Include maintenance actions in the schedule
You can define maintenance actions that display on the Gantt chart as part of a production 
schedule. A maintenance action is assigned to a specific primary resource and is triggered by a 
time or quantity threshold value. Maintenance actions can be set to recur at a defined interval 
and are account for other actions on the schedule such as a changeover or downtime. See 
Schedule maintenance actions.
ES2411
New option for defining relationships between operations
You can now set OperationPredecessorType.DependencySchedulingRule to EarliestTime 
to schedule successor operations as early as possible within the defined maximum wait duration 
window. See Operation Predecessor Types worksheet.
ES2410
The Scheduling algorithm supports manufacturing BOM types
When the pegging source in the schedule configuration is set to SchedulingInProgress, the 
Scheduling algorithm uses the BOM types defined by Maestro to define the BOM relationships  
between operations in the schedule. See the Maestro Data Model and Algorithm Guide (Java 
client).

===== PAGE 26 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Newandchangedin2024 16
All secondary resource requirement mapping happens in one location
All mappings of a secondary resource requirement to a group are added and configured in the 
the Secondary Resource Requirement Group Resource Mapping worksheet. See Set 
requirements for secondary resources.
ES2409
Mapping additional secondary resource requirements to groups
You can now map additional secondary resource requirement to a secondary resource 
requirement group using the Secondary Resource Requirement Group Resource Mapping 
worksheet. See Set requirements for secondary resources.
Configure campaigns
Campaigns can be set up to produce groups of consecutive operations to reduce costs incurred 
by changeovers, setup, or inventory holding. Campaigns are run for a specified time period or set 
quantity and can be applied to broad groups of products or to specific subsets of product. A 
campaign must be defined, mapped to one or more primary resources, and then the conditions 
of the campaign must be defined. See Create campaigns.
Controlling component sourcing
Users can assign component consumption types to component parts to specify the orders that 
can be used to fulfill BOM requirements. The component consumption type must be defined and 
then assigned to one or more component parts. See Define component consumption types.
ES2408
Backward scheduling
A new method of calculations has been introduced that lets you include backward scheduling 
when generating the schedule. Backward scheduling determines how best to align predecessor 
operations to successor operations — using just in time or by scheduling predecessors as early 
as possible. See Backward scheduling.
Size constraints
You can now use size to model physical constraints on primary resources such as limiting the 
number of operations that can be processed simultaneously or the maximum size of an 
operation. See Create resources.

===== PAGE 27 =====
17 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER2:IntroductiontoEnterpriseScheduling
Order priorities
Order priorities can now be included in dispatch rules and objective function components. In 
dispatch rules, order priority can be used to contribute to how operations are selected for 
scheduling. See Define dispatch rules.
For the Search Algorithm option, you can include order priorities in the score calculations for 
objective function components. See Search Algorithm option.
Inter and Intra Route predecessor parameters
New columns have been added to the Scheduling Configuration worksheet to support how 
dependencies are configured between operations in a single order (intra route) or between 
successive orders (inter route). See Create resources. 
Worksheet and column name change
The BatchFeasibilityExplanation table has been changed to OperationFeasibilityExplanation and 
accordingly the following columns and worksheet names have been updated:
 l Area Groups worksheet > Enable log to Explain Batch Feasibility is changed to Enable log 
to Explain Operation Feasibility.
 l Area Groups worksheet > Enable log to Explain Batch Feasibility Failures is changed to 
Enable log to Explain Operation Feasibility Failures.
 l The Batch Feasibility Explanation worksheet is changed to Operation Feasibility 
Explanation.
Configuring the Gantt chart
A new chapter on how to configure and modify Gantt chart properties is now available. See 
Configure  the Gantt chart.
ES2407
The following configuration points have been updated:
 l New capability to adjust the secondary resource requirement quantity based on the 
primary resource efficiency. See Create resources.
 l Define and modify scheduled receipt operations in one workbook: Enterprise Scheduling 
Demands. See Create scheduled receipt operations.
 l Determine when the secondary resource is held or released during an operation segment. 
See Set requirements for secondary resources.
 l New forms and scripts to modify and override due dates for scheduled receipt operations. 
See Enterprise Scheduling resources.

===== PAGE 28 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Newandchangedin2024 18
ES2406
Define fixed routing on resources
You can set route operations to only be produced on a specified set of primary resources by 
configuring fixed routes between resources. See Define fixed routing.
Changes to the Search Algorithm option
To provide you with more flexibility in defining how a Search Algorithm modifies the schedule to 
find the best candidate schedule by defining components that make up a Search Algorithm Rule. 
See Search Algorithm option.
More control of precedence relationships
You can define how precedence relationships with a defined maximum wait duration are defined.
New issue logging for verifying Search Algorithm configuration
Additional data issue logs have been added to the Enterprise Scheduling Input Data Verification 
workbook. The following issues are now tracked in a new Parameter Group for Search Algorithm:
 l When the cooling factor is set to value that is not between 0 and 1.
 l When the Search Algorithm rule is invalid from a number of possible conditions.
 l When a Search Algorithm rule has no components.
 l New logs  track when the combination of maximum iterations set below or above a specific 
threshold and the scoring mode or Search Algorithm rule component cause an issue.
 l New logs  track when the combination the Search Algorithm rule component and other 
settings cause an issue.
 l When no maximum runtime is set for the Search Algorithm option.
 l When the perturbation intensity factor is <= 0.
The following issues have been shifted from other parameter groups to Search Algorithm: 
 l Area groups having resources tagged Schedule by Search Algorithm without objective 
function linked
 l Area groups having resources tagged Schedule by Search Algorithm without search 
algorithm rule linked
 l Area groups with more than one primary resource group tagged Schedule by Search 
Algorithm
 l Objective function without component
 l Resource groups with Type is Secondary and tagged Schedule by Search Algorithm
See Review Data Issues.

===== PAGE 29 =====
19 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER2:IntroductiontoEnterpriseScheduling
ES2405
Log for infeasible operations
A new log that tracks which operations weren't scheduled because they were flagged as 
infeasible can be enabled for area groups. These infeasible operations are identified in the Batch 
Feasibility Explanation worksheet. Users can also enable this log in the Configuration worksheet 
in the Enterprise Scheduling Workbench workbook. See Set up an area. 
New minimum wait quantity for predecessor operations
For both route operation and scheduled receipt operation predecessors, you can specify a 
minimum wait quantity that specifies a minimum quantity of the predecessor operation that 
must be produced before its successor operation can start. See Define routes and Create 
scheduled receipt operations.
ES2404
Search Algorithm option
You can enable an option on the Scheduling algorithm that runs the algorithm multiple times to 
search for the most effective schedule. The parameters are defined when Enterprise Scheduling 
is configured in your system. Users can modify the algorithm runtimes and number of schedule 
iterations when this option is enabled. The Search Algorithm option can impact performance so 
its use and configuration should be judiciously planned.
See Search Algorithm option.
New parameter set and worksheets for Search Algorithm option
A new parameter is available in the Enterprise Scheduling Parameters workbook. The 
Search Algorithm parameter filter set contains new worksheets to configure the Search 
Algorithm option:
 l Search Algorithm Rule
 l Search Algorithm Rule Component
 l Objective Function
 l Objective Function Component
 l Objective Function Component Override
Configuration points that were previously in the Scheduling Configuration worksheet have been 
moved to one of these worksheet sets above or to the Area Group worksheet.
See Search Algorithm option.

===== PAGE 30 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Newandchangedin2024 20
New issue logging for data verification
Additional data issue logs have been added to the Enterprise Scheduling Input Data Verification 
workbook:
 l Scheduled receipt operations with assigned start and end times but with no primary 
resource assigned.
 l Areas that have no defined timezone.
In addition, issue names have been updated for consistency. See the Maestro Applications Guide 
(Java client).
ES2403
Enhancements to secondary resources
You can now configure the following on secondary resources:
 l Assign a priority that determines when that secondary resource is scheduled when there are 
multiple eligible resources available. See Create resources
 l Assign changeovers to secondary resources. 
 l How secondary resources are selected for us on a primary resource when more than one 
eligible secondary resource is available. See Set requirements for secondary resources.
 l How secondary resource available quantities are consumed. See Create resources.
Trace orders
Enterprise Scheduling users can trace how scheduled receipt orders are processed through the 
Scheduling algorithm. For details, see the Maestro Applications Guide (Java client).
Extension points and the Extension Development Kit
Using the Extension Development Kit (EDK) you can add custom logic to the Enterprise 
Scheduling application using extension points that you modify and then publish to Maestro as 
an application extension. See Application extensions.
ES2402
New forms
The Insert New Intervals and Insert Shift Pattern forms have been added to the Enterprise 
Scheduling Parameters workbook. Users can fix missing shift intervals and unmapped shift 
patterns by drilling down from issues in the Enterprise Scheduling Input Data Verification 
workbook. See Enterprise Scheduling Input Data Verification.

CHAPTER 3: Configure Enterprise Scheduling
This chapter discusses the steps for the initial configuration of Enterprise Scheduling that need to 
be done in Maestro.

===== PAGE 37 =====
27 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Data configuration
After importing the Enterprise Scheduling KPK, data must be populated in the input tables in the 
Scheduling namespace. 
The following table outlines the sets of data configuration points required to set up the 
Enterprise Scheduling application using the Enterprise Scheduling Parameters workbook.
Data 
configuration 
point
Description
Set up an area Create area groups and areas to assign to resources.
Define dispatch 
rules
Create dispatch rules to assign to resources to determine when operations are scheduled on 
that resource.
Create resources Create primary and secondary resources, resource groups, operation segments to run on 
primary resources, and assign resources to groups. 
Define shift 
patterns
Create shift patterns and intervals to define when operations are scheduled.
Define routes Define routes, route operations and segments, and predecessors.
Define fixed 
routing
Define fixed routes between primary resources that produce predecessor and successor 
operations.
Create scheduled 
receipt operations
Create scheduled receipt operations and segments.
Define operation 
specifications
Define the rules and preferences used to schedule route operations.
Define operation 
properties
Define property types and properties to assign to operations.
Define 
changeovers
Create changeovers and assign to resources.
Define downtime 
periods
Create downtimes and assign to resources.
Create campaigns Create campaigns, campaign conditions, and assign campaigns to primary resources.
Schedule 
maintenance 
actions
Create maintenance actions and map to applicable primary resources. Optionally, map 
maintenance actions to other impacted action on the schedule such as changeovers, 
downtimes, or other maintenance actions.
