EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration28
Data 
configuration 
point
Description
Define component 
consumption types
Create component consumption types and assign to component parts.
Set requirements 
for secondary 
resources
Define the parameters for secondary resources.
Define secondary 
resource 
replenishment
Define how secondary resources are replenished over time.
Search Algorithm 
option
Define parameters for the Search Algorithm option and enable it in the system.
Additional 
configuration 
points
The Configuration worksheet in the Enterprise Scheduling Workbench workbook provides a 
location for common configuration points such as enabling logging, setting a frozen 
window, or changing the schedule horizon or start date.

===== PAGE 39 =====
29 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Set up an area
Areas are the locales for a set of resources scheduled together. Typically, an area represents a 
plant, facility, or department. Each area has an assigned timezone using the IANA (Internet 
Assigned Numbers Authority) format.
Areas are collected into Area Groups which are used to calculate scheduling.


===== PAGE 40 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration30
Each area can belong to a different site and sites can span across area groups.
Data configuration
To define areas, the following steps are required:
 1. Create a new record for the area group. Some of the properties for the area group record 
determine how the schedule is configured, such as how many days the schedule displays 
data for and if it tracks and logs any data.
 2. Create a new record for the area.
Caution: Resources located in different areas, but with the same site and area group 
can't have the same name.
Tip:  Setting up areas is part of the Resources parameter. See Enterprise Scheduling 
Parameters workbook.

===== PAGE 41 =====
31 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Area Groups worksheet
Required fields
 l Name: name of the area group.
 l Start Date Time: start date and time of the schedule.
 l Horizon Duration: how long the schedule runs for.
 l Demand Horizon Duration: number of days of demand to consider for scheduling.
 l Enable log to Explain Operation Feasibility: if the system tracks and generates logs to 
explain the feasibility of operations.
 l Enable Log to Explain Operation Feasibility Failures: if the system tracks and generates 
logs to explain operations that were not scheduled because they were flagged as infeasible.
 l Enable log to Explain Batch Selection: if the system tracks and generates logs to explain 
how operations are selected.
 l l Enable log to Explain Where Consumed: if the system tracks and generates logs to 
explain the orders selected to fulfill BOM requirements.
 l Enable Log to Trace Order: if selected scheduled receipt orders are traced in the system. 
This log is used to understand how those orders are selected. 
 l Scheduling Configuration Set: the configuration applied to the schedule. See Create 
resources.
Optional fields
 l Frozen Window End Date Time: end date of the frozen window period.
 l Historical Actuals Horizon: Specifies how many days prior to the horizon’s Start Date to 
consider actual operations for scheduling. The start date for this historical horizon is 
calculated as the horizon’s Start Date – Historical Actuals Horizon (days). Only actual 
operations that start on this historical horizon Start Date or within the defined horizon for 
historical actuals are included.
 l Search Algorithm Maximum Iterations: maximum number of schedule iterations  
searched. The initial operation sequence the algorithm starts with isn't an iteration.
 l Search Algorithm Maximum Runtime: maximum number of seconds that the algorithm 
can run. This value is checked every iteration so the limit can be exceeded by the duration 
of a single iteration. A value of <=0 implies no time limit.
 l Objective Function: the objective function associated with this area group. 
 l Search Algorithm Rule Set: set of Search Algorithm parameters for this area group. These 
parameters are defined in the Search Algorithm Rule worksheet.

===== PAGE 42 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration32
 l Backward Schedule Just In Time: if backward scheduling is used, then set this value to 
determine how backward scheduled operations are aligned to success operations. The 
default value is Y. See Backward scheduling. Valid values are:
 l Y: predecessor operations are right aligned. Predecessors are scheduled just-in-time to 
fulfill successor operations.
 l N: predecessor operations are left aligned. Predecessors are scheduled as early as 
possible without being scheduled before a prior successor operation.
Areas worksheet
Required fields
 l Name: the name for the area.
 l Area Group: select the area group to assign this area to.
 l Site: the site associated with the area.
 l Time Zone: specify the time zone for the area. This is defined using the IANA (Internet 
Assigned Numbers Authority) format.

===== PAGE 43 =====
33 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Define dispatch rules
Dispatch rules are defined by your organization and are used to determine when eligible 
operations can be scheduled on a resource. A dispatch rule is made up of one or more 
components which define the conditions for the rule. Each component specifies a property for 
the condition and how eligible operations will be sorted and scheduled based on a defined 
hierarchy or calculated score.
For example, you can schedule a dispatch rule that uses two properties (changeover and slack 
time) to determine scheduling. This is configured by creating two components for the rule. One 
component is assigned the property type “changeover duration” and a weight of 1 and the other 
component is assigned “slack time” for its property type and a weight of 2. When applied, the 
rule looks for operations with the lowest changeover duration and schedules them first. When it 
comes to operations that have the same changeover duration, it looks to each operation's slack 
time to determine which operation to schedule next.
Global dispatch rules
Dispatch rules are applied to individual resources to allow for maximum customization. However, 
when multiple primary resources in the same area group use dispatch rules with incomparable 
properties share the same secondary resource, it can lead to scheduling conflicts. For this 
situation, you can enable a global dispatch rule for the area group that uses a specified dispatch 
rule to equitably share the secondary resource between the primary resources.
For example, two primary resources use the same secondary resource "Y". Primary resource 34 is 
assigned a dispatch rule that prioritizes operations for scheduling with the earliest due date and 
primary resource 23 prioritizes operations with the lowest changeover penalties. These 
properties are not directly comparable, so a global dispatch has been defined that uses the 
minimum critical ration value to prioritize which operations from both resources are allocated to 
the secondary resource. 
Types of dispatch rules
Dispatch rules can be hierarchical or weighted rules and are made up of one or more 
components that determined how the operations are scheduled. 
Hierarchical dispatch rules
Hierarchical dispatch rules sort and schedule eligible operations based on a defined hierarchy. 
The rule's components define how eligible operations are sorted for each level of the hierarchy 
using a defined property and score types, and a weighting value. The property type and score 
type determine which eligible operations are sorted to the top of the list. The weighting value 
represent which level in the hierarchy the operation is assigned to.

===== PAGE 44 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration34
Weighted dispatch rules
Weighted dispatch rules sort and schedule eligible operations based on a calculated score. The 
score is calculated using a weighted expression. The components in a weighted dispatch rule 
represent the values used in the weighted expression that determine when eligible operations 
are scheduled. The property type for an operation is determined by its score type.
Order priorities
One of the property types you can apply to a dispatch rule component is order priority which 
uses the assigned planning priority value to determine how operations are scheduled. 
When a dispatch rule is only made up of one component that uses the property type 
OrderPriority and score type Minimum, operations are scheduled in ascending order based 
on the planning priority value for that order. Specifically for planned orders, the 
PlannedOrder.OrderPriority.PlanningPriority value is used, and for scheduled receipts the 
ScheduledReceipt.EffOrderPriority.PlanningPriority value is used. The planning priority value 
sets the relative importance of each work item in the order with lower values representing higher 
effective priority.
Examples
For a hierarchical dispatch rule made up of two components:
 l Component 1: property type = OrderPriority, and Score = Minimum
 l Component 2: property type = ChangeoverPenalty, and Score = Minimum
Orders are sequenced in ascending order based on their planning priority values. For two orders 
with the same priority value the ChangeoverPenalty is used to determine which to schedule first.
For a weighted dispatch rule made up of two components:
 l Component 1: property type = OrderPriority, and Score = Weighted
 l Component 2: property type = ChangeoverPenalty, and Score = Weighted
 Orders are sequenced in ascending order based on the result from (weight value * planning 
priority value) + (ChangeoverPenalty * 1, else 0).

===== PAGE 45 =====
35 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Overrides
You can override the scores and weighting values defined for dispatch rules. For hierarchical 
dispatch rules you specify maximum and minimum scores that will override the currently defined 
score if the override criteria are met. For weighted dispatch rules you can override the value 
specified for the component weighting.
Three different input tables are used to define dispatch rules:
 l DispatchRule: This table contains information about the type of rule used to sort and 
schedule operations. Dispatch rules can be hierarchical or weighted types of rules. See 
Types of dispatch rules.
 l DispatchRuleComponent: This table contains information on the components associated 
with a dispatch rule. The components determine how eligible operations are sorted to be 
scheduled. A dispatch rule must contain at least one component.
 l DispatchRuleComponentOverride: This table contains override values for different 
dispatch rule parameters including score and weighting overrides.
Note: Dispatch rules can be added or modified at any time, including by users.

===== PAGE 46 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration36
Data configuration
To define dispatch rules, the following steps are required:
 1. Create a new  record for the dispatch rule.
 2. Create one or more new records for the dispatch rule components.
 3. Optionally, create one or more records to override score or weighting values in certain 
situations.
 4. Optionally, create a new record to define the mapping between an area group and a 
dispatch rule to enable a global dispatch rule.
Dispatch Rules worksheet
Required fields
 l Name: the name of the dispatch rule.
 l Area Group: the area group for the area associated with this dispatch rule.
 l Area Site: the site for the area associated with this dispatch rule.
 l Area: the area associated with this dispatch rule. Click Add to create a new Area record.
 l Type: the type of rule. Select between hierarchical and weighted. See Types of dispatch 
rules.
Optional fields
 l  Maximum Due Date Pull Ahead: specify a number of days before an operation can be 
considered eligible for scheduling.

===== PAGE 47 =====
37 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Dispatch Rule Component Detail worksheet
Required fields
 l Area Group: the area group the area associated with this component.
 l Area Site: the site for the area associated with this component.
 l Area: the area associated with this component.
 l Dispatch Rule: the dispatch rule associated with this component. Click Add to create a new 
Dispatch Rule record.
 l Property Type Name: Select the property type to apply to this dispatch rule. Click Add to 
create a new Property Type record.
 l Score Type: Depending on which type of dispatch rule this component is associated with 
select the following: 
For hierarchical dispatch rules:
 l Maximum: sorts operations in descending order.
 l Minimum: sorts operations in ascending order.
For weighted dispatch rules:
 l Weighted: the defined values are used as input values into the weighted expression to 
determine how orders are scheduled.
 l Weighting: specify a numeric value for the weight.

===== PAGE 48 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration38
Dispatch Rule Component Override worksheet
The Dispatch Rule Component Override worksheet defines the values used to override score or 
weighting values for dispatch rule components. Configuring data in this worksheet is optional.
Overrides are triggered when the component score falls within a defined range of maximum and 
minimum values. For example, a minimum score of 2 and maximum of 6 defines a range where 
the dispatch rule component score must be above 2 or below 6 to trigger the overrides.
You can set inclusive fields to include the maximum and minimum values in the range. For 
example, a minimum score of 2, maximum of 6, and MaximumScoreInclusive = Y defines a range 
where the dispatch rule component score must be above 2 or any value up to and including 6 to 
trigger the overrides.
Required fields
 l Area Group: the area group for the area associated with this override.
 l Area Site: the site for the area associated with this override.
 l Area:  the area associated with this override.
 l Dispatch Rule Name: the dispatch rule associated with this override.
 l Property Type Name: select the property to associate with the dispatch rule.
 l Score Type: depending on which type of dispatch rule this component is associated with 
select the following:
For hierarchical dispatch rules:
 l Maximum: sorts operations in descending order.
 l Minimum: sorts operations in ascending order.
For weighted dispatch rules:
 l Weighted: the defined values are used as input values into the weighted expression to 
determine how orders are scheduled.
 l Priority: the numeric values used to determine which record takes precedence when two 
dispatch rule component records overlap. Records with lower priority are scheduled first.
 l Minimum Score: the minimum value for the range.
 l Minimum Score Inclusive: to include the minimum value as part of the range, set to Y.
 l Maximum Score: the maximum value for the range.
 l Maximum Score Inclusive: to include the maximum value as part of the range, set to Y.
 l Score Override: the value that overrides the score specified in the Dispatch Rule 
Component Detail worksheet.
 l Weighting Override: the value that overrides the weighting specified in the Dispatch Rule 
Component Detail worksheet.

===== PAGE 49 =====
39 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Area Group To Dispatch Rule
A global dispatch rule is set at the area group level and uses the property from a defined 
dispatch rule to assign the secondary resource to each primary resource. 
Required fields
 l Area Group Name: the area group that contains the primary and secondary resources that 
you want the global rule to apply to.
 l Dispatch Rule Name: the dispatch rule that will be applied globally when applicable.
 l Dispatch Rule Area Site: the site for the area that the dispatch rule is associated with.
 l Dispatch Rule Area Name: the area that the dispatch rule is associated with.

===== PAGE 50 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration40
Create resources
Resources represent the apparatus required to produce an order and can be primary resources 
or secondary resources. Each resource is assigned a resource type that determines its class 
(primary or secondary) and other properties such as if operations can pause on or span over the 
resource or how durations for scheduled receipt operation are calculated on the resource. Use 
the Resource To Resource Group worksheet to review which resource groups a resource 
belongs to.
Resources that are scheduled together are grouped into areas which in turn are collected into 
area groups. Each area group has a scheduling configuration applied to the schedule which 
determines pegging rules and sources for the schedule, how predecessors for inter-route and 
intra-route operations are configured, and other properties such as if application extensions are 
enabled or if planned orders are included in the schedule. See Set up an area and Define 
precedence relationships.
Primary resources
Primary resources carry out manufacturing processes, for example machinery or workstations, 
and are represented as swimlanes on the Gantt chart in the Enterprise Scheduling Workbench 
workbook.


===== PAGE 51 =====
41 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Operations are scheduled on primary resources and display as operation bars on the Gantt chart.
Size constraints
You can model the physical constraints of primary resource by apply size constraints on the 
resource and on operations that run on that resource.
At the resource level, you can define the following size constraints:
 l MaximumOperationSize: the maximum size of an operation that can be scheduled on the 
resource. This value must be > 0.
 l MaximumTotalSize: the maximum total size of all operations that can be simultaneously 
scheduled on the resource. This value must be > 0.
At the operation level, you can define sizes for route operations and scheduled receipt 
operations that are then used to assess if the operation can be scheduled on a resource with size 
constraints applied to it. If there are scheduled receipt operations with size values that are not 
defined or missing, you can use the size values set for corresponding route operations by setting 
the Use Route Operation Specification field in the Scheduled Receipt Operations worksheet to 
Y.

===== PAGE 52 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration42
Secondary resources
Secondary resources typically represent labor or tools and affect production on primary 
resources such as when operations can be scheduled or  the maximum number of operations that 
can be scheduled in parallel.
On the Gantt chart, secondary resources display in their own swimlanes as shaded area charts.
Data configuration
To create resources, the following steps are required:
 1. Optionally create a new record for a scheduling configuration.
 2. Optionally, create a new record for the resource type.
 3. Create a new record for the resource group.
 4. Create a new record for the resource.
 5. Create a new record to map the resource to a resource group.

===== PAGE 53 =====
43 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Scheduling Configurations worksheet
Required fields
 l Value: the name of this scheduling configuration.
Inter Route Predecessors
The following Inter Route Predecessor fields are used to configure dependencies between 
the operations of one order and the operations of its successive order.
 l Inter Route Predecessor Control Set: the control set for this inter route predecessor.
 l Inter Route Predecessor Type: select which predecessor type to use for precedence 
relationships between the last operation in the preceding order and the first operation in its 
successive order. 
 l Inter Route Predecessor Maximum Wait Duration: specify the maximum time allowed 
between the completion of the last operation in the preceding order and the first operation 
in its successive order. Measured in days. 
 l Zero or positive values: applies the maximum wait duration between operations.
 l Negative values: no time restriction is applied between operations.
 l Inter Route Predecessor Minimum Wait Duration: specify the minimum time allowed 
between the completion of the last operation in the preceding order and the first operation 
in its successive order. Measured in days. 
Intra Route Predecessors
The following Intra Route Predecessor fields are used to configure dependencies between 
the operations in a Scheduled Receipt or Planned Order. Intra route predecessors are only 
required for route operations that are multiple steps.
 l Intra Route Predecessor Control Set: the control set for this intra route predecessor.
 l Intra Route Predecessor Type: select which predecessor type to use for precedence 
relationships between the operations in a Scheduled Receipt or Planned Order. 
 l Intra Route Predecessor Maximum Wait Duration: specify the maximum time allowed 
between the operations in a Scheduled Receipt or Planned Order. Measured in days. 
 l Zero or positive values: applies the maximum wait duration between operations.
 l Negative values: no time restriction is applied between operations.
 l Intra Route Predecessor Minimum Wait Duration: specify the minimum time allowed 
between the operations in a Scheduled Receipt or Planned Order. Measured in days.

===== PAGE 54 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration44
 l Enable Application Extensions: select to allow application extensions to run in your 
system. 
 l Pegging Rule: select a pegging rule to apply to the schedule:
 l FirstInFirstOut: completed work items are pegged to orders based on completion 
order.
 l QuantityMatching: completed works items are pegged to orders based on how closely 
quantities match.
 l Pegging Source: select a pegging source for the schedule:
 l SchedulingUpFront: pegging decisions are completed before generating the schedule.
 l SchedulingInProcess: orders are organized based on the value specified for the 
Pegging Rule.
 l SupplyPlanning: orders are organized based on Maestro planning decisions.
 l SchedulingUpFrontAndSupplyPlanning: Pegging constraints from upstream supply 
planning are first applied and then those constraints are modified by upfront extension 
logic before the scheduling algorithm runs.
 l Schedule Planned Orders: select to include planned orders in the schedule.
 l Changeover Caching Mode: specify how changeover results are cached.
 l None: changeover results are not cached.
 l ByOperation: Changeover results are cached by operation. This option is 
recommended when operations and parts have multiple properties.
 l ByProperty: Changeover results are cached by property. This option is recommended 
when operations and parts have few properties or if there are many operations.
Tip:  When the Search Algorithm option is enabled, then set this value to either 
ByOperation or ByProperty to reduce the time used in calculating changeovers by 
caching them. This can improve performance times when the algorithm runs multiple 
times. See Search Algorithm option.

===== PAGE 55 =====
45 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Resource Types worksheet
Required fields
 l Value: the name of the resource type.
 l Control Set: the control set associated with this resource type. Typically this is set to 
Default.
 l Allow Operation Pausing: identify if operation pausing is allowed for this resource type.
 Enabling operation pausing allows operations to pause on the resource when it has a 
downtime or non-production period and then continue with the operation when the 
machine is running again.
 l Allow Operation Shift Spanning: identify if shift spanning is allowed for this resource 
type.
Shift panning allows operations to span across a resource when a shift pattern day changes.
 l Class: which class resources of this type will belong to: Primary or Secondary.
 l Locking Mode: select a method to lock the sequence of operations for this resource type:
 l Day: operations locked by the user will be left-aligned to the beginning of the day.
 l Horizon: operations locked by the user will be left-aligned to the beginning of the 
horizon. 
 l Shift: operations locked by the user will be left-aligned to the beginning of the shift.
 l Time: operations locked by the user will remain at the date and time they were 
scheduled.
 l Maximum Percentage Duration Increase for Pausing: the maximum percentage value 
that an operation duration can be increased and still considered valid when the operation is 
paused across a downtime for this resource type.
 l Minimum Duration Before Pausing: the number of days that operations should have 
been started from before operation pausing can be applied for this resource type.
 l Minimum Duration Before Shift Span): the number of days that operations should have 
been started from before shift spanning can be applied for this resource type.
 l Duration Calculation Mode: select how the primary resource for this resource type 
calculates operation segment durations:
 l Duration: the Duration or Duration Override value from one the following tables is 
used. The tables are ordered by priority with a defined value in the first table taking 
precedence over a value in the second table and so forth.
 1. OperationSpecificationResourceSegmentOverride
 2. OperationSpecificationSegmentOverride
 3. ScheduledReceiptOperationSegment (used only for Scheduled Receipts)
 4. RouteOperationSegment (used only for Planned Orders)

===== PAGE 56 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration46
 l Rate: the value from the duration calculation (operation order quantity / rate) is used. 
Depending on the order type, the quantity used is either 
PlannedWorkOrderItem.Quantity or ScheduleReceipt.Quantity. The rate is taken 
from the defined Rate or RateOverride value from one of the following tables. The tables 
are ordered by priority with a defined value in the first table taking precedence over a 
value in the second table and so forth.
 1. OperationSpecificationResourceSegmentOverride
 2. OperationSpecificationSegmentOverride
 3. ScheduledReceiptOperationSegment (used only for Scheduled Receipts)
 4. RouteOperationSegment (used only for Planned Orders)
 5. Resource
 l UpstreamRate: the value from the calculation (operation order quantity / rate of 
immediate predecessor operation) is used. The rate used depends on the duration 
calculation mode applied to the resource for the predecessor operation.
 l Duration: the duration for the route operation segment or scheduled receipt operation 
segment associated with an operation is used.
 l Rate: a rate calculated by multiplying the operation order quantity by the operation or 
resource rate.
 l UpstreamRate: a duration (either duration or rate-based) calculated from the operation's 
immediate predecessor.
 l Secondary Resource Consumption Rule: select the rule to apply to determine how the 
available quantity of a secondary resource is consumed:
 l QtyPerOperation: the quantity consumed is the default quantity specified for the 
secondary resource requirement. This is the most common rule used for standard labor 
and tooling.
 l QtyPerWorkItemQuantity: the quantity consumed is the value from multiplying the 
secondary resource requirement quantity by the work item quantity.
 l QtyPerOperationRate: the quantity consumed is the rate calculated by multiplying the 
secondary resource requirement quantity by the primary resource production rate.
 l Secondary Resource Apply Primary Resource Efficiency: specify if the secondary 
resource requirement quantity should be adjusted based on the efficiency of the primary 
resource.
Valid values include:
 l True: the secondary resource required quantity is multiplied by the primary resource 
efficiency value. If this adjusted quantity of the required resource is available, the 
operation is then eligible to be scheduled.
 l False: the secondary resource required quantity doesn't change.
 l Enable Qualifications: Specify in this resource can have fixed routing for operations 
applied to it. Select from:

===== PAGE 57 =====
47 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
 l True: all resources are ineligble except those resources specified in a record on the 
PredecessorResourceEligiblity table.
 l False: all resources are eligible except for resources set as disqualified in the 
PredecessorResourceEligiblity table.
 l Actual Duration Calculation: Select how the duration of a scheduled receipt operation is 
calculated using the remaining quantity.
Valid values are:
 l Fixed: The duration uses the original planned duration (actual start time + original 
planned duration). 
 l Nominal: The duration is calculated based on the planned rate (planned rate * 
remaining quantity). 
 l Realized: The duration is calculated based on the realized rate (realized rate * remaining 
quantity). 
Resource Groups worksheet
Required fields
 l Name: the name of the resource group.
 l Sort Key: an integer value used to sort the resource.
 l Type: identify if it's grouping of primary or secondary resources.
 l Schedule by Search Algorithm: if the Search Algorithm option is used on this resource 
group to find a better schedule than the one that was generated. The default value is N.
 l Backward Schedule: if backward scheduling is applied to scheduling. The default value is 
N. See Backward scheduling.
Resources worksheet
Required fields
 l Name: specify the name of the resource.
 l Area records (Group, Site, Name): select the area associated with the resource. Click Add 
to create a new Area record.
 l Type: identify if it's a primary or secondary resource.
 l Resource Type Name: select the type of resource.
 l Resource Type Control Set: select the control set associated with this resource type. 
Typically this is set to Default.
 l Dispatch Rule Name: select the dispatch rule to apply to the resource. Click Add to create 
a new Dispatch Rule record.

===== PAGE 58 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration48
 l Maximum Concurrent Batches: specify a number > 0.
 l Rate (per day): specify the production rate of the resource.
 l Efficiency: specify a value to be applied to the resource as a scaling factor.
 l Sort Key: specify a numeric value that is used to sort resources.
 l Maximum Operation Size: specify the maximum size an operation can be to be scheduled 
on this resource. This field only applies to primary resources that you want to apply size 
constraints on.
 l Maximum Total Size: specify the maximum total size of all operations that can be 
simultaneously scheduled on this resource. This field only applies to primary resources that 
you want to apply size constraints on.
 l Constraint Site: select the site for the constraint associated with this resource.
 l Constraint Name: select the constraint to associated with this resource.
Resource Group To Resource worksheet
Required fields
 l Area Group: the area group for the area associated with the resource group.
 l Area Site: the site for the area associated with the resource group.
 l Area: the area associated with the resource group.
 l Resource: the name of the resource to map to the group.
 l Resource Group: the name of the resource group.
 l Priority: assign a value to the resource to determine its priority when multiple eligible 
secondary resources are available. A lower value corresponds to higher priority.
Define shift patterns
Shift patterns define the uptime for resources and have to be defined in the system to schedule 
operations on a resource. Using shift patterns you can specify specific available times and 
quantities to model recurring multi-day patterns.
Data configuration
To create shift patterns, the following steps are required:
 1. Create a new record for the shift pattern.
 2. Create a new record for the shift interval.
 3. Create a new record to map the shift pattern to a resource.
 4. Optionally, create one or more records to override shift patterns.

===== PAGE 59 =====
49 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Shift Patterns worksheet
Required fields
 l Name: the name of the shift pattern.
 l Area Group: the area group for the area associated with this shift pattern.
 l Area Site: the site for the area associated with this shift pattern.
 l Area: the area associated with this shift pattern.
 l Pattern Duration Days: specify the number of days for the pattern 
Shift Intervals worksheet
Required fields
 l Area Group: the area group for the area for the shift pattern associated with this shift 
interval.
 l l Area Site: the site for the area for the shift pattern associated with this shift interval.
 l Area: the area for the shift pattern associated with this shift interval.
 l Shift Pattern: the name of the shift pattern associated with this shift interval.
 l Day of Pattern: the specific day (represented as a number) that the interval starts.
 l Start Time: the time the shift interval starts.
 l End Time: the time the shift interval ends.
 l Efficiency: the scaling factor on the operation rate of the shift. Must be >=0.
Shift Pattern Resource Availability worksheet
For resources with shift intervals that cross Daylight Savings Time (DST) dates, multiple shift 
pattern resource availability records must be created — one for each DST period. For example, if 
a resource is available from September through to December and DST ends November 5, one 
record with a start date in September and an end date on November 4 must be created. A 
second record with start and end dates from November 5 to December must also be created. 
These two records will ensure that the schedule respects DST without having the schedule run an 
inaccurate time after November 5.
Required fields
 l Area Group: the area group for the area for the resource associated with the shift pattern.
 l Area Site: the site for the area for the resource associated with the shift pattern.
 l Area: the area for the resource associated with the shift pattern.

===== PAGE 60 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration50
 l Resource: the name of the resource that the shift pattern is mapped to.
 l Shift Pattern: the name of the shift pattern .
 l Apply Start Date: the date the resource is available to start.
 l Apply End Date: the date the resource is no longer available.
 l Override: set to Y if the record should override other records when they overlap in the shift 
pattern.
 l Quantity Available: the number of available resources in the shift pattern. Quantity values 
greater than 1 are only applicable to secondary resources. For primary resources, any 
quantity value > 1 is assumed to be 1 by the Scheduling algorithm.
 l Apply Start Date Offset Days: the number of offset days applied to the start date of the 
shift pattern.
Shift Availability Override worksheet
This worksheet defines the period when a shift pattern can be overridden on a resource. 
Configuring data in this worksheet is optional.
 l Area Group: the area group for the area associated with this override.
 l Area Site: the site for the area associated with this override.
 l Area: the area associated with this override.
 l Shift Interval Shift Pattern: the shift pattern to override.
 l Shift Interval Day of Pattern: the specific day (represented as a number) that the shift 
interval associated with the specified shift pattern starts.
 l Shift Interval Start Time: the time that the shift interval associated with the specified shift 
pattern start.
 l Shift Pattern Resource Availability Shift Pattern: the shift pattern resource availability 
record associated with the specified shift pattern.
 l Shift Pattern Resource Availability Resource: the resource associated with the specified 
shift pattern.
 l Shift Pattern Resource Availability Apply Start Date: the date that the resource 
associated with the specified shift pattern is available to start.
 l Quantity Available: the quantity available for the override.

===== PAGE 61 =====
51 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Define routes
Routing data provides the set of operations required to manufacture a specified part and 
supports the generation of planned order operations to be scheduled. Routes define the 
manufacturing path of operations and are made up of one or more route operations. Every route 
is assigned a route type which determines how those operations are processed along the route.
Route operations are divided into one or more segments. For example, a sub-assembly 
operation can contain three segments: loading, working, and unloading. Each segment is 
assigned a type which defines how the segments are run on a resource during an operation.
Note: Each operation segment type can calculate the duration for a segment differently 
when you specify a duration calculation mode override value for the type.
For routes with multiple route operations, you must create predecessor relationships to organize 
how production flows through each operation. See Define precedence relationships.

===== PAGE 62 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration52
Data configuration
To create routes, the following steps are required:
 1. If required, create a new record for a route type.
 2. Optionally, exclude a part from scheduling or define it's lot size.
 3. Create a new record for the route. You can do this by inserting a new record in the Routes 
worksheet (Option A) or by opening the Create Route from Part Source form in the Part 
Sources worksheet (Option B).
 4. Create new records for route operations.
 5. Optionally map a route to a property
 6. Optionally, create a new record for a new operation segment type.
 7. Create new records for route operation segments.
 8. Optionally, create a new record for the operation predecessor type.
 9. Create new records to define predecessor relationships between operations.
Route Type worksheet
Required fields
 l Value: specify the name of the route type.
 l Control Set: select the control set associated with this route type. Typically this is set to 
Default.
 l Precedence Mode: select how operations move through this route type. Select from:
 l OperationPredecessor: each operation starts after a preceding operation completes 
and only after meeting constraint requirements that have been defined in the Route 
Operation Predecessor worksheet.
 l OperationPredecessorAndSequence: each operation starts after a preceding 
operation is completed. Each route operation is ranked using a sequence value.
Parts worksheet
Optional fields
 l Exclude From Scheduling: select to exclude that part from the schedule.
 l Lot Size: specify the maximum size for a planned order work item for this part.

===== PAGE 63 =====
53 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Option 1: Routes worksheet
Required fields
 l Name: specify the name of the route.
 l Part Source records (Base Key, Part Site, Part, Destination Site,  Source ID, Supplier 
Id): select the part source associated with this route. All values must be populated.
 l Route Type: select the type for this route. The type determines how operations for the 
route will be processed. Click Add to create a new Route Type record.
 l Route Type Control Set: select the control set associated with this route type. Typically 
this is set to Default.
 l Preference: specify the preferred route to use for new planned orders when a part source 
has multiple possible routes that could be used.
Option B: Part Sources worksheet
The records in the Part Sources worksheet are view only, however you can create a new route 
based on a specific part source by opening the Create Route from Part Source form.
 l In the Part Sources worksheet, click the form icon 
  in the column after Allotment 
Override ID.
Required fields
 l Route Name: specify the name of the route.
 l Control Set: select the control set associated with the route type. Typically this is set to 
Default.
 l Route Type: select the type for this route. The type determines how operations for the 
route will be processed.
 l Preference: specify a numeric value to indicate a preference for this route which 
determines if it is a preferred route for planned order operations that use a part source that 
has multiple possible routes available.

===== PAGE 64 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration54
Route Operation worksheet
Required fields
 l Name: specify the name of the route operation.
 l Route: select the route associated with this route operation. Click Add to create a new 
route.
 l Sequence: specify the sequence number assigned to the route operation that determines 
when the operation is processed in the route.
 l Part Source records (Base Key,  Part Site, Part, Source Destination Site, Source 
Supplier Site, Source ID, Source Supplier Id): the part source associated with this route 
operation. All values must be populated.
 l Part Source Effectivity Type: select how part source compatibility and effectivity windows 
are applied when selecting a resource and start time for an operation. Valid values include: 
 l Unconstrained: Neither part source compatibility or effectivity windows are enforced. 
Operations can be scheduled on any eligible resource at any time.
 l EffectivityWindow: Only the effectivity window is enforced.
 l EffectivityWindowAndOrderPartSource: Both part source compatibility and the 
effectivity window are enforced.
 l Constrain by Bom Effectivity: select how the route operation is constrained by BOM 
effectivity windows. Valid values include:
 l False: The first and last effective BOM dates are ignored and the operation can be 
scheduled at any time. This is the default.
 l True: The route operation must start within the BOM effectivity window 
(BillOfMaterialFirstEffectiveDate to BillOfMaterialLastEffectiveDate)
 l Primary Resource Name: select the primary resource that the route operation will be 
processed on.
 l Primary Resource Group : select the resource group that the primary resource belongs to. 
Primary resources can belong to multiple groups.
 l Primary Resource records (Area Group, Area Site, Area): select the associated area for 
the primary resource that the route operation will be processed on.
Optional fields
 l Description: specify description of the route operation.
 l Size: specify a size for the operation that will determine if it can be scheduled on a 
physically constraint primary resource.

===== PAGE 65 =====
55 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Route Operation To Property worksheet
Mapping a route operation to a property allows for properties with that change values along a 
route to be scheduled. For example, the width of a metal coil changes as it is processed through 
each operation as the coil is trimmed.
Required fields
 l Property Property Type: select the type for the property to associate with the route 
operation.
 l Property Name: select the property to associate with the route operation.
 l Route Operation Name: select the route operation.
 l Route Operation Route: select the route associated with the route operation.
 l Route Operation Sequence: specify the same sequence number assigned to the selected 
route operation.
 l Route Operation Part Source records (Base Key, Site, Part, Destination Site, Supplier 
Site, ID, Supplier Id): specify properties for the part source associated with the route 
operation.
Operation Segment Type
Required fields
 l Value: the name of the operation segment type.
 l Control Set: the control set associated with this segment type. Typically this is set to 
Default.
 l Allow Operation Pausing: select to allow operation pausing on a resource.
 l Allow Operation Shift Spanning: select to allow operation shift spanning on a resource.
 l Duration Calculation Mode Override: select which mode to use to calculate durations for 
this operation segment type. Valid values include:
 l Duration: the Duration or Duration Override value from one the following tables is 
used. The tables are ordered by priority with a defined value in the first table taking 
precedence over a value in the second table and so forth.
 1. OperationSpecificationResourceSegmentOverride
 2. OperationSpecificationSegmentOverride
 3. ScheduledReceiptOperationSegment (used only for Scheduled Receipts)
 4. RouteOperationSegment (used only for Planned Orders)
 l Rate: the value from the duration calculation (operation order quantity / rate) is used. 
Depending on the order type, the quantity used is either 
PlannedWorkOrderItem.Quantity or ScheduleReceipt.Quantity. The rate is taken 

===== PAGE 66 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration56
from the defined Rate or RateOverride value from one of the following tables. The tables 
are ordered by priority with a defined value in the first table taking precedence over a 
value in the second table and so forth.
 1. OperationSpecificationResourceSegmentOverride
 2. OperationSpecificationSegmentOverride
 3. ScheduledReceiptOperationSegment (used only for Scheduled Receipts)
 4. RouteOperationSegment (used only for Planned Orders)
 5. Resource
 l Upstream Rate: the value from the calculation (operation order quantity / rate of 
immediate predecessor operation) is used. The rate used depends on the duration 
calculation mode applied to the resource for the predecessor operation.
 l None: the value specified for ResourceType.DurationCalculationMode is used.
 l Value Add: select to identify this segment type as a value-add to producing the operation. 
Note: When creating an operation segment type for maintenance actions, do not allow 
operation pausing or value add. Neither of these properties are applicable to 
maintenance actions. See Schedule maintenance actions.

===== PAGE 67 =====
57 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Route Operation Segment worksheet
Required fields
 l Route Operation: the name of the route operation associated with this route operation 
segment.
 l Route Operation Route: the route associated with the route operation.
 l Route Operation Sequence: the sequence number for the route operation. Use 1 when 
there is only one segment in the operation.
 l Route Operation Route Part Source fields (Base Key, Part Site, Part Name, Source ID, 
Source Supplier Id): the part source associated with this route operation segment. All 
values must be populated.
 l Operation Segment Type: identifies if the route operation segment is working or a 
changeover segment. Click Add to create a new Operation Segment Type record.
 l Operation Segment Type Control Set: the control set associated with this operation 
segment type. Typically this is set to Default.
 l Duration (days): the number of days that this segment requires to be produced on a 
resource.
 l Rate (per day): The production rate for this segment.
 l Sequence: specify a sequence number for the route operation segment.
Operation Predecessor Types worksheet
Required fields
 l Value: the name of this operation predecessor type.
 l Control Set: the control set associated with this operation predecessor type. Typically this 
is set to Default.
 l Precedence Rule: the precedence rule to apply to this type. Select from:
 l EndFromPredecessorEnd: the successor operation must be finished at the same time 
as its predecessor operation finishes production.
 l EndFromPredecessorStart: the successor operation must be finished before its 
predecessor can start production.
 l StartFromPredecessorEnd: the successor operation is eligible to run at the end of the 
production time of its predecessor.
 l StartFromPredecessorStart: the successor operation is eligible to run at the start of 
the production time of its predecessor.

===== PAGE 68 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration58
 l Dependency Scheduling Rule: set the rule to determine how predecessor and successor 
operations are scheduled when RouteOperationPredecessor.MaximumWaitDuration or 
ScheduledReceiptOperationPredecessor.MaximumWaitDuration >= 0. 
Select:
 l EarliestTime: the predecessor operation is only scheduled if its successor operation can 
be scheduled within the maximum wait duration window. The successor operation is 
scheduled as early as possible.
 l SchedulingRule: the predecessor operation isn’t constrained by when its successor 
operation can be scheduled.
 l Maximum Wait - Exceeded Rule: define how operations are handled when the maximum 
wait duration between the predecessor and success operations is exceeded. This rule only 
applies when the Dependency Scheduling Rule is set to SchedulingRule. 
Select from:
 l AllowExceedMaximumWaitDuration: allow the maximum wait duration to be 
exceeded.
 l UnscheduleSuccessor: the successor operation is removed from the schedule and only 
the predecessor operation is scheduled.
 l UnschedulePredecessorAndSuccessor: both successor and predecessor operations 
are removed from the schedule
 l Maximum Wait - Outside Horizon Rule: define how predecessor operations are 
scheduled if their successor operations are not scheduled within the scheduling horizon  
and the end time for the maximum wait duration is outside the scheduling horizon. This 
rule only applies when the Dependency Scheduling Rule is set to SchedulingRule.
Select from:
 l SchedulePredecessor: the predecessor operation is scheduled.
 l UnschedulePredecessor: the predecessor operation is removed from the schedule.
Notes: 
 l Locked operations respect the dependency constraints set by value set for the 
DependencySchedulingRule.
 l Operations that can be scheduled are logged in the Operation Feasibility Explanation 
and Trace Operation Explanation worksheets in the Enterprise Scheduling Analysis 
workbook. See Enterprise Scheduling Analysis workbook.

===== PAGE 69 =====
59 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Route Operation Predecessor worksheet
Required fields
 l Predecessor Route Operation fields (including Route, Sequence): the route operation 
that will be the preceding step. All values must be populated.
 l Route Operation fields (including Route, Sequence): the route operation that will follow 
the preceding route operation selected above. All values must be populated.
 l Predecessor Route Operation Route Part Source fields (Part Site, Base Key, Part 
Name, Source ID, Source Supplier Id): the part source associated with this route 
operation predecessor. All values must be populated.
 l Route Operation Route Part Source fields  (Part Site, Base Key, Part Name, Source 
ID, Source Supplier Id): the part source associated with this route operation. All values 
must be populated.
 l Maximum Wait Duration (days): the maximum time allowed between these operations. 
Use -1 if no wait time is required.
 l Minimum Wait Duration (days): the minimum time allowed between these operations. 
Use 0 if no wait time is required.
 l Minimum Wait Quantity: the minimum quantity of the predecessor that must be 
produced before its successor operation can be started.
 l Operation Predecessor Type: the type of operation predecessor associated with this route 
operation. These types are defined in the Operation Predecessor Types worksheet in the 
Enterprise Scheduling Parameters workbook. 
Select from:
 l StartFromPredecessorEnd: the successor operation is eligible to run when the 
predecessor ends.
 l StartFromPredecessorStart: the successor operation is eligible to run when the 
predecessor starts.
 l Operation Predecessor Type Control Set: control set associated with the operation 
predecessor type. Typically this is set to Default.
Define fixed routing
You can set route operations to be produced on a specified set of primary resources. Eligible 
resources are set using a defined path in a PredecessorResourceEligibility record and if the 
resource type for the resource is set to enable qualifications. When an operation with a fixed 
routing group is scheduled on a resource, its successor operation only becomes eligible for 
scheduling on resources that are listed in that same eligibility group.
Locking an operation on a resource takes precedence over a defined fixed route path.

===== PAGE 70 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration60
Data configuration
To create fixed routes for operations, the following steps are required:
 1. Define the fixed routes between eligible primary resources. Each route is a record on the 
PredecessorResourceEligibility table.
 2. Enable qualifications for that resource type.
PredecessorResourceEligibility worksheet
Create one record for each route operation path and specify if that route is a qualified or 
disqualified route. Depending on what value is specified for ResourceType.EnableQualifications, 
the route path will either be eligible or ineligible for scheduling.
Required fields
 l Value: the name of this fixed route.
 l Predecessor Resource Name: the name of the primary resource that produces the 
predecessor operation.
 l Predecessor Resource Site: the site for the predecessor resource.
 l Predecessor Resource Area: the area for the predecessor resource.
 l Predecessor Resource Area Group: the area group for the area associated with this 
predecessor resource.
 l Resource Name: the name of the primary resource that produces the successor operation.
 l Resource Area: the area for the resource that produces the successor operation.
 l Resource Area Group: the area group for the area associated with the produces the 
successor operation.
 l Qualification: Specify if this path between resources is qualified or disqualified from using 
fixed routing.
ResourceType worksheet
 l For the resource type record associated with your primary resources, select the Enable 
Qualifications checkbox.

===== PAGE 71 =====
61 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Define precedence relationships
For routes and orders with multiple operations, you must define precedence relationships 
between the operations to organize how the they flow through the schedule. Typically this is a 
linear relationship where one operation (predecessor operation) must be completed before a 
following operation (successor operation) can take place. 
For planned orders precedence is defined on route operations. 
For scheduled receipts you can use the precedence defined for the associated route operation, 
use the precedence defined in a scheduled receipt operation predecessor record, or a 
combination of both. This builds precedence relationships based on what is pegged to the 
scheduled receipt and its operations. Relationships such as minimum and maximum wait times, 
start-to-start dependencies, quantity-based constraints, and part source sequencing are 
enforced. See Define precedence relationships.
In the example below, the sub-assembly operation (three segments: loading, working, 
unloading) must take place before the heat treatment operation (two segments: loading, 
working) on the route. 
The sub-assembly route operation is assigned a sequence of "1" and the heat treatment route 
operation a sequence of "2". However, there are multiple factors that determine how precedence 
is defined.

===== PAGE 72 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration62
The relationship between one operation and the operation that precedes it is defined by 
predecessor records which are constrained by defined durations or quantities and an operation 
predecessor type. 
The following duration and quantity fields define thresholds between predecessor and successor 
operations that determine when successor operations can be scheduled based on how its 
predecessor is scheduled. Successor operations can't be scheduled until their predecessor 
operations are scheduled and the earliest time a successor can be scheduled is determined by 
the precedence rule applied to it.
 l MaximumWaitDuration: the maximum time allowed between predecessor and successor 
operations. Only values >= 0 are valid. This is measured in days.
When the maximum wait duration is greater than or equal to 0, this determines the latest 
time that a successor operation can be scheduled by adding the maximum wait duration to 
the earliest time that the successor can be scheduled.
For operations with precedence relationships that aren't defined on a route operation 
predecessor or scheduled receipt predecessor, fields on the Scheduling Configuration table 
are used. See Scheduling Configuration precedence relationships.
 l MinimumWaitDuration: the minimum time allowed between predecessor and successor 
operations. This is measured in days.
 l MinimumWaitQuantity: the minimum quantity that must be produced by the predecessor 
operation before the successor operation can start.
Note: If the MinimumWaitDuration is set to a negative value, it will automatically be 
changed to 0 on the Gantt chart.
Operation predecessor types
Each operation predecessor is assigned a type that is governed by multiple rules:
Dependency schedule rule
Determines how predecessor and successor operations are scheduled when the maximum wait 
duration on the predecessor is greater than or equal to zero:
 l EarliestTime: where the predecessor operation is only scheduled if its successor operation 
can be scheduled within the maximum wait duration. Successor operations are scheduled 
as early as possible.
 l SchedulingRule: where the predecessor operation can be scheduled at any time and isn't 
constrained by when its successor operation can be scheduled.

===== PAGE 73 =====
63 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Maximum wait exceeded rule
Defines how operations are scheduled when the maximum wait duration between predecessor 
and successor is exceeded. 
 l AllowExceedMaximumWaitDuration: the maximum wait duration can be exceeded.
 l UnscheduleSuccessor: the successor operation is unscheduled and only the predecessor 
operation is scheduled.
 l UnschedulePredecessorAndSuccessor: both the predecessor and successor operations 
are not scheduled.
This rule is only applied when the dependency scheduling rule is set to SchedulingRule.
Maximum wait outside horizon rule
Defines how predecessor operations are scheduled if successor operations aren't scheduled 
within the scheduling horizon and if the maximum wait duration is outside of the schedule 
horizon.
 l SchedulePredecessor: the predecessor operation is still scheduled.
 l UnscheduledPredecessor: the predecessor operation is not scheduled.
Precedence rule
Determines when a successor operation is eligible to be scheduled in relation to its predecessor 
operation.
 l EndFromPredecessorEnd: the successor operation must be finished at the same time as its 
predecessor operation finishes production.
 l EndFromPredecessorStart: the successor operation must be finished before its 
predecessor can start production.
 l StartFromPredecessorEnd: the successor operation is eligible to be scheduled at the end 
of its predecessor operation.
 l StartFromPredecessorStart: the successor operation is eligible to be scheduled at the 
start of its predecessor operation.
This rule is only applied when the dependency scheduling rule is set to SchedulingRule and 
the pegging source for the schedule is set to SupplyPlanning.
Note: If a successor operation can't be scheduled based on the values defined for 
PrecedenceRule, OperationPredecessorType, MinimumWaitDuration, and 
MaximumWaitDuration, then the predecessor remains in it scheduled position.

===== PAGE 74 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration64
Precedence rule and locked or frozen operations
The precedence rule behaves differently when operations are locked or scheduled in a frozen 
window. The table below outlines precedence behavior based on whether a predecessor or 
successor operation is locked or frozen.
Predecessor Successor Behavior
  Locked If the locked successor operation will finish earlier than the 
earliest time permitted by MinimumWaitDuration, the 
successor operation is moved so it finishes at the earliest 
allowed time. 
  Locked If the locked successor operation will finish later than 
latest time permitted by MaximumWaitDuration, the 
successor operation stays locked and its predecessor is 
scheduled base on the values defined for 
DependencySchedulingRule, 
MaximumWaitExceededRule, and PeggingSource.
Locked   If the successor operation can't be scheduled based on 
the values defined for PrecedenceRule, 
OperationPredecessorType, MinimumWaitDuration, 
and MaximumWaitDuration, , the locked predecessor 
operation will remain in its locked position.
  Frozen If the frozen successor operation finishes earlier than the 
earliest time permitted by MinimumWaitDuration, the 
successor operation is moved so it finishes at the earliest 
allowed time. 
  Frozen If the frozen successor operation will finish later than 
latest time permitted by MaximumWaitDuration, both the 
predecessor (immediate) and successor operations stay 
locked and the values defined for 
DependencySchedulingRule, 
MaximumWaitExceededRule, and PeggingSource are 
ignored.
 

===== PAGE 75 =====
65 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Scheduling Configuration precedence relationships
When the maximum wait duration between  predecessor and successor operations isn't defined 
on the route operation predecessor or scheduled receipt operation predecessor, either the 
InterRoutePredecessorMaximumWaitDuration or 
IntraRoutePredecessorMaximumWaitDuration fields in Scheduling Configuration table are 
used to define the precedence relationship.
Depending on whether the operations are for the same or different parts, the following fields are 
used:
 l InterRoutePredecessorMaximumWaitDuration is used when the predecessor and 
successor operations are for different parts, representing a BOM transformation.
 l IntraRoutePredecessorMaximumWaitDuration is used when the predecessor and 
successor operations are for the same part. 

===== PAGE 76 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration66
Create scheduled receipt operations
Scheduled receipt operations (SR operations) are equivalent to route operations and define the 
steps that the scheduled receipt must complete to be manufactured. They represent the firm 
orders to be scheduled and are created in Maestro or imported from an ERP or Manufacturing 
Execution Systems (MES). Defining SR operations lets work order routes that are created in 
external  systems using non-standard route components to be included in the production 
schedule. Examples of non-standard route components include rework steps, different operation 
orders, or non-standard timings. 
After creating a scheduled receipt operation, you can map it to an operation specification that 
determines the rules and preferences that define when it can be scheduled. See Define operation 
specifications.
Scheduled receipt operation segments are assigned to a specific secondary resource 
requirement, however you can control which resource requirements are used for a segment by 
mapping it to a different secondary resource requirement. See Control secondary resource 
requirements.
Scheduled Receipt Operations are configured in the Enterprise Scheduling Demand workbook.
Data configuration
To create scheduled receipt operations, the following steps are required:
 1. Create a new record for the scheduled receipt operation.
 2. Create one or more new records to define the segments that make up the operation.
 3. Optionally, create a  scheduled receipt operation predecessor record to define predecessor 
relationships between operations. Depending on how the scheduled receipt operation is 
configured and if an associated route operation predecessor record exists, different 
precedence relationships area applied. See Define precedence relationships.
For orders with multiple operations, predecessor relationships must be defined between 
operations. See Define precedence relationships.
 4. Optionally, modify the priority for a scheduled receipt.
 5. Optionally, set up mapping from specific operation segments to secondary resource 
requirements and enable the use of those settings by turning it on at the scheduled 
receipt operation level.

===== PAGE 77 =====
67 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Scheduled Receipt Operations worksheet
Required fields
 l Scheduled Receipt Order records (Site, Id): select the values associated with the 
scheduled receipt for this  operation.
 l Scheduled Receipt Line: specify the line number for the scheduled receipt associated with 
this  operation.
 l Scheduled Receipt Order records (Control Set, Type): select the values associated with 
the scheduled receipt for this  operation.
 l Name: select the name of this scheduled receipt operation.
 l Sequence: specify the sequence number to assign to the  operation.
 l Is Locked: identify if the scheduled receipt operation is locked.
 l Produced at Datetime: specify the date and time that the quantity for this  operation was 
last produced.
 l Total Quantity: specify the total quantity to be produced for this  operation.
 l Quantity: specify the remaining quantity to be produced for this  operation.
 l Use Route Operation Specification: identify if route operation specifications are used for 
this operation. When a record that maps this scheduled receipt operation to an operation 
specification exists, this is set to N. When Y, this operation will use the operation 
specification mapped to its corresponding route operation. See Size constraints.
 l Use Route Operation Precedence: identify if the precedence relationships defined at the 
route operation level will be used for this scheduled receipt operation. See Define 
precedence relationships.
 l Actual End Date Time: select the date and time for the actual end of the  operation.
 l Actual Start Date Time: select the date and time for the actual start of the  operation.
 l Primary Resource Name: select the name of the primary resource associated with this  
operation. Click Add to create a Resource record.
 l Primary Resource Area records (Site, Name, Area Group Name): select the values for 
the area associated with the input primary resource for this operation.
 l Primary Resource Group Name: select the group for the primary resource associated with 
this  operation. Click Add to create a Resource Group record.
Optional fields:
 l Is Locked: select if this operation is locked in the schedule.
 l Produced At Datetime: the date and time that this  operation is produced.
 l Quantity: the quantity for this  operation.
 l Size: specify a size for the operation that will determine if it can be scheduled on a 
physically constrained primary resource.

===== PAGE 78 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration68
Scheduled Receipt Operation Segment worksheet
Required fields
 l Scheduled Receipt Operation Name: select the scheduled receipt associated with this 
segment.
 l Scheduled Receipt Line: select the line number for the scheduled receipt associated with 
this segment.
 l Scheduled Receipt Order records (Site, Id, and Type): select the values for the 
scheduled receipt order associated with this segment.
 l Operation Segment Type: select the name of the scheduled receipt operation segment.
 l Duration: the number of day required to produce this segment.
 l Duration Override: specify a value that is used to override the duration for this segment.
 l Rate: the production rate for this segment.
 l Rate Override: specify a value that is used to override the resource rate for this segment.
 l Sequence: the sequence number assigned to the scheduled receipt operation segment that 
determines when the segment is processed in the route.
 l Exclude Unassigned Secondary Resource Requirements: to use values from a different 
secondary resource requirement for this specific segment, select N. Otherwise select Y. See 
Control secondary resource requirements.
Scheduled Receipt Operation Predecessor worksheet
 l Predecessor Scheduled Receipt Operation Scheduled Receipt Order Site: select the site 
for the scheduled receipt associated with the predecessor operation.
 l Predecessor Scheduled Receipt Operation Name: specify the name for the predecessor 
operation.
 l Predecessor Scheduled Receipt Operation Scheduled Receipt fields (Line, Order Id, 
Order Type): the scheduled receipt associated with the predecessor operation.
 l Scheduled Receipt Operation Name: specify the scheduled receipt operation that is the 
successor operation.
 l Scheduled Receipt Operation Scheduled Receipt fields ( Line, Order Id, Order Type): 
the scheduled receipt operation associated with the predecessor operation.
 l Operation Predecessor Type: select the type of relationship between the predecessor and 
successor operations.  Select from:
 l StartFromPredecessorEnd: the successor operation is eligible to run when the 
predecessor ends.
 l StartFromPredecessorStart: the successor operation is eligible to run when the 
predecessor starts.

===== PAGE 79 =====
69 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
 l Maximum Wait Duration: the maximum time (in days) allowed between predecessor and 
successor operations.
 l Minimum Wait Duration: the minimum time (in days) allowed between predecessor and 
successor operations.
 l Minimum Wait Quantity: the minimum quantity that must be produced for the successor 
operation to start.
Note: The operation predecessor types are defined in the Operation Predecessor Types 
worksheet in the Enterprise Scheduling Parameters workbook.
Scheduled Receipt worksheet
 l In the Scheduled Receipt worksheet, modify the Order Priority - Value column to change 
what priority is associated with the scheduled receipt order.
Control secondary resource requirements
Two steps are required to control which secondary resource requirement is used for an 
operation segment:
 1. Create a new record to map the specific operation segment to a secondary resource 
requirement.
 2. Disable the Exclude Unassigned Secondary Resource Requirements option for that 
operation segment.
Scheduled Receipt Operation Segment Resource Assignment 
worksheet
 l Scheduled Receipt Operation Name: select the scheduled receipt associated with the 
segment.
 l Scheduled Receipt records (Line, Order Site, Id): select the values for the scheduled 
receipt associated with the segment.
 l Scheduled Receipt Order Type Control Set: select the control set for the scheduled 
receipt order type associated with the segment.
 l Scheduled Receipt Order Type: select the order type for the scheduled receipt associated 
with the segment.
 l Scheduled Receipt Operation Segment Type: select the segment type.
 l Resource Area records (Group, Site, Area): select the resource area values associated 
with the resource that the segment is assigned on.
 l Resource Name: select the resource that the segment is assigned on.

===== PAGE 80 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration70
 l Resource Group: select the group for the resource e that the segment is assigned on.
 l Secondary Resource Requirement Operation Segment Type: select the operation 
segment type associated with the secondary resource requirement.
 l Secondary Resource Requirement Secondary Resource Group: select the resource 
group associated with the secondary resource requirement.
 l Secondary Resource Requirement Secondary Resource Requirement Group: select the 
group associated with the secondary resource requirement.
Scheduled Receipt Operation Segment worksheet
 l For the scheduled receipt operation segment that was mapped, clear the check box in the 
Exclude Unassigned Secondary Resource Requirements column.
Resource requirements from both the SecondaryResourceRequirement and 
ScheduledReceiptOperationSegmentResourceAssignment table are used, with the 
values specified in the ScheduledReceiptOperationSegmentResourceAssignment table 
overriding the default values from the SecondaryResourceRequirement table.
When this check box is selected (or set to Y), only resource requirements from the 
SecondaryResourceRequirement table are used.
Tip:   If you are modifying this value in the Insert Record dialog, select N.

===== PAGE 81 =====
71 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Define operation specifications
Operation specifications define a set of rules and preferences for scheduling route and 
scheduled receipt operations. The operation specification is assigned to an eligible resource 
within it's associated resource group to run operations on. Optionally you can define overrides 
for the duration and rate values that are applied to operations assigned to a specific operation 
specification.
If no resource is assigned to the operation specification, then any resource within the associated 
resource group can be used to run operations. The preference and wait duration values assigned 
to resources are used to determine which resource within the group an operation will run on.
Notes: 
 l If no mapping record is created for a scheduled receipt operation, then it will use the 
mapping defined between its corresponding route operation and an operation 
specification.
 l An operation specification can be applied to multiple operations however a route 
operation or scheduled receipt operation can only have one operation specification 
assigned to it.
 l If no overrides have been defined in the Operation Specification Resource Segment 
Override worksheet, then values from the OperationSpecificationSegmentOverride 
table are used.
Data configuration
To create operation specifications, the following steps are required:
 1. Create a new record for the operation specification.
 2. Create a new record to map the operation specification to a resource.
 3. Depending on whether you are mapping to a route operation or a scheduled receipt 
operation, do one of the following:
 l Create a new record to map the operation specification to a route operation.
 l Create a new record to map the operation specification to a scheduled receipt 
operation.
 4. Optionally, create a new record to override the rate and/or duration for an operation 
specification segment.
 5. Optionally, create a new record to override the rate and/or duration for a resource 
segment.

===== PAGE 82 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration72
Operation Specifications worksheet
 l Name: the name of the operation specification.
 l Area Group: the area group for the area associated with this operation specification.
 l Area Site: the site for the area associated with this operation specification.
 l Area: the area associated with this operation specification.
 l Resource Group: the name of the resource group associated with this operation 
specification.
Operation Specification To Resource worksheet
Required fields
 l Area Group: the area group for the area associated with this operation specification.
 l Area Site: the site for the area associated with this operation specification.
 l Area: the area associated with this operation specification.
 l Operation Specification: the operation specification to map to the resource. Click Add to 
create a new Operation Specification record.
 l Resource: the resource to assign to the operation specification. This is the resource that will 
run operations associated with this operation specification. Click Add to create a new 
Resource record.
 l Preference: the preference value assigned to the resource. 
 l Maximum Wait Duration For Preference: the maximum amount of days an operation will 
wait for the highest preference resource before being scheduled on a lower preference 
resource.
Route Operation To Operation Specification worksheet
Required fields
 l Route Operation fields (including Route and Sequence): the route operation associated 
with the operation specification.
 l Area Group: the area group for the area associated with this operation specification.
 l Area Site: the site for the area associated with this operation specification.
 l Area: the area associated with this operation specification.
 l Operation Specification Name: the name of the operation specification.
 l Route Operation Route Part Source fields (Part Site, Base Key, Part Name, Source ID, 
Source Supplier Id): the part source for the route associated with the route operation.

mapped to the corresponding route operation. You can identify if a scheduled receipt operation 
uses route operation specifications in the Scheduled Receipt Operation workbook. See X.

 l Rate Override: the production rate applied to this override.

worksheet

 l Area Group: the area group for the area associated with this operation specification 
resource segment.

EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration74
