segment.
 l Area: the area associated with this operation specification resource segment.
 l Operation Specification: the operation specification  associated with this override.
 l Resource: the resource associated with this override.
 l Operation Segment Type: the type of operation segment associated with this override.
 l Operation Segment Type Control Set: the control set associated with this operation 
segment. Typically this is set to Default.
 l Duration Override (days): the number of days this override is applied. 
 l Rate Override (per day): the production rate applied to this override.

===== PAGE 85 =====
75 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Define properties
Properties are configurable attributes which can be assigned to operations, such a priority code, 
operation size, or color. These properties can be defined at the part, scheduled receipt, 
scheduled receipt operation, or route operation level and hold customer-specific information 
against parts or operations. When the properties are mapped at different levels, the properties 
are prioritized in the following order (from highest to lowest priority):
Other scheduling parameters have properties assigned to them to determine conditions, 
rankings, groupings, or multiplier values. 
Operation properties
When properties are used as visual indicators, users can select which property to view from the 
Selected Property Type control on the workbook toolbar.


===== PAGE 86 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration76
Only properties with no processing rule can be added to the control for users. Properties with an 
assigned processing rule are used by the Scheduling algorithm in calculating changeovers, 
dispatch rules, or application extension points. See Application extensions. 


===== PAGE 87 =====
77 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Properties and other parameters
Properties are also assigned to the following scheduling parameters:
 l Campaign conditions: the property type assigned to a condition determines which 
operations are eligible to run in a campaign. Optionally, you can assign a specific property 
to the campaign. For example, a campaign could run for all sizes of a product when the 
property type is specified as Packsize. For a campaign that is targeted to the largest size, 
you would also specify the property name "Jumbo" so the campaign will only run for jumbo 
sized orders. 
Only operations with mutually exclusive properties, for example the operation can only 
have a color or a flavor property but not both, can have multiple campaigns applied to the 
same resource. See Create campaigns.
 l Changeover conditions: the property type assigned to a condition is used to trigger the 
changeover. For example, when the property type for a changeover condition is “flavor” 
then when production on a toothpaste line changes from mint to citrus a changeover is 
triggered by the change in flavor. See Define changeovers.
 l Dispatch rule components: the property type assigned to a component is used to 
determine the hierarchy level of the component in hierarchical dispatch rules. See Define 
dispatch rules.
 l Search algorithm rule components: the property type associated with an operation is 
used to group like operations when using the Search algorithm option to sequence a 
resource. See Search Algorithm option.
 l Objective function components: the property referenced by an objective function 
component for the Search algorithm option is used as a multiplier in the function’s 
calculated score. See Search Algorithm option.
Properties and custom logic 
You can map properties to area groups, resources, or scheduled receipts to support custom 
functionality in application extensions. For example, if you want a customized frozen window 
duration for each resource on the scheduled, you can map a new property 
"FrozenWindowPeriod" to each resource with a value that represents the number of days for that 
period. The extension would then reference this property value.
Data configuration
To create properties for operations, the following steps are required:
 1. Create a new record for a property type. 
 2. Create a new record for the property.
 3. Create a new record to map the property to a part.

===== PAGE 88 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration78
 4. Optionally,  to use properties for custom logic, map the property to an area group, 
resource, and/or scheduled receipt.
Property Types worksheet
For properties that are used in dispatch rules, this is a required record. 
 l Name: the name of the property type.
 l Data Type: The data value for the property type.
 l Processing Rule: the calculation used to determine the property value of this property 
type. For hierarchical dispatch rules, this rule sorts the operation relative to other eligible 
operations. For weighted dispatch rules, this rule contributes to the overall operation score, 
with lower scoring operations being selected first.
Valid values are:
 l ChangeoverDuration: the property value is calculated using the operation changeover 
durations
 l ChangeoverPenalty: the property value is calculated using the penalty values of 
operation changeovers.
 l CriticalRatio: the property value is calculated using on the critical ratio representing 
slack time for an operation.
 l DueDate: the property value is calculated using operation due dates.
 l InverseOrderPriority: the property value is calculated by highest priority with low 
numerical values corresponding to high priorities.
 l MaximumWaitDurationEnd: the property value is calculated using by the earliest 
maximum duration end time for the operation's predecessor operations with a 
maximum wait duration >= 0.
 l None: the property value is not calculated and the value specified for Property.Value is 
used. This is the value that should be set for properties that are used as visual indicators 
on the Gantt chart. These properties will display in the Selected Property Type control 
on the workbook toolbar.
 l OrderPriority: the property value is calculated by highest priority with high numerical 
values corresponding to high priorities.
 l ProcessingDuration: the property value is calculated using the processing duration of 
operations.
 l QueuedDuration: the property value is calculated by the duration taken to mark an 
operation as eligible and have it enter the queue to be scheduled.
 l SlackTime: the property value is calculated using slack time. Slack time is the amount of 
time an operation can be delayed from its start date without it passing its due date and 
becoming late. This calculated ratio = time until due date / remaining processing 
duration for the operation.

===== PAGE 89 =====
79 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
 l SlackTimeToMaximumWaitDurationEnd: the property value is calculated using the 
maximum wait duration end time that has the least difference from the current time for 
the operation's predecessor operations with a maximum wait duration >= 0.
Properties worksheet
Required fields
 l Property Type Name: the name of the property type assigned to this property.
 l String Value: the name of the property.
 l Color Code: the hex code value representing the color assigned to this property.
Part Property worksheet
Required fields
 l Part Site: the site associated with the specified part.
 l Part Name: the name of the part.
 l Property Type Name: the name of the property type associated with the property for this 
part.
 l Property String Value: the name of the property associated with this part.
Area Group To Property worksheet
This mapping lets you associate custom rules with an area group to use in application 
extensions. 
Required fields
 l Area Group: select the area group to map to the associated property.
 l Property Type Name: select the name of the property type to map to the selected area 
group.
 l Property String Value: select the specific property to map to the area group. Only 
properties of the selected property type display in the list.
Resource To Property worksheet 
This mapping lets you associate custom rules with a resource to use in application extensions. 

===== PAGE 90 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration80
Required fields
 l Resource Area Group: select the area group for the resource.
 l Resource Area Site: select the site for the resource.
 l Resource Area: select the area for the resource.
 l Resource Name: select the resource to map to the associated property. 
 l Property Type Name: select the name of the property type to map to the selected 
resource.
 l Property String Value: select the specific property to map to the resource. Only properties 
of the selected property type display in the list.
Scheduled Receipt To Property worksheet
This worksheet is in the Enterprise Scheduling Demand workbook. This mapping lets you 
associate custom rules with a scheduled receipt to use in application extensions.
Required fields
 l Order Site: select the site for the order.
 l Order Id: select the ID for the order to map to the associated property.
 l Order Line: select the line for the order.
 l Order Type: select the order type.
 l Property Type Name: select the name of the property type to map to the selected 
scheduled receipt.
 l Property String Value: select the specific property to map to the scheduled receipt. Only 
properties of the selected property type display in the list.

===== PAGE 91 =====
81 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Define changeovers
Changeovers represent non-working time for a resource based on the sequence of operations 
that have been scheduled. Typically changeovers happen when a resource switches between 
operations. You can review the mappings between properties and changeovers in the 
FromTo Property Changeover Condition Matrix worksheet.
Notes: 
 l Operation segments can't be scheduled concurrently with changeovers on secondary 
resources.
 l Secondary resources that handoff operations can only have a maximum of one 
changeover applied to them.
Data configuration
To create changeovers, the following steps are required:
 1. Create a new record for the changeover.
 2. Create a new record for the changeover condition
 3. Create a new record to map the changeover to a resource.
Changeovers worksheet
Required fields
 l Name: the name of the changeover.
 l Area Group: the area group for the area associated with this changeover.
 l Area Site: the site for the area associated with this changeover.
 l Area: the area associated with this changeover. Click Add to create a new Area record.
 l Operation Segment Type: the type of operation segment that triggers this changeover. 
Click Add to create a new Changeover record.
 l Operation Segment Type Control Set: the control set associated with this segment 
type. Typically this is set to Default.

===== PAGE 92 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration82
Changeover Conditions worksheet
Required fields
 l Area Group: the area group for the area associated with this changeover condition.
 l Area Site: the site for the area associated with this changeover condition.
 l Area: the area associated with this changeover condition.
 l Changeover: the changeover associated with this condition.
 l Property Type: the property type associated with this condition.
 l From Property fields (including Type): the property that the operation is changed from 
for this condition.
 l To Property fields (including Type): the property that the operation is changed to for this 
condition.
 l Duration Override (days): the number of days this condition is overridden when a 
changeover is applied to it.
 l Penalty Override: the value that overrides the penalty assigned to this condition.
 l From To Delta: the difference between two conditions when multiple changeovers happen 
at the same time on the schedule.
 l Operator: the mathematical operator applied to the From To Delta column. Valid values 
include:
 l Equal
 l GreaterThan
 l GreaterThanEqual
 l LessThan
 l LessThanEqual
 l NotEqual
Changeover Resource Mapping worksheet
Required fields
 l Area Group: the area group for the area associated with this changeover.
 l Area Site: the site for the area associated with this changeover.
 l Area: the area associated with this changeover.
 l Changeover: the name of the changeover.
 l Resource Area Area Group Name: the name of the area group associated with the area 
for this resource.
 l Resource Area Name: the name of the area for this resource.

===== PAGE 93 =====
83 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
 l Resource Name: the primary resource assigned to this changeover.
 l Resource Group Name: the resource group for the resource assigned to this changeover.
 l Penalty: The size of the impact from the changeover on production. Changeovers are 
ranked by penalty with higher penalty vales assigned to more severe changeovers. The 
Scheduling algorithm prefers lower penalty changeovers. In the factors that affect 
production, changeover penalties supersede changeover durations.
 l Concurrency Group: the concurrency group assigned to the primary resource that 
determines if the changeover runs in series or parallel when multiple changeovers with 
overlapping conditions are assigned to a resource.
 l Different values: changeovers run in series.
 l The same value: changeovers run in parallel.
 l Duration (days): the number of days the changeover runs on the schedule. This is  a 
fraction of a 24 hour day, expressed in a decimal format.

===== PAGE 94 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration84
Define downtime periods
Downtimes periods represent time when a resource is unavailable and no operations can be 
scheduled on it. Typically downtime periods reflect planned maintenance times or vacations.
Data configuration
To create downtime periods, the following steps are required:
 1. Create a new record to define the type of downtime.
 2. Create a new record for the downtime.
 3. Create a new record to map a downtime to a resource.
DownTime Type worksheet
Required fields
 l Name: the name of the downtime type.
Downtimes worksheet
Required fields
 l Name: the name of the downtime.
 l Area Group: the area group for the area associated with this downtime.
 l Area Site: the site for the area associated with this downtime.
 l Area: the area associated with this downtime. Click Add to create a new Area record.
 l Type: the type of downtime. For example, "maintenance" or "repair".
 l Start Date Time: the date and time the downtime starts.
 l End Date Time: the date and time the downtime ends.
 l All Day: If the downtime period is applied to the resource for a full day .
 l Efficiency: specify an efficiency value that is applied to resources that run during this 
downtime period. 
 l Require Primary Resource Empty: If the primary resource must be empty over the 
downtime period.

===== PAGE 95 =====
85 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Downtime Resources worksheet
Required fields
 l Area Group: the area group for the area associated with this downtime resource.
 l Area Site: the site for the area associated with this downtime resource.
 l Area: the area associated with this downtime resource.
 l Downtime Name: the name of the downtime to map to the resource.
 l Resource Name: the name of the resource to map the downtime to.

===== PAGE 96 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration86
Create campaigns
Campaigns run successive operations of the same product to reduce costs incurred by 
scheduling interruptions such as changeovers or downtimes. They can also be used to reduce 
inventory holding costs or improve conformance quality by using longer campaigns. Campaigns 
run for a specified duration of time or quantity of product.
Configuring multiple campaigns on the same resource
You can configure multiple campaigns on the same resource using different properties only if 
the properties are mutually exclusive. For example, if operations can only have a color or a flavor 
property applied to them, then both a color-based and a flavor-based campaign can be defined 
on the same resource since they will run as separate campaigns. If properties for an operation 
are not mutually exclusive, for example the operation can have both a color and a flavor property 
at the same time, then multiple campaigns using color and flavor can't be defined on the same 
resource.
Data configuration
To create a campaign, the following steps are required:
 1. Create a new record to define the campaign.
 2. Map the campaign to one or more primary resources.
 3. Create a new record to specify the conditions of the campaign.
Campaigns worksheet
Required fields
 l Name: the name of the campaign.
 l Campaign Area Group: select the area group for the area associated with this campaign.
 l Campaign Area Site: select the site for the area associated with this campaign.
 l Campaign Area: select the area associated with this campaign. Click Add to create a new 
Area record.
 l Allow Changeovers: select Y to allow changeovers to be applied during the campaign. 
Otherwise select N.
 l Allow Downtimes: select Y to allow downtimes to be applied during the campaign. 
Otherwise select N.
 l Allow Off Shift Time: select Y to allow off shift time to be applied during the campaign. 
Otherwise select N.

===== PAGE 97 =====
87 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
 l Allow Shift Changes: select Y to allow shift changes to be applied during the campaign. 
Otherwise select N.
 l Restart Threshold: specify how many days the campaign must wait before it can be 
restarted after it has ended.
Campaign Resources Mapping worksheet
Required fields
 l Resource Area Group: select the area group for the area associated with the resource.
 l Resource Area Site: select the site for the area associated with the resource.
 l Resource Area: select the area associated with the resource.
 l Resource Name: select the primary resource to associate with this campaign.
 l Campaign Name: select the campaign that you are mapping to the resource.
Campaign Conditions worksheet
The constraint fields are all required and are used to define how long the campaign runs for. The 
property type is a required field, however a specific property defined by a property name is 
optional. For example, if you wanted to run the campaign for all sizes of a product, you would 
specify Campaign Property Type as PackSize and leave Property Name blank. However, if 
the campaign is only targeted to producing the smaller size of a product, you would select 
PackSize for the property type and then select Snack Pack for Property Name.
Required fields
 l Campaign Name: select the campaign this condition will be defined for.
 l Campaign Area Group: select the area group for the area associated with this campaign.
 l Campaign Area Site: select the site for the area associated with this campaign.
 l Campaign Area: select the area associated with this campaign.
 l Campaign Property Type:
 l Constraint Type: select how to constrain the campaign condition.
Valid values:
 l Maximum: the constraint value is set the maximum value required to constrain the 
campaign.
 l Minimum: the constraint value is set the minimum value required to constrain the 
campaign.
 l Constraint Value: specify the numeric value for the constraint. Depending on the value 
selected for Constraint Measure, this representa the number of days or a quantity value.

===== PAGE 98 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration88
 l Constraint Measure: select the measure to define the constraint value. Valid values 
include:
 l Duration: select this to specify the constraint as a total working duration of consecutive 
operations  scheduled in the campaign. Measured in Days.
 l Quantity: select this to specify the constraint as a total quantity produced by the 
consecutive operations scheduled in the campaign.
 l Gap Threshold: specify the maximum duration allowed in the idle time (gaps) between 
consecutive operations in the campaign. 
Optional fields
 l Property Name: select the specific property to define the campaign. When a value is 
selected for this field, the campaign is defined by this specific property. When no value is 
specified, the campaign is applied to all property values for the specified property type.

===== PAGE 99 =====
89 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Schedule maintenance actions
Maintenance actions such as cleaning or replacement of a machine component can be added to 
schedules to identify the action and the period when that primary resource is unavailable for 
production. Maintenance actions display as gray diagonally striped bars on the chart and can 
only be assigned to primary resources.
Maintenance actions are triggered to start by a defined threshold that can be one of the 
following:
 l ElapsedDuration: total number of days.
 l IdleDuration: total elapsed time with no "working" operation segment types (days).
 l OperationCount: total number of operations.
 l Quantity: total quantity produced by the primary resource.
 l WorkingDuration: total number of days that the primary resource runs.
Each maintenance action must be assigned to an operation segment type. The selected 
operation segment type should be one exclusively used for maintenance actions. You also 
specify how long the action takes and if it recurs in the schedule by defining its duration and 
interval. 
Other actions on the schedule can affect when maintenance actions are scheduled. Specifically 
changeovers, downtimes, and other maintenance actions can reset the interval start for a 
maintenance actions so that it is adjusted in the schedule to restart its cycle after the other 
action is completed. For example, a day-long maintenance action to clean machine A is 
scheduled to run every 7th day, on Wednesdays. A one-day changeover is scheduled on 
machine A for Thursday. The changeover, if mapped to the maintenance action, will reset its 
interval to start after the changeover finishes. Now, instead of starting on a Monday, the 
maintenance action starts on Friday and continues with its 7-day cycle until another action 
possibly resets its interval schedule. See Modify the start interval for a maintenance.

===== PAGE 100 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration90
You can also schedule maintenance actions to start right after a campaign finishes. This allows 
the campaign to immediately restart after the maintenance action is completed. Typically a 
campaign includes a defined restart threshold or it is mapped to a maintenance action. Using the 
restart threshold allows the primary resource to be available for use during that time period. See 
Set up a maintenance action after a campaign.
Note: If the threshold for a campaign is undefined or isn't met, the Scheduling algorithm 
automatically schedules any maintenances for that campaign to take place at the end of 
the campaign.
Maintenance operation records
The Maintenance Operation worksheet in the Enterprise Scheduling Parameters workbook stores 
historical maintenance records that have been manually created and modified maintenance 
records generated by the Scheduling algorithm whenever a user modifies a maintenance on the 
Gantt chart (unassigning, holding, locking, or moving the maintenance).
Historical maintenance records
To more accurately plan maintenance operations, the Scheduling algorithm needs to know what 
maintenances have happened prior to the start of the scheduling horizon. These historical 
maintenance records must be manually input into Maestro using the Maintenance Operation 
worksheet in the Enterprise Scheduling Parameters workbook. These historical records are also 
required whenever the scheduling horizon is updated. Historical maintenance records can be 
identified as records with a StartDateTime that is before the start of the scheduling horizon. See 
Create a historical maintenance record.
Model maintenances using properties
Maintenances can be triggered to run by the properties defined on scheduled receipt operation, 
route operations, scheduled receipts, or parts. By assigning a property and/or property type to a 
maintenance, you can generate a more precise schedule. See Trigger maintenances using 
properties.

===== PAGE 101 =====
91 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Data configuration
To create a maintenance action, the following steps are required:
 1. If not already defined, create an operation segment type to use exclusively with 
maintenance actions.
 2. Create a maintenance record.
 3. Map the maintenance operation to a resource.
Optionally, you can also:
 l Set up a maintenance action after completion of a campaign
 l Map a maintenance action to another action on the schedule such as  a changeover, 
downtime, or other maintenance action
 l Trigger a maintenance based on properties
 l Create historical maintenance records
Operation Segment Type worksheet
Required fields
This segment type should only be used for maintenance operations.
 l Value: specify a name for the operation segment type. It is recommend that the name 
clearly identify it's only used for maintenance actions. For example, "Maintenance".
 l Control Set: select the control set associated with this segment type. Typically this is set to 
Default.
 l Allow Operation Pausing: leave this value unselected because it's not applicable to 
maintenance actions.
 l Allow Operation Shift Spanning: select to allow operation shift spanning on a resource.
 l Value Add:  leave this value unselected because it's not applicable to maintenance actions.
Maintenance worksheet
Required fields
 l Name: specify a name for the maintenance action.
 l Operation Segment Type Control Set: select the control set for the operation segment 
type assigned to this maintenance action.
 l Operation Segment Type Name: select the operation segment type used exclusively for 
maintenance actions.

===== PAGE 102 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration92
 l Duration: specify how many days the maintenance action takes.
 l Type: select how the maintenance interval is calculated:
Valid values include:
 l ElapsedDuration: total number of days.
 l IdleDuration: total elapsed time with no "working" operation segment types (days).
 l OperationCount: the total number of operations.
 l Quantity: total quantity produced by the primary resource.
 l WorkingDuration: total number of days that the primary resource runs.
 l Interval: specify how often the maintenance operation is performed. This is measured in 
either days or quantity depending on what value is selected for Type.
 l Interval Unit Of Measure: select the unit of measure (UOM) to use for quantity-based 
maintenance intervals. Operation quantities are converted by to this target UOM using the 
defined conversion rates in the UnitOfMeasure table (including part-specific overrides 
defined in the PartUOMConversion table) before being used to determine the quantity that 
triggers the maintenance.
 l Property Type: set the type to Operation Count, Quantity, or Working Duration when a 
specified property is used to trigger the maintenance.
 l Property: select a property to trigger the maintenance to run. If left blank when a property 
type is defined for the maintenance, any operation that matches the property type can 
trigger the maintenance.
 l Allow Operation To Pause: specify if the operations can pause over this maintenance.
 l Allow Maintenance Pausing Over Downtime: specify if this maintenance can pause over 
a downtime period on the schedule.
 l Allow Maintenance Pausing Over Off Shift: specify if this maintenance can pause over an 
off-shift period on the schedule.
 l Allow Maintenance Overlapping Downtime: specify if this maintenance can overlap with 
a downtime period on the schedule.
 l Allow Maintenance Overlapping Off Shift: specify if this maintenance can overlap with 
an off-shift period on the schedule.
Optional fields
 l Property Type: select the property type to associate with the maintenance record that will 
be used to filter which operations can trigger the maintenance when its type is set to 
Operation Count, Quantity, or Working Duration. For example, “Color”.
 l Property: select the value of the property associated with this maintenance record that can 
trigger the maintenance when the property type is set to Operation Count, Quantity, or 
Working Duration. For example, “Red” or “Yellow”. If this value is blank but a property type 
has been specified, any operation that matches the property type can trigger the 
maintenance.

===== PAGE 103 =====
93 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Note: If the Type is set to OperationCount, any pausing configured for the maintenance 
will be ignored and instead the maintenance will always be scheduled at the end of the 
operation that triggered it.
Maintenance Resource Mapping worksheet
Required fields
 l Resource Area Site: select the site for the resource.
 l Maintenance Name: select the maintenance action to apply to the resource.
 l Resource Name: select the resource to apply the maintenance on.
 l Resource Area Group: select the area group for the resource.
 l Resource Area: select the area for the resource.
Create a historical maintenance record
Historical maintenance records are manually input and used by the Scheduling algorithm to 
understand how and when maintenance actions prior to the start of the scheduling horizon were 
performed.
Maintenance Operations worksheet
Required fields
 l Name: specify the name of the maintenance operation.
 l Input Primary Resource Area records (Site, Area Group Name, Name): the area 
associated with the primary resource that the maintenance operation takes place on.
 l Input Primary Resource Name: select the resource the maintenance action is applied on.
 l Maintenance Name: select the name of the associated maintenance action
 l Duration Override: optionally, specify how many days the override on the duration was 
applied. If this value is not null, the EndDateTime of the maintenance operation was 
overridden by it's StartDateTime + DurationOverride.
 l Input Start Date Time: select the date and time that the operation started.
Optional fields
 l Is Bypass: identifies if this maintenance operation has been set to unassigned and held by 
a user.
When Y, the overridden maintenance operation is bypassed by the Scheduling algorithm 
and displays with a duration of 0 on the Gantt chart. 
 l Input End Date Time: the date and time that the operation ended.

===== PAGE 104 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration94
Sample configuration
For example, you have a line the produces chocolate granola that requires Mixer A to be cleaned 
for a half-day after every 10,000 operations that run on it. Every three months, Mixer A must have 
its mesh screen replaced. This replacement maintenance action already exists in the system.
First you need to create the maintenance action record and specify its properties.
Then you need to map the maintenance action to the resource it is applied on.
Lastly, the screen replacement maintenance action must be mapped to the cleaning 
maintenance action. The maintenance action whose interval will be reset, the cleaning action 
here, is considered the Reset Maintenance.


===== PAGE 105 =====
95 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Set up a maintenance action after a campaign
You can set up a maintenance action to take place at the end of a campaign. The minimum 
number of days before the campaign can restart after ending (Campaign.RestartThreshold) is 
removed and the campaign can restart immediately after the maintenance action is completed.
Maintenance to Campaign worksheet
Required fields
 l Campaign Area Site: select the site for the area associated with the campaign.
 l Campaign Name: select the campaign that the maintenance takes place after.
 l Campaign Area Group: select the area group for the area associated with the campaign.
 l Campaign Area: select the area associated with the campaign.
 l Maintenance Name: select the maintenance to map to the selected campaign.
Trigger maintenances using properties
Maintenances can be triggered by the property relationships defined on parts, scheduled 
receipts, or operations. For example, a maintenance can be configured to take place after every 
100 operations of a blue part have been completed.
To schedule maintenances using property relationships, you must assign a property and 
property type to the maintenance and set the maintenance type to Operation Count, Quantity, 
or Working Duration. When different properties have been defined at different levels, the 
following priority order is used with scheduled receipt operations as the highest priority:
 l Scheduled receipt operation
 l Route operation
 l Scheduled receipt
 l Part
For example, if a color property is assigned at multiple levels:
 l Scheduled receipt operation = "yellow"
 l Route operation = "red"
 l Scheduled receipt "blue"
 l Part = "pink"
The maintenance will use the assigned property for the scheduled receipt operation based on 
the priority order. If the scheduled receipt operation has no defined property, then the 
maintenance use the property defined at the next level of the priority order. 

===== PAGE 106 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration96
Note: If the property for a maintenance record is blank but a property type is defined, 
then any operation with that property type can trigger the maintenance.
Maintenance  worksheet
Required fields
 l Name: specify a name for the maintenance action.
 l Operation Segment Type Control Set: select the control set for the operation segment 
type assigned to this maintenance action.
 l Operation Segment Type Name: select the operation segment type used exclusively for 
maintenance actions.
 l Duration: specify how many days the maintenance action takes.
 l Type: select one of the following values that determines how the maintenance interval is 
calculated:
 l OperationCount: the total number of operations.
 l Quantity: total quantity produced by the primary resource.
 l WorkingDuration: total number of days that the primary resource runs.
 l Interval: specify how often the maintenance operation is performed. This is measured in 
either days or quantity depending on what value is selected for Type.
 l Interval Unit Of Measure: select the unit of measure (UOM) to use for quantity-based 
maintenance intervals. Operation quantities are converted by to this target UOM using the 
defined conversion rates in the UnitOfMeasure table (including part-specific overrides 
defined in the PartUOMConversion table) before being used to determine the quantity that 
triggers the maintenance.
 l Property Type: select the property type to associate with the maintenance record that will 
be used to filter which operations can trigger the maintenance when its type is set to 
Operation Count, Quantity, or Working Duration. For example, “Color”.
 l Property: select the value of the property associated with this maintenance record that can 
trigger the maintenance when the property type is set to Operation Count, Quantity, or 
Working Duration. For example, “Red” or “Yellow”. If this value is blank but a property type 
has been specified, any operation that matches the property type can trigger the 
maintenance.
Note: Specifically for the maintenance type Operation Count, if pausing has been 
enabled on an operation, pausing is ignored and the maintenance is always scheduled at 
the end of the operation that triggered it.

===== PAGE 107 =====
97 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Modify the start interval for a maintenance
You can reset when the interval for a maintenance starts based on a changeover, downtime, or 
another maintenance action.
Modify the start interval for a maintenance action for a 
changeover
A changeover in the schedule can reset the interval start for a maintenance action to begin again 
after the changeover is completed.
Maintenance to Changeover worksheet
Required fields
 l Changeover Area Site: select the site for the area associated with the changeover.
 l Changeover Name: select the changeover that resets the interval start of the mapped 
maintenance .
 l Changeover Area Group: select the group for the area associated with the changeover.
 l Changeover Area: select the area associated with the changeover.
 l Maintenance Name: select the maintenance to map to the selected changeover.
Modify the start interval for a maintenance action for a 
downtime
A downtime in the schedule can reset the interval start for a maintenance action to begin again 
after the downtime is completed. Downtimes are mapped using the downtime type so that every 
downtime of that type triggers an interval restart.
Maintenance to Downtime Type worksheet
Required fields
 l Downtime Type Name: select the type of downtime that resets the interval start of the 
mapped maintenance action.
 l Maintenance Name: the maintenance action to map to the selected downtime type.
Modify the start interval for a maintenance action for 
another maintenance action
One maintenance action (action 1) resets the interval start for another maintenance action 
(action 2).

===== PAGE 108 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration98
Maintenance to Maintenance worksheet
Required fields
 l Maintenance Name: select the maintenance action that resets the interval start of the 
mapped maintenance action (action 1).
 l Reset Maintenance Name: select the mapped maintenance action (action 2).

===== PAGE 109 =====
99 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Define component consumption types
The types of orders that can be used to source component parts to fulfill BOM requirements for 
assembly operations can be customized to meet different production situations. The component 
consumption type assigned to a part specifies if it can use on hand, scheduled receipt, or 
planned orders to satisfy its supply allocation requirements for component operations. Each 
order type quantity generates a SchedulingWhereConsumed record.
Alternately, the consumption type can be set to unconstrained where BOM requirements are 
bypassed when considering the availability of sources for component operations. On the Gantt 
chart, users can schedule successor operations before predecessor operations when the 
component consumption type is set to unconstrained for those parts.
The ComponentAvailabilityType field determines when components become consumable to 
satisfy BOM requirements when the pegging source for the schedule 
(SchedulingConfiguration.PeggingSource) is set to SupplyPlanning.
Data configuration
To create and apply a component consumption type, the following steps are required:
 1. Create a new record to define the component consumption type.
 2. Assign the component consumption type to one or more parts.

===== PAGE 110 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration100
Component Consumption Type worksheet
Required fields
 l Value: specify a name for this consumption type.
 l Control Set: select the control set to associate with this consumption type.
 l Use On Hand: select Y to use on hand orders for component operations. Otherwise select 
N.
 l Use Planned Orders: select Y to use planned orders for component operations. Otherwise 
select N.
 l Use Scheduled Receipts: select Y to use scheduled receipts orders for component 
operations. Otherwise select N.
 l Component Availability Type: select from one of the following:
 l ScheduledCompletion: components become consumable after their scheduled 
completion time.
 l PlanningAvailableDate: components become consumable on the date when the entire 
supply is available (WhereConsumed.SupplyAvailableDate).
 l LatestScheduledCompletionAvailableDate: components become consumable at their 
latest is available (WhereConsumed.SupplyAvailableDate).
 l Unconstrained: components are consumable at any time.
Parts worksheet
Required fields
 l For the part that you want to assign a consumption to, type the consumption type name in 
the Component Consumption Type column.

===== PAGE 111 =====
101 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Set requirements for secondary resources
Secondary resources such as tools or skilled personnel act as constraints on scheduling. To 
define the parameters for the constraint, requirements for the secondary resource need to be set 
at the operation segment level such as default, maximum, and minimum quantities of secondary 
resource for a segment or if the secondary resource is required at the start or end of the 
segment.
For secondary resources that will use the hold feature, that resource must be available 24/7, that 
is have a shift pattern defined for 24 hour, 7 days a week availability, to avoid situations where 
the secondary resource is unavailable and assigned to be held until manually released.
Secondary resource requirements are set at the operation segment level. You can override 
default settings in the SecondaryResourceRequirement table by mapping a specific scheduled 
receipt operation segment to a different secondary resource requirement in the 
ScheduledReceiptOperationSegmentResourceAssignment table. See Create scheduled receipt 
operations.
Data configuration
To set requirements for secondary resources, the following steps are required:
 1. Create a new record for the secondary resource requirement group.
 2. Create a new record for the secondary resource requirement.
 3. Map the secondary resource requirement to one or more primary resources.
Tip:  The Secondary Resource Requirements worksheet with the "s" (plural) is for creating 
groups.
Secondary Resource Requirements Groups worksheet
Required fields
 l Name: the name of the secondary resource requirement group.
 l Route Operation fields (including Route and Sequence): the route operation associated 
with this requirements group.
 l Route Operation Route Part Source records (Base Key, Part Site, Part Name,  Source 
Destination Site, Source Supplier Site, Source ID, Source Supplier Id): the part source 
records for the route operation associated with this requirements group.

===== PAGE 112 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration102
Secondary Resources Requirements worksheet
Required fields
 l Secondary Resource Group: the resource group that the secondary resource with this 
requirement is assigned to.
 l Secondary Resource Requirement Group: the group this secondary resource 
requirement belongs to.
 l Operation Segment Type: the type of operation segment this secondary resource 
requirement is associated with.
 l Operation Segment Type Control Set: the control set associated with this segment 
type. Typically this is set to Default.
 l Secondary Resource Selection Rule: select the rule to apply that determines which 
secondary resource is used for an operation when more than one eligible secondary 
resource is available:
 l Priority: the secondary resource with the highest priority is selected.
 l NearestQtyAvailable: an average of eligible resource quantities over the expected time 
period is used to select the secondary resource with the average closest to the 
secondary resource requirement quantity.
 l LeastGroups: the secondary resource that is a member of the fewest resource groups is 
selected. Secondary resources that belong to multiple groups are considered to have 
higher flexibility and are used when necessary.
 l Default Quantity: the quantity of secondary resource required to run its associated route 
operation.
 l IsHeld: if the operation segment is held as it waits for a secondary resource. The secondary 
resource travels with the work item in the operation until it is no longer held.
 l Held over Operation Pauses: if the secondary resource is held during the period that the 
operation pauses because its primary resource is unavailable
 l Require At: specify when the secondary resource is held or released during an operation 
segment.
Valid values are:
 l SegmentStart: 
If the required quantity is > 0, then the secondary resource is required from the start of 
the operation segment and held until released.
If the required quantity is < 0, then the secondary resource is released at the start of the 
operation segment.
 l SegmentEnd:
If the required quantity is > 0, then the secondary resource is required from the end of 
the operation segment and held until released.

===== PAGE 113 =====
103 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
If the required quantity is < 0, then the secondary resource is released at the end of the 
operation segment.
 l Enforce Concurrent Primary Resource Runs: select if all resources associated with the 
same secondary resource will start or run operations concurrently.
Valid values area:
 l True: all resources connected to the same secondary resource requirement must run 
operations concurrently.
 l False: the value specified in the Allow Dispatch Rule Gaps column for this resource is 
ignored.
Optional fields
 l Maximum Quantity: the maximum quantity of the secondary resource required for the 
operation segment.
 l Minimum Quantity: the minimum quantity of the secondary resource required for the 
operation segment.
 l Maximum Handoffs: specify the maximum number of times that an operation can be 
handed off between secondary resources in the same resource group. This value is also 
dependent on the availability of primary and secondary resources to process the 
operations.
Secondary Resource Requirement Group Resource Mapping
Required fields
 l Secondary Resource Requirement Group: select the group to map the secondary 
resource requirement to.
 l Resource Name: select the secondary resource to map to the secondary resource 
requirement group.
 l Resource Area Group: select the area group for the area associated with the resource.
 l Resource Area Site: select the site for the area associated with the resource.
 l Resource Area: select the area associated with the resource.

===== PAGE 114 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration104
Define secondary resource replenishment
To keep the schedule running efficiently, you can specify replenishment rules for secondary 
resources that ensure that they are continuously replenished by a defined quantity at a specified 
time. For example, you can restrict the maximum number of changeovers within a specified time 
period on a secondary resource by replenishing the resource to a predefined quantity.
Replenishment happens at a resource level and the rule must be mapped to a specific secondary 
resource. To apply a replenishment rule to a resource, route operations should be held until that 
secondary resource is available by setting the IsHeld value on the Secondary Resource 
Requirements worksheet to true.
When you first create a replenishment rule, you must define properties for it such as setting the 
replenishment cycle, mode, and quantity.
The replenishment cycle is set by defining a time unit (RecurrenceFrequency) such as a day, 
week, or shift and a number value (RecurrenceInterval). For example a frequency of week and 
interval of 2 means that the replenishment on the secondary resource happens every 2 weeks.
There are two types of replenishment mode that you can set:
 l Replenish To: the secondary resource quantity is increased to the value defined in the 
Quantity field.
 l Replenish By: the secondary resource quantity is increased by the value defined in the 
Quantity field. The quantity can keep replenishing until it reaches the MaxAccrual quantity.
Note: When a replenishment rule is applied to a secondary resource, its available 
quantity might exceed the value of available quantity defined for its shift pattern in the 
Shift Pattern Resource Availability worksheet.
After defining the replenishment rule, you must map to a specific secondary resource and define 
the start and end dates for the rule’s effectivity period.
Data configuration
To create and apply a replenishment rule, the following steps are required:
 1. Define the replenishment rule
 2. Map the rule to a secondary resource

===== PAGE 115 =====
105 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Replenishment Rule worksheet
Required fields
 l Name: specify a name for the rule.
 l Recurrence Frequency: select the time unit for the replenishment interval. Valid values:
 l Day: replenish every x day.
 l Hour: replenish every x hour.
 l Month: replenish every x month.
 l Shift: replenish every x shift.
 l Week: replenish every x week.
Where x is the value specified for RecurrenceInterval.
 l Recurrence Interval: specify a number of time units that will be used with the Recurrence 
Frequency value to define the replenishment cycle.
 l Mode: select the replenishment mode. Valid values include:
 l Replenish To: increases the secondary resource quantity to the value defined in the 
Quantity field.
 l Replenish By: increases the secondary resource quantity by the value defined in the 
Quantity field. It can keep replenishing until it reaches the value specified for the 
MaxAccrual quantity.
 l Max Accrual: specify the maximum allowable quantity of accumulated secondary resource. 
This limit is only applied when Mode is set to Replenish By.
 l Quantity: specify the quantity of secondary resource to replenish.
 l Startup Condition: select the condition the determines the initial condition for the 
replenishment rule. Valid values include:
 l ShiftAvailability: the initial quantity of the secondary resource is determined by the 
available resource quantity  defined in the ShiftPatternResourceAvailability table.
Resource To Replenishment Rule worksheet
Required fields
 l Value: specify a name to identify this mapping record.
 l Resource Area Site: select the site for the secondary resource.
 l Resource Area Group: select the site for the secondary resource.
 l Resource Area: select the site for the secondary resource.
 l Resource Name: select the secondary resource to map to the replenishment rule.
 l Replenishment Rule: select the rule to map to the secondary resource.

===== PAGE 116 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Dataconfiguration106
 l Effective In Date Time: specify the start date and time for when the rule is effective.
 l Effective Out Date Time: specify the end date and time for when the rule is effective.

===== PAGE 117 =====
107 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Configure  the Gantt chart
Data in the Gantt chart is generated by mapping columns to chart properties and by mapping 
scripts to chart actions. It is recommended that you configure or modify Gantt chart properties in 
the Maestro Java client.
 1. Check out the Enterprise Scheduling Workbench workbook and click Design Mode 
  
on the toolbar.
 2. Use the Data tab to configure properties for resources, scheduled and unscheduled 
operations, and the dependencies between operations. See Map Gantt chart properties.
 3. Use the Settings tab to map scripts to chart actions such as setting a frozen window or 
displaying secondary resource data as swimlanes on the chart. See Map Gantt chart 
actions.
Note: The Style tab currently has no properties to configure.
Map Gantt chart properties
The Data tab contains all of the properties that need to be mapped to applicable data columns 
to display data in the Gantt chart. Four sections of mapping are required to display the following 
on the chart, however not all properties are required.
 l Resource: How resources display on the Gantt chart.
 l Scheduled Event: How scheduled operations display on the Gantt chart.
 l Unscheduled Event: How unscheduled operations display in the Unscheduled Operations 
pane.
 l Dependency: How precedence relationships between operations are shown in the Gantt 
chart.
Each property is outlined using the following format and where applicable, images or 
instructions are included that describe how the mapped property is applied.
Property Name
Description of 
the property
Data type: The required data type for the column(s) to map to this property.
Required for Enterprise Scheduling: If this property must be mapped for the Gantt chart to 
display data as expected for Enterprise Scheduling.
Default column: The default column mapped to this property. If applicable, the specific data 
model field is included in brackets.

Scheduled Event section
Operations that are scheduled display as colored bars on the Gantt chart. Bars are placed in the 
swimlane of the resource they are produced on at the time that they are produced. The length of 
a bar represents the operation duration and the color represents a defined part property for the 
operation. 
The properties in the Scheduled Event section define how scheduled operations display in the 
Gantt chart.
ID  
Unique identifier for each operation. This is required to link operations within 
the Gantt chart.
Data type: Integer
Required for Enterprise 
Scheduling: Y
Default column: Event Id
Parent ID  
Operations are further divided into segments. The Parent ID is used to connect 
operation segments that belong to the same operation.
Data type: Integer
Required for Enterprise 
Scheduling: Y
Default column: Parent 
Id

Start Date Override  
The start date for scheduled operations defined by user 
changes. When changes to the schedule are saved, the 
Scheduling algorithm runs again to fill in any remaining 
gaps. This override prevents the algorithm from overwriting 
those changes made to operation start dates

changes. When changes to the schedule are saved, the 
Scheduling algorithm runs again to fill in any remaining 
gaps. This override prevents the algorithm from overwriting 
those changes made to operation end dates

(ScheduledReceiptOperation.InputEndDateTime)

===== PAGE 134 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)ConfiguretheGanttchart 124
Map Gantt chart actions
The Settings tab is used to configure actions for the Gantt chart that includes both automated 
and custom actions.
 l Automated actions: display different data aspects on the chart such as setting a frozen 
window or displaying the defined scheduling horizon. 
 l Custom actions: actions ones that users can interact with from an actions menu.
 Each type of action must be mapped to predefined scripts that specify how those actions are 
applied. Most actions are mapped to default scripts however you can modify those scripts or 
map an action to a custom script. By default, all scripts are public however, you can map a private 
script to a chart action by clearing the Public checkbox.
Actions that must be mapped to a script to display data in the Gantt chart are identified as 
Required. Other actions are optional, but depending on what data and options you want visible 
on the chart can be highly effective to map.
Each action is outlined below using the following format and where applicable, additional details 
about the default script are provided.
Action Name
Description of this 
set of actions.
Default Script: The default script mapped to this set of actions.
Enterprise Scheduling Configuration: If this set of actions must be mapped for the Gantt 
chart to behave as expected in Enterprise Scheduling.
Automated actions
The following mappings support automated actions that display data on the Gantt chart.
Three actions are required for Enterprise Scheduling and must be mapped:
 l Get Resource Calendar
 l Get Frozen Window
 l Set Frozen Window
It is recommended that you map the  Get Scheduling Horizon action, however if you want to use 
a different timeline such as running a long horizon where every operation is scheduled or are 
using resource specific horizons defined in an extension then this action doesn't need to be 
mapped.
Get Custom Event Colors is an optional action.

===== PAGE 135 =====
125 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Get Resource Calendar
Defines the calendar used in the Enterprise Scheduling 
Workbench workbook.
Default Script: GetResourceCalendar
Enterprise Scheduling Configuration: Required
Script Details
The default script retrieves calendar data from another worksheet or table.
 l Returns an array of JSONCalendarData items with each item defining an interval in one calendar.
 l startDate and endDate properties are ISO8601 formatted dates.
 l isWorking and unspecifiedIsWorking properties contain Maestro Boolean values (Y/N).
 l resourceCount defines the capacity of the resource.
 l scheduledColumns array values should have the same order as the Scheduled Columns column mappings in 
the Resource section on the Data tab in the Enterprise Scheduling Workbench workbook.
 l order and schedulerColumns properties can be used to define attributes on empty resources. These 
attributes might be missing from the main worksheet.
 l style is an optional property and can be used to customize non-working intervals.
 l Intervals are plain gray by default.
 l Options break1 to break5 offer diagonal patterns that can be applied to the intervals.
 l type is an optional property that is currently not implemented in the default script.
Frozen Window 
A frozen window can be set on the schedule by users to prevent operations with due dates in the 
frozen window period from being moved or altered by the scheduling algorithm. The Frozen 
Window capability requires two actions to be mapped: Get Frozen Window and Set Frozen 
Window.
Get Frozen Window
Gets the end date for the frozen window. Default Script: GetFrozenWindow
Enterprise Scheduling Configuration: Required
Script Details
Retrieves the current value for the frozen window end date. 
 l If neither of the frozen window scripts are customized then create an "Approved Actions" private scenario and 
insert an initial value in the FrozenWindowEndDateTime field in the Area Groups worksheet.
Set Frozen Window
Determines when the frozen window is applied on the 
Gantt chart based on the end date specified by the user.
Default Script: SetFrozenWindow
Enterprise Scheduling Configuration: Required

===== PAGE 136 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)ConfiguretheGanttchart 126
Set Frozen Window
Script Details
Takes the new value of the frozen window end date as input and updates the AreaGroup table.
 l If neither of the frozen window scripts are customized then create an "Approved Actions" private scenario and 
insert an initial value in the FrozenWindowEndDateTime field in the Area Groups worksheet.
Get Scheduling Horizon
Defines the  scheduling horizon. Default Script: GetSchedulingHorizon
Enterprise Scheduling Configuration: Recommended
Script Details
Retrieves the start date and horizon duration values that are then used to specify the number of days for the 
scheduling horizon on the Gantt chart.
 l The start and end dates are used to define the Full Horizon zoom setting.
Get Custom Event Colors
Defines the colors used to color operation bars that represent specific categories. For 
example, operations used to produce antiseptic might be colored green and operations to 
produce washing liquid are colored yellow.
Default Script: Get 
CustomEventColors
Enterprise 
Scheduling 
Configuration: 
Optional
Script Details
Retrieves custom colors defined on the Gantt chart.
 l Fields from the Property table are used to define which colors to use for the operation bars. 
 l ColorCode values must be defined as hex code values.
