Retrieves data for secondary resource utilization and displays this data as area charts in swimlanes on the Gantt 
chart.
Enable operation-based mode  
When enabled, changes the chart to display as an operation-based Gantt chart where 
eligible orders for a single resource display on the Y axis and time on the X axis. 
Note: This mode can only display operations for one resource at a time. Users are 
prompted to select a single resource in the Resource filter.
Enterprise Scheduling 
Configuration: Optional


===== PAGE 139 =====
129 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Scheduling algorithms
Enterprise Scheduling relies on four embedded  algorithms that provide the logic to generate 
production schedules. The algorithms are  created in Java and implemented using an application 
container that is external to Maestro. The algorithm code is stored and managed in an external 
location and communicates with Maestro to populate and update the production schedule. 
Different aspects of the application container are monitored and can be viewed in the 
administration logs. See the Maestro Administration Guide.
Algorithm Description Owning 
Record
Depends On
EnterpriseScheduling
ResourceAvailaibility
Calculates intervals of 
availability for a resource 
based on the intersection of 
shift patterns and downtimes 
throughout the scheduling 
period.
Resource 
(Scheduling)
 
EnterpriseScheduling
WorkBreakdown
Calculates planned work order 
items by dividing planned 
orders into work items that can 
be scheduled. The work item 
sizes are determined by the 
quantity defined in the 
PlannedWorkItemQuantity 
table.
Site  
EnterpriseScheduling
WorkBreakdownOperations
Calculates the necessary 
options to be scheduled for 
each planned work item. This 
calculation is based on the 
referenced part routing.
Site EnterpriseScheduling
WorkBreakdown results
EnterpriseScheduling Calculates the production 
schedule for the defined 
scheduling period by assigning 
start and end times to 
operations based on the 
priorities defined in the 
scheduling input tables.
Area EnterpriseScheduling
ResourceAvailaibility, 
EnterpriseScheduling
WorkBreakdown, and 
EnterpriseScheduling
WorkBreakdownOperations 
results
Enterprise Scheduling logic can be customized to meet customer needs by overriding parts of 
the logic using application extensions. See Application extensions.

===== PAGE 140 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)SearchAlgorithmoption 130
Search Algorithm option
By default, the preliminary production schedule  is generated by running the Scheduling 
algorithm once and then displaying the results in the Gantt chart in the Enterprise Scheduling 
Workbench workbook. You can enable the Search Algorithm option to run the algorithm 
multiple times to find a more effective schedule based on defined parameters. Each run of the 
algorithm produces an iteration of the schedule which is a candidate schedule that is measured 
on its effectiveness using a calculated score, the objective function. Maestro then searches for 
the iteration with the lowest objective function which identifies it as the most effective of all the 
candidate schedules. This is the schedule selected as the preliminary schedule that displays in 
the Gantt chart.
The Search Algorithm option must be actively enabled to run and its parameters defined at 
configuration. Only the maximum iterations and runtime values are editable by users and are 
associated with the assigned area group for a resource. See Set up an area.
Issues with the Search Algorithm configuration are identified in the Enterprise Scheduling Input 
Data Verification workbook.
Caution: The Search Algorithm option can impact performance as it runs the Scheduling 
algorithm multiple times. 
Operation priorities
Search algorithm components can use property values as weighting criteria when calculating the 
score for an iteration.  Lower values are considered as having higher effective priorities. The 
PropertyCoefficient field references the specified property value and how that value is 
calculated is determined by its assigned processing rule. 
Depending on the configuration, the property coefficient is calculated in one of the following 
ways:
 l When PropertyType = null or the data type for its value is not numeric, the 
PropertyCoefficient = 1 and has no impact on the score calculation.
 l When the PropertyType.ProcessingRule NOT = 1, the PropertyCoefficient is a calculated 
value that corresponds to the processing rule.
 l When no processing rule is applied, the PropertyCoefficient value = value for that 
property type.
Note: Order priority values are defined in the OrderPriority (Mfg) table and can be used 
to prioritize orders for scheduling.

===== PAGE 141 =====
131 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Data configuration
To enable the Search Algorithm option, the following steps are required:
 1. Define the parameters for the Search Algorithm rule set.
 2. Create one or more components for the Search Algorithm rule.
 3. Create a record for the objective function.
 4. Create one or more components that make up the objective function.
 5. Optionally, specify overrides for any of the objective function components.
 6. Enable the Search Algorithm option for a specific resource group.
 7. Optionally, specify the maximum iterations and/or runtime values used by the area group. 
You can also leave this for the user to specify.
Search Algorithm Rule
Required fields
 l Value: the name of this Search Algorithm rule.
 l Search Algorithm Type: select the search method to use. 
Valid values are:
 l SimulatedAnnealing: the search starts with an initial schedule and in each run of the 
Scheduling algorithm it perturbs the schedule by making one or more changes. These 
changes are defined by value selected for the neighbor selection mode.
 l Search Algorithm Temperature Cooling Factor: specify a value that controls the 
probability that the Scheduling algorithm will move to a candidate schedule with a worse 
score than the current schedule. 
Valid values are a number between 0 and 1.
The probability of accepting worse scores depends on the size of the difference between 
the schedule scores. A value of 1 allows the Search Algorithm option to accept schedules 
that have worse scores. When the value is below 1 the probability decreases with each 
iteration.
 l Search Algorithm Scoring Mode: select the method used to schedule a candidate.
Valid values are:
 l SchedulingEngine: runs the Scheduling algorithm each time a candidate schedule is 
generated. This option is recommended for schedules with complex secondary resource 
requirements or shift patterns. This option is not recommended for schedules with a 
large number of operations or resources because it can affect performance.
 l Approximate: runs a simplified simulation algorithm to approximate a schedule a its 
score. This option is recommended for schedules where changeovers largely drive the 
objective function score.

===== PAGE 142 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)SearchAlgorithmoption 132
 l Search Algorithm Perturbation Intensity Factor: specify a factor that determines the 
number of perturbations that happen in each run of the algorithm. The recommended 
value is 1. Higher values introduce more randomness into the search.
 l Search Algorithm Initial Sequence Mode: specify the mode used to generate an initial 
sequence of operations in the schedule to start the search.
Valid values are:
 l Random: the initial sequence of operations is randomly generated.
 l DispatchRule: the initial sequence of operations is generated based on the dispatch 
rule assigned to the resource.
Search Algorithm Rule Component
Required fields
 l Search Algorithm Rule Value: Select which Algorithm Rule this component is assigned to.
 l Search Algorithm Rule Component Type: Select the method used to generate a 
candidate schedule.
Valid values are:
 l ExtensionPoint: An extension point is used to generate a candidate schedule.
 l GroupProperty: Two random operations with the same property type are selected and 
the first operation is moved to a position right before the second operation in the 
schedule. All of the operations adjacent to the first operation with the same property 
type are also moved with the first operation.
 l InsertionImprovement: A random operation is selected and inserted into each eligible 
position in the schedule. The position that results in the lowest score is selected as the 
final position for that operation.
 l Perturb: Three random operations are selected and exchange positions backwards in 
the schedule. For example, operation A, B, and C change positions to B, C, A. This 
exchange is repeated multiple times in each iteration to find the configuration with the 
lowest score.
 l SingleRandomRelocation: A random operation is selected and inserted into the 
schedule in a new randomly selected position.
 l SingleRandomSwap: Two random operations are selected and switch positions in the 
schedule.
 l SwapImprovement: A random operation is selected and exchanges position with each 
eligible operation in the schedule. The exchange with the lowest score is the one used.
Note: The SingleRandomRelocation and SingleRandomSwap methods require 
a larger number of iterations to succeed at improving the schedule.

===== PAGE 143 =====
133 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
 l Sequence: Specify a number value to assign to the component which determines when it 
will be executed during an iteration.
 l Property Type: Select the property type to assign to the component. When there are a 
large number of iterations, this type is used to by the algorithm to group operations.
 l Probability: Specify a number value to determine what probability this component has of 
being executed during an iteration.
 l Start Iteration: Specify which iteration the component becomes eligible for execution in 
within a range of executions. This range is determined by the maximum runtime or 
maximum iterations.
 l End Iteration: Specify which iteration is the last iteration that the component is eligible for 
execution within a range o executions. This range is determined by the maximum runtime 
or maximum iterations.
Notes: 
 l Start and End Iteration values of 0 imply that the component should run through all 
iterations.
 l The SingleRandomRelocation and SingleRandomSwap methods require a larger 
number of iterations to succeed at identifying the best candidate schedule than the 
PerturbWithInsertionAndSwapImprovement method.
Objective Function
Required fields
 l Name: the name of the objective function value.

===== PAGE 144 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)SearchAlgorithmoption 134
Objective Function Component
Objective functions are made up of one or more components with a component being made up 
of a defined coefficient value and a selected variable.
Required fields
 l Objective Function: the name of the objective function that this component belongs to.
 l Objective Function Variable: the variable assigned to this objective function.
Valid values include:
 l ChangeoverDuration: the number of days incurred by the changeover.
 l ChangeoverPenalty: the sum of all changeover penalties incurred. No units are 
assigned to this value.
 l IdleTime: the total number of idle days.
 l InventoryHolding: the total number of inventory holding days.
 l Tardiness: the total number of tardy days.
 l Coefficient: the coefficient applied to this component that determines how much in 
contributes to its assigned objective function.
 l Property Coefficient: the property value used as a multiplier on the objective function's 
calculated score. How the property value is calculated is determined by the processing rules 
selected in the PropertyType table. This value is nullable.
 

===== PAGE 145 =====
135 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Objective Function Component Override
Overrides let you refine objective function scores. For example, if you want tardiness to impact 
the score but only when orders are over a week late you can set the score override to a penalized 
value for orders with two weeks or more of lateness. 
Required fields
 l Objective Function: name of the objective function that the component being overridden 
belongs to.
 l Objective Function Variable: the variable assigned to the objective function that the 
component being overridden belongs to.
 l Priority: specify a priority to assign to the override. This value determines which override 
takes precedence when two different overrides are applicable. For example, if two overrides 
have overlapping date ranges, the priority determines which override is applied first.
 l Coefficient Override: specify a value to override the objective function component 
coefficient value.
 l Maximum Score: the maximum score that an objective function value has to be below for 
its score to be overridden. 
 l Maximum Score Inclusive: determines if the maximum score value is included in the 
maximum-minimum core range
 l Minimum Score: the minimum score that an objective function value has to be below for 
its score to be overridden. 
 l Minimum Score Inclusive: determines if the minimum score value is included in the 
maximum-minimum core range
 l Score Override: specify a value to override the score calculated for the objective function.
Area Group
Required fields
 l Search Algorithm Rule Set: select the Search Algorithm rule to apply to this area group.
Optional fields
 l Search Algorithm Maximum Iterations: specify the maximum number of schedule 
iterations that are searched. The initial sequence of operations that the algorithm starts 
with doesn't count as an iteration.
 l Search Algorithm Maximum Runtime: specify the maximum number of seconds that the 
algorithm can run. This value is checked every iteration so the limit can be exceeded by the 
duration of a single iteration. A value of <=0 implies no time limit.

===== PAGE 146 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Applicationextensions 136
Application extensions
You can add custom logic in Enterprise Scheduling to get desired planning, pegging, or 
scheduling behavior. For example, if a constraint can't be modeled using the standard table 
configuration in Enterprise Scheduling, you can use one of the predefined extension points on 
the Scheduling algorithm to define custom logic for the constraint. See Extension points.
The custom code for one or more extension points is packaged into an application extension 
that must be uploaded into Maestro to function. See Building and deploying the extension.
Extension points are written in Java and require a set of tools to modify, build, and deploy. One 
of those tools is the Extension Development Kit which exposes stub functions for the available 
extension points. See Extension Deployment Kit (EDK).
 
Extension Deployment Kit (EDK)
The Extension Deployment Kit provides you with ability to modify any of the available extension 
points for the Enterprise Scheduling algorithm. It exposes the stub functions that you use to 
write your extensions and build the jar file for the application extension. 
The EDK includes access to the following:
Helper methods
 l PrimaryResourceChangeoverCalculation.calculate(fromBatch, toBatch): calculates 
changeover results between two operatoins
 l ResourceEarliestFeasibleTimingsCalculation.calculate(batch): identifies feasible resource 
booking time
 l ResourceGetPreviousScheduledBatch(eventTime): returns the last operation scheduled on a 
resource. This method is a list on Resource that stores all the scheduled operations
Additional arguments
 l AreaGroup
 l DisreteTimeEvent
 l PrimaryResource (except for BOMAssembly-related events)
 l WorkQueueManager

===== PAGE 147 =====
137 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Tools
Extension points are defined and modified in Java. The following tools are required to modify 
application extension in Maestro:
 l Java OpenJDK from Oracle: version 17.0.1
 l A Java IDE of your choice
 l Apache Maven: minimum version 3.9.1
 l EDK project: download this from the Enterprise Scheduling Extension Development Kit 
page on the Kinaxis Knowledge Network.
This project version must be compatible with the Enterprise Scheduling version. Currently. 
EDK 1.0.0 is compatible with Enterprise Scheduling ES2403 (1.6.0).

===== PAGE 148 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Applicationextensions 138
Modifying an extension point
The custom code for extension points is added using the EDK in your Java IDE of choice. 
 1. After downloading the EDK and setting it up by cloning the project files into a Project from 
Version Control.
 2. Click the Maven reload button to refresh your dependencies. 
 3. Implement one or more of the available extension points by replacing the logic in the 
function with your own custom logic. See Extension points.
 4. Add a metadata file for the extension.

===== PAGE 149 =====
139 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Add a metadata file
The extension jar file is versioned using this metadata file.
 1. Create a json file named 'metadata' and saved it under in this folder path: 
extensions/<extension-name>/src/main/resources.
 2. Include the following in the json file:
 l applicationId: the name of the application that the extensions are overriding. This 
must remain as "Enterprise-Scheduling".
 l extensionName: allows the user to give their application extension a specific name to 
identify it once compiled.
 l extensionVersion: The version of the extension file that will be read and compared by 
Maestro when the file is uploaded. Every time a new jar file is produced for 
deployment, increment the version number. 
 l minContainerVersion: The minimum version of Enterprise Scheduling that this 
extension will be enabled with. This should be managed by the EDK developer to 
ensure compatibility. Currently, EDK 1.0.0 is compatible with Enterprise Scheduling 
ES2403 (1.6.0).
 l maxContainerVersion: The maximum version of Enterprise Scheduling that this 
extension will be enabled with. This should be managed by the EDK developer to 
ensure compatibility. Currently, EDK 1.0.0 is compatible with Enterprise Scheduling 
ES2403 (1.6.0).
Logging and debugging
 l To enable logging within your extension, add the following line to your class:
private final SimpleLogging log = SimpleLogging.getAppLoggerInstance
(<classname>.class);
Once the logger has been defined, you can write then to the log file.

===== PAGE 150 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Applicationextensions 140
Extension points
The Scheduling algorithm has the following predefined extension points that you can modify 
with custom logic:
Extension Point Name Use this extension 
point to
General extension points  
BatchSelectionPostProcessing  Specify custom logic for a 
selected operation and its 
properties.
DispatchRuleComponent Override the dispatch rule score 
method and return an updated 
component score for the 
operation.
IsBatchFeasible Specify custom conditions that 
impact a specified operation's 
feasibility to run. 
IsOperationFeasible Define the conditions that would 
make an operation unfeasible to 
process on a primary resource at 
a specified time. 
OverrideBackwardScheduledPredecessorTargetTimeOverride the calculated start 
target start or end time for 
predecessor batches that are 
backward scheduled.
OverrideBatchComponentRequirements Override existing BOM 
requirements for a specified 
operation.
OverrideBatchSelection Determine which operation to 
process next from a list of 
potential operations at a 
specified event time.

===== PAGE 151 =====
141 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Extension Point Name Use this extension 
point to
OverrideDurationForBatchSegment Override the segment duration 
for a specified operation.
OverrideGetBackwardScheduledPredecessors Augment or replace the list of 
predecessor operations that are 
scheduled.
OverridePlannedOrderWorkItems Create work items and generate 
a custom list of planned order 
work items
OverrideSchedulingInlinePegging Override the allocation of work 
items to the BOM for a batch 
when the pegging source is set 
to 'SchedulingInProcess'.
OverrideSecondaryResourceRequirements Override the existing secondary 
resource requirements for a 
specified operation.
OverrideSelectBestBackwardScheduledBatch Determine which batch, out of 
multiple that are feasible for 
backward scheduled, should be 
scheduled.
OverrideTargetTimeForBackwardScheduledTriggeringBatchOverride the calculated start time 
of a triggering batch for 
predecessors that are backward 
scheduled.
SchedulingUpFrontPeggingWhereConsumed Specify material pegging in 
advance of scheduling
SchedulePostProcessing Adjust the properties of 
scheduled operations after the 
algorithm logic is complete but 
before the data is transformed 
into results.

EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Applicationextensions 142
Extension Point Name Use this extension 
point to
Search Algorithm extension points  
OverrideSearchCandidateScheduleAcceptance Define custom criteria to 
compare current and candidate 
schedules and decide whether to 
update the current schedule with 
the candidate when running the 
Search Algorithm option.

the Search Algorithm process.

score and return an updated 
component score .

current iteration by overriding 
the scoring method.
SearchCandidateScheduleFromNeighbourhood Create the schedule for the next 
iteration by altering the current 
schedule produced by the 
algorithm.
SearchSchedulePostProcessing Alter the final schedule from 
running the Search algorithm 
option and produce a new 
schedule.
After testing and debugging, to implement application extensions, they must be uploaded to 
Maestro. See Building and deploying the extension.

===== PAGE 153 =====
143 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
BatchSelectionPostProcessing
This extension point allows you to specify custom logic that impacts a selected operation and its 
properties.
Inputs
 l AreaGroup: holds relevant information including explainability Booleans, parts, resources, 
resource groups, pegging sources and rules, work items, discrete start date, and frozen 
window end date
 l EventTime: the current time in the scheduling horizon (measured in seconds from Jan 1, 
1970)
 l PrimaryResource: the primary resource the operation is being scheduled on
 l Batch: current operation
Outputs
BatchSelectionPostProcessingExtResult*
Returns the Batch with updated properties
Example
The following example outlines how to modify this extension point to remove any gap between 
a manufacturing step and a packaging part for a step. On scheduling the packaging step, we will 
backwards schedule the manufacturing step to end at the start of the manufacturing step.
Setup
 1. Delete all 'On Hand' entries
 2. Delete all 'Independent Demand' entries
 3. In 'MFG Part', change all 'Lot Size' and 'Work Item Quantity' to 5600
 4. In 'Resource' delete 'MFG-B', 'PACK-22' and 'PACK23' resources
 5. In 'Scheduled Receipt' change all 'FG-xxx' Part Names to 'FG-208' and a'' WIP-xx'' to 
'WIP23', and  change the quantity to 5600
 6. In 'Scheduling Configuration' set 'Pegging Source' = 'Scheduling In Process'

===== PAGE 154 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Applicationextensions 144
Code
Result
Before applying the extension point, operations are run back to back on resource MFG-A. This is 
visible in both the Gantt chart and by looking at scheduled operations.
After applying the extension point, there are gaps between operations run on MFG-A that end 
right before the start of the associated PACK-21 operation. 

===== PAGE 155 =====
145 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
DispatchRuleComponentScore
This extension point allows you to override the score method used in a dispatch rule and return 
the updated component score for the operation.
Inputs
 l EventTime: the current time in the scheduling horizon (measured in seconds from Jan 1, 
1970)
 l PrimaryResource: the primary resource the operation is being scheduled on
 l VariableValue: this value differs based on where this method is called from. It could be 
slack time in days, critical ratio, or property double value.
 l Batch: current operation
Outputs
DispatchRuleComponentScoreExtResult*
Returns the updated component score for the current operation
Example
The following example outlines how to override the score for specified operation. A competitor 
has just introduced a peanut-flavored granola to compete with our own version of the product. 
We want to still use slack time as a weighted dispatch rule, however we want to score the 
peanut-flavored operations an extra 10% higher to make sure we don't run out of the flavor. If 
we have a operation with the property type = flavor and the flavor for the operation is 'Peanut', 
we'll multiple the slack time by 0.9. This gives that flavor an additional 10% priority because our 
initial configuration multiplies by 1 (our dispatchRuleComponent weighting).
Setup
 1. Change type of DispatchRule where name is 'Minimum due date' to 'Weighted'
 2. Delete record from DispatchRuleComponent where name is 'Minimum due date' and 
scoreType is 'minimum', and add a new record with name 'Minimum due date' and 
scoreType 'Extension'

===== PAGE 156 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Applicationextensions 146
Code
Result
Before applying the extension point, peanut flavored operations have no priority over other 
flavors.
After applying the extension point, peanut flavored operations are prioritized on both types of 
primary resource.

===== PAGE 157 =====
147 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
IsBatchFeasible
This extension point allows you to specify custom conditions that impact a specified operation's 
feasibility to run. This extension point can't override the standard feasibility checks that 
determine if an operation can run. For example, if there is no resource availability for an 
operation, then irrespective of the custom code that you define in the extension point, the 
operation would not be able to run.
Inputs
 l AreaGroup: holds relevant information including explainability Booleans, parts, resources, 
resource groups, pegging sources and rules, work items, discrete start date, and frozen 
window end date
 l EventTime: the current time in the scheduling horizon (measured in seconds from Jan 1, 
1970)
 l PrimaryResource: the primary resource the operation is being scheduled on
 l Batch: current operation
Outputs
IsBatchFeasibleExtResult*
Returns:
 l isFeasible property populated with true or false indicating whether the operation is 
feasible to execute.
 l BatchFeasibilityResultLog property can be populated with any logging data 
(optional)
 l NextBatchSelection property can be populated to indicate specified time to run 
operation selection (optional)

===== PAGE 158 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Applicationextensions 148
Example
The following example outlines how to modify your schedule to only produce a specific 
operation type on a specified day. If we only produce family packs of granola on the first 
Monday of each month we would set feasibility = false for any potential operation that are 'Pack 
Size' = 'Snack Pack' OR 'Individual Pack'.
Code
Result
Before applying the extension point, we see 'Snack Pack' and 'Individual Pack' operations 
scheduled on Monday, June 5.
After applying the extension point, only family pack operations are scheduled on Monday, June 
5.

===== PAGE 159 =====
149 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
IsOperationFeasible
This extension point lets you specify the conditions that would make an operation unfeasible to 
process on a primary resource at a specified time. This extension point can't override the inability 
to run on operation on a primary resource at a time when it's unavailable, such as due to a 
downtime. 
Inputs
 l AreaGroup: holds relevant information including explainability Booleans, parts, resources, 
resource groups, pegging sources and rules, work items, discrete start date, and frozen 
window end date
 l EventTime: the current time in the scheduling horizon (measured in seconds from Jan 1, 
1970)
 l PrimaryResource: the primary resource the operation is being scheduled on
 l WorkItemOperation: current operation that is set to run
Outputs
IsOperationFeasibleExtResult*
Returns:
 l isFeasible property set to true or false indicating whether the operation is feasible to 
process on the primary resource at the event time
 l NextBatchSelection property can be populated to indicate specified time to run 
operation selection (optional)
Example
The following example outlines how to schedule around a primary resource that is unable to 
process operations at a specified date and time. We have a packaging station that can't package 

===== PAGE 160 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Applicationextensions 150
dark chocolate flavored granola bars on Monday, June 5. Other products can be packaged, so 
this is not a downtime or off-shift period. We will set the extension point so that if the primary 
resource: ;PACK-21" and flavor = 'Dark Chocolate' and EventTime = June 5, then the operation is 
unfeasible to schedule,
Code
Result
Before applying the extension point, just like any other flavor, dark chocolate granola bar 
operations are scheduled to be produced on PACK-21 on Monday, June 5. 
After applying the extension point, dark chocolate granola bar operations are moved forward or 
packaged using other resource.

===== PAGE 161 =====
151 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
OverrideBackwardScheduledPredecessorTargetTime
This extension point allows you to override the calculated start target start or end time for 
predecessor batches that are backward scheduled. For example, this extension point can be used 
to insert custom buffers such as cooling or cleaning periods between related operations.
Inputs
 l AreaGroup: holds relevant information including explainability Booleans, parts, resources, 
resource groups, pegging sources and rules, work items, discrete start date, and frozen 
window end date
 l BackwardScheduledTempBatches: the list of backward batches that are already 
scheduled.
 l CalculatedTime: the target time calculated by the Scheduling algorithm.
 l IsCalculatedTimeJustInTime: identifies if the calculated time is the latest time allowable 
(just-in-time)
 l PredecessorBatch: the predecessor operation being scheduled.
 l TriggeringBatch: the batch that triggers backward scheduling.
 l TriggeringBatchStartTime: the start time of the triggering batch.
Outputs
OverrideBackwardScheduledPredecessorTargetTimeExtResult*
Returns the targetTime property populated with the overridden start or end time for the 
backward scheduled predecessor batch.
Example
The following example outlines how to add buffer time between baking and packaging to allow 
for cooling or sanitation by offsetting the predecessor operation's target time by 15 minutes. 

===== PAGE 162 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Applicationextensions 152
Code
package com.kinaxis.ps.extensions.overrideext;
 
import 
com.kinaxis.ps.extensions.extresult.OverrideBackwardScheduledPredecessorTargetT
imeExtResult;
import 
com.kinaxis.ps.extensions.interfaces.IExtOverrideBackwardScheduledPredecessorTa
rgetTime;
import com.kinaxis.ps.schedulingengine.*;
 
import java.util.List;
 
public class ExtOverrideBackwardScheduledPredecessorTargetTimeImpl
        implements IExtOverrideBackwardScheduledPredecessorTargetTime {
 
    @Override
    public OverrideBackwardScheduledPredecessorTargetTimeExtResult 
overrideBackwardScheduledPredecessorTargetTime(
            Batch predecessorBatch,
            List<Batch> backwardScheduledTempBatches,
            DiscreteEventTime triggeringBatchStartTime,
            Batch triggeringBatch,
            AreaGroup areaGroup,
            boolean isCalculatedTimeJustInTime,
            long calculatedTime) {
 
        var result = new 
OverrideBackwardScheduledPredecessorTargetTimeExtResult();
 
        if ("Baking".equals(predecessorBatch.getOperationType())) {
            result.setUseResult(true);
            result.setTargetTime(calculatedTime - 900); // 15-min cooling 
offset
        }
 
        return result;
    }
}

===== PAGE 163 =====
153 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Result
After implementing the extension point, instead of the product being packaged immediately 
after baking, there is a 15 minute cooling period added before the packaging operation.
OverrideBatchComponentRequirements
This extension point allows you to override existing BOM requirements (inputs that the 
operation consumes) for a specified operation.
Inputs
 l AreaGroup: holds relevant information including explainability Booleans, parts, resources, 
resource groups, pegging sources and rules, work items, discrete start date, and frozen 
window end date
 l EventTime: the current time in the scheduling horizon (measured in seconds from Jan 1, 
1970)
 l PrimaryResource: the primary resource the operation is being scheduled on
 l Batch: current operation
 l List of BOM requirements:  based on the BOM alternate assoicated with part source of the 
SR/PO that the operationis for
Outputs
OverrideBatchComponentRequirementsExtResult*
Returns the BOMRequirements property populated with list of updated BOM requirements 
(required materials and quantity of each)
Example
The following example outlines increasing the quantity of WIP components in the BOM. For 
example, the sales for carmamel and milk chocolate granola are lower than expected so we've 
decided to offer a promotion of 10% extra granola for both flavors. When flavor = 'Caramel' or 
'Milk Chocolate' then increase the quantity of WIP components from 1.0 to 1.1.
Setup
 1. Remove where 'Component Name' = 'RM' from 'Bill of Material' table
 2. Set 'Pegging Source' to 'SchedulingInProcess' in the 'Scheduling Configuration' table

===== PAGE 164 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Applicationextensions 154
Code
Result
Before the extension point is applied, parent scheduled receipt operations for the caramel or 
milk chocolate flavors were consuming 100% from scheduled receipt or planned order parts. For 
example, FG-208-PLANT-01-06-05-23_Op1 consumes 5,600 quantity.
After the extension point is applied, parent scheduled receipt operations for caramel or milk 
chocolate are consuming 110% from scheduled receipt or planned order parts. For example, FG-
208-PLANT-01-06-05-23_Op1 now consumes 6,160 quantity (5,600 * 1.1).
OverrideBatchSelection
This extension point lets you determine which operation to process next from a list of potential 
operations at a specified event time.

===== PAGE 165 =====
155 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Inputs
 l AreaGroup: holds relevant information including explainability Booleans, parts, resources, 
resource groups, pegging sources and rules, work items, discrete start date, and frozen 
window end date
 l EventTime: the current time in the scheduling horizon (measured in seconds from Jan 1, 
1970)
 l PrimaryResource: the primary resource the operation is being scheduled on
 l List potential operations that can run on the specified primary resource
Outputs
OverrideBatchSelectionExtResult*
Returns:
 l Batch property populated with batch to be executed at the given event time
 l SelectBestBatchResultLog property can be populated with any logging data (optional)
Example
The following example outlines prioritizing a specified operation at a specified point in time. For 
example, you want to prioritize the production of peanut-flavored granola bars at the start of the 
week (Monday, June 5, 2023) to minimize allergen cross-contamination. With the current time 
being Monday June 5, 2023, we would have any potential operation with flavor = 'Peanut' return 
the operation with the earliest due date.

===== PAGE 166 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Applicationextensions 156
Code
Result
Before applying the extension point, all flavors are produced on the schedule where they are 
eligible and available to be produced.
After applying the extension point, peanut flavored operations are favored in the schedule over 
other flavors.

===== PAGE 167 =====
157 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
OverrideDurationForBatchSegment
This extension point allows you to override the segment duration for a specified operation . For 
example, you might use it to define a custom changeover time.
Inputs
 l AreaGroup: holds relevant information including explainability Booleans, parts, resources, 
resource groups, pegging sources and rules, work items, discrete start date, and frozen 
window end date
 l PrimaryResource: the primary resource the operation is being scheduled on
 l Batch: current operation
 l OperationSegment: the segment the duration is overridden for which we are overriding 
duration for
 l Quantity: operation quantity (For example to be used for rate calculations)
Outputs
OverrideDurationForBatchSegmentExtResult*
Returns the Duration property populated with duration of the given segment on the specified 
operation (in seconds)
Example
The following example outlines how to define a custom production time for a specified product 
on a specific resource. For example, the packing operation for your dark chocolate granola bar is 
usually determined using calculations of the rate, quantity, and resource efficiency values. 
However, on the Pack-21 packaging machine, it takes a set time of 4 hours (14400 seconds) to 
package the dark chocolate granola bars. We will set the operation segment type to 'Working' 
and return 14400 seconds when the resource = 'Pack-21' and property type = flavor of 'Dark 
Chocolate'.

===== PAGE 168 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Applicationextensions 158
Code
Result
After implementing the extension point, instead of being a calculated value, the packaging step 
for the dark chocolate flavor has a set time of 4 hours on the Pack-21 resource as a 'Working' 
operation segment.
OverrideGetBackwardScheduledPredecessors
This extension point allows you augment or replace the list of predecessor operations that are 
scheduled. For example, when multiple production lines share a common allergen resource, you 
can use the extension point to include  cleanup operations that should happen before a batch of 
operations is triggered for scheduling on a line.

===== PAGE 169 =====
159 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Inputs
 l AreaGroup: holds relevant information including explainability Booleans, parts, resources, 
resource groups, pegging sources and rules, work items, discrete start date, and frozen 
window end date
 l TriggeringBatch: the batch the contains the scheduled predecessor operations.
 l TriggeringBatchStartTime: the intended triggering start time (in seconds)
 l List<WorkItemOperation>: the list of predecessor operations generated by the 
Scheduling algorithm.
Outputs
OverrideGetBackwardScheduledPredecessorsExtResult*
Returns list of nonPredecessors operations to replace or augment the default predecessor 
operations.
Example
The following example outlines how to insert a cleanup batch before flavor changeover even 
though the cleanup operations are not strict predecessors. 
Code
package com.kinaxis.ps.extensions.overrideext;
 
import 
com.kinaxis.ps.extensions.extresult.OverrideGetBackwardScheduledPredecessorsExt
Result;
import 
com.kinaxis.ps.extensions.interfaces.IExtOverrideGetBackwardScheduledPredecesso
rs;
import com.kinaxis.ps.schedulingengine.*;
 
import java.util.List;
 
public class ExtOverrideGetBackwardScheduledPredecessorsImpl implements 
IExtOverrideGetBackwardScheduledPredecessors {
 
    @Override
    public OverrideGetBackwardScheduledPredecessorsExtResult 
overrideGetBackwardScheduledPredecessors(

===== PAGE 170 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Applicationextensions 160
            AreaGroup areaGroup,
            Batch triggeringBatch,
            long triggeringBatchStartTime,
            List<WorkItemOperation> predecessors) {
 
        var result = new OverrideGetBackwardScheduledPredecessorsExtResult();
 
        var propertyType = new PropertyType("Flavor", DataType.STRING);
        if (triggeringBatch.getProperty(propertyType) != null &&
            "Peanut".equals(triggeringBatch.getProperty(propertyType).getValue
())) {
            result.setUseResult(true);
            result.setNonPredecessors(predecessors); // could be augmented with 
cleanup op
        }
 
        return result;
    }
}
Result
After implementing the extension point, instead of having flavor changeovers occur immediately 
without any allergen separation measures, cleanup operations are automatically added before 
any "Peanut" operations run.
OverridePlannedOrderWorkItems
This extension point lets you override the work breakdown logic and create work items and 
generate a custom list of planned order work items.
Inputs
Site: The site object
PartPlannedOrder (Map): Map of part and list of planned orders
Outputs
OverridePlannedOrderWorkItemsExtResult*
Returns updated list of PlannedOrderWorkItems

===== PAGE 171 =====
161 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Example
The following example outlines how to create smaller operations for a specified part and order 
line. For example, if a customer has requested smaller operations for planned orders where 
RouteName = 'FG-201' and PlannedOrderLine == '4.0' then we would divide each 
WorkItemQuantity by two to create the smaller operations (and twice the number of 
PlannedOrderWorkItems).

===== PAGE 172 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Applicationextensions 162
Code


===== PAGE 173 =====
163 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Result
Before implementing this extension point, there is no check for Route Name and Planned Order 
Line to set the custom quantity for the Planned Order Work Item.
After implementing this extension point, the Quantity is set to half for records where PartName 
= 'FG-201' and PlannedOrderLine == '4.0' 
OverrideSchedulingInlinePegging
This extension point allows you to override the allocation of work items to the BOM for an 
operation when the pegging source is set to 'SchedulingInProcess'.
Inputs
 l AreaGroup: holds relevant information including explainability Booleans, parts, resources, 
resource groups, pegging sources and rules, work items, discrete start date, and frozen 
window end date
 l PrimaryResource: the primary resource the operation is being scheduled on
 l Batch: current operation
 l List of BOM requirements: based on the BOM alternate associated with the part source 
for the planned order or scheduled receipt for the operatoin
 l List of available WorkItems: completed inventory that can be consumed in the next 
operation
Outputs
OverrideSchedulingInlinePeggingExtResult*

===== PAGE 174 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Applicationextensions 164
Returns:
 l isRequirementFulfilled boolean that indicates whether the full BOM Requirement has 
been fulfilled
 l workItemAllocation hashmap that tracks the input work items allocated to this 
operation's BOM
Example
The following example outlines how to use this extension to achieve FEFO (First Expired, First 
Out). For example, at your granola plant you have 1 scheduled receipt for finished good sand 3 
scheduled receipts for WIP. The WIP part is a BOM requirement for the finished goods and since 
we will produce more WIP quantity than required we would like to peg the WIP with the closest 
expiry date to the finished goods. Given that we know the expiry date of the WIP operations, we 
would order work items based on the expiry date and allocate those that expire first to the 
finished goods.
Setup
 1. Delete all 'On Hand' entries
 2. Delete all 'Independent Demand' entries
 3. In 'Scheduled Receipt' delete all entries that do not correspond to 'FG-210' Part Names or 
'' WIP-21'' as well as the 'FG-210' SRs that have an effective date < June 19, 2023
 4. In 'Scheduling Configuration' set 'Pegging Source' = 'Scheduling In Process'
 5. In 'Bill of Materials' delete rows which reference 'RM-xxx' (delete any raw material BOMs as 
they are not used in this set of data)
 6. In 'AreaGroup' set 'EnableWhereConsumedExplainability' to true (to visualize the results, 
not needed for the schedule itslef).
 7. In 'Shift Pattern Resource Availability' delete the existing 'OperatorMonTue' entry and re-
add it with the 'StartDate' set to June 6, 2023 and the Start Date Offset = 0
We have simplified the data for this example and are assuming the following:
Scheduled Receipt Order ID Expiry Date
WIP-21-PLANT-01-06-05-23 June 15, 2024
WIP-21-PLANT-01-06-12-23 June 8, 2024
WIP-21-PLANT-01-06-19-23 June 12, 2024

===== PAGE 175 =====
165 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Code
Result
Before applying this extension point, the pegging rule was initially set to FIFO and ranking is 
based on Available Date Time.
After applying the extension point, the ranking has changed with WIP-21-Plant-01-06-12 is now 
ranked 1 as opposed to 2, while WIP-21-Plant-01-06-12 is now ranked 3 as opposed to 1.


===== PAGE 176 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Applicationextensions 166
OverrideSecondaryResourceRequirements
This extension points allows you to override the existing secondary resource requirements for a 
specified operation.
Inputs
 l AreaGroup: holds relevant information including explainability Booleans, parts, resources, 
resource groups, pegging sources and rules, work items, discrete start date, and frozen 
window end date
 l EventTime: the current time in the scheduling horizon (measured in seconds from Jan 1, 
1970)
 l PrimaryResource: the primary resource the operation is being scheduled on
 l Batch: current operation 
 l List of current resource requirements: based on requirements defined on operation 
segments
Outputs
OverrideSecondaryResourceRequirementsExtResult*
Returns the resourceRequirements property populated with list of updated resource 
requirements
Example
The following example outlines how to customize the assignment of a secondary resource at 
specified workstations when a specified product is in production. For example, for new 
packaging stations, a station operator (PackOp) is not required as a secondary resource. 
However, when we produce the peanut flavored granola on stations PACK-21, PACK-22, and 
PACK-23 we have an operator to ensure cross-contamination is minimized. We will set a custom 
secondary resource requirement where 'PackOp' is removed fro the list of requirements for 
resource = 'PACK-21, PACK-22, PACK-23' when flavor NOT = 'Peanut'.

===== PAGE 177 =====
167 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Code
Result
Before the extension point is applied:
After the extension point is applied, only when peanuts are produced on stations PACK-21, 
PACK-22, or PACK-23 a station operator is assigned as a secondary resource requirement. In 
addition, since PackOp is removed from other flavors, the PACK stations can run more 
concurrent operations.

===== PAGE 178 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Applicationextensions 168
OverrideSelectBestBackwardScheduledBatch
This extension point allows you implement custom logic to determine which batch to select from 
multiple backward scheduled batches of operations that are feasible to be scheduled. For 
example, when multiple batches are feasible you might want to select the batch with the smallest 
quantity or earliest expiry date to minimize risks to inventory and product freshness.
Inputs
 l AreaGroup: holds relevant information including explainability Booleans, parts, resources, 
resource groups, pegging sources and rules, work items, discrete start date, and frozen 
window end date
 l CandidateBatch: the batch currently being evaluated.
 l CurrentBestBatch: the best batch so far (nullable value)
 l EarliestStartByPreds: the earliest start time allowed by forward predecessor operations.
 l TriggeringBatchStart: the start time of the triggering batch (in seconds)
Outputs
OverrideSelectBestBackwardScheduledBatchExtResult*
Returns the selectedBatch property populated with the selected batch.
 Example
The following example outlines how to select the batch that minimizes inventory when multiple 
batches that are feasible for backward scheduling exist.

===== PAGE 179 =====
169 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Code
package com.kinaxis.ps.extensions.overrideext;
 
import 
com.kinaxis.ps.extensions.extresult.OverrideSelectBestBackwardScheduledBatchExt
Result;
import 
com.kinaxis.ps.extensions.interfaces.IExtOverrideSelectBestBackwardScheduledBat
ch;
import com.kinaxis.ps.schedulingengine.*;
 
public class ExtOverrideSelectBestBackwardScheduledBatchImpl implements 
IExtOverrideSelectBestBackwardScheduledBatch {
 
    @Override
    public OverrideSelectBestBackwardScheduledBatchExtResult 
selectBestBackwardScheduledBatch(
            Batch candidateBatch,
            Batch currentBestBatch,
            DiscreteEventTime earliestStartByPreds,
            AreaGroup areaGroup,
            DiscreteEventTime triggeringBatchStart) {
 
        var result = new OverrideSelectBestBackwardScheduledBatchExtResult();
 
        if (currentBestBatch == null ||
            candidateBatch.getQuantity().getValue() < 
currentBestBatch.getQuantity().getValue()) {
            result.setUseResult(true);
            result.setSelectedBatch(candidateBatch);
        }
 
        return result;
    }
}
Result
After implementing the extension point, instead of having larger batches dominate resource 
allocation, smaller batches are prioritized for backward scheduling.

===== PAGE 180 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Applicationextensions 170
OverrideTargetTimeForBackwardScheduledTriggeringBatch
This extension point allows you to override the calculated start time of a triggering batch of 
operations for predecessors that are backward scheduled. For example, you might use the 
extension in situations where your scheduling must align with external constraints such as 
deliveries or inspections.
Inputs
 l AreaGroup: holds relevant information including explainability Booleans, parts, resources, 
resource groups, pegging sources and rules, work items, discrete start date, and frozen 
window end date
 l CurrentTargetTime: the default target time before this override is applied (in seconds)
 l TriggeringBatch: the batch that triggers backward scheduling.
 l CalculatedTime: the target time calculated by the Scheduling algorithm.
Outputs
OverrideTargetTimeForBackwardScheduledTriggeringBatchExtResult*
Returns the targetTime property populated with the overridden start time for the batch.
Example
The following example outlines how to delay the start of a backward scheduled batch of 
operations by one hour because it can't start until an external quality control approval or 
supplier delivery window is met. 
Code
package com.kinaxis.ps.extensions.overrideext;
 
import 
com.kinaxis.ps.extensions.extresult.OverrideTargetTimeForBackwardScheduledTrigg
eringBatchExtResult;
import 
com.kinaxis.ps.extensions.interfaces.IExtOverrideTargetTimeForBackwardScheduled
TriggeringBatch;
import com.kinaxis.ps.schedulingengine.*;
 

===== PAGE 181 =====
171 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
public class ExtOverrideTargetTimeForBackwardScheduledTriggeringBatchImpl
        implements IExtOverrideTargetTimeForBackwardScheduledTriggeringBatch {
 
    @Override
    public OverrideTargetTimeForBackwardScheduledTriggeringBatchExtResult 
overrideTargetTimeForBackwardScheduledTriggeringBatch(
            AreaGroup areaGroup,
            long currentTargetTime,
            Batch triggeringBatch,
            long calculatedTime) {
 
        var result = new 
OverrideTargetTimeForBackwardScheduledTriggeringBatchExtResult();
 
        var qcHold = triggeringBatch.getProperty(new PropertyType
("NeedsQCRelease", DataType.BOOLEAN));
        if (qcHold != null && Boolean.TRUE.equals(qcHold.getValue())) {
            result.setUseResult(true);
            result.setTargetTime(calculatedTime + 3600); // delay 1 hour
        }
 
        return result;
    }
}
Result
After implementing the extension point, instead of having batches triggered before the quality 
control approval, there is an automatic 1 hour delay enforced on quality control-held batches.
SchedulingUpfrontPeggingWhereConsumed
This extension point lets you specify material pegging in advance of scheduling.
Inputs
 l AreaGroup: holds relevant information including explainability Booleans, parts, resources, 
resource groups, pegging sources and rules, work items, discrete start date, and frozen 
window end date
 l List of all planned order workitems

EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Applicationextensions 172
 l List of all on hand workitems
 l List of all scheduled receipt workitems
Outputs
SchedulingUpfrontPeggingWhereConsumedExtResult*
Returns the list of whereConsumedWorkItemRelationships (pegging results for each 
consumption/demand requirement)
 Example
The following example outlines pegging WIP scheduled receipts to applicable finished good 
scheduled receipts. We want to peg WIP to finished goods to fully account for the quantity of 
scheduled receipts. Our data shows that we have 12 WIP scheduled receipts that can fully satisfy 
12 finished goods scheduled receipts.
Setup
 l Set the 'Pegging Source' in the 'Scheduling Configuration' table to 'SchedulingUpFront'

===== PAGE 183 =====

When the extension point is applied, the Scheduling Where Consumed worksheet shows the FG 
operations dependent on the WIP operations. 

===== PAGE 184 =====

The precedence relationships are also respected in the Gantt chart.
SchedulePostProcessing
This extension point lets you adjust the properties of scheduled operations after the algorithm 
logic is complete but before the data is transformed into results.
Inputs
 l AreaGroup: Holds relevant information including explainability Booleans, parts, resources, 
resource groups, pegging sources and rules, work items, discrete start date, and frozen 
window end date
 l ScheduledBatches: List of operations scheduled on primary resources
Outputs
SchedulePostProcessingExtResult*
Returns the updated list of scheduled operations
Example
The following example outlines not keeping any operation without any completed successors to 
reduce the amount of waste. Any scheduled operation must have at least one completed 

===== PAGE 185 =====
175 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
successor operation and this is done by checking operations for successors and if none are 
completed , then removing that operation from the list of scheduled operations.
Code
Result
Before applying the extension point, work items without a successor are included in the 
schedule.

===== PAGE 186 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Applicationextensions 176
After applying the extension point, all work items with no successors are removed from the 
schedule.
OverrideSearchCandidateScheduleAcceptance

compare current and candidate schedules and decide whether or not to update the current 
schedule with the candidate schedule.
Inputs

 l CandidateSchedule: scheduled for the current iteration
 l LocalSearchAlgorithm: access to the class and any data it has including schedules, 
scoreModes, eligibleResourcesByOperation, and neighbourSelectionMod
Outputs
OverrideSearchCandidateScheduleAcceptanceExtResult*

===== PAGE 187 =====
177 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling

extension

resource groups, pegging sources and rules, work items, discrete start date, and frozen 
window end date
 l Operations: list of all eligible WorkItemOperations needed to be scheduled

scoreModes, eligibleResourcesByOperation, and neighbourSelectionMode

===== PAGE 190 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Applicationextensions 180
Building and deploying the extension
After you have modified your extension and confirmed it's expected results after debugging, you 
need to build a jar file using Maven.
 l Double click the Maven package to create the extension deployment kit jar file.
Deploying application extensions
The application extension jar files must be uploaded using the Upload Application Extension 
form in Maestro. 
Upload an application extension
 1. In the Maestro Web client, open the Upload Application Extension form and specify the 
following:
 l Application Identifier: the name of the application that the extension applies to.
 l Extension Version: the version number of the extension.
 2. Select a file in the Extension File control and then click Upload.

===== PAGE 191 =====
181 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:ConfigureEnterpriseScheduling
Archive, Delete, or Activate an application extension
You can archive or remove an extension using the Application Extensions worksheet in the 
Embedded Algorithm Details and Log workbook. For extensions that are currently archived, 
you can activate them.
 l Click the value in the State column.
 l In the Manage Application Extension form, select one of the following and then click Go:
 l Activate: Makes the extension active in your Maestro instance.
 l Archive: Disables the extension.
 l Delete: Deletes the extension from your Maestro instance.

===== PAGE 192 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG) 182
CHAPTER 3: Enterprise Scheduling resources
Application packages migrate resources, scenarios, user groups and integration settings in a 
single package. For more information on how to migrate application packages across systems, 
see the Maestro Administration Guide.
The application package for Enterprise Scheduling includes the following resources:
Name Type Purpose
User Resources
Enterprise Scheduling Workbench Workbook Displays the generated production 
schedule, details for scheduled 
operations, and a list of unscheduled 
operations.
Users review, maintain, and monitor 
their production schedule here.
Enterprise Scheduling Analysis 
workbook
Workbook Displays scheduling KPIs and logs for 
the Scheduling algorithm.
Enterprise Scheduling Parameters 
workbook
Workbook Displays the parameters modeled in 
the production schedule.
Use this workbook to modify or add 
parameters to the schedule. See Data 
configuration.

Workbook Used to review and identify any 
integrity issues with the data used to 
generate the production schedule.

receipts and planned orders eligible 
for scheduling.
Enterprise Scheduling Calendar Workbook Used to maintain and update shift 
patterns and downtimes using a 
calendar view.
Enterprise Scheduling Dashboard Reports the performance of the 
production schedule.
Enterprise Scheduling Scorecard Review and evaluate key metrics for 
the production schedule.

forms. Do not delete or modify this 
workbook.

the widgets that display in the 
Enterprise Scheduling dashboard.

scorecard.

===== PAGE 199 =====
189 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:EnterpriseSchedulingresources
Enterprise Scheduling Workbench workbook
The Enterprise Scheduling Workbench workbook is the main resource for planners to view, 
manage, and modify their production schedule. It consists of a Gantt worksheet and a 
Configuration worksheet.
Gantt worksheet
This worksheet consists of a Gantt chart that displays the schedule, a panel displaying the list of 
unscheduled operations, and a details worksheet with more information on scheduled 
operations. 
Scheduled operations display as colored bars on a Gantt chart along swimlanes that represent 
the primary resource that the operation is assigned to. The length of the bar is determined by 
the production time for that operation. Operations are either rate-based or an absolute duration 
of production time. Bar colors represent a defined part property for that operation and when 
multiple properties are defined, users can change which property is applied to the operation 
bars.
Details for each operation displays in the worksheet below the chart.  Unscheduled operations 
are listed in the upper right pane and can be dragged and dropped onto the schedule where 
they are eligible to be produced. These orders were not scheduled because of constraints such 
as resource feasibility or eligibility.
Customize tooltips
When users hover over a schedule operation bar on the Gantt chart or over an unscheduled 
operation in the panel, a tooltip displays with more information. 

===== PAGE 200 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Applicationextensions 190
 1. Open the Enterprise Scheduling Workbench workbook in Design Mode.
 2. In the Gantt worksheet, click the Data tab in the Scheduler panel. 
 3. Scroll down to the Tooltip section.
 4. To add a new column, click 
  and select the column from the list.
 5. To remove a column, click 
  in the row for that column.
Configuration worksheet
This worksheet is used to define some properties for the Gantt chart such as its start date or 
horizon duration.
For more information on these settings, see the Maestro Applications Guide (Web client).

===== PAGE 201 =====
191 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:EnterpriseSchedulingresources
Enterprise Scheduling Parameters workbook
This workbook is used to review and configure different parameters for the production schedule. 
All of the underlying data for the production schedule displays here and you can focus on 
specific parameter sets using the Parameter control on the workbook toolbar. 
For more about how to configure specific parameters for the schedule, see Data configuration.
View parameter sets
 l To view a specific set of parameters, select a value in the Parameter control.
By default, all parameter worksheets display in the workbook.

===== PAGE 202 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Applicationextensions 192
Enterprise Scheduling Analysis workbook
Use this workbook to review KPIs and details for the schedule. You can investigate decisions 
made by the scheduling algorithm to assess how well resources are utilized during downtimes or 
off-shift periods. In addition, you can see an overview of the schedule that can be published.
Worksheet Use this resource to...
Resource Utilization Review how resources are utilized over the scheduling horizon. Filter the 
worksheet to display resource utilization by day, hour, or half hour.
Use the chart to identify how many minutes each day the selected 
resource spends in different statuses.
Changeover Metrics Review details for all changeovers in the schedule by resource. Only 
changeovers with start times within the scheduling horizon display in the 
worksheet.
Operation Feasibility 
Explanation
View logs that explain the feasibility of scheduled operations. Failed 
operations are identified by which resource or resource group failed.
Batch Selection 
Explanation
View logs the explain which operations are selected to run in the 
schedule. Multiple operations are considered, but typically the operation 
with the lowest score is scheduled.
Scheduling Where 
Consumed Explanation
View logs that explain which orders were considered to fulfill the Bill of 
Materials requirements.
Scheduling Details View schedule details for selected areas and area groups. This worksheet 
can be exported to Excel to publish the schedule for the shop floor or 
other stakeholders. See Publish the schedule.
Trace Order Explanation View logs that trace how operations for a scheduled receipt order are 
processed through the Scheduling algorithm. See Trace orders.
Objective Function 
Explanation
View the results of running the Search Algorithm option for scheduling 
that generates multiple candidate schedules looking for the most 
effective schedule.

===== PAGE 203 =====
193 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:EnterpriseSchedulingresources
Enterprise Scheduling Input Data Verification 
Workbook
The following categories of integrity issues are tracked. Click a value in the No of Issues column 
to see more details about that issue. 
 l Area/AreaGroup
 l ChangeOver
 l Dispatch Rule
 l DownTime
 l Operation Specification
 l Part
 l Resource Shift Pattern
 l Resource/ResourceGroup
 l Route
 l Scheduled Receipt
 l Search Algorithm
 l Secondary Resource
 l Shift Patterns/Interval
For detailed descriptions of the issues within a category, see Review Data Issues.

===== PAGE 204 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Applicationextensions 194
Review Data Issues
You can review data integrity issues in your scheduling data that affect the effectiveness and 
accuracy of your production schedule in the Enterprise Scheduling Input Data Verification 
workbook. 
 l The Input Data Verification worksheet displays specific integrity issues and links to more 
details on each issue.
 l The Data Integration Validation worksheet displays the number of records that exist in your 
key scheduling tables.
Resolve data issues 
In the Input Data Verification worksheet, specific data integrity issues display with a count of the 
number of that issue that exists in your scheduling data. Clicking on a value in the No of Issues 
column will open a details worksheet that displays more data on that issue.
For example, there are 2 changeover records that don't have a duration or penalty assigned at 
the resource level.
Clicking on the No of Issues value takes you to the Changeover worksheet that identifies which 
changeover records have the issue.
Clicking on the value in the relevant column (Has No Duration or No Penalty - Resource?) for the 
first record, Changeover_Flavor takes you to the Changeovers worksheet in the Enterprise 
Scheduling Parameters workbook where you can drill to the mapped resources for that record to 
find the missing or broken resource mapping record.

===== PAGE 205 =====
195 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:EnterpriseSchedulingresources
Issue categories
Each set of is issues is grouped under a category and descriptions for each issue and how to 
resolve it are detailed below.
 l Area/AreaGroup
 l ChangeOver
 l Dispatch Rule
 l Downtime
 l Maintenance
 l Operation Specification
 l Part
 l Resource Shift Pattern
 l Resource/ResourceGroup
 l Route
 l Scheduled Receipt
 l Search Algorithm
 l Secondary Resource
 l Shift Patterns/Interval
Area/AreaGroup
Areas with Historical Actuals Horizon < 0
The historical actuals horizon specifies the number of days of historical actuals data that displays 
in the schedule. The number of days can't be less than 0 days.
 1. Click the value in Historical Actuals Horizon < 0 to open the Area Groups worksheet.
 2. Update the Historical Actuals Horizon value for the affected area group record(s).
Areas with invalid time zones
Areas must have a time zone selected from the defined list of time zones. Other values will be 
flagged as invalid.
 l Click the value in the   Is Time Zone Invalid? column to open the Area worksheet to review 
and assign a valid time zone to the affected area record(s).

===== PAGE 206 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Applicationextensions 196
Areas without time zone
Areas must have an assigned time zone that is defined using the IANA (Internet Assigned 
Numbers Authority) format. 
 l Click the value in the Time Zone column to open the Area worksheet to review and assign 
a time zone to the affected area record(s).
ChangeOver
Changeover Conditions with the same Changeover and From/To 
Property
Changeover conditions trigger a changeover to occur when the property of a part changes on a 
resource during production. Each condition associated with a changeover must be mapped to a 
unique property change (From/To Property values).
 1. Click Yes in the Is Same Name and Type for From and To Property? column for one of 
the duplicated changeover conditions.
 2. In the Changeover Conditions worksheet, map the condition to a different Changeover 
or change the From/To Property values.
Changeovers with neither duration or penalty at component level
Changeovers must have a duration and penalty defined at the component level. The duration 
determines how long the changeover is applied. The penalty defines the size of the impact of the 
changeover. The penalty value is involved in prioritizing operations for scheduling.
 1. Click the value in the Has No Duration or No Penalty - Component? column to open 
the Changeover worksheet.
 2. Click the value in Basic Conditions to focus in on the specific condition with the missing 
duration or penalty value.
 3. Define a Duration and/or Penalty value in the Changeover Conditions worksheet.
Changeovers with neither duration or penalty at resource level
Changeovers must have a duration and penalty defined at the resource level. The duration 
determines how long the changeover is applied. The penalty defines the size of the impact of the 
changeover. The penalty value is involved in prioritizing operations for scheduling.
 1. Click the value in the Has No Duration or No Penalty - Resource? column to open the 
Changeovers worksheet.
 2. Click the value in Mapped Resources to focus in on the specific condition with the 
missing duration or penalty value.

===== PAGE 207 =====
197 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:EnterpriseSchedulingresources
 3. Define a Duration and/or Penalty value in the Changeover Resource Mapping 
worksheet.
Changeovers without condition
Changeovers that have no condition defined. The condition defines how the changeover is 
triggered and for how long it is applied to the resource.
 1. Click the value in the Changeover - Has conditions? column to open the Changeovers 
worksheet.
 2. Specify a condition for the changeover.
Changeovers without mapped resource
Changeovers are triggered by properties assigned to operations processed on a resource. When 
the resource switches from one operation to another operation and the assigned property 
changes, for example the operation size, then the changeover is triggered. 
 1. Click the value in the Changeover - Has resources? column to open the Changeovers 
worksheet.
 2. Click the value in Mapped Resources.
 3. Create a new record in the Changeover Resource Mapping worksheet to map the 
changeover to a resource.
Dispatch Rule
Dispatch rules without component
Dispatch rules must have one or more components defined for them to determine how 
operations are scheduled in relation to one another.
 1. Click the value in the Has Dispatch Rule Components? column to open the Dispatch 
Rule Component Detail worksheet .
 2. Insert a new record to create a component for the dispatch rule missing components.
 3. Repeat step 2 to add as many components as required for the rule.
Downtime
Invalid downtimes
Downtimes with invalid properties such as an end time that occurs before a start time or a 
downtime not assigned to a resource. Why the downtime is flagged as invalid is described in the 
Invalid DownTime Detail column.

===== PAGE 208 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Applicationextensions 198
 l Click the value in the Name column to open the Downtimes worksheet where you can fix 
the issue. The invalid column values are highlighted in yellow.
Maintenance
Maintenances with duration or interval <= 0
Maintenance actions must have a specified duration that they operate and a defined interval that 
determines how often the maintenance is performed.
 l Click the value in the Name column to open the Maintenance worksheet where you can fix 
issue. The invalid column values are highlighted in yellow.
Operation Specification
Operation specifications without mapped resource
Operation specifications must be mapped to an eligible resource to run operations on. 
Operation specifications define sets of rules and preferences for scheduling route operations.
 1. Click the value in the Name column to open the Operation Specifications worksheet.
 2. Click on the value in the Name column  to drill to the Operation Specification To 
Resource worksheet.
 3. Insert a new record to assign the operation specification with a resource. 
Note: Operation specifications that have an assigned resource group but are not 
assigned to a specific resource can scheduled operations on any resource in that 
resource group.
Part
Parts without lot size quantity
Parts must have a lot size quantity defined.
 1. Click the value in the Work Item - Quantity column to open the Parts worksheet.
 2. Specify a Lot Size for that part.

===== PAGE 209 =====
199 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:EnterpriseSchedulingresources
Resource Shift Pattern
Resources not mapped to any shift pattern
Resources must be mapped to a shift pattern to determine when the resource is run. 
 1. Click the value in the Count - Shift Pattern column to open the Shift Pattern Resource 
Availability worksheet.
 2. Insert a new record to map the resource to an available shift pattern.
Resources not mapped to any shift pattern on the scheduling horizon
Resources must be mapped to a shift pattern that is defined within the schedule's horizon. 
 1. Click the value in the Count - Shift Pattern column to open the Shift Pattern Resource 
Availability worksheet.
 2. Insert a new record to map the resource to an available shift pattern that is specifically 
within the schedule's defined horizon.
Shift pattern resource availabilities with available quantity <=0
Shift pattern resource availability records should have available quantities defined as greater 
than 0. If no quantity is available on the resource, then no operations can be scheduled on it.
 1. Click the value in the Quantity Available column to open the Shift Pattern Resource 
Availability worksheet.
 2. Specify a value greater than 1 in the Quantity Available column.
Shift pattern resource availabilities with offset days > 0. Offset days 
must be negative values
Shift pattern resource availability records should have offset days defined as a negative value. 
 1. Click the value in the ApplyStartDateOffsetDays>0? column to open the Shift Pattern 
Resource Availability worksheet.
 2. Specify a negative value or 0 in the Start Data Offset column.
Shift pattern resource availabilities with start date > end date
Shift pattern resource availability records can't end before they start. Records with an end date 
define before the start time are flagged as invalid.
 1. Click the value in the StartDate > EndDate column to open the Shift Pattern Resource 
Availability worksheet.
 2. Edit the start and/or end dates accordingly.

===== PAGE 210 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Applicationextensions 200
Resource/ResourceGroup
Primary resource group type and Resource type class mismatch
Resources must be assigned to the appropriate resource group. A mismatch of a resource type 
and resource group type are flagged. 
 1. Click the value in the Is Resource Class = Primary and ResourceGroup Type = 
Secondary? column to open the Resource Group to Resource worksheet.
 2. Delete the mismatched record and create a new record for that primary resource and 
assign it to an appropriate primary resource group.
Resource groups without mapped resource
Resource groups must have at least one resource that is used to schedule operations on. 
 1. Click the value in the No. of Resources column to open the Resource Group to 
Resource worksheet.
 2. Insert a new record that maps a resource to that resource group.
Resource to replenishment rule with effective in date time > effective 
out date time
Replenishment rules applied to secondary resources can't have a start (effective in date time) 
later than then end (effective out date time) for the period when the rule is effective.
 1. Click the value in the Is effective date time > Effective out date time? column to open 
the Resource to Replenishment Rule worksheet.
 2. Modify the Effective In Date Time and/or Effective Out Date Time values accordingly.
Resource to replenishment rule without an effective in date time or an 
effective out date time
Replenishment rules applied to secondary resources must have an effective period for the rule 
defined by both a start (effective in date time) and end (effective out date time) for the period.
 1. Click the value in the Is either effective date time or effective out date time null? 
column to open the Resource to Replenishment Rule worksheet.
 2. Modify the Effective In Date Time and/or Effective Out Date Time values accordingly.
Resource types with Enable Qualification = true but no qualification 
rules defined in Predecessor Resource Eligibility
If a resource type is set to have qualification rules then at least one rule must be defined in the 
Predecessor Resource Eligibility worksheet. These rules control which downstream resources an 

===== PAGE 211 =====
201 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER3:EnterpriseSchedulingresources
operation ban be produced on based on the resource it was last scheduled on by qualifying or 
disqualifying a route between those two resources. 
 1. Click the value in the Name column to open the Predecessor Resource Eligibility 
worksheet.
 2. Insert a new record that defines the eligibility rule.
Resources not mapped to a resource group
Resource groups are used to determine the availability and qualifications of an operation 
processed on a resource. 
 1. Click the value in the Resource Groups Count column to open the Resource Group to 
Resource worksheet.
 2. Insert a new record to assign the unmapped resource to a group.
Resources with Efficiency = 0
Resources must have an efficiency value defined that is greater than 0. 
 1. Click the value in the Is Efficiency = 0? column to open the Resources worksheet.
 2. Specify a value greater than 0 in the Efficiency column.
Resources with MaxConcurrentBatches = 0
Resources must have a value or 1 or more defined for maximum current batches. 
 1. Click the value in the Is Max Concurrent Batches - 0? column to open the Resources 
worksheet.
 2. Specify a value of 1 or more in the Max Concurrent Batches column. This value must be a 
whole number.
Resources with rate = 0 and Duration Calculation Mode = Rate
