===== PAGE 273 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Controltables 263
Field name Description Data type Key
IgnoreAssembly
Yield
Controls whether or not an assembly's yield is 
ignored from one component to another 
when calculating dependent demand 
quantities (that is, is the dependent demand 
quantity inflated for the assembly's yield or 
not). Valid values are:
 l Y: The component from the BOM record 
ignores the assembly's yield when 
calculating the dependent demand 
quantity (the quantity is not inflated in 
consideration of the assembly's yield). For 
example, if an assembly containing 
expensive components needs to be 
scrapped due to issues with another 
component, it might be desirable for the 
expensive components to be recovered 
from the assembly before scrapping it.
 l N: The component from the BOM record 
considers the assembly's yield when 
calculating the dependent demand 
quantity (the quantity is inflated in 
consideration of the assembly's yield). 
Default value: N
Note: This setting is ignored on BOMType 
records that define a co-product or by-
product configuration (always treated as Y).
Boolean  
Table 6.1: BOMType (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 274 =====
264 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
LimitEffective
Date
Specifies for each BOMType.Value where an 
assembly has a past due demand whether 
effectivity of components is determined using 
the current DueDate (considering that it is 
prior to the Assembly.RunDate) or the 
RunDate itself.
 l Y: The Assembly.RunDate is used. This 
ensures that any past due demand uses 
BillOfMaterial records currently in effect 
(as of the RunDate). This setting overrides 
the explosion date (StartDate of the peg 
supply). As a result, bills not effective at 
RunDate are ignored (supply does not 
explode through them).
 l N: The DueDate is used. This ensures that 
the BillOfMaterial configuration at the 
time of the DueDate will be used.
Default value: N
Note: This field is not supported by Supply 
chain optimization. See Supply chain 
optimization BOMType control table 
limitations.
Boolean  
Table 6.1: BOMType (Mfg) fields

BillOfMaterial table are considered when 
looking for component actual sales to 
consume assembly forecast within a 
PlanningBOM relationship. Four conditions 
must be met for this to occur:
 l Assembly part has 
PartType.ProcessingRule set to 
MPSConfig

relationship between Assemblies and

BOMType.MPSConfigDemandSource=

demand records where 
DemandType.ProcessingRule 
=SalesActual

records where 
DemandType.ProcessingRule = 
SalesForecast or 
ProductionForecast.

consumption of assembly forecast within 
a PlanningBOM.
 l N: This BOMType does not use 
component consumption of assembly 
forecast within a PlanningBOM.

266 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
limitations.
MUERule Specifies how model and unit effectivity are 
to be applied. MUERule is used regardless of 
whether the parent or component parts are 
netted by model or unit. If Model-Unit 
Effectivity is not enabled then MUERule is 
treated as Ignore. Valid values are:
 l Model: The Bill record is effective if the 
Model value on the Bill record matches 
the ScheduledReceipt.InputModel or 
the model on the planned order.
 l Unit: The Bill record is effective if the 
StartUnit on the Supply record is in the 
range of the StartUnit and EndUnit from 
the Bill record. DependentUnitRule and 
EffectivityRule are tested to determine 
whether the unit is in range. 
 l ModelUnit: Use both model and unit 
tests simultaneously. Model matches and 
the unit must be in range for the Bill 
record to be effective.
 l Ignore: Do not test model or unit for this 
Bill record.
Default value: Ignore
Note: This field is not supported by Supply 
chain optimization. See Supply chain 
optimization BOMType control table 
limitations.
String
(Enum)
 
Table 6.1: BOMType (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 277 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Controltables 267
Field name Description Data type Key
ProductType Specifies the component's relationship to the 
assembly. This setting determines whether 
dependent requirements are passed to the 
component, as well as how certain values on 
the BOM record are interpreted. Valid values 
are:
 l Normal: A normal dependent component 
used in the production of the assembly.
 l CoProduct: A co-product that can be 
manufactured and planned together with 
the assembly (primary) product due to 
common structure, components and 
process similarities. For example, a co-
product might be a lower grade version of 
the primary product. Netting and CTP 
calculations for parts with a co-product 
relationship are performed at the same 
time. Therefore, two-way co-product 
relationships are supported.
 l ByProduct: A by-product that is 
generated from, and residual to, the 
production of the assembly (primary) 
product. For example, a by-product might 
be a chemical generated from the 
production of one or more assemblies in a 
given product structure. Netting and CTP 
calculations for a by-product part are 
performed after all of its BillOfMaterial 
assemblies have been planned. Therefore, 
two-way by-product relationships are not 
supported.
For more information about co-products or 
by-products, see Co-products and by-
products.
Default value: Normal
String
(Enum)
 
Table 6.1: BOMType (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 278 =====
268 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
RatioRule Specifies how the 
BillOfMaterial.OptionRatio is used. Valid 
values are:
 l Ignore: Do not use the OptionRatio field 
in explosion
 l Fraction: Value from 0 through 1 (1 
means used in all assemblies)
 l Percent: Value from 0 through 100 (100 
means used in all assemblies)
Default value: Fraction
String (Enum)  
RoundingRule Specifies for each BOMType the rules for 
rounding numbers when exploding supplies 
through the BOM to place dependent 
demands on components. Valid values are:
 l None: Do not round.
 l Up: Round up to next integer.
 l Down: round down to next integer.
 l Nearest: round to nearest integer (.5 
goes up).
 l QtyPerOrder: ignores the supply 
quantity on the order and pass dependent 
demand equal to the 
BillOfMaterial.QuantityPer value. For 
example, if the supply quantity is 10 and 
QuantityPer is 2, then the dependent 
demand will be 2. 
Default value: None
String (Enum)  
Table 6.1: BOMType (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 279 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Controltables 269
Field name Description Data type Key
ScrapRule Specifies how to handle scrap and yield 
values on BOMType records. Valid values are:
 l Ignore: Do not use the Scrap factor
 l YieldFraction: Value from 0 through 1 
(1 = no loss)
 l YieldPercent: Value from 0 through 
100 (100 =no loss)
 l ScrapFraction: Value from 0 through 1 
(0 = no loss)
 l ScrapPercent: Value from 0 through 
100 (0 = no loss)
 l InflationFraction: Formula = 1 / (1 + 
BillOfMaterial.Scrap)
 l InflationPercent: Formula = 1 / (1 + 
BillOfMaterial.Scrap * 0.01)
Default value: YieldFraction 
String (Enum)  
UseLeadTime
Offset
Specifies for each BOMType whether to use a 
separate lead time factor on BillOfMaterial 
records. Valid values are:
 l Y: The lead time value that is provided is 
used in all lead time calculations. Setting 
this value to Y enables the use of the 
BillOfMaterial.LeadTimeOffset field as a 
factor in addition to Part and PartSource 
based lead time elements such as 
FixedLeadTime, VariableLeadTime, and 
SafetyLeadTime, in calculating product 
lead time and start dates.
 l N: The lead time value that is provided is 
not used in all lead time calculations.
Default value: N
Boolean  
Value The string value for this type of bill. This value 
appears in the BillOfMaterial.Type field.
Default value: blank string
String Yes
Table 6.1: BOMType (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 280 =====
270 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
BillOfMaterials Set of BillOfMaterial records that reference 
this BOMType value.
Set  
Table 6.1: BOMType (Mfg) fields
ComponentConsumptionType table
The ComponentConsumptionType table is used to determine which sources a component is 
allowed to be consumed from to fulfill demand. The component consumption type allows you to 
overrule BOM requirements by specifying which part sources to use (on hand, schedule receipt, 
and/or planned order) or if component parts are unconstrained. When the component 
availability type is set to Unconstrained, successor operations can be scheduled before 
predecessor operations.
Field name Description Data type Key
Value The name of the component consumption 
type.
String Y
ControlSet The control set associated with this 
consumption type.
Reference Y
Table 6.2: ComponentConsumptionType (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 281 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Controltables 271
Field name Description Data type Key
Component
AvailabilityType
Controls when components become 
consumable to satisfy BOM requirements 
when the pegging source for the schedule is 
set to SupplyPlanning or 
SchedulingInProcess.
Valid values include:
 l ScheduledCompletion: components 
become consumable after their scheduled 
completion time.
 l PlanningAvailableDate: components 
become consumable on the date when 
the entire supply is available 
(WhereConsumed.SupplyAvailableDate).
 l LatestScheduledCompletion
AvailableDate: components become 
consumable at the latest date on with 
their entire supply becomes available 
(WhereConsumed.SupplyAvailableDate) 
or at the latest time that they are 
scheduled.
 l Unconstrained: components become 
consumable at any time.
String 
(enum)
UseOnHand If on hand supplies can be used to satisfy the 
supply allocation requirements for the 
assembly operation. When set to Y, for each 
on hand allocation quantity a record is 
exported to the 
SchedulingWhereConsumed table. 
Boolean  
UsePlannedOrders If planned order supplies can be used to 
satisfy the supply allocation requirements for 
the assembly operation. When set to Y, for 
each planned order allocation quantity a 
record is exported to the 
SchedulingWhereConsumed table. 
Boolean

UseScheduledReceiptsIf scheduled receipt supplies can be used to 
satisfy the supply allocation requirements for 
the assembly operation. When set to Y, for 
each scheduled receipt allocation quantity a 
record is exported to the 
SchedulingWhereConsumed table. 
Boolean

Table 6.3: ComponentConsumptionType (Scheduling) fields
OperationPredecessorType table
The OperationPredecessorType table is used to define the precedence rule applied to 
operation predecessors to manage when they run. These types are applied to route operation 
predecessors.
Field name Description Data type Key
Value Name of the operation predecessor type. String Y
ControlSet The control set associated with this operation predecessor type.
Referenced table: ControlSet table
Reference Y
Table 6.4: OperationPredecessorType  (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 283 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Controltables 273
Field name Description Data type Key
Dependency
SchedulingRule
Determines the dependency constraints that affect how 
predecessor and successor operations are scheduled when 
MaximumWaitDuration >= 0.
• For Planned Orders, the Maximum wait duration is set on the 
route operation predecessor.
• For Scheduled Receipts, the Maximum wait duration is on the 
scheduled receipt operation predecessor.
Valid values are:
 l EarliestTime: the predecessor operation is only scheduled if 
its successor operation can be scheduled within the maximum 
wait duration window. The successor operation is scheduled as 
early as possible.
 l SchedulingRule: the predecessor operation isn’t constrained 
by when its successor operation can be scheduled.
Note: Locked operations respect the dependency constraints set 
by value set for the DependencySchedulingRule.
String 
(Enum)
 
MaximumWait
ExceededRule
Defines how operations are handled when the maximum wait 
duration between the predecessor and successor operations is 
exceeded. This rule only applies when the 
DependencySchedulingRule is set to SchedulingRule.
Valid values are: 
 l AllowExceedMaximumWaitDuration: allow Maximum Wait 
Duration to be exceeded. 
 l UnscheduleSuccessor: the successor operation is removed 
from the schedule and only the predecessor operation is 
scheduled. 
 l UnschedulePredecessorAndSuccessor: both successor 
and predecessor operations are removed from the schedule.
String 
(Enum)
 
Table 6.4: OperationPredecessorType  (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 284 =====
274 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
MaximumWait
OutsideHorizonRule
Defines how predecessor operations are scheduled if their 
successor operations are not scheduled within the scheduling 
horizon  and the end time for the maximum wait duration on an 
operation predecessor is outside the scheduling horizon. 
Valid values are: 
 l SchedulePredecessor: the predecessor operation is 
scheduled.
 l UnschedulePredecessor: the predecessor operation is 
removed from the schedule
String 
(Enum)
 
PrecedenceRule The precedence rule applied to this operation predecessor type. 
Valid values are:
 l StartFromPredecessorEnd: The successor operation is 
eligible to run at the end of the production time of its 
predecessor.
 l StartFromPredecessorStart: The successor operation is 
eligible to run at the start of the production time of its 
predecessor.
String 
(Enum)
 
Table 6.4: OperationPredecessorType  (Scheduling) fields

OperationSegmentType table
The OperationSegmentType table is used to define how operation segments are run by a 
primary resource during an operation. For example, an operation segment type could be 
“Loading”, “Working”, or “Unloading”. These segment types apply to both route and scheduled 
receipt operation segments.
Field name Description Data type Key
Value The name of this operation segment type. String Y
ControlSet The control set associated with this operation 
segment type.
Referenced table: ControlSet
Reference Y
AllowOperation
ShiftSpanning
Identifies if shift spanning is allowed on 
operations.
Boolean  
AllowSegment
Overlapping
Downtime
Identifies if this type of operation segment can 
overlap with a downtime period on the schedule.
Boolean  
AllowSegment
OverlappingOffshift
Identifies if this type of operation segment can 
overlap with an off-shift period on the schedule.
Boolean

Field name Description Data type Key
DurationCalculation
ModeOverride
Override applied to how a primary resource 
calculates the duration for this type of operation 
segment.
Valid values include:
 l Duration: the Duration or Duration Override 
value from one the following tables is used. 
The tables are ordered by priority with a 
defined value in the first table taking 
precedence over a value in the second table 
and so forth.
 1. OperationSpecificationResourceSegmentOve
rride
 2. OperationSpecificationSegmentOverride
 3. ScheduledReceiptOperationSegment (used 
only for Scheduled Receipts)
 4. RouteOperationSegment (used only for 
Planned Orders)
 l Rate: the value from the duration calculation 
(operation order quantity / rate) is used. 
Depending on the order type, the quantity 
used is either 
PlannedWorkOrderItem.Quantity or 
ScheduleReceipt.Quantity. The rate is taken 
from the defined Rate or RateOverride value 
from one of the following tables. The tables are 
ordered by priority with a defined value in the 
first table taking precedence over a value in the 
second table and so forth.
 1. OperationSpecificationResourceSegmentOve
rride
 2. OperationSpecificationSegmentOverride
 3. ScheduledReceiptOperationSegment (used 
only for Scheduled Receipts)
 4. RouteOperationSegment (used only for 
Planned Orders)
 5. Resource
String 
(Enum)

 l Upstream Rate: the value from the 
calculation (operation order quantity / rate of 
immediate predecessor operation) is used. The 
rate used depends on the duration calculation 
mode applied to the resource for the 
predecessor operation.

considered a value add to the process.

PropertyType table
This table is referenced by the Property table and is used to determine which dispatch rule is 
applied to operations have that property.
Field name Description Data 
type
Key
Name Name of the property type. String Y
DataType The data value for the property type.
Valid values are:
 l Boolean
 l Date
 l DateTime
 l Integer
 l Quantity
 l String
String 
(Enum)
 
Table 6.8: PropertyType  (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 290 =====
280 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data 
type
Key
ProcessingRuleDefines dynamically calculated properties to support dispatch rules. This 
calculation is used to determine the property value of a particular property 
type. For hierarchical dispatch rules, this rule sorts the operation relative to 
other eligible operations. For weighted dispatch rules, this rule contributes 
to the overall operation score with the operation with the lowest score 
being selected.
Valid values are:
 l ChangeoverDuration: the property value is calculated using the 
operation changeover durations
 l ChangeoverPenalty: the property value is calculated using the penalty 
valuse of operation changeovers.
 l CriticalRatio: the property value is calculated using on the critical ratio 
representing slack time for an operation.
 l DueDate: the property value is calculated using operation due dates.
 l InverseOrderPriority: the property value is calculated by highest 
priority with low numerical values corresponding to high priorities.
 l MaximumWaitDurationEnd: the property value is calculated using  by 
the earliest maximum duration end time for the operation's 
predecessor operations with a maximum wait duration >= 0.
 l None: the property value is not calculated and the value specified for 
Property.Value is used.
 l OrderPriority: the property value is calculated  by highest priority with 
high numerical values corresponding to high priorities.
 l ProcessingDuration: the property value is calculated using  the 
processing duration of operations.
 l QueuedDuration: the property value is calculated  by the duration 
taken to mark an operation as eligible and have it enter the queue to be 
scheduled.
 l SlackTime: the property value is calculated  using slack time. Slack time 
is the amount of time an operation can be delayed from its start date 
without it passing its due date and becoming late. This calculated ratio 
= time until due date / remaining processing duration for the operation.
 l SlackTimeToMaximumWaitDurationEnd: the property value is 
calculated using the maximum wait duration end time that has the least 
difference from the current time for the operation's predecessor 
operations with a maximum wait duration >= 0.
String 
(Enum)
 
Table 6.8: PropertyType  (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 291 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Controltables 281

ResourceType (Scheduling) table
The ResourceType table is used to define properties for a resource type that is assigned to a 
specified resource.
Field name Description Data type Key
Value Name of the resource type. String Y
ControlSet The control set associated with this ResourceType record.
Referenced table: ControlSet table
Reference Y
Table 6.10: ResourceType (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 292 =====
282 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
ActualDurationCalculationDetermines how the duration of a scheduled receipt 
operation is calculated. This calculation is based on the 
remaining quantity and valid values are: 
 l Fixed: The duration uses the original planned duration 
(actual start time + original planned duration). 
 l Nominal: The duration is calculated based on the 
planned rate (planned rate * remaining quantity). 
 l Realized: The duration is calculated based on the 
realized rate (realized rate * remaining quantity). 
Default : Fixed 
String 
(Enum)
 
AllowDispatchRuleGaps Determines which operation to run next on primary 
resources set to run concurrent operations. This field is 
only considered if the 
EnforceConcurrentPrimaryResourceRuns field on the 
SecondaryResourceRequirement table is set to Y for all 
mapped primary resources. 
Valid values: 
 l True: the resource is paused until the "best" operation 
becomes feasible based on defined properties such as 
assigned dispatch rules or objective function 
parameters. An operation is determined as “best” based 
on which scheduling criteria have been defined as most 
important. For example, if only due date should be 
considered then the operation with the order due the 
soonest is the “best” operation to run.  
 l False: the first feasible operation that is eligible is run 
on the resource. 
Default: True 
Boolean  
AllowOperation
ShiftSpanning
Identifies if shift spanning is allowed on operations.
Shift panning allows operations to span across a resource 
when a shift pattern day changes.
Boolean  
AllowOperationToOverlapIdentifies if operations on this resource type can overlap 
with a downtime, off-shift period, or maintenance action.
Boolean  
Table 6.10: ResourceType (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 293 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Controltables 283
Field name Description Data type Key
AllowOperationToPause Identifies if operations on this resource type can pause 
over a downtime, off-shift period, or maintenance action.
For example, Enabling operation pausing allows operations 
to pause on the resource when it has a downtime period 
and then continue with the operation when the machine is 
running again.
Boolean  
Class Determines which class the resource type belongs to. 
Valid values:
 l Primary: resources that carry out production such as 
workstations or machinery.
 l Secondary: resources that support production such as 
tools or skilled labor.
String 
(Enum)
 
Table 6.10: ResourceType (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 294 =====
284 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
DurationCalculationModeIdentifies how a primary resource calculates operation 
segment durations.
Valid values include:
 l Duration: the Duration or Duration Override value 
from one the following tables is used. The tables are 
ordered by priority with a defined value in the first table 
taking precedence over a value in the second table and 
so forth.
 1. OperationSpecificationResourceSegmentOverride
 2. OperationSpecificationSegmentOverride
 3. ScheduledReceiptOperationSegment (used only for 
Scheduled Receipts)
 4. RouteOperationSegment (used only for Planned 
Orders)
 l Rate: the value from the duration calculation 
(operation order quantity / rate) is used. Depending on 
the order type, the quantity used is either 
PlannedWorkOrderItem.Quantity or 
ScheduleReceipt.Quantity. The rate is taken from the 
defined Rate or RateOverride value from one of the 
following tables. The tables are ordered by priority with 
a defined value in the first table taking precedence over 
a value in the second table and so forth.
 1. OperationSpecificationResourceSegmentOverride
 2. OperationSpecificationSegmentOverride
 3. ScheduledReceiptOperationSegment (used only for 
Scheduled Receipts)
 4. RouteOperationSegment (used only for Planned 
Orders)
 5. Resource
 l Upstream Rate: the value from the calculation 
(operation order quantity / rate of immediate 
predecessor operation) is used. The rate used depends 
on the duration calculation mode applied to the 
resource for the predecessor operation.
String 
(Enum)
 
Table 6.10: ResourceType (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 295 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Controltables 285
Field name Description Data type Key
EnableQualifications Determines if this resource type can have fixed routing 
applied to it. To apply fixed routing to route operations you 
must also have paths between resources defined in the 
PredecessorResourceEligiblity table.
Valid values: 
 l True: all resources are ineligble except those resources 
specified in a record on the 
PredecessorResourceEligiblity table.
 l False: all resources are eligible except for resources set 
as disqualified.
Boolean  
LockingMode The method used to lock the sequence of operations for 
this resource type in the schedule. Valid values: 
 l Day: operations locked by the user will left align 
themselves to the beginning of the day. 
 l Horizon: operations locked by the user will left align 
themselves to the beginning of the horizon.
 l Shift: operations locked by the user will left align 
themselves to the beginning of the shift.
 l Time: operations locked by the user will remain at the 
specific date and time.
String 
(Enum)
 
MaximumPercentage
DurationIncreaseForPausing
The maximum percentage that an operation duration can 
be increased and still considered valid when the operation 
pauses across a downtime.
Quantity  
MinimumDuration
BeforePausing
The minimum amount of time required before operation 
pausing can be applied to the resource.
Quantity  
MinimumDuration
BeforeShiftSpan
The minimum amount of the operation that is allowed to 
run before it can span across to another shift.
Quantity  
Table 6.10: ResourceType (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 296 =====
286 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
SecondaryResourceApply
PrimaryResourceEfficiency
Determines if the secondary resource requirement quantity 
should be adjusted based on the efficiency of the primary 
resource. 
 l When set to True, the secondary resource required 
quantity is multiplied by the primary resource efficiency 
value. If this adjusted quantity of the required resource 
is available, the operation is then eligible to be 
scheduled.
 l When set to False, the secondary resource required 
quantity doesn't change.
Boolean  
SecondaryResource
ConsumptionRule
Determines how the available quantity of a secondary 
resource is consumed.
Valid values are:
 l QtyPerOperation: the quantity consumed is the 
default quantity specified for the secondary resource 
requirement. This is the most common rule used for 
standard labor and tooling.
 l QtyPerWorkItemQuantity: the quantity consumed is 
the value from multiplying the secondary resource 
requirement quantity by the work item quantity.
 l QtyPerOperationRate: the quantity consumed is the 
rate calculated by multiplying the secondary resource 
requirement quantity by the primary resource 
production rate.
String 
(Enum)

(Scheduling) table.

type.

RouteType table

route will follow: 
 l OperationPredecessorAndSequence: by 
sequential precedence which is determined 
by the order specified for 
RouteOperation.Sequence.
 l OperationPredecessor: by the constraints 
defined in the 
RouteOperationPredecessor table.

Table 6.13: RouteType  (Scheduling) fields
SchedulingConfiguration table
The SchedulingConfiguration table is used to configure different scheduling modes for 
pegging.

===== PAGE 298 =====
288 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
Value The name of the scheduling configuration 
record.
String Y
Changeover
CachingMode
Determines how changeover results are 
cached.
Valid values are:
 l None: changeover results are not cached.
 l ByOperation: Changeover results are 
cached by operation. This option is 
recommended when operations and 
parts have multiple properties.
 l ByProperty: Changeover results are 
cached by property. This option is 
recommended when operations and 
parts have few properties or if there are 
many operations.
Note: When the Search Algorithm option is 
enabled, then set this value to either 
ByOperation or ByProperty to reduce 
the time used in calculating changeovers by 
caching them. This can improve 
performance times when the algorithm runs 
multiple times.
String 
(Enum)
 
EnableApplication
Extensions
Determines if application extensions can run 
in this system. Typically this is used to 
identify if any issues being investigated are 
located in or triggered by an extension or 
other component in Enterprise Scheduling.
Boolean  
Table 6.14: SchedulingConfiguration  (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 299 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Controltables 289
Field name Description Data type Key
InterRoutePredecessor
MaximumWaitDuration
The maximum time allowed between the 
completion of the last operation in the 
preceding order and the first operation in its 
successive order. Measured in days.
Zero or positive value applies the maximum 
wait duration between operations.
Negative value implies that there is no time 
restriction between operations.
Quantity  
InterRoutePredecessor
MinimumWaitDuration
The minimum time allowed between the 
completion of the last operation in the 
preceding order and the first operation in its 
successive order. Measured in days.
Quantity  
IntraRoutePredecessor
MaximumWaitDuration
The maximum time allowed between 
predecessor and successor operations for a 
Scheduled Receipt order or Planned Order. 
Measured in days. 
Zero or positive value applies the maximum 
wait duration between operations. 
Negative value implies that there is no time 
restriction between operations. 
Quantity  
IntraRoutePredecessor
MinimumWaitDuration
The minimum time allowed between 
predecessor and successor operations for a 
Scheduled Receipt order or Planned Order. 
Measured in days.
   
Table 6.14: SchedulingConfiguration  (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 300 =====
290 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
PeggingRule The pegging rule applied to the schedule.
Valid values include:
 l FirstInFirstOut: Completed work 
items are pegged to orders based on 
their completion order.
 l QuantityMatching: Completed work 
items are pegged to orders based on 
how closely their quantities match.
String 
(Enum)
 
PeggingSource The pegging source for the schedule.
Valid values include: 
 l SchedulingUpFront: Pegging 
decisions are completed before the 
schedule is generated.
 l SchedulingInProcess: Orders are 
organized based on the value specified 
for PeggingRule.
 l SupplyPlanning: Orders are organized 
based on Maestro planning decisions.
 l SchedulingUpFrontAndSupplyPlanni
ng: Pegging constraints from upstream 
supply planning are first applied and 
then those constraints are modified by 
upfront extension logic before the 
scheduling algorithm runs.
String 
(Enum)
 
SchedulePlannedOrdersIdentifies if planned orders are included  in 
the production schedule.
Default value: N
Boolean  
Table 6.14: SchedulingConfiguration  (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 301 =====

Field name Description Data type Key
InterRoute
PredecessorType
A reference to the 
OperationPredecessorType table that 
determines which predecessor type to use 
for precedence relationships between the 
last operation in a preceding order and the 
first operation in its successive order. 
Referenced table: 
OperationPredecessorType
Reference  
IntraRoutePredecessorTypeA reference to the 
OperationPredecessorType table that 
determines the precedence relationships 
between the operations of a Scheduled 
Receipt order with multiple operations when 
the precedence mode is set to 
OperationPredecessorAndSequence 
and no Scheduled Receipt or Route 
Operation predecessor records are defined.
Referenced table: 
OperationPredecessorType
Reference

Table 6.15: SchedulingConfiguration  (Scheduling) fields
SearchAlgorithmRule table
The SearchAlgorithmRule table is contains parameters that define how the Search Algorithm 
option runs the Scheduling algorithm. By default, the Scheduling algorithm runs once and 

===== PAGE 302 =====
292 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
generates a preliminary production schedule for you to review and modify. This schedule 
displays in the Enterprise Scheduling Workbench workbook.
You can enable the Search Algorithm option to run multiple iterations of the algorithm to find a 
more effective schedule using the parameters in this table. The Search Algorithm option 
produces a calculated score, the objective function value, that ranks the effectiveness of the 
schedule. Lower objective function values are indicative of a more effective schedule as 
compared to the current one. See ObjectiveFunctionExplanation table.
These Search Algorithm parameters are typically set when your Maestro instance is configured. 
You can modify how many iterations run or how long the Scheduling algorithm runs using fields 
in the Area Group table.
Field name Description Data 
type
Key
Value The name of this Search Algorithm Rule record. String Y
SearchAlgorithmInitial
SequenceMode
Determines which mode is used to generate the initial 
sequence of operations in the schedule.
Valid values are:
 l Random: The initial sequence of operations is 
randomly generated.
 l DispatchRule: The initial sequence of operations is 
generated based on the dispatch rule assigned to the 
resource.
Note: Starting with an initial schedule with a better 
objective function value often results in a better final 
schedule.
String 
(Enum)
 
SearchAlgorithm
PerturbationIntensityFactor
The factor that determines the number of perturbations 
that happen in each iteration of the schedule
Recommended value: 1
Note: Setting higher values will introduce more 
randomness into the search.
Quantity  
Table 6.16: SearchAlgorithmRule (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 303 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Controltables 293
Field name Description Data 
type
Key
SearchAlgorithm
ScoringMode
The method used to score a candidate schedule.
Valid values:
 l SchedulingEngine: Runs the Scheduling algorithm 
each time a candidate schedule is generated. 
This option is preferable for schedules with complex 
secondary resource requirements or shift patterns. 
However, it is a slower method and not recommended 
for schedules with a large number of operations or 
resources.
 l Approximate: Runs a simplified simulation algorithm 
to approximate a schedule and its score. 
This option is faster than using the SchedulingEngine 
and is recommended when changeovers largely drive 
the objective function value.
String 
(Enum)
 
SearchAlgorithmTemperature
CoolingFactor
The factor that controls the probability that the 
Scheduling algorithm will move to a candidate schedule 
with a worse score than the current schedule. 
Valid values: Number between 0 and 1. 
Large values allow the search algorithm to accept 
schedules with worse scores. The probability of accepting 
worse scores depends on the size of the difference 
between the schedule scores. The probability also 
decreases with each iteration unless this factor is set to 1.
Quantity  
SearchAlgorithmType The search method to use when the Search Algorithm 
option is enabled.
Valid values are:
 l SimulatedAnnealing: Starts with an initial schedule 
and in each iteration of the Scheduling algorithm, it 
perturbs the schedule by making one or more 
changes. 
These changes are defined by the value selected for 
the SearchAlgorithmNeighbourSelectionMode.
String 
(Enum)
 
Table 6.16: SearchAlgorithmRule (Scheduling) fields
SearchAlgorithmRule:  calculated and set fields
The following describes the calculated and set fields in the SearchAlgorithmRule table. For an 
introduction to this table, and descriptions of its input fields, see SearchAlgorithmRule table.

===== PAGE 304 =====

Set of SearchAlgorithmRuleComponent 
records associated with this Search 
Algorithm Rule record.

Table 6.17: SearchAlgorithmRule (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 305 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 295
Input tables
Input tables hold data imported from your enterprise data sources and are used as input for the 
Scheduling algorithms and extensions. Some input tables also contain calculated fields where 
are part of the record, but are generated by the algorithms and aren't editable. 
The Enterprise Scheduling data model includes the following input tables:
Area table
The Area table defines the logical grouping of a set of resources that are scheduled together. 
Typically an area can represent a plant, facility, or department within a facility.
Field name Description Data type Key
Name Name of an area. String Y
AreaGroup The area group associated with this area.
Referenced table: AreaGroup
Reference Y
Site The site associated with this area.
Referenced table: Site
Reference Y
TimeZone The timezone for the area. Defined using the IANA 
(Internet Assigned Numbers Authority) format for 
timezones. 
String  
Table 6.18: Area  (Scheduling) fields

The following describes the calculated and set fields in the Area table. For an introduction to this 
table, and descriptions of its input fields, see Area table.

type

this area.

with this area.

with this area.

this area.

AreaGroup table
The AreaGroup table identifies the group that an area belongs to. 

===== PAGE 307 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 297
Field name Description Data type Key
Name Name of an area group. String Y
BackwardSchedule
JustInTime
Determines how  backward scheduled 
operations are aligned to successor 
operations when backward scheduling 
is enabled. Valid values include:
 l Y: predecessor operations are right 
aligned. Predecessors are 
scheduled just-in-time to fulfill 
successor operations.
 l N: predecessor operations are left 
aligned. Predecessors are 
scheduled as early as possible 
without being scheduled before a 
prior successor operation.
Default value: Y
Boolean  
DemandHorizonDuration Defines the number of days of 
demand considered for scheduling
Integer  
EnableBatchSelection
Feasbility
Determines if logging for batch 
selection explanations is enabled in 
the system. 
If Y, report logs display in the 
Enterprise Scheduling Analysis 
workbook.
Boolean  
EnableOperation
FeasibilityExplainability
Determines if logging for operation 
feasibility explanations is enabled in 
the system. 
If Y, report logs display in the 
Enterprise Scheduling Analysis 
workbook.
Boolean  
Table 6.20: AreaGroup (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 308 =====
298 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
EnableOperationFeasibility
InfeasibleExplainability
Determines if logging for infeasible 
operation is enabled in the system. 
These logs identify which operations 
were not scheduled and why they 
were flagged as infeasible.
If Y, report logs display in the 
Enterprise Scheduling Analysis 
workbook.
Boolean  
EnableTraceOrder
Explainability
Determines if selected scheduled 
receipt orders are traced to 
understand how they are scheduled. 
If Y, report logs display in the 
Enterprise Scheduling Analysis 
workbook.
Boolean  
EnableWhere

consumed explanations is enabled in 
the system. 
If Y, report logs display in the 
Enterprise Scheduling Analysis 
workbook.
Boolean

window.

Field name Description Data type Key
HistoricalActualsHorizon Specifies how many days prior to the 
horizon’s Start Date to consider actual 
operations for scheduling. 
The start date for this historical 
horizon is calculated as the horizon’s 
Start Date – Historical Actuals Horizon 
(days).    
Only actual operations that start on 
this historical horizon Start Date or 
within the defined horizon for 
historical actuals are included.
Integer  
HorizonDuration The number of days in the schedule 
horizon.
Integer  
SearchAlgorithm
MaximumIterations
The maximum number of schedule 
iterations that are searched. 
 
Note: The initial sequence of 
operations that the algorithm starts 
with doesn’t count as an iteration.
Integer  
SearchAlgorithm
MaximumRuntime
The maximum number of seconds that 
the algorithm can run. This value is 
checked every iteration so the limit 
can be exceeded by the duration of a 
single iteration of the algorithm. A 
value of <=0 implies no time limit.
Quantity  
StartDateTime The date and time that the schedule 
starts.
DateTime

AreaGroupToDispatchRule table
The AreaGroupToDispatchRule table maps an area group to a specified dispatch rule. This lets 
you associate custom rules with an area group and use it in extensions.

The AreaGroupToProperty table maps an area group to a specified property. This lets you 
associate custom rules with an area group and use it in extensions.

Table 6.23: AreaGroupToProperty (Scheduling) fields
BillOfMaterial table
The BillOfMaterial table identifies parent and child relationships between assembly parts 
(products assemblies and sub-assemblies) and component parts (assemblies, sub-assemblies, 
and raw materials) used in the production of the assembly.
Netting can explode bill of material records with a negative QuantityPer value. Negative 
QuantityPer values are ignored unless the bill record has a Type.ExplodeNegatives control 
value of Y, or a Type.ProductType control value of either CoProduct or ByProduct. For 
information about negative dependent demands, see Exploding bill records with a negative 
QuantityPer value.

===== PAGE 313 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 303
Field name Description Data type Key
Alternate  A reference to the alternate BOM to 
which this component belongs. When 
Netting logic performs explosion 
calculations for dependent demands, this 
value is matched to either 
PartSource.BOMAlternate or 
ScheduledReceipt.BOMAlternate. If this 
value matches the default record in the 
BOMAlternate table, then the component 
is used by all Make part sources and 
scheduled receipts for this assembly.
For more information about alternate 
BOMs, see Alternate BOM calculations. For 
information about identifying the 
"default" BOMAlternate record, see 
Default BOMAlternate record.
Referenced table: BOMAlternate
Note: When phantom components are 
included in the alternate BOM structure, 
the BlowThroughBOMAlternate field can 
also be used for further control. 
Reference  
Assembly The assembly being produced. 
Referenced table: Part
Reference Syntax: Assembly.Site, 
Assembly.Name
Reference  
BaseKey Can optionally be used as a key field to 
uniquely identify each BillOfMaterial 
record.
Note: In legacy versions of Maestro, this 
was the only field that could be used to 
define uniqueness on BillOfMaterial 
records.  However, other standard or 
custom fields can now optionally be used 
as keys. For more information, see Key 
fields.
String  
Table 6.24: BillOfMaterial (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 314 =====
304 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
BlowThroughBOMAlternateIf the component on the record is a 
phantom part, this optional reference 
allows specifying the alternate BOM to 
which that phantom's components 
belong. 
This referenced value then takes 
precedence over the Alternate passed 
down from the parent  assembly and 
ensures demand can  flow through the 
phantom to the intended components.  
For example, an alternate BOM under a 
given phantom might represent a group 
of component ingredients that are mixed 
together when building the assembly. If 
these components belong to a different 
alternate BOM then the one passed down 
from the assembly, this field can be used 
to ensure dependent demands are 
correctly placed on them.
Otherwise, if this reference is left Null, 
then the dependent demands will be 
placed on those components of the 
phantom that match will use the alternate 
BOM passed down from the parent 
assembly (or which use the 
default/<blank> BOMAlternate record /
For more information, see  Phantoms and 
Alternate BOMs. 
Referenced table: BOMAlternate 
(Nullable)
NEW  This field was added in Service 
Update 2507.
Reference  
Table 6.24: BillOfMaterial (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 315 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 305
Field name Description Data type Key
Component The component used to produce the 
assembly. 
Referenced table: Part
Reference Syntax: Component.Site, 
Component.Name
Reference  
ComponentPool The pool into which the component's 
dependent demands should be placed 
when exploding the assembly's supply.  
Explosion to the specified pool will 
continue through any subsequent lower-
level parts under the component unless 
subsequently overridden (such as, by 
specifying a different ComponentPool or 
disabling pooling for a part).
This field allows for pool values to be 
injected at the bill-of-material level and 
override any pool applicable to the parent.  
In cases where multiple assemblies share 
the same mid-level part, this allows 
exploding through that common part to 
different components and pools below 
based on the higher level assembly that 
drove the demand. 
If this reference is left Null, the pool 
applicable to the parent assembly is used 
instead (this provides compatibility with 
the logic used prior to Service Update 
2004). 
 For more information about using this 
field, see Pools and BillOfMaterial records. 
Referenced table: Pool (Nullable)
Reference  
EffectiveInDate Date on which this BillOfMaterial record 
will be effective. For more information, see 
BOMType table.
Default value: Past
Date  
Table 6.24: BillOfMaterial (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 316 =====
306 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
EffectiveInEC The engineering change that introduced 
this BillOfMaterial record.
Referenced table: EngineeringChange 
(this reference is nullable in new 
installations of Maestro 10.1 and later).
Reference Syntax: EffectiveInEC.Id
Reference  
EffectiveOutDate Date on which this BillOfMaterial record 
is no longer in effect. 
Default value: Undefined
Date  
EffectiveOutEC The engineering change that makes this 
BillOfMaterial record obsolete. 
Referenced table: EngineeringChange 
(this reference is nullable in new 
installations of Maestro 10.1 and later).
Reference Syntax: EffectiveOutEC.Id
Reference  
EndUnit The end of the unit range to which this 
BillOfMaterial record applies. The end 
unit is either included in the range or 
excluded depending upon the values of 
the BOMType.EffectivityRule and 
DependentUnitRule. This field is ignored 
unless BOMType.MUERule is Unit or 
ModelUnit. Every supply will be seen as 
being before EndUnit if less than zero. 
Default value: -1
Note: This field supports the Model-Unit 
Effectivity algorithm and is ignored if this 
capability is not enabled.
Integer  
Table 6.24: BillOfMaterial (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 317 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 307
Field name Description Data type Key
FeatureRule If this record pertains to a configurable 
end-item (that is, a made to order 
assembly where 
PartType.FeatureBOMRule is Normal or 
HighVolume), then this field is used to 
specify a feature-based rule defining the 
conditions under which the component is 
needed in supply for the assembly. For 
example, a component might only be 
required in production of the assembly if a 
given demand contains a particular set of 
features.
To determine if the component is required 
in planned orders to satisfy a given 
assembly demand, the specified rule is 
evaluated against the set of features in the 
IndependentDemand.FeatureList vector 
set. Similarly, to determine if the 
component is required in a a given 
assembly scheduled receipt, the  the 
specified rule is evaluated against the set 
of features in the  
ScheduledReceipt.FeatureList vector set.
This rule can be comprised of feature 
names as well as any of the following 
logical operators.
 l And: Used to indicate multiple features 
that must all be present on the 
assembly demand for the component 
to be used in satisfying it. By default, 
an ampersand character (&) is used to 
represent "and". For example, a1 & b2. 
If necessary, a different "and" symbol 
can be chosen by an administrator as 
discussed in Defining the And operator 
for feature rules.
 l Or: Used to indicate multiple features 
any one (or more) of which must be 
String  
Table 6.24: BillOfMaterial (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 318 =====
308 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
present on the assembly demand for 
the component to be used in satisfying 
it. A comma (,) is used to represent "or" 
and the specified features should also 
be enclosed in parentheses. For 
example, (a1, a2, a3).
 l Not: Used to indicate features that 
must not be present on the assembly 
demand for the component to be used 
in satisfying it. An exclamation (!) is 
used to represent "not". For example, ! 
(a1, a3) & b2.
If this field is left blank, then the 
component is assumed to be required in 
all supply for the configurable end-item 
assembly regardless of features.
Default value: blank string
Note: For information about the tables 
used to specify features on independent 
demands or scheduled receipts, see 
IndependentDemandFeatureList table and 
ScheduledReceiptFeatureList table 
respectively. 
Table 6.24: BillOfMaterial (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 319 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 309
Field name Description Data type Key
IntervalToExpiry
Offset
If the BillOfMaterial record represents a 
co-product or by-product configuration, 
this field indicates the number of intervals 
to add to or subtract from the assembly's 
PartSource.IntervalsToExpiry and 
PartSource.CTPIntervalsToExpiry values 
when calculating estimated and actual 
expiry dates on the co-product or by-
product. These intervals are expressed in 
terms of the assembly part's 
PlanningCalendars.ExpiryCalendar 
value.
This setting allows for a co-product or by-
product to have a different shelf life than 
the primary part whose production it is 
derived from. If the co-product or by-
product has a longer shelf life than the 
primary part, this field should be set to a 
positive value. If the co-product or by-
product has a shorter shelf life than the 
primary part, this field should be set to a 
negative value. Otherwise, this value 
should be left at zero.
Default value: 0
Integer  
Table 6.24: BillOfMaterial (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 320 =====
310 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
LeadTimeOffset Specifies the number of units defined by 
the 
Assembly.PlanningCalendars.TimeUnits 
calendar (typically, workday) to offset 
(move later) the required DueDate of the 
components for use in the assembly. 
Allows material to be staggered in time for 
long assembly operations. 
If the BillOfMaterial record defines a co-
product or by-product configuration, this 
field allows for the due and available dates 
on the co-product or by-product to be 
either earlier or later then the due and 
available dates on the primary product by 
a specified amount of time.
Default value: 0
Quantity  
Model The model to which this bill record 
applies. The Model value is ignored unless 
the MUERule for the BOMType 
associated with this record is Model or 
ModelUnit. 
Referenced table: Model
Note: This field supports the Model-Unit 
Effectivity algorithm, and is ignored if this 
capability is not enabled.
Reference  
Table 6.24: BillOfMaterial (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 321 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 311
Field name Description Data type Key
Operation The specific operation in the assembly 
supply's routing where the component is 
used. The referenced operation can be in 
the routing's standard sequence or one of 
its optional parallel sequences.
Use of this field allows dependent 
demands to be placed at the start of the 
operation where they are actually needed 
(which may be after the parent order start 
date).
Note that this reference is only considered 
if the parent supply has 
SupplyStatus.PartialExplode set to Y 
and/or uses capacity-based variable lead 
time (has OrderPolicy.UseVarLeadTime 
set to CRP). 
Referenced table: CRPOperation 
(nullable)
Reference Syntax: Operation.Id, 
Operation.Sequence.Id, 
Operation.Sequence.Routing.Id, 
Operation.Sequence.Routing.Site.
Reference  
OperationNumber Operation sequence number at which the 
component is needed. This value is only 
used in Netting calculations if the 
Operation reference field is Null (and 
refers to operations defined in the 
Operation table). 
Default value: 0
Integer  
Table 6.24: BillOfMaterial (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 322 =====
312 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
OptionRatio Probability the assemblies will need these 
components. The range should be 
between 0 - 1 (if BOMType.RatioRule = 
Fraction) or between 1 - 100 
(BOMType.RatioRule = Percent). The 
supply quantity is multiplied by the ratio, 
then rounded (depending on rounding 
rules), then multiplied by QuantityPer 
(and possibly rounded again according to 
the rounding rules). MRP bills have this set 
to 1 or 100.
Default value: 1
Note: This field is ignored on 
BillOfMaterial records that define co-
product or by-product configurations.
Quantity  
QuantityPer The amount of this component that is 
required to build each finished assembly. 
A negative value can be processed to 
cancel positive demand. 
If the BillOfMaterial record defines a co-
product or by-product configuration, then 
a negative quantity per should be used to 
define the amount of the supply 
generated for the primary product that 
ends up as a co-product or by-product. 
This is done using the ratio
-(co or by-product% / primary 
product%) 
For more information about configuring 
co-products and by-products, see Co-
products and by-products.
Default value: 1
Quantity  
Table 6.24: BillOfMaterial (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 323 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 313
Field name Description Data type Key
Scrap Specifies the fraction of the components 
that is lost during the production of the 
assembly. For example, due to breakage.
The interpretation of the value specified in 
this field is configurable as described in 
ScrapRule.
Default value: 1
Note: This field is ignored on 
BillOfMaterial records that define co-
product or by-product configurations.
Quantity  
Shop The shop floor location associated with 
this BillOfMaterial record.
Referenced table: Shop (this reference is 
nullable in new installations of Maestro 
10.1 and later).
Reference  
StartUnit The beginning of the unit range to which 
this bill record applies. The start unit is 
either included in the range or excluded 
depending upon the values of the 
BOMType EffectivityRule and 
DependentUnitRule. StartUnit is ignored 
unless MUERule is Unit or ModelUnit. 
Every supply will be seen as being after 
StartUnit if StartUnit is less than zero. 
Default value: -1
Note: This field supports the Model-Unit 
Effectivity algorithm, and is ignored if this 
capability is not enabled.
Integer  
Table 6.24: BillOfMaterial (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 324 =====
314 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
SubstituteGroup A reference to the SubstituteGroup table 
that is used to define groups of 
substitutable components within an 
assembly. All components that are 
assumed to be substitutable for one 
another within a given assembly must 
point to the same SubstituteGroup 
record. In cases where an entire group of 
components can be substituted for 
another group of components, the 
SubstituteGroup reference should be 
made on the phantom BOM that defines 
each group of components.
To ensure expected results/performance 
in bom-level substitution, avoid having 
multiple assemblies reference the same 
group for their substitutable components. 
That is, each assembly should have its own 
unique SubstituteGroup record
(s) available for grouping substitutable 
components. 
Referenced table: SubstituteGroup (this 
reference is nullable in new installations of 
Maestro 10.1 and later).
Note: This field is ignored on 
BillOfMaterial records that define co-
product or by-product configurations. 
However, co-products can still be defined 
as substitutable components under an 
assembly (that is, each co-product should 
be configured as a component with the 
primary part in the group as the 
assembly).
Reference  
Table 6.24: BillOfMaterial (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 325 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 315
Field name Description Data type Key
SubstitutionTo
DateQty
Indicates the quantity to date that has 
already been allocated to this substitute 
BOM (for example, it might represent 
known allotments on input supplies). This 
value should be expressed in component 
stock units, and is then used as an initial 
value for ongoing BOM-level substitution 
decisions.
This field is applicable to substitute BOM 
records where 
SubstituteGroup.Type.SourceRule is set 
to OnGoing, and is used in the logic that 
determines which substitutable 
components in a group should have 
planned orders generated on them to 
satisfy a dependent demand from the 
assembly. For more information, see 
Define planned order generation.
Quantity  
Table 6.24: BillOfMaterial (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 326 =====
316 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
Substitution
Sequence
Defines the preferred sequence in which 
an assembly should look for 
scheduled/input supply within a group of 
substitutable components (components 
with lower values are used first). A value of 
0 should be assigned to the primary 
component in the group. When 
substitution logic is turned off for the 
group (or if there is no SubstituteGroup 
record defined), the primary component is 
the only one for which dependent 
demand is generated when supply for the 
assembly is exploded. Also, ensure that no 
two of an assembly's components in a 
given SubstituteGroup have the same 
value in this field (duplicate 
SubstitutionSequence values in a group 
can result in excess dependent demand 
being generated). For more information, 
see Substitute BOM configuration.
In cases where an entire group of 
components can be substituted for 
another group of components, the 
component with the lowest value in this 
field acts as the indicator component 
within the group (the component whose 
supply determines if substitutions can be 
made). For example, typically a value of -1 
is assigned to the indicator component, 
and all other components in the group are 
given a value of 0. If all values are the 
same, the component with the highest 
Part.StdUnitCost is used as the indicator. 
For more information, see Substituting 
groups of components
Default value: 0
Integer  
Table 6.24: BillOfMaterial (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 327 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 317
Field name Description Data type Key
Note: This value defines a preference 
between substitutable components only 
when they can provide scheduled supply 
equally well (on-time or least late). For 
example, if Component A has scheduled 
supply that is on-time to the demand and 
Component B has scheduled supply that is 
late to the demand, then the supply of A is 
preferred regardless of the value in this 
field.
Table 6.24: BillOfMaterial (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 328 =====
318 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
Target Determines the ratio, or percentage, in 
which dependent demand from the 
assembly is exploded down to the 
components in a substitute group when 
creating planned orders. This value should 
be expressed in terms of the assembly's 
supply unit.
The percentage of demand that should be 
exploded down to a given component is 
then calculated as the Target value for the 
component divided by the sum of the 
Target values across all components in 
the group. Note that depending on the 
setting in the ComponentSourceRule 
field on the SubstituteGroupType table, 
each dependent requirement from the 
assembly is either split proportionally 
between substitutable components or 
fully assigned to the substitutable 
component which would otherwise be 
furthest away from its ongoing target.
If planned orders should only be created 
on the primary component in the group, 
then it can be assigned a positive value in 
this field and all other components should 
be assigned a value of 0 (zero).
If planned orders should be evenly spread 
across all components in the group, then 
each can be assigned the same positive 
value in this field (for example, a value of 1 
could be specified).
Default value: 0
Quantity  
Table 6.24: BillOfMaterial (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 329 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 319
Field name Description Data type Key
Note: This value defines a preference 
between substitutable components only 
when they can plan new supply  equally 
well (on-time or least late). For example, if 
Component A can plan a new order that is 
one day late to the demand and 
Component B can plan a new order that is 
five days late to the demand, then the 
entire dependent demand is placed on A 
regardless of the value in this field.
Type A value that is associated with processing 
rules that defines the explosion, effectivity, 
and netting behavior.  This field references 
the BOMType table. 
Referenced table: BOMType
Reference  
Table 6.24: BillOfMaterial (Mfg) fields
BillOfMaterial table:  calculated and set fields
The following describes the calculated and set fields in the BillOfMaterial table. For an 
introduction to this table, and descriptions of its input fields, see BillOfMaterial table.

===== PAGE 330 =====
320 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
AssemblyLLC The low-level code of the assembly. This field is 
intended for internal use in propagating the 
LLC down from the assembly to the 
component.
Integer  
CumLeadTime The component's CumLeadTime adjusted by 
the LeadTimeOffset. Cumulative lead time is 
calculated using BillOfMaterial records that 
are effective at the MRP date.
If a component is treated as a phantom to a 
bill, the component's lead time is subtracted 
from its cumulative lead time. For example, 
only its component cumulative lead times are 
used. 
If it is not a phantom, or conditionally a 
phantom, then it is assumed to contribute its 
lead time. Parts that are Buy parts will have 
cumulative lead time equal to its lead time, (for 
example, it will not consider the lead times of 
the components). 
This calculation is done only within a part's site. 
For multi-site cumulative lead time calculations 
(that is, calculations that consider transfer part 
sources), use the MultiSiteCumLeadTime 
field.
For more information about lead time, see 
Lead time calculations.
Note:  If using time-phased lead time. then the 
lead times effective for the component's 
primary part source at the latter of the part's 
RunDate or PartSource.FirstEffectiveDate 
are used in this calculation.
Quantity  
Table 6.25: Calculated BillOfMaterial (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 331 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 321
Field name Description Data type Key
EffOffSet This value reflects the value of the 
LeadTimeOffset field but will be zero if the 
UseLeadTimeOffset field is N. This value is 
based on the value of:
BillOfMaterial.Type.UseLeadTimeOffset
(if UseLeadTimeOffset= N then EffOffset=0, 
otherwise EffOffset= LeadTimeOffset.)
Quantity  
EffOptionRatio
 
This field reflects the value of the OptionRatio 
field expressed as a fraction (value between 0 
and 1). However, it always returns 1 if 
Type.RatioRule is set to Ignore.
Quantity  
Table 6.25: Calculated BillOfMaterial (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 332 =====
322 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
EffQuantityPer The effective quantity of the component per 
unit of the assembly (after consideration for 
Scrap value and the setting in 
BOMType.ScrapRule). If 
BOMType.RoundingRule is set to 
QtyPerOrder, then this field is always set to 
the QuantityPer value (all other 
RoundingRule settings are ignored by this 
field). If BOMType.RoundingRule is not set to 
QtyPerOrder, then this calculation is 
dependent on ScrapRule as follows:
1. ScrapFraction
BillOfMaterial.QtyPer / (1 - 
BillOfMaterial.Scrap)
2. ScrapPercent
BillOfMaterial.QtyPer / (1 - BillMaterial.Scrap * 
0.01)
3. YieldFraction
BillOfMaterial.QtyPer /  BillMaterial.Scrap
4. YieldPercent
BillOfMaterial.QtyPer /  (BillMaterial.Scrap * 
0.01)
5. InflationFraction
BillOfMaterial.QtyPer /  (1 / (1 + 
BillOfMaterial.Scrap))
6. InflationPercent
BillOfMaterial.QtyPer / ( 1 / (1 + 
BillOfMaterial.Scrap * 0.01))
Quantity  
FirstEffectiveDate Represents the adjusted value for the 
EffectiveInDate taking into account 
Type.EffectivityRule (for example, 
Inclusive). If the record will never be 
effective, the dates will be Undefined.
Date  
Table 6.25: Calculated BillOfMaterial (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 333 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 323
Field name Description Data type Key
FirstEffectiveUnit The first unit to which this BillOfMaterial 
record applies.
Note: If not using Unit Effectivity, this field is 
set to -1.
Integer  
IsRecursive Indicates whether recursive data has been 
found during processing (this happens when a 
part ends up being a component of itself). 
BillOfMaterial records that have a 
BOMType.EffectivityRule of Never are 
ignored. Valid values are:
 l Y: recursive data has been found. The 
calculation skips the parts involved in the 
recursive relationship.
 l N: recursive data has not been found
Boolean  
LastEffectiveDate Represents the adjusted value for the 
EffectiveOutDate taking into account 
Type.EffectivityRule (for example, 
InclusiveExclusive). This field is always 
inclusive. If the record will never be effective, 
the dates will be Undefined.
Date  
LastEffectiveUnit The last unit to which this BillOfMaterial 
record applies.
Note: If not using Unit Effectivity, this field is 
set to 2,147,483,647.
Integer  
MultiSite
AssemblyLLC
The multi-site low level of the assembly (any 
transfer part sources and their bill of 
material structures are considered in this 
calculation). This field is intended for internal 
use in propagating the low level code down 
from the assembly to the component.
Quantity  
Table 6.25: Calculated BillOfMaterial (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 334 =====
324 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
MultiSiteCum
LeadTime
The component's cumulative lead time, 
calculated using BillOfMaterial records that are 
effective at the MRP date, adjusted by 
LeadTimeOffset. This field uses a calculation 
similar to the CumLeadTime field, however 
this calculation will follow a component's 
product structure across site boundaries. That 
is, if a component's product structure contains 
a part with a primary supply type of transfer, 
then cumulative lead time associated with the 
transfer part is included in the value reported 
in this field.
Because this field adds together different lead 
time values without specifying the calendar to 
be used in the calculation, it is possible that the 
different lead time values reported when a 
product structure crosses site boundaries may 
not all use the same calendar. Therefore, 
depending on the calendars used in different 
sites, this field may not always return the 
expected result.
For more information about lead time, see 
Lead time calculations.
Note: If using time-phased lead time. then the 
lead times effective for the component's 
primary part source at the latter of the part's 
RunDate or PartSource.FirstEffectiveDate 
are used in this calculation.
Quantity  
FeatureRulesList Set of BillOfMaterialFeatureRule records that 
reference this BillOfMaterial record.
Set  
NewAllocations Set of NewAllocation records that define the 
dependent demands on the component due to 
ScheduledReceipts of the assembly that were 
exploded.
Set  
Table 6.25: Calculated BillOfMaterial (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 335 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 325
Field name Description Data type Key
PlannedAllocations Set of PlannedAllocation records that define 
the dependent demands on the component 
due to PlannedOrders generated for the 
assembly.
Set  
Table 6.25: Calculated BillOfMaterial (Mfg) fields
Visualizing the bill of material in a BOM chart
Some workbooks can include a chart that shows the bill of material. A BOM chart provides you 
with an up-to-date view of the product structure, and you can filter the chart to focus only on the 
components or sites (or other criteria) that are relevant to you. The  BOM Visualization worksheet 
in the BOM Analysis workbook includes a BOM chart.
In a BOM chart you can:
 l Control your view by moving the whole chart, moving individual nodes, or zooming in and 
out of the chart.
 l Focus on a single component and its relationships.
 l View substitutes, co-products, and by-products for all components or a specific 
component.
 l Reset the chart to return to its default view.
For more information, see the Maestro User Guide (Java client).
BOMAlternate table
For Maestro configurations using alternate BOM logic, this table holds all alternate BOMs (that is, 
named variations of an assembly's BOM). 
One record in this table is the "default" record and contains a special or common alternate code 
to be used by components that belong to all alternate BOM configurations under a given 
assembly. Typically, this special BOMAlternate record will have an empty string (<blank>) in the 
Value field. For information about finding the default BOMAlternate record, see Default 
BOMAlternate record.

===== PAGE 336 =====
326 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data
type
Key
Description A description of this alternate BOM. String  
Value The name of this alternate. Each alternate BOM value 
can be used by many part sources for many different 
parts.
Default value: blank
String Yes

blowthrough record.

Table 6.27: Calculated BOMAlternate (Mfg) fields
Campaign table
The Campaign table defines the properties of a campaign, such as if it allows shift changes or 
changeovers.  Campaigns are used to run a series of successive operations for a specified 
duration of time or quantity produced. A Campaign record is associated with one or more 
CampaignCondition records that define when and for how long the campaign runs. Campaigns 
must be mapped to a specific resource using the ResourceToCampaign table.
Field name Description Data type Key
Name The name of the campaign. String Y
Area The area associated with the campaign. Reference Y
AllowChangeovers If changeovers can be applied during the 
campaign.
Boolean  
AllowDowntimes If downtimes can be applied during the 
campaign.
Boolean  
AllowOffShiftTime If off-shift times can be applied during the 
campaign.
Boolean  
AllowShiftChanges If shift changes can be applied during the 
campaign.
Boolean  
RestartThreshold How long a campaign must wait before 
restarting after it has ended. Measured in 
days.
Quantity  
Table 6.28: Campaign (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 338 =====
328 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Campaign table:  calculated and set fields
The following describes the calculated and set fields in the Campaign table. For an introduction 
to this table, and descriptions of its input fields, see Campaign table.
Field name Description Data type Key
CampaignConditions Set of CampaignCondition records 
associated with this campaign.
Set  
ResourceToCampaigns Set of ResourceToCampaign records 
associated with this area.
Set  
Table 6.29: Campaign (Scheduling) fields
CampaignCondition table
The CampaignCondition table defines the condition that determines when and how the 
campaign runs. The constraint fields determine how long the campaign runs, and the property 
fields determine which operations are eligible to run in the campaign. All constraint fields are 
required, but for property, only the PropertyType field must have a value specified. For example, 
if PropertyType is set to “flavor”, then groups of operations for any flavor run for the specified 
duration of the campaign. If the Property field is also set, for example to “cheese” then only 
groups of operations with the property “cheese” run for the specified duration of the campaign.

===== PAGE 339 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 329
Field name Description Data type Key
ConstraintMeasure The measure the constraint value is defined in. 
This is a required field.
Valid values include:
 l Duration: the total working duration of 
consecutive operations scheduled in the 
campaign. Measured in days.
 l Quantity: the total quantity produced by 
consecutive operations scheduled in the 
campaign.
 String (Enum)  
ConstraintType Determines how the campaign condition is 
constrained. This is a required field.
Valid values include:
 l Minimum: the minimum constraint value 
required to constrain the campaign.
 l Maximum: the maximum constraint value 
required to constrain the campaign. 
 String (Enum)  
ConstraintValue The numeric value that is used to define the 
constraint on the campaign condition. This 
value represents a duration or a quantity 
value depending on what value is selected for 
the ConstraintMeasure. This is a required 
field.
Quantity  
GapThreshold The maximum allowable duration allowed in 
gaps between operations within the 
campaign. A gap is the idle time between 
consecutive operations. 
 If a gap exceeds the GapThreshold value, the 
operation scheduled after the gap is not 
considered as contributing to minimum or 
maximum length of the campaign condition. 
Quantity  
Table 6.30: CampaignCondition (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 340 =====
330 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
Campaign The campaign this condition is associated 
with.
Reference  
Property The property value that defines the campaign. 
This is an optional field.  
 l If a property value is specified, then the 
campaign is only defined by this property 
value and not all other property values for 
its property type. 
 l If property value is not entered, then 
campaigns are defined for all matching 
property values for the specified property 
type. 
Reference  
PropertyType The type of property assigned to operations 
that are eligible to run in this campaign. This is 
a required field. 
When the property type changes between 
groups of operations, then the campaign 
stops. 
Reference  
Table 6.30: CampaignCondition (Scheduling) fields

Table 6.33: Changeover  (Scheduling) fields
ChangeoverCondition table
The ChangeoverCondition table defines the conditions applied to a specified changeover. 
Multiple changeovers that happen at the same time are combined in parallel when they are in 
the same concurrency group or in a series when they are in different concurrency groups. 

===== PAGE 342 =====
332 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
DurationOverride The time duration for the override. Measured in 
days. 
This override supersedes the duration specified 
for this changeover condition on the 
ChangeoverResource table.
Quantity  
FromToDelta When the property type is a numeric value, a 
comparison factor of the From and To Property 
values of a condition. When this expression is 
true the condition applies and a changeover is 
incurred.
Quantity  
Operator The mathematical operator applied when 
comparing From and To property values.
Valid values are:
 l Equals
 l GreaterThan
 l GreaterThanEqual
 l LessThan
 l LessThanEqual
 l NotEqual
String (Enum)  
PenaltyOverride The value that overrides the penalty value 
defined in the ChangeoverResource table.
Quantity  
Changeover The changeover associated with this 
changeover condition.
Referenced table: Changeover
Reference  
FromProperty The property that the operation is changed 
over from that is associated with this 
changeover condition. This property must be of 
the same type as the value for ToProperty.
Referenced table: Property
Reference  
Table 6.34: ChangeoverCondition  (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 343 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 333
Field name Description Data type Key
PropertyType The property type associated with this 
changeover condition.
Referenced table: PropertyType
Reference  
ToProperty The property that the operation changes to 
that is associated with this changeover 
condition. This property must be the same type 
as the value for FromProperty.
Referenced table: Property
Reference
