ChangeoverResource table
The ChangeoverResource table shows the mapping between changeovers and resources.
Field name Description Data type Key
Changeover The changeover associated with this 
changeover resource.
Referenced table: Changeover
Reference Y
Resource The resource associated mapped to this 
changeover resource.
Referenced table: Resource (Scheduling)
Reference Y
ResourceGroup The resource group for the resource mapped 
to this changeover resource.
Referenced table: ResourceGroup
(Scheduling)
Reference Y
Table 6.35: ChangeoverResource  (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 344 =====
334 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
ConcurrencyGroup The concurrency group assigned to the 
changeover resource. When a resource has 
multiple changeovers associated with it and 
conditions overlap on the resource, the 
concurrency group is used to determine if 
those changeovers run in a series or in 
parallel.  
When the concurrency group is set to: 
 l Different values: changeovers run in 
series. 
 l The same value: changeovers run in 
parallel. 
When multiple changeovers are combined in 
parallel, the time for the longest changeover is 
used. When combined in a series, the total 
time for all changeovers is used.
Integer  
Duration The duration of the changeover. Measured in 
days.
Quantity  
Penalty The size of the impact on the changeover. 
Along with the assigned dispatch rule, this 
value is used to prioritize operations. 
Operations with changeovers that have a 
lower penalty are preferentially scheduled 
before operations with higher penalty 
changeovers. 
Quantity  
Table 6.35: ChangeoverResource  (Scheduling) fields
ChangeoverToMaintenance table
The ChangeoverToMaintenance table maps a changeover to a maintenance action that resets 
the start of the interval of the mapped maintenance every time that changeover happens in the 
schedule.

===== PAGE 345 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 335
Field name Description Data type Key
Changeover The changeover that resets the interval start of the 
mapped maintenance action.
Referenced table: Changeover
Reference Y
Maintenance The mapped maintenance action.
Referenced table: Maintenance
Reference Y
Table 6.36: ChangeoverToMaintenance(Scheduling) fields
Configuration (Scheduling) table
The Configuration table is used to enable the Search Algorithm option for Enterprise Scheduling.
Field name Description Data type Key
Id A unique identifier for this configuration record. String Y
Comments Additional information about this configuration 
record.
String  
Value The string value for this configuration. This field can 
be used to enable or disable a configuration, or to 
specify a configuration value.
Default value: Y
String  
Table 6.37: Configuration  (Scheduling) fields
ControlSet table
This table holds all control sets defined in your Maestro system. Each control set is comprised of 
all entries in other tables that point to that record's Value field. The tables that reference the 
ControlSet table include the Site table and a large number of control tables; for a full listing of 
these tables, see ControlSet table:  calculated and set fields. 
Control sets allow for the same control table value to potentially have different meanings across 
Maestro sites. For example, a PartType value of P may indicate a purchased part in the control 
set associated with one site, and a phantom part in the control set associated with another site. 
Control sets can also be used to hold all expected characteristics for a particular type of Maestro 
installation within your demand or supply network. The control set can then be exported, 

===== PAGE 346 =====
336 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
imported, and otherwise manipulated across sites as needed. For complete information on 
managing controls sets, see the Maestro Administration Guide.
Field name Description Data type Key
Value A unique identifier for this control set. String Yes
Description A description of this control set. String  
Table 6.38: ControlSet (Core) fields
Note: For more information on control sets, see Control sets.

DispatchRule table
The DispatchRule table contains information on the dispatch rules applied to resources. 
Dispatch rules are used to sort and schedule eligible operations on a resource.
Field name Description Data type Key
Name Name of the dispatch rule formula. String Y
Area The area associated with this dispatch rule.
Referenced table: Area
Reference Y
MaximumDueDate
PullAhead
The number of days before an operation due 
date that the operation can be considered 
eligible for scheduling. A value of 0 means 
that no limit is applied to when an operation 
is eligible.
Quantity  
Table 6.40: DispatchRule  (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 351 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 341
Field name Description Data type Key
Type The type of dispatch rule. Valid values are:
 l Hierarchical: eligible operations are 
sorted and scheduled according to a 
defined hierarchy.
 l Weighted: eligible operations are 
assigned a score calculated using a 
weighted expression. Operations are 
scheduled in ascending order (from lowest 
to highest score).
String (Enum)

Table 6.41: DispatchRule  (Scheduling) fields
DispatchRuleComponent table
The DispatchRuleComponent table defines the properties for components that make up a 
dispatch rule. A dispatch rule must have at least one component defined.

===== PAGE 352 =====
342 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
ScoreType Select the type of score to apply to the component. 
For hierarchical dispatch rules, set the component 
score type to one of the following:
 l Maximum: operations are sorted in descending 
order.
 l Minimum: operations are sorted in ascending 
order.
For weighted dispatch rules, set the component 
score type to:
 l Weighted: defined values are used to calculate 
the score for the component.
String (Enum) Y
DispatchRule The dispatch rule associated with this  component.
Referenced table: DispatchRule
Reference Y
PropertyType The property type associated with this  component.
Referenced table: PropertyType
Reference Y
Weighting  l For hierarchical dispatch rules: the hierarchy 
level assigned to the property type.
 l For weighted dispatch rules: the coefficient used 
in the weighted expression to calculate scores.
Quantity  
Table 6.42: DispatchRuleComponent  (Scheduling) fields

table.

DispatchRule
ComponentOverrides

records associated with this component.
Set  
Table 6.43: DispatchRuleComponent  (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 353 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 343
DispatchRuleComponentOverride table
The DispatchRuleComponentOverride table contains values used to override dispatch rule 
component scores and weights when the score falls within a specified range of values. The range 
is defined by maximum and minimum score values. For example, a minimum score of 2 and 
maximum of 6 defines a range where the dispatch rule component score must be above 2 or 
below 6 to trigger the overrides.
You can set inclusive fields to include the maximum and minimum values in the range. For 
example, a minimum score of 2, maximum of 6, and MaximumScoreInclusive = Y defines a range 
where the dispatch rule component score must be above 2 or any value up to and including 6 to 
trigger the overrides.
Field name Description Data type Key
Priority The value used to resolve scheduling 
conflicts between overlapping override 
records. Records with the lower priority 
takes precedence in scheduling.
Integer Y
DispatchRuleComponentThe dispatch rule component 
associated with this override.
Referenced table: 
DispatchRuleComponent
Reference Y
MaximumScore The maximum score that a property 
value has to be below for its score 
and/or weighting values to be 
overriden.
Quantity  
MaximumScoreInclusive Determines if the maximum score value 
is included in the maximum-minimum 
core range.
Boolean  
MinimumScore The minimum score that a property 
value has to be above for its score 
and/or weighting values to be 
overriden.
Quantity  
Table 6.44: DispatchRuleComponentOverride  (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 354 =====
344 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
MinimumScoreInclusive Determines if the minimum score value 
is included in the maximum-minimum 
core range.
Boolean  
ScoreOverride The value used to override the score 
specified in the 
DispatchRuleComponent table.
Quantity  
WeightingOverride The value used to override the weight 
specified in the 
DispatchRuleComponent table.
Quantity

Maintenance table
The Maintenance table defines maintenance actions in the schedule that are applied to a 
specified type of operation segment. The maintenance action can be set to recur at defined 
intervals based on a specified duration or quantity produced.
Field name Description Data type Key
Name The name of the maintenance. String Y
AllowMaintenance
OverlappingDowntime
Identifies if this maintenance can 
overlap with a downtime period on 
the schedule.
Boolean

OverlappingOffshift
Identifies if this maintenance can 
overlap with an off-shift period on the 
schedule.
Boolean  
AllowMaintenance
PausingOverDowntime
Identifies if this maintenance can 
pause over a downtime period on the 
schedule.
Boolean  
AllowMaintenancePausing
OverOffshift
Identifies if this maintenance can 
pause over an off-shift period on the 
schedule.
Boolean

AllowOperation
ToPause
Identifies if operations can pause over 
this maintenance action.
Boolean

takes. Measured in days.
Quantity  
Interval How frequently this maintenance 
operation is performed. Measured in 
days or quantity which is determined 
by the value selected for the Type 
field.
Quantity  
Type The type of maintenance which 
determines how the maintenance 
interval is calculated. Valid values 
include:
 l ElapsedDuration: total elapsed 
time (days).
 l IdleDuration: total elapsed time 
with no "working" operation 
segment types (days).
 l OperationCount: total number of 
operations.
 l Quantity: total quantity produced 
by the primary resource.
 l WorkingDuration: total working 
duration (days) of the primary 
resource..

quantity-based maintenance intervals. 
Operation quantities are converted by 
to this target UOM using the defined 
conversion rates in the UnitOfMeasure 
table (including part-specific overrides 
defined in the PartUOMConversion 
table) before being used to determine 
the quantity that triggers the 
maintenance.

maintenance is applied to. This should 
be a type created specifically for use 
only with maintenance operations.

OperationSegmentType
Reference

with this maintenance record that can 
trigger the maintenance when the 
property type is set to Operation 
Count, Quantity, or Working Duration. 
For example, “Red” or  “Yellow”.
If this value is blank but a property 
type has been specified, any operation 
that matches the property type can 
trigger the maintenance.

Table 6.50: Maintenance (Scheduling) fields
MaintenanceOperation table
The MaintenanceOperation table contains contains two types of maintenance records:
 l Historical maintenance records: these are manually input by users and can be identified 
as record with a StartDateTime that is before the start of the scheduling horizon.
 l Modified maintenance records: these are generated by the Scheduling algorithm and 
have been modified by a user on the Gantt chart. For example, a maintenance action that 
has been locked or unlocked by a user.
Field name Description Data type Key
Name Name of the maintenance operation. String Y
DurationOverride How long the override is applied. 
Measured in days. This value is optional.
If this value is not null, the EndDateTime of 
the maintenance operation is overridden 
by it's StartDateTime + DurationOverride.
Default: -1
Quantity  
InputEndDateTime The date and time the maintenance 
operation ends.
DateTime  
InputStartDateTime The date and time the maintenance 
operation starts.
DateTime  
IsBypass Identifies if this maintenance operation 
has been set to unassigned and held by a 
user. 
Boolean  
Table 6.51: MaintenanceOperation (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 361 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 351
Field name Description Data type Key
InputPrimaryResource The primary resource this maintenance 
operation applies to. 
Referenced table:Resource
Reference  
Maintenance The maintenance action associated with 
this maintenance operation.
Referenced table: Maintenance
Reference  
Table 6.51: MaintenanceOperation (Scheduling) fields
MaintenanceToDowntimeType table
The MaintenanceToDowntimeType table maps a downtime type to a maintenance action. The 
interval start for the mapped maintenance action will be reset for any downtime of that type that 
happens in the schedule.
Field name Description Data type Key
DownTimeType The type of downtime that resets the interval 
start of the mapped maintenance action.
Referenced table: DowntimeType
Reference Y
Maintenance The mapped maintenance action.
Referenced table: Maintenance
Reference Y
Table 6.52: MaintenanceToDowntimeType (Scheduling) fields
MaintenanceToMaintenance table
The MaintenanceToMaintenance table maps one maintenance action to another maintenance 
action where action 1 resets the interval start for action 2. For example, a regular maintenance 
action (action 1) is scheduled to clean resource A every 7 days, starting on Mondays. Another 
maintenance action (action 2) replaces a filter on resource A once a quarter and takes 1 day to 
complete. When you map action 1 to action 2, action 2 resets the interval start of action 1 to start 
on the completion of action 2. Therefore, action 1 will resume with its interval of 7 days but will 
start on Tuesdays now.

===== PAGE 362 =====
352 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
Maintenance The new maintenance action that resets the 
interval start of the mapped maintenance 
action.
Referenced table: Maintenance
Reference Y
ResetMaintenance The mapped maintenance action.
Referenced table: Maintenance
Reference Y
Table 6.53: MaintenanceToMaintenance (Scheduling) fields

ObjectiveFunctionComponent table
The ObjectiveFunctionComponent table contains information on the components that make up 
objective functions.

===== PAGE 364 =====
354 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
Objective
Function
Variable
A variable assigned to the objective function that this 
component is associated with.
Valid values include:
 l ChangeoverDuration: The total number of days 
incurred by changeovers.
 l ChangeoverPenalty: The sum of all changeover 
penalties incurred. No units are assigned to this 
value.
 l IdleTime: The total number of idle days.
 l InventoryHolding: The total number of days that 
inventory is held. This value can be considered a 
make-penalty where Inventory holding time = 
due date – completion date.
 l Tardiness: The total number of tardy days 
calculated by completion date – due date.
 l UnscheduledOperation: The total sum of days 
that an operation ends after the horizon. This 
value is weighted by operation quantity and 
captures how many operations are not scheduled 
within the horizon
 l ValueOverMaximumCampaignConstraint: The 
amount the constraint value exceeds the 
maximum defined constraint for a specified 
candidate schedule.
 l ValueUnderMinimumCampaignconstraint: The 
amount of additional constraint required to meet 
the minimum defined constraint for a specified 
candidate schedule.
 l WaitDurationExceedingMaximum: The total 
sum of days that a successor operation exceeds 
its specified maximum wait duration limit.
String (Enum) Y
Objective
Function
Name of the objective function that this component 
is associated with.
Referenced table: ObjectiveFunction
Reference Y

EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 357
OnHand table
The OnHand table stores the amount of inventory and other details for a part in a particular 
location within a warehouse. When on-hand inventory is allocated to demands for pegging, high 
priority demands are protected. However, if there is no priority, then on hand for a part is 
allocated by the following fields in this table:
 l ExpiryDate (earliest first)
 l Date (earliest first)
 l UnitCost (least cost first)
 l Quantity (smallest first)
 l Location.Id and Location.Warehouse
Field name Description Data type Key
BaseKey
 
Can optionally be used as a key field to uniquely 
identify each OnHand record.
Note: In some legacy versions, this was the only 
field that could be used to define uniqueness on 
OnHand records.  However, other standard or 
custom fields can now optionally be used as 
keys. For more information, see Key fields.
String  
Date Indicates the date at which inventory was 
received. 
This field can affect when given on hand 
inventory is allocated (as described above), and 
is also used in determining the appropriate 
currency conversion rate (if any) to apply in cost 
calculations.
Default value: Past 
Date  
DispositionCode An inventory code used to determine how the 
part is handled in an excess or obsolete 
situation. 
Note: This field is in the Solutions namespace.
String  
Table 6.59: OnHand (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 368 =====
358 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
ExpiryDate The date on which this inventory expires (is no 
longer usable).
Default value: Undefined
Date  
ExpiryStartDate
 
The date when this on-hand inventory begins to 
expire. The date provided here is rolled up the 
product structure through parts that use a 
dependent expiry type.
For assembly parts with a PartType.ExpiryRule 
setting of DependentStart, the earliest rolled 
up ExpiryStartDate from a component supply is 
then used to start expiry date calculations for 
the assembly supply. Thus, this field should 
generally be populated on supply of 
components that use an 
ExpiryType.ProcessingRule of 
LimitingComponent. 
Date  
Quantity The amount in stock.
Default value: 0
Quantity  
StartUnit The unit number corresponding to the first item 
in this inventory. The quantity should be one if 
Part.MUEPoolNettingType.UnitRule is Net 
(the entire quantity is treated with the same 
StartUnit number).
Default value: -1
Note: This field supports the Model-Effectivity, 
and Unit-Effectivity algorithms, and is ignored if 
Model-Unit Effectivity is not enabled.
Integer  
UnitCost Unit cost for parts in this inventory store. If 
UnitCost is zero, then inventory is valued at 
Part.StdUnitCost. Negative values are treated 
as zero. 
Default value: 0
Money

The OperationSpecification table and its associated tables define a set of rules and preferences 
to schedule route operations. These rules and preferences can be shared across multiple route 
operations.

OperationSpecificationSegmentOverride table
The OperationSpecificationSegmentOverride table contains the duration and rate overrides 
applied to operations that are associated with an operation specification. The overrides are 
applied irrespective of which resource an operation is scheduled on and are only used if no 

===== PAGE 373 =====

overrides have been specified in an operation specification resource segment override record for 
the operation.

Table 6.64: OperationSpecificationSegmentOverride  (Scheduling) fields
OperationSpecificationToResource table
The OperationSpecificationToResource table defines an eligible resource that operations  
associated with a specific operation specification can run on. If the operation specification is not 
assigned to a specific resource, then the operation can be scheduled on any resource in the 
resource group associated with that operation specification. When an operation specification has 
multiple eligible resources, the preference and wait duration values are used to determine which 
resource will be used to schedule the operation.

===== PAGE 374 =====
364 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
OperationSpecification The operation specification associated 
with this OperationSpecification
ToResource record.
Referenced table: 
OperationSpecification
Reference Y
Resource The resource associated with this 
OperationSpecification
ToResource record.
Referenced table: Resource (Scheduling)
Reference Y
MaximumWaitDuration
ForPreference
The maximum amount of time that the 
Scheduling algorithm will wait for an 
operation to be scheduled on the highest 
preferences resource. After this period, if 
the operation hasn't been scheduled it 
will be scheduled on a lower preference 
resource.
This time is taken from the available time 
for the operation. Measured in days.
Quantity  
Preference The preference order assigned to eligible 
resources associated with the operation 
specification.
Integer  
Table 6.65: OperationSpecificationToResource  (Scheduling) fields
OperationSpecificationToResource table:  calculated and set 
fields
The following describes the calculated and set fields in the OperationSpecificationToResource 
table. For an introduction to this table, and descriptions of its input fields, see 
OperationSpecificationToResource table.

===== PAGE 375 =====

associated with this batch type resource.

The OperationStagedChange table used to consolidate and commit updates for scheduled 
receipt operations and maintenance operations.

item.

Table 6.67: OperationStagedChange (Scheduling) fields
Part table
The Part table identifies a unique part and site. A record in this table includes data such as: 
name, description, price, and cost data. 

===== PAGE 377 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 367
Field name Description Data type Key
Name Part number or item number. String Yes
Site The site associated with this part. This field is a 
reference to the Value field on the Site table.
Referenced table: Site
Note: A value is required in this field for both 
Single-Site and Multi-Site installations. 
Reference Yes
AfterForecast
Interval
Number of calendar intervals after an actual 
demand date in which that demand can 
consume forecast (exclusive). 
The value specified here is taken in terms of 
either PlanningCalendars.ForecastCalendar 
or PlanningCalendars.SecondCalendar 
intervals as set by the control setting in the 
PartType.ForecastConsumptionCalendar 
field (ForecastCalendar is the default)
For example, 1 means forecast can only be 
consumed up to the end of the current forecast 
or second calendar interval, 2 means forecast 
can be consumed up to the end of the next 
forecast or second calendar interval, and so on.
For more information, see Forecast 
consumption.
Default value: 0
Quantity  
Table 6.68: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 378 =====
368 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
Allocation
Multiple
If the part is used in fair-share or equal-share 
allocation, the allocated quantities will be 
rounded to the nearest multiple of this number. 
For example, if AllocationMultiple = 1.0, the 
quantity allocated for the part will always be 
rounded to the nearest whole number, such as 
2.0 or 5.0
If this value is less than or equal to 0.0, the 
allocated quantity is not rounded. The last 
allocation of a fractional supply or an allocation 
to a fractional demand is also not rounded.
Default value: 1.0
Quantity  
AttachToConstraint Indicates if the storage constraint is attached to 
a Part record. A PartConstraint record is 
generated for every Part and Constraint 
relationship. This field is used by Supply chain 
optimization.
Note: This field is in the Solutions namespace.
NEW  This field was added in Service Update 
2501.
Default value:N
Boolean  
AverageQty Average order quantity used when the value of 
the SafetyStockPolicy.SafetyStockRule field 
is OrderPointAverage, OrderPointOver, or 
OrderPointUnder. 
If SafetyStockPolicy.SafetyStockRule is 
OrderPointAverage, this value is the amount 
ordered to replenish inventory.
Default value: 0
Quantity  
AverageSelling
Price
The average selling price for this part. This can 
be zero if the part will never have independent 
demand (for example, not sold).
Default value: 0
Money  
Table 6.68: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 379 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 369
Field name Description Data type Key
BeforeForecast
Interval
Number of calendar intervals before an actual 
demand date in which that demand can 
consume forecast (inclusive). 
The value specified here is taken in terms of 
either PlanningCalendars.ForecastCalendar 
or PlanningCalendars.SecondCalendar 
intervals as set by the control setting in the 
PartType.ForecastConsumptionCalendar 
field (ForecastCalendar is the default)
For example, a value of 0 means forecast can 
be consumed starting from the beginning of 
the current forecast or second calendar interval 
containing the demand date, 1 means forecast 
can be consumed from the beginning of the 
previous forecast or second calendar interval, 2 
means forecast can be consumed from the 
beginning of the second forecast or second 
calendar interval before the actual demand, 
and so on.
For more information, see Forecast 
consumption.
Default value: 0
Quantity  
CarryingCost The cost of holding inventory.
Default value: 0
Money  
Table 6.68: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 380 =====
370 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
ConstraintShare
Fence
Within each OrderPriority level, the number of 
ConstraintAllocationCalendar intervals out 
from RunDate in which to include this part's 
demands for use in the fair sharing of limited 
constraint availability amongst parts whose 
sources use common constraints. For example, 
if set to 0, then only demands due before 
RunDate (past due) would be eligible for 
constraint sharing.
The window defined by this field is only 
applicable if the parts' 
Type.ConstraintShareRule setting is 
WithinFence and only for demands where the 
OrderPriority.ConstraintAllocationRule is 
FairShare.
Default value: - 1 (this indicates that 
constraint sharing logic, if enabled, applies 
throughout the part's entire planning horizon).
Integer  
Table 6.68: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 381 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 371
Field name Description Data type Key
DemandTime
Fence
Number of units of time after the 
Part.PlanningCalendar.RunDate.FirstDate to 
establish a fence for controlling the use of 
unconsumed forecast demands. Within the 
demand time fence, any unconsumed forecast 
is ignored and not planned for by Netting 
(does not contribute to the total demand 
picture). However, any unconsumed forecast 
outside of the time fence does contribute to 
total demand and will be planned for by 
Netting.
Values in this field should be expressed in 
Part.PlanningCalendar.TimeFenceCalendar 
intervals. If this is set to 0 (zero), then the 
demand time fence is placed at the RunDate 
and all unconsumed forecast drives demand (in 
cases where the RunDate does not fall on a 
TimeFenceCalendar marker, it set to the 
previous marker in the calendar). 
Note also that the demand time fence applies 
only to SalesForecast, and not to 
ProductionForecast.
Default value: 0
Quantity  
Description A description of the part.
Default value: blank string
String  
Table 6.68: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 382 =====
372 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
ExcessFence
 
The number of PlanningCalendars.TimeUnit 
intervals out from RunDate that defines the 
window for including a substitute part's 
demands and supplies when determining 
excess. This is applicable both to parts used as 
global substitutes (if their Type.ExcessRule is 
GlobalFence), as well as parts used as BOM-
level substitutes (if their Type.ExcessRule is 
Fence).
Within the excess window set by this field, 
existing supply (typically, on hand and 
scheduled receipts) of a substitute component 
is first used to satisfy any of its own demand. If 
any supply remains afterward, then that 
"excess" can be used to satisfy demand from 
the parts or components for which it is 
substitutable. Outside of this window, supply of 
a substitute part or component is used to 
satisfy demand on a first-come, first-served 
basis regardless of whether the demand is its 
own or that of another part or component for 
which it is substitutable.
Note that setting this field to a negative value 
(for example, -1) extends the excess window to 
the end of the planning horizon. This ensures 
that supplies of a substitute part or component 
are only ever eligible for use in substitution if 
they are deemed to be excess. Note also 
setting this field to 0 (zero) means that only 
demands and supplies due on 
PlanningCalendars.RunDate.FirstDate are 
used in determining excess.
The end of the excess window is reported in the 
ExcessFenceDate field. 
Default value: 0
Integer  
Table 6.68: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 383 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 373
Field name Description Data type Key
IntermediateSpread
ForecastInterval
The number of forecast intervals over which 
intermediate forecast spreading applies. This 
value should be expressed in terms of the part's 
ForecastCalendarand defines the number of 
those  intervals over which forecast is spread 
onto dates belonging to the 
IntermediateSpreadCalendar. 
Note that the value specified in this field is 
applied subsequent to the 
SpreadForecastInterval value (which itself is 
applied from RunDate). For example, assuming  
monthly intervals, forecast might be spread 
using a daily SecondCalendar for the first 2 
months and then using a weekly 
IntermediateSpreadCalendar for the next 4 
months. 
For more information, see Spreading to the 
intermediate calendar.
Default value: 0 (if set to 0 or set to a negative 
value, intermediate spreading is not used).
Integer  
Table 6.68: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 384 =====
374 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
Inventory
HoldingRate
The inventory holding rate for this part 
(applicable to parts used in multi-echelon 
safety stock calculations). Together with the 
setting in the 
MultiEchelonSafetyStockRule.CostRule field, 
this is used to calculate the unit holding cost 
for the part. 
Unit holding costs are required as input by 
multi-echelon calculations that recommend 
strategic safety stock placement based on 
minimizing total inventory holding costs across 
a network of parts.
This value should be expressed as a fractional 
percentage (typically, a value between 0 - 1). 
For example, if CostRule is set to 
StdUnitCost, StdUnitCost is 200, and this 
field is .2, then the unit holding cost for the 
part is $40 (20% of 200).
Note that all negative values are treated the 
same as 0 (zero) and indicates there is no cost 
to holding the part in inventory. Also, values 
greater than 1 are allowed and imply the cost 
of holding the part in inventory is greater than 
the cost of the part itself. 
Default value: .2
Quantity  
LeadTimeAdjust An additional term used to adjust (typically, 
increase) the total lead time. 
Typically, this field is not used and left at zero. 
However, when dealing with certain enterprise 
systems where a fixed lead time of zero means 
there is actually a one-day difference between 
Due and StartDate, this field can be set to 1 for 
all affected parts. 
Note that the value in this field is only applied if 
PartType.UseLeadTimeAdjust is set to Y. 
Default Value: 0
Quantity  
Table 6.68: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 385 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 375
Field name Description Data type Key
MaterialCost The cost of material associated in producing 
the part. 
Default value: 0
Note: This field is obsolete.
Money  
MinimumShelf
Life
This field specifies the number of 
PlanningCalendars.ExpiryCalendar intervals 
that are added to either an independent 
demand's or consensus forecast's DueDate 
when calculating its EarliestExpiryDate. The 
EarliestExpiryDate is the date before which 
supplies must not expire if they are to be 
allotted to the demand. 
For example, if 
PlanningCalendars.ExpiryCalendar is set to 
Month and this value is set to 3, then an 
independent demand for this part with a 
DueDate of July 1, 2009 would have its 
EarliestExpiryDate set to October 1, 2009 (that 
is, any supply expiring before October 1, 2009 
could not be used to satisfy the demand).
For independent demands, this can be 
overridden on a demand basis by providing a 
value in the 
IndependentDemand.MinimumShelfLife. For 
consensus forecast demands, this can be 
overridden on a part customer basis by 
providing a value in the MinimumShelfLife 
field on either of the 
PartCustomerTimePhasedAttributes or 
PartCustomer tables.
Default value: 0
Note: If a value of 0 is specified, this indicates 
that the EarliestExpiryDate should be set to the 
demand due date.
Integer  
Table 6.68: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 386 =====
376 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
MinimumSpread
Quantity
Specifies a minimum quantity for forecast 
records generated from spreading. For 
example, a value of 1 might be used to ensure 
forecast is not spread below a single unit. 
If forecast spreading logic would otherwise 
produce a forecast value lower than the 
specified minimum, that forecast is collected  
with others in the spreading calendar interval 
until the minimum is reached. The 
SpreadProfile.SpreadLoadOption control 
setting can be used to further specify whether 
any accumulated forecast is planned towards 
the beginning or end of a given 
SecondCalendar or 
IntermediateSpreadCalendar interval. 
For more information, see Minimum spread 
quantity.
Default value: 0 
Quantity  
Table 6.68: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 387 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 377
Field name Description Data type Key
NextUnit In conjunction with 
Part.MUEPoolNettingType.UnitRule field, 
this field controls how StartUnit is to be 
propagated from this part. It also determines 
the unit number to use as the StartUnit for 
planned orders and forecast to be exploded 
from this part. The following rules apply: 
 l If NextUnit is < 0 and the part is not 
netting by unit, then that value is copied 
into StartUnit for all planned supplies. 
 l If UnitRule = Block, the StartUnit of the 
planned supply is set to the larger of 
NextUnit or the StartUnit field on the 
demand order.
 l If UnitRule = Increment, and NextUnit 
>= 0, the next unit will be used to create the 
StartUnit of the planned order. Note that 
NextUnit is ignored for phantom parts. 
 l If UnitRule = Fixed, every planned supply 
StartUnit will be set to NextUnit. 
 l If UnitRule = Net, and Model-Unit 
Effectivity is enabled, the unit on planned 
orders is copied from the demand record. 
Otherwise, StartUnit is set to -1.
Default value: -1
Integer  
Table 6.68: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 388 =====
378 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
NumberOf
DaysSupply
 l If PartType.UseDaysSupply is set to Y and 
the DaysSupplyPolicy reference is Null, 
then when creating a new planned order 
this field defines the period to cover 
requirements for as follows:
 l If PartType.DaysSupplyRule = 
FromDemand or FromSupply, indicates the 
number of units (as defined by 
Part.PlanningCalendars.TimeUnits) to 
cover requirements for.
 l If PartType.DaysSupplyRule = ByPeriod 
or ByPeriodWithPriority, indicates the 
number of calendar intervals (as defined by 
Part.PlanningCalendars.PeriodSupplyInt
erval) to cover requirements for.
The quantity of the order is reduced if the 
resulting order exceeds 
PartSource.MaximumQty.
Default value: 0 
Notes: 
 l A value of 0 or less in this field is treated as 
if it were set to 1; that is, supply is planned 
to satisfy one day/interval of demand.
 l If the DaysSupplyPolicy reference is 
populated, then the reference record in the 
DaysSupplyPolicy table determine days of 
supply settings, and this field is ignored. For 
more information, see Days of Supply 
calculations.
Quantity  
PercentSafety
IntervalCount
This value corresponds to the 
SafetyStockPolicy.PercentSafetyInterval 
field. If this value is less than one, then one is 
used.
Integer  
Table 6.68: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 389 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 379
Field name Description Data type Key
PercentSafety
Percent
This is used in the PercentOfDemand (and 
FractionOfDemand) safety calculation. The 
safety quantity for a period is determined by 
multiplying the total gross demand in that 
period by this percentage value (which may be 
a fraction or a percent, based on the 
SafetyStockPolicy.SafetyStockQuantityRule 
field). If this value is less than 0, then 0 is 
assumed.
Quantity  
PickPackTime The amount of time required to pull an order 
for this part from inventory and prepare it for 
shipment to the customer. 
This value is used in calculating planned ship 
and receipt dates, as well as available ship and 
receipt dates, on IndependentDemand 
records. 
Note that if this field is set to a negative value, 
the DefaultPickPackTime field on a given 
DeliveryRoute record is used instead. 
Integer  
Table 6.68: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 390 =====
380 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
PrimarySubstitution
Sequence
For parts configured as the primary part in a 
global part substitution relationship, this field 
can be used to define a relative preference 
between input supplies of that primary part 
and its substitutes. 
In global substitution logic, this value is 
compared against the 
AlternatePart.Sequence value(s) associated 
with the part's substitute(s). Lower values 
indicate a higher substitution preference. That 
is, if this value is less than or equal to the 
AlternatePart.Sequence value for a substitute, 
then this part's input supplies are preferred. 
Otherwise, if the AlternatePart.Sequence 
value for a given substitute is less than this 
value, then input supplies of the substitute are 
used up first (and planned orders will never be 
generated on the substitute to satisfy this part's 
demands).
Default value: 0
Note: The specific usage of this field further 
depends on the primary part's 
PartType.SubstitutionPreference setting. For 
more information, see Define when part 
substitutes are used.
Integer  
ProductGroup1 This field can be used to describe a product 
grouping with which a part is associated. For 
example, this could be a product family.
Default value: blank string
String  
ProductGroup2 This field can be used to describe a different 
product grouping than ProductGroup1. For 
example, this could be a product line.
Default value: blank string
String  
Table 6.68: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 391 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 381
Field name Description Data type Key
RangeOf
CoverageBuffer
This field specifies a quantity to add to 
calculated range of coverage safety stock levels 
to account for some amount of expected 
demand variability. It is also used along with 
the value in the 
RangeOfCoverageThresholdFactor field to 
determine if the new calculated safety stock 
level should replace the value from the 
previous range of coverage interval.
Applicable only to those parts where 
SafetyStockPolicy.SafetyStockQuantityRule 
is set to either RangeOfCoverage, 
RangeOfCoverageLeadTime or 
TimePhasedRangeOfCoverage.
Default value: 0
Quantity  
Table 6.68: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 392 =====
382 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
SafetyLeadTime Number of days to offset the demand due date 
when determining when supply should be 
planned to cover that demand. This is intended 
to protect against fluctuations in supply and 
demand and will impact planned order due 
dates as well as EffLeadTime and 
CumLeadTime calculations. This value should 
be expressed in either the part's 
PlanningCalendars.TimeUnits calendar or the 
Everyday/Gregorian calendar as determined by 
the part's 
PartType.SafetyLeadTimeCalendarRule 
setting. 
Note that a value provided in this field is only 
used when the SafetyLeadTimeProfile 
reference is Null. Usage of this field also 
depends on the setting in the 
PartType.UseSafetyLeadTime field, with 
additional PartType settings available to 
specify whether safety lead time should be 
used for specific types of demands. Also, if 
safety lead time should be applied to demand 
request dates and/or only used in cases where 
doing so won't make other demands worse off, 
then use of settings on the 
OrderPriority.TwoDatePlanningRule field 
should be considered.
Default value: 0
Notes: 
 l SafetyLeadTime is not included in 
NeedDate calculations.
 l SafetyLeadTime is ignored and not used to 
offset due dates for safety stock demands.
Quantity  
SafetyStockQty A quantity of stock in inventory to protect 
against fluctuations in demand or supply.
Default value: 0
Quantity  
Table 6.68: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 393 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 383
Field name Description Data type Key
SpreadForecast
Interval
The number of forecast calendar intervals over 
which forecast spreading logic is applied to 
those forecast demands where the 
DemandType.SpreadRule value is Spread. 
Between the RunDate and the number of 
ForecastCalendar intervals specified in this 
field, forecast demand is spread from the 
forecast calendar into intervals on the 
SecondCalendar. 
Optionally, if a subsequent intermediate 
spreading calendar is being used, a value 
should also be set in the 
IntermediateSpreadForecastInterval field. 
For example, spreading might be made against 
a daily calendar (defined by the 
SecondCalendar) early in the horizon before 
changing to use a weekly calendar (defined by 
the IntermediateSpreadCalendar) later in the 
horizon.
In cases where intermediate spreading is not 
used, a zero (0) or negative value in this field 
can be used to specify that the  
SecondCalendar should used for forecast 
spreading throughout the entire planning 
horizon. 
Default value: 0
Note: For more information, see Spreading to 
the second calendar.
Integer  
StdUnitCost The cost to produce this part. Includes all 
material, labor, and overhead costs.
Default value: 0
Money  
Table 6.68: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 394 =====
384 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
Substitution
Tolerance
When faced with demand for the primary part 
in a substitute part relationship, this is the 
number of 
PlanningCalendars.SubstitutionToleranceCa
lendar intervals out from the demand date to 
look for existing supply of one part before 
supply from other parts is considered. 
For example, if the primary part's 
PrimarySubstitutionSequence value is less 
than or equal to the AlternatePart.Sequence 
for a given substitute, this defines the number 
of SubstitutionToleranceCalendar intervals 
out from the demand date to look for existing 
input supply of the primary part before 
considering supply from the substitute. 
However, if the primary part's 
PrimarySubstitutionSequence value is strictly 
greater than the AlternatePart.Sequence for a 
given substitute, then this is instead the 
number of SubstitutionToleranceCalendar 
intervals out from the primary part's demand 
date to look for (use up) existing supply of the 
substitute before supply from the primary part 
is considered.
Default value: 0
Integer  
Table 6.68: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 395 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 385
Field name Description Data type Key
SupplyShare
Fence
Within each OrderPriority level, the number of 
AllocationCalendar intervals out from the 
RunDate in which this part's demands are 
eligible for the fair or equal sharing of limited 
supplies. For example, if set to 0, then only 
demands due before RunDate (past due) 
would be eligible for supply sharing.
The window defined by this field is only 
applicable if the parts' Type.SupplyShareRule 
setting is WithinFence, and only for demands 
where the OrderPriority.AllocationRule is set 
to either FairShare or EqualShare.
Default value: - 1 (this indicates that supply 
sharing logic, if enabled, applies throughout 
the part's entire planning horizon).
Integer  
Table 6.68: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 396 =====
386 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
UnsatisfiedDemand
Tolerance
Specifies the part's tolerance level for late or 
unsatisfied demands. This field should be 
expressed in 
PlanningCalendars.ForecastCalendar 
intervals and is used in both partial planning 
(Netting) and on-time reallocation logic (CTP).
If using partial planning logic 
(DemandType.PlanningRule is set to 
Partial), demands or demand portions that 
cannot be planned within the lateness 
tolerance  are left unsatisfied and not planned. 
In other words, this field indicates the number 
of forecast intervals after it's due date upon 
which a demand should be given up on if 
supply cannot be planned for it. 
If using on-time reallocation logic 
(PartType.OnTimeAllocationRule is set to 
ImproveWithTolerance), supply quantities 
that are allocated to  demands late and beyond 
tolerance can be reallocated. The goal of the 
reallocation process is improving the on-time 
quantity on late demands. But these 
reallocations also have the potential to make 
the available date on some late demands worse 
(later) than they otherwise would be.  Thus, this 
field indicates the number of forecast intervals 
past its due date at which a late demand's 
overall available date is less important than 
improving the on-time quantity of late 
demands.
Default value: 0 (indicates that the part's 
lateness tolerance is at the end of the current 
forecast calendar interval; for example, if the 
forecast calendar was set to Everyday this 
would imply any late demand is considered 
beyond tolerance).
Integer  
Table 6.68: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 397 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 387
Field name Description Data type Key
ExcludeFromSchedulingIdentifies if production orders that use this part 
are excluded from being scheduled.
Default value: N
Note: This field is in the Scheduling 
namespace.
Boolean  
WorkItemQuantity The number of work items for this part.
Note: This field is in the Scheduling 
namespace.
Quantity  
ABCCode Classification based on Activity Based Costing 
(ABC) analysis. ABC is simply a classification of 
parts, usually by the extended cost of annual 
usage (quantity used * unit cost).
Referenced table: ABCCode
Reference  
AvailableRule Control whether normal capable-to-promise 
rules are used when calculating availability for 
the part. 
If this field is left null, then the part's available 
rule setting comes from 
PartType.AvailableRule.
Referenced table: AvailableRule (Nullable)
Reference  
BuyerCode A text code that identifies the buyer for this 
part. 
Referenced table:BuyerCode
Reference Syntax: BuyerCode.Value
Reference  
Table 6.68: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 398 =====
388 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
DaysSupply
Policy
A reference to the days of supply policy for this 
part. This policy defines time-phased (short-
term and long-term) parameters used in days 
of supply calculations. For example, the number 
of days or periods of accumulated demand that 
planned orders should satisfy in both the short-
term and long-term horizon.
Referenced table: DaysSupplyPolicy (Nullable)
Note:  If Null and PartType.UseDaysSupply is 
set to Y, then days of supply logic can still be 
used based on legacy fields in the Part, 
PartType, and PlanningCalendar tables. 
However, those fields only support a single set 
of days of supply settings applicable 
throughout the horizon (are not time-phased). 
For more information about days of supply, see 
Days of Supply calculations.
Reference  
Distribution
PlanningRule
Controls whether Distribution Planning within 
CTP calculations can be used for the part. If the 
Distribution Planning rule is enabled, when a 
part exceeds the maximum inventory level at 
the sourcing site, this triggers a request for the 
finished goods to be transferred to a 
destination site before it is required.
Referenced table:DistributionPlanningRule 
(Nullable)
Reference  
ExpiryType
 
Contains rules affecting the use of components 
in expiry logic under assemblies that use 
certain dependent expiry rules. 
Referenced table: ExpiryType (Nullable)
Reference  
IncrementalRule Controls whether incremental availability 
calculations can be performed for orders for 
this part.
Referenced table: IncrementalRule
Reference  
Table 6.68: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 399 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 389
Field name Description Data type Key
MUEPool
NettingType
The MUEPoolNetting type for this part. The 
MUEPoolNettingType value controls the 
model, pool and unit netting for a part.
Referenced table:MUEPoolNettingType
Reference  
MultiEchelon
SafetyStockRule
A value associated with rules that set how this 
part is used in multi-echelon safety stock 
calculations.
For example, it controls whether a given part in 
a multi-echelon family is eligible to hold safety 
stock as inventory.
Referenced table: 
MultiEchelonSafetyStockRule (Nullable)
Reference  
MultiLevel
SearchRule
A reference to a control table value that is used 
to enable or disable multi-level search logic, 
and contains related options determining how 
multi-level search logic is performed when 
enabled. 
Multi-level search logic can be used to modify 
standard part sourcing and substitution logic to 
consider component availabilities when making 
sourcing decisions, and also modifies standard 
supply allotment logic to consider sibling 
component allotments when allocating a 
component part's supplies to the demands that 
require it. For more information, see Multi-level 
search logic.
Referenced table: MultiLevelSearchRule 
(Nullable)
Note: If this reference is left Null, then multi-
level search logic is always disabled for the part.
Reference  
Table 6.68: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 400 =====
390 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
ObjectiveConfigurationA reference to the 
SPOEObjectiveConfiguration record that's 
used to configure optimization objectives for 
this part. If an objective configuration value is 
specified for a part, it will override the 
equivalent 
OptimizationConfiguration.ObjectiveConfig
uration value associated with the family. For 
example, if the Lateness objective is set for 
both the part and family, the Lateness value set 
for the part will be used instead.
 
Referenced table: 
SPOEObjectiveConfiguration
Note: This field  supports Supply chain 
optimization. 
NEW  This field was added in Service Update 
2403.
Reference  
Optimization
Configuration
This field defines the configuration settings to 
use for the owner part and its family members 
in the supply planning optimization process. 
The head part and all family member parts 
meant to be included in the optimization 
should reference the same 
OptmizationConfiguration record. 
Also note that the calculated 
OptimizationFamily reference can be used to 
identify all parts that belong to the same 
optimization family. 
Referenced table: OptimizationConfiguration
(Nullable)
Note: This field can be set using the 
Optimization Family Details worksheet in the 
SPOE Manager workbook. 
Reference  
Table 6.68: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 401 =====

Field name Description Data type Key
PartClass The name of the part class, if any, associated 
with this part. 
For example, a part class might be finished 
goods, work in progress, or raw materials. 
These classes are used by certain application to 
assign planning, safety stock, and 
replenishment strategies.
Referenced table: Class (Nullable)
Note: This field is in the Solutions namespace.
Reference  
PlannerCode A text code that identifies the planner 
responsible for this part. 
Referenced table:PlannerCode
Reference Syntax: PlannerCode.Value
Reference

Calendars
A value associated with processing rules that 
define the Part's Calendar behavior. 
Referenced table: PlanningCalendars
Reference Syntax: PlanningCalendars.Value
Reference  
ProductFamily A reference to the product family (if any) that 
the part belongs to. Product families can be 
used to enable aggregate level reporting for 
groups of related or similar parts.
Referenced table: ProductFamily (Nullable)

Table 6.68: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 402 =====
392 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
ReferencePart
 
Depending on how your company is using the 
reference part functionality, this column 
displays:
 l The name of this part as known throughout 
your company (a common part number or 
equivalent part).
 l The part name according to the naming 
conventions used in a different division.
 l A name used to group a product line the 
part belongs to.
 l The same value as the Name field.
 l Blank if this field is not populated in your 
implementation of Maestro. 
Referenced table: ReferencePart
Reference  
SafetyLeadTime
Profile
A reference to a safety lead time profile that 
defines a series of time-phased safety lead time 
values for the part. If this reference if Null, then 
the value in the SafetyLeadTime field defines a 
single safety lead time value that is always 
effective for the part.
Note that usage of this field depends on the 
setting in the PartType.UseSafetyLeadTime 
field, with additional PartType settings 
available to specify whether safety lead time 
should be used for specific types of demands. 
Also, if safety lead time should be applied to 
demand request dates and/or only used in 
cases where doing so won't make other 
demands worse off, then use of settings on the 
OrderPriority.TwoDatePlanningRule field 
should be considered.
Referenced table: SafetyLeadTimeProfile 
(Nullable)
Reference  
Table 6.68: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 403 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 393
Field name Description Data type Key
SafetyStock
Policy
A reference to the safety stock policies 
applicable to this part. For example, how are 
safety stock levels determined and under what 
conditions are those levels maintained.
If this reference is left Null, then various fields 
on the PartType and PlanningCalendars 
tables (identical to those on 
SafetyStockPolicy) are used instead.
Referenced table: SafetyStockPolicy (Nullable)
Reference  
SourceRule If there are multiple active part sources defined 
for a part, this reference defines rules outlining 
how Netting chooses the source to use when 
satisfying a planned requirement.
Referenced table:SourceRule 
Reference  
SPOEConstraintConfigura
tion
A reference to the 
SPOEConstraintConfiguration record that's 
used to configure model constraints for this 
part. The purpose of model constraints is to try 
to improve solution performance. If a 
constraint configuration value is specified for a 
part, it will override the equivalent 
OptimizationConfiguration.ConstraintConfi
guration value associated with the family.
Reference: SPOEConstraintConfiguration
NEW  This field was added in Service Update 
2510.
Reference  
Type A value that is associated with processing rules 
that defines the part's behavior. This field 
references the PartType table. For more 
information about this table, see PartType 
table.
 Referenced table:PartType
Reference

Table 6.68: Part (Mfg) fields
Part table:  calculated and set fields
The following describes the calculated and set fields in the Part table. For an introduction to this 
table, and descriptions of its input fields, see Part table.
Note: When pool-based netting flow-to-common logic is enabled, those calculated 
fields that report a sum of something (for example, TotalDemand or TotalSupply) are 
summed across all pools whereas those fields that report the first of something (for 
example, FirstShortageDate or FirstNeededSupplyDate) return the first value across 
and from just one of those pools. 

===== PAGE 405 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 395
Field name Description Data type Key
AlternatePrimary
Part
The primary part this part can be used 
interchangeably with.
Referenced table: Part
Reference  
AverageFirst
YearInProcess
Calculated to show work in process (WIP) for 
supplies starting earlier than RunDate + 365 
days.
Calculated as 1/365 * the sum over scheduled 
receipts and planned orders based on 
SupplyType.Source.
If SupplyType.Source is:
 l Buy: If SupplyType.BuyInProcessRule is 
set to IncludeDockToStock, this field is 
calculated as StockUnitQuantity * 
(ReschedDate - ReschedDockDate). 
Otherwise, it is set to zero.
 l Make: This field is calculated as: 
StockUnitQuantity * (ReschedDate - 
CalcStartDate).
 l Transfer: This field is calculated as: 
StockUnitQuantity * (ReschedDate - 
(ShipDate - PreShipLT using source time 
units)).
Quantity  
AverageFirst
YearInventory
Average inventory level at the end of the day 
over the first 365 days. Useful for inventory 
turns calculations.
Quantity  
Constrained
Demand
Total demand to be satisfied up to RunDate 
+ 365 days. Uses AvailableDate for 
independent demands and 
SynchronizedDate for dependent 
requirements.
Quantity  
Table 6.69: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 406 =====
396 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
Constrained
ExternalDemand
The total units of external demand with 
AvailableDate before RunDate + 365. 
(excludes intersite demand).
Quantity  
ConstrainedIn
Process
Projects the work in process (units of supply) 
between the time an order is started (shipped 
for transfer) and received into inventory. This 
calculation is based on supplies with 
AvailableStartDate earlier than RunDate + 
365 days, and is influenced by the value of 
SupplyType.Source as shown in the 
following list:
 l Buy: If SupplyType.BuyInProcessRule is 
set to IncludeDockToStock, this field is 
calculated as StockUnitQuantity * 
(AvailableDate - AvailableDockDate). 
Otherwise, set to zero.
 l Make: This field is calculated as 
StockUnitQuantity * (AvailableDate - 
AvailableStartDate).
 l Transfer: This field is calculated as 
StockUnitQuantity * (AvailableDate - 
(AvailableShipDate - PreShipLT using 
source time units)).
Quantity  
Constrained
IntersiteDemand
The total demand which acts like 
independent demand from another site with 
AvailableDate before RunDate + 365 days.
Quantity  
Constrained
Inventory
Projects average daily inventory (unit) 
balance based on supply AvailableDate and 
demand SynchronizedDate (greater of 
EffDueDate and AvailableDate for 
independent demands).
Quantity  
Table 6.69: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 407 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 397
Field name Description Data type Key
CumLeadTime The time from ordering all the components, 
to the lowest level of the structure, until the 
current part can be used to satisfy demand. 
This calculation is done only within a part's 
site. For multi-site cum lead time calculations 
(that is, calculations that consider transfer 
part sources), use the 
MultiSiteCumLeadTime field.
For more information about lead time 
calculations, see Lead time calculations.
Notes: 
 l Any lead time associated with phantom 
assemblies will not be included in the 
calculation of this value.
 l If using time-phased lead time, then the 
lead times effective for the primary part 
source at the latter of the part's RunDate 
or PartSource.FirstEffectiveDate are 
used in this calculation.
Quantity  
DaysSupply
FenceDate
The date fence that indicates the transition 
point between short-term and long-term 
days of supply settings. That is, before this 
date the short-term days of supply settings 
are used, and on or after this date the long-
term days of supply settings are used.
This field is only populated if the part has a 
non-Null reference in the DaysSupplyPolicy 
field. 
Date  
Table 6.69: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 408 =====
398 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
DecoupledLeadTime This calculated field contains the multi-site 
cumulative lead time between buffer points.  
The calculation will follow the same logic as 
multi-site cumulative lead time, but the 
calculation will stop at any decouple points. 
Note:  Only primary part source is 
considered for this calculation.
DecoupledLeadTime calculation for make 
parts: 
EffectiveLeadTime + Max ( NON-Decoupling-
Point component's DLT)
 
DecoupledLeadTime calculation if transfer 
part is DDMRP part:
 EffectiveTransferLeadTime
 
DecoupledLeadTime calculation if transfer 
part is regular part: 
EffectiveTransferLeadTime + TransferPart's 
DLT
Integer  
DTFDate The date of the demand time fence.
 Calculated from RunDate by adding the 
number of intervals specified in 
DemandTimeFence (based on the 
PlanningCalendars.TimeFenceCalendar). If 
set to 0, then this demand time fence is 
placed at RunDate (or if the RunDate does 
not fall on a TimeFenceCalendar marker, 
than it set to the previous marker).
Date  
Table 6.69: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 409 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 399
Field name Description Data type Key
EffectiveCommit
Level
The commit level used for this part in order 
priority logic. Typically, this returns the same 
as the value found on the part's 
PartType.CommitLevel reference. 
However, certain algorithms don't support all  
CommitLevel settings and will therefore 
force a lower effective level than the one set 
on the PartType record. For example, when 
using order generation threshold logic, the 
CommitLevel of High is treated as 
MediumExplodePriority instead. 
Similarly, if a part days of supply logic, this 
field always returns a value of Low. 
Valid values are:
 l High
 l Low
 l Medium
 l MediumExplodePriority
String
(Enum)
 
EffectiveOrder
PointDateRule
Indicates the OrderPointDateRule setting 
that is effective for this part. 
Valid values are:
 l Due
 l Start
The effective value comes from the 
corresponding field on the 
SafetyStockPolicy reference (if not Null) or 
else the PartType reference. Refer to the 
field description on either of those tables for 
more information.
String
(Enum)

part.

to N or SafetyStockPolicy.SafetyStockRule

Table 6.69: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 413 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 403
Field name Description Data type Key
EndingSafety
Stock
The safety stock level in effect at the end of 
the planning horizon. This is the latest safety 
stock level calculated for this part in Maestro. 
For information about how safety stock levels 
are determined, see Safety stock calculations.
For order point parts (that is, parts where 
SafetyStockPolicy.SafetyStockRule is 
either OrderPointUnder, 
OrderPointOver, or OrderPointAverage), 
this field returns the latest order point 
threshold calculated for the part.
Quantity  
ExcessFenceDate The date that marks the end of the excess 
window applicable to either global or BOM-
level part substitution. Within this window, a 
substitute part or component's current 
supplies will be used to satisfy all of its own 
demand before that of any part it is a 
substitute for (thus, only excess supply is 
available for use in substitution). Beyond this 
date, a substitute part's current supplies will 
satisfy the demands that require them first, 
regardless of whether the demands are its 
own or come from another part.
Calculated based on the Part.ExcessFence 
and PartType.ExcessRule fields.
Date  
FirstNeeded
SupplyDate
The date when the on hand inventory of the 
part will run out, that is the day when a 
supply other than on hand is needed to 
satisfy demand by the scheduled due date.
Date  
FirstShortage
Date
The earliest date that inventory will drop 
below zero (not safety stock) if no 
rescheduling or new orders are generated.
Date  
Table 6.69: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 414 =====
404 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
FirstYearExternal
Demand
The total units of external demand due 
before RunDate + 365 (within the next year).  
Includes independent demand and 
unconsumed forecast for real parts.
Quantity  
FirstYearIntersite
Demand
The total demand which acts like 
independent demand from another site with 
DueDate before RunDate + 365.
Quantity  
IsPhantom A flag to tell if the part will blow through 
(ignore processing of) all its dependent 
demand. 1 means all dependent demand is 
bypassing, 0 means any dependent demand 
from bills that are not marked as blow 
through will be processed.
Integer  
LowLevelCode Lowest level at which this part is used as a 
component in any BOM structure. 0 indicates 
a top level part.
Integer  
MEIOFamily The part that is the head of this part's MEIO 
family.
Reference  
Table 6.69: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 415 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 405
Field name Description Data type Key
MultiSiteCum
LeadTime
The amount of time required from ordering 
all components, down to the lowest level of 
the product structure, until the part can be 
used to satisfy demand. This field uses a 
calculation similar to the CumLeadTime 
field, but it also follows product structures 
across site boundaries. That is, if a product 
structure contains a part with a primary 
supply type of "transfer", then lead time 
associated with the transfer part and its 
components is included in the value reported 
in this field.
Because this field sums together different 
lead time values without specifying the 
calendar to be used in the calculation, it is 
possible that the different lead time values 
summed when a product structure crosses 
site boundaries may not all use the same 
calendar. Therefore, depending on the 
calendars used at different sites, this field 
may not always return the expected result.
For more information about lead time, see 
Lead time calculations.
Note: If using time-phased lead time), then 
the lead times effective for the primary part 
source at the latter of the part's RunDate or 
PartSource.FirstEffectiveDate are used in 
this calculation.
Quantity  
MultiSiteLow
LevelCode
The level of the longest path from the part, 
considering all bill of material structures 
including transfer parts, to a top level part. 
Top level parts have a level of 0.
Quantity  
Table 6.69: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 416 =====
406 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
OptimizationFamily A reference to the head or owner of the 
supply planning optimization family that this 
part belongs to (if any). The 
OptimizationFamily.RecordId field can be 
used to get the unique id for the 
optimization family. 
An optimization family consists of parts that  
both reference the same 
OptimizationConfiguration record and 
which are linked together by a common or 
shared relationship. For example, parts that 
are within the same BOM structure, have 
shared materials or constraints, are defined 
as substitutes for one another, and so on. 
Referenced table: Part
Note:  Details of optimization families can be 
viewed in the Optimization Family 
Statistics and Optimization Family Details 
worksheets in the SPOE Manager workbook. 
Reference  
PartSolution A calculated reference to the PartSolution 
record pertaining to this part (if one exists). 
This field is used within the Responsibility 
column expression in several standard 
workbooks. 
Referenced table: PartSolution
Reference  
Table 6.69: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 417 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 407
Field name Description Data type Key
PrimaryPart
Source
Reference to the primary PartSource record. 
The primary record is found as the first 
PartSource record after sorting by:
 l If (
Part.PlanningCalendars.RunDate.FirstD
ate >=PartSource.FirstEffectiveDate 
AND 
Part.PlanningCalendars.RunDate.FirstD
ate <= PartSource.LastEffectiveDate) 0 
1
 l PartSource.Priority (if Ascending) or -
1*PartSource.Priority (if Descending)
 l PartSource.Target (descending)
 l PartSource.FirstEffectiveDate
 l PartSource.Source.Supplier.ID
PrimaryPartSource = <NULL> if there is no 
PartSource record for the part.
For information about part source selection, 
see Planned orders.
Referenced table: PartSource
Reference  
PrimarySupply
Type
The value of 
PrimaryPartSource.Type.PlannedOrderSu
pplyType. This is a calculated reference 
provided to simplify finding whether a part is:
 l Make
 l Buy
 l Transfer
Referenced table: SupplyType
Reference  
TotalAllocations Total quantity of allocations used in netting. Quantity  
TotalByProduct
Supply
Total quantity of supply for this part that was 
generated as a by-product residual to the 
production of a primary product. 
Quantity

Field name Description Data type Key
TotalCoProduct
Supply
Total quantity of supply for this part that was 
generated as a co-product alongside the 
production of a primary product.
Quantity  
TotalDemand Total quantity of demand processed by 
netting.
Note: Prior to Service Update 2501, any 
negative on hand quantities for the part were 
considered when calculating this field (added 
towards the total demand quantity). As of 
Service Update 2501 and later, negative on 
hand quantities no longer contribute to the 
calculation of this field. 
Quantity  
TotalEffPlanned
Order
The total of the expected quantity to receive 
into inventory of all planned orders for the 
part. This calculation handles unit of measure 
(UOM) conversion, and scrap/yield.
Quantity  
TotalEff
ScheduledReceipt
The total of the expected quantity to receive 
into inventory of all scheduled receipts for 
the part.  This calculation handles unit of 
measure (UOM) conversion, and scrap/yield.
Quantity

Demand
Total of demand that must be shipped in the 
first 365 days. Useful for inventory turns 
calculations.
Quantity

Table 6.69: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 419 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 409
Field name Description Data type Key
TotalIndependent
Demand
Total quantity of independent demand used 
in netting. This does not include forecasted 
independent demands. That is, 
IndependentDemand records where 
Order.Type.ProcessingRule is set to 
SalesForecast are excluded from this 
calculation.
Quantity  
TotalIndependent
DemandCount
The number of independent demand records 
that are reflected in the 
TotalIndependentDemand field (for 
example, records that were not ignored in 
MRP processing).
Quantity  
TotalNeeded
Supply
Total quantity from input supply (including 
on hand, inventory transfers, and scheduled 
receipts) that are needed to meet demand. 
This value is in inventory units, and is not 
shrunk in consideration of the yield factor.
This summarization is faster than doing a 
summarized query on the Supply table. 
Quantity  
TotalNettable
OnHand
Total quantity of OnHand for the part that is 
available to meet demand. This 
summarization is faster than doing a 
summarized query on the OnHand table.
Quantity  
TotalNew
Allocations
Total quantity of NewAllocations used in 
netting.
Quantity  
TotalNon
NettableOnHand
Total quantity of OnHand for the part that is 
not available for satisfying demand.
Quantity  
TotalPlanned
Allocations
Total quantity of PlannedAllocations used in 
netting.
Quantity  
Table 6.69: Part (Mfg) fields

410 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
TotalPlanned
Order
Total quantity of planned orders being 
requested. This value is in inventory units, 
and is not shrunk in consideration of the 
yield factor.
This summarization is faster than doing a 
summarized query on the planned order 
table. 
Quantity  
TotalPlanned
OrderCount
The total number of PlannedOrder records 
that are reflected in the TotalPlannedOrder 
field (for example, the number of planned 
orders requested).
Quantity  
TotalSupply Total quantity from ScheduledReceipt 
records that are available to meet demand. 
This value is in inventory units, and is not 
shrunk in consideration of the yield factor.
This summarization is faster than doing a 
summarized query on the Supply table. 
Quantity  
TotalSupply
Count
The total number of ScheduledReceipt 
records that are reflected in the TotalSupply 
field (for example, the number of scheduled 
receipts available to meet demand).
Quantity  
TotalUnconsumed
Forecast
The amount of forecast that is not covered by 
actual sales demand.
Quantity  
ValidationDifferences
RollUpSum
Sum of ValidationDifferencesThisLevel for 
this part and all of its components, including 
all part sources. This field supports data 
validation and is intended for internal use 
only.
Integer  
Table 6.69: Part (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 421 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 411
Field name Description Data type Key
ValidationDifferences
RollUpCount
Count of parts below and including this one 
that have validation differences. This field 
supports data validation and is intended for 
internal use only.
Integer  
ValidationDifferences
ThisLevel
Count of all dates on which the sum of 
PlannedOrder quantities differs from the 
sum of BaselinePlannedOrder quantities. 
This field supports data validation and is 
intended for internal use only.
Integer  
Activity Set of Activity records. Set  
Allocations Set of Allocation records belonging to this 
part.
Set  
AllotmentOverride
Details
Set of AllotmentOverrideDetail records that 
reserve supply of this component part.
Set  
AlternateParts A set of parts from the AlternatePart table 
that can be substituted for this part in 
production or planning calculations.
Set  
AlternateRoutings A set of alternate Routing records that are 
used for informational purposes only at this 
time.
Set
