Table 6.69: Part (Mfg) fields
PartSource table
The PartSource table defines multiple sources of supply for a part (typically, there should be at 
least one valid PartSource record per part).
The BaseKey, Part, and Source fields form the PartSource table's primary key. In many cases, 
BaseKey can be blank. When only one part source for a part exists, a blank BaseKey value is 
sufficient. Similarly, in cases where there is never more than one PartSource record for each 
supplier of a part, a blank BaseKey value is also valid.
Where there is more than one PartSource record per part, a BaseKey value must be created to 
ensure each PartSource record is unique.
For information on how Netting handles the selection of multiple part sources, see Planned 
orders.
Caution: Any part without at least one valid PartSource record will be ignored and not 
planned during Netting calculations (treated the same as if PartType.ProcessingRule 
were set to Ignore). Additionally if a Make part contains any components that lack valid 
part sources, then that assembly is planned without passing dependent requirements to 
those specific components.. Similar logic is applied if the sourcing part in a transfer 
relationship lacks a valid PartSource (for more information, see Transfer part sources). 

===== PAGE 433 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 423
Field name Description Data type Key
BaseKey The combination of Part (including Site), 
Source (including Supplier) and this field 
forms the primary key of this table. 
This field can remain blank if the Part and 
Source values are unique.
Default value: A blank string.
String Yes
Part The name of the part replenished by this part 
source.
Referenced table:Part
Reference Syntax: Part.Name, Part.Site
Reference Yes
Source Provides information about the source. For 
example: name, phone, and email.
Referenced table: Source
Reference Syntax: Source.Id, 
Source,Supplier.Id
Reference Yes
Table 6.70: PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 434 =====
424 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
Absolute
PrePlanLimit
Maximum number of calendar days before the 
normal constraint consumption date that 
constraint can be planned or used by supplies 
that use this part source.
It's recommended that this value be set high 
enough so that there is time to build the 
largest supply within its limit.
If this field is set to a negative value, then it is 
assumed that there be no limit on how early 
the logic can try to pre-plan constraints for 
supplies using this part source.
If this field is set to 0, then depending on your 
Maestro installation, this means that no pre-
planning is allowed for supplies using this part 
source or there is no limit on the amount of 
pre-planning allowed for supplies using this 
part source. The interpretation of zero values in 
this field is configured in the Global Settings. 
See Configuring AbsolutePrePlanLimit usage.
Default value: - 1
Notes: 
 l If using production planning, any non-zero 
value enables pre-planning in production 
cycles. See Preplanning production groups.
 l If using production planning with 
recurrences, a value of zero (0) means the 
supply can be preplanned as early as the 
beginning of the most recent recurring 
cycle. For example, suppose the recurring 
cycles are the first week of every month, 
and the demand is in the third week of the 
month. To satisfy the demand, supplies will 
be generated in the first week of the month 
even though the AbsolutePrePlanLimit is 
set to 0.
 l If using material expiry logic, this field 
should be set in consideration of the 
Integer  
Table 6.70: PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 435 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 425
Field name Description Data type Key
IntervalsToExpiry field. Typically, the 
AbsolutePrePlanLimit value should be 
much smaller than the value in 
IntervalsToExpiry. This helps ensure that 
the component supplies are able to fulfill 
the driving demands with the expiry risks 
for those supplies being minimized.
 l If using Supply chain optimization, you can 
use AbsolutePrePlanLimit  as an 
alternative for enforcing expiry in 
optimization. Expiry is not supported by 
Supply chain optimization.
AttachTo
BinaryDecisions
Indicates if a BinaryDecision is attached to this 
PartSource record. A BinaryDecisionSource 
record is generated for every PartSource and 
Constraint relationship. This field is used by 
Supply chain optimization.
Note: This field is in the Solutions namespace.
Default value:N
Boolean  
Table 6.70: PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 436 =====
426 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
CoProductYield Indicates the percentage of a primary 
product's supply production that actually ends 
up as the primary product. The remainder is 
distributed between its co-products and by-
products based on the QuantityPer values 
provided on the relevant BillOfMaterial 
records.
This field respects the setting in the 
OrderPolicy.YieldUsage field, is applied after 
the Yield field, and is always considered by 
Netting calculations. If not using co-product 
and by-product logic, this field should be set to 
0 (indicates the field should be ignored).
Default Value: 0
Note:  If this yield is expected to vary over 
time, then the TimePhasedYield table should 
be used instead.
Quantity  
CRPFloatAfter If using variable lead time based on work 
center capacity 
(OrderPolicy.UseVarLeadTime = CRP), this 
optional field indicates the amount of time to 
leave in the schedule after the final operation 
in the supply's routing completes. This can 
serve as a planning buffer to account for 
unexpected delays or interruptions incurred 
during work center operations.
Expressed in 
Part.PlanningCalendars.TimeUnits.
Integer  
Table 6.70: PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 437 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 427
Field name Description Data type Key
CRPFloatBefore If using variable lead time based on work 
center capacity 
(OrderPolicy.UseVarLeadTime = CRP), this 
optional field indicates the amount of time to 
leave in the schedule before the first operation 
in the routing begins. This can serve as a 
planning buffer to account for unexpected 
delays in the receipt of component supplies.
Expressed in 
Part.PlanningCalendars.TimeUnits.

Table 6.70: PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 438 =====
428 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
CTPIntervalsTo
Expiry
This value is used to calculate the actual expiry 
date on scheduled receipts and planned 
orders. It should be expressed in intervals of 
the part's PlanningCalendars.ExpiryCalendar, 
and indicates the number of those intervals it 
takes supply from the part source to expire.
The specific usage of this field in expiry date 
calculations depends on the part's setting in 
PartType.ExpiryRule as follows:
 l Dependent: Not used (expiry date is 
entirely determined from the part's 
component supplies).
 l DependentStart: Number of 
ExpiryCalendar intervals to add to the 
earliest expiry start date rolled up from a 
component supply (by default, that 
component's available date).
 l DependentLimit: Number of 
ExpiryCalendar intervals to add to the 
supply's DueDate (planned orders) or 
AvailableDate (scheduled receipts), and 
used only if that date is earlier than the 
earliest expiry date found on one of the 
part's component supplies).
 l Ignore: Not used (supply never expires).
 l Self/SelfReset: Number of 
ExpiryCalendar intervals to add to the 
supply's DueDate (planned orders) or 
AvailableDate (scheduled receipts).
This value can be overridden at the scheduled 
receipt level if a date is provided in the 
ScheduledReceipt.ExpiryDate field.
If the value in this field is 0 or negative, then 
the IntervalsToExpiry field is used instead. 
However, because IntervalsToExpiry is used in 
estimated expiry date calculations, which don't 
necessarily start from the same point as actual 
expiry date calculations (based on ExpiryRule), 
Integer  
Table 6.70: PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 439 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 429
Field name Description Data type Key
this should be done with caution.
Default value: 0
DockToStockLead
Time
The time required to move materials from the 
receiving dock to available inventory. The 
setting in this field increases the effective lead 
time for all types of supply; ensure it is set to 0 
unless needed. It is measured using 
Part.PlanningCalendars.TimeUnits.
Default value: 0
Note: If this value is expected to change over 
time, the TimePhasedPartSourceLeadTime 
table can be used instead. 
Quantity  
EffectiveInDate Defines the start of the period from which this 
part source record can be considered valid. 
This date along with the control setting in 
PartSourceType.EffectivityRule determines 
the first date from which supply can be 
planned from the part source (reported in the 
calculated FirstEffectiveDate field).
Date  
EffectiveOutDate Defines the end of the period in which this part 
source record is valid. 
This date along with the control setting in 
PartSourceType.EffectivityRule determines 
the last date from which supply can be planned 
from the part source (reported in the 
calculated LastEffectiveDate field).
Date  
Table 6.70: PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 440 =====
430 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
FixedLeadTime Order release offset from due date. This 
represents a fixed amount of time to produce 
each order for the part from this source. 
Notes: 
 l  If this value is expected to change over 
time, the TimePhasedPartSourceLeadTime 
table can be used instead. 
 l  On transfer part sources, this field value is 
excluded from dependent demand date 
calculations and typically isn't provided. 
However, for historical reasons, some 
environments use the FixedLeadTime on 
transfer sources to serve as a production 
level lead time. This is reflected in Netting 
calculations as a StartDate on planned 
orders (reflecting the start date at the next 
level) and in CTP as a start date marker for 
scheduling limit.
Quantity  
GroupLotSizeFactor A factor applied to a unit of supply from this 
part source to determine its contribution to the 
group minimum and multiple quantities.
Valid values are: 
 l Null: No lot size factor is applied to supply 
from this part source when group planning 
is enabled. 
 l Not null: The lot size factor applied to a 
unit of supply from this part source when 
group planning is enabled. 
Default value: 1 
Notes: 
 l Values less than or equal to 0 are treated as 
1. 
Quantity  
Table 6.70: PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 441 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 431
Field name Description Data type Key
InputSupplyAttribute1The particular characteristics that are used to 
identify supplies using this part source. Supply 
characteristics that get modeled as attributes 
might be things like supplier names, plant 
locations, and manufacturing technologies.
Note: This field belongs to the Attributes 
namespace and is available only with the Life 
Sciences industry template.
String  
Table 6.70: PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 442 =====
432 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
IntervalsToExpiry This field is used internally by the Netting 
algorithm in calculating an estimated expiry 
date on a potential planned order (this 
estimation is then used in determining if the 
part source can be used to supply a given 
demand). 
This value should be expressed in intervals of 
the part's PlanningCalendars.ExpiryCalendar, 
and indicates the number of those intervals it 
takes supply from the part source to expire. For 
parts with a PartType.ExpiryRule of 
Dependent, this field should be set to account 
for the part's fastest expiring component.
A value equal to or less than zero indicates that 
supplies using this part source should never 
expire. However, expiry environments should 
avoid situations where this field is set to zero 
(or negative) values. Doing so disables the 
safety stock logic which swaps out old supply 
in inventory with newer supply in order to 
minimize the expiring quantity, and also 
requires safety stock to have a very long expiry 
date (for example, the end of the planning 
horizon). Therefore if a part source does not 
expire, or expires based on properties of its 
dependent component supplies, this field 
should still be set to a non-zero value in expiry 
environments (and one that is also greater 
than or equal to the  Part.MinimumShelfLife 
value). This ensures that estimated expiry dates 
can be calculated and the safety stock logic 
described above is preserved.
Default value: 0
Note: In previous versions, this field was also 
used in actual expiry date calculations. 
However, as of Version 2016.2, Service Update 
5, the CTPIntervalsToExpiry field is used in 
actual expiry date calculations instead. 
Integer  
Table 6.70: PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 443 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 433
Field name Description Data type Key
However, if CTPIntervalsToExpiry is set to 0 
or negative, then this field is still used in 
calculating the expiry date (as it was in 
previous versions).
LaborCost The standard labor cost associated with one 
unit of this supply (in SupplierUOM). Negative 
values are treated as zero.
Note: The PartSourceTimePhasedAttributes 
table can be used to specify time-phased labor 
cost values. Values provided on that table 
override those on PartSource.
Money  
MaterialCost Material cost associated with one unit of this 
supply. Used to calculate 
CostOfGoodsSold.MaterialCost.
Also used to calculate 
CostRollUp.MaterialCost if 
PartSource.Type.CostRule control value is 
MaterialLaborOverheadCosts.
Negative values are treated as zero.
Default value: 0
Note: The PartSourceTimePhasedAttributes 
table can be used to specify time-phased 
material cost values. Values provided on that 
table override those on the PartSource table.
Money  
Table 6.70: PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 444 =====
434 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
MaximumQty The maximum order quantity (more than one 
planned order can be placed in a period).
If OrderPolicy.MaximumUsage is set to either 
Use or Average, then the quantity on any 
planned order generated from this part source 
will not exceed the value in this field (an 
exception is made on the last planned order in 
a period if the maximum number of orders per 
period has been reached).
However, in cases where MaximumUsage is 
set to CampaignPlanning, this field 
represents the maximum planned order 
quantity across all supply batches (orders) in a 
production campaign, and a new campaign 
must be started once that maximum is 
reached. In other words, the maximum number 
of batches allowed in a given production 
campaign is defined as 
MaximumQty/MultipleQty (and rounded up 
to the next integer where necessary). Note also 
that a global maximum on the number of 
batches in any campaign from any part source 
can be set as discussed in Specify global batch 
limit.
For all MaximumUsage settings other than 
those discussed above, the value in this field is 
ignored when generating planned orders.
Default value: 0 (no maximum)
Quantity  
Table 6.70: PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 445 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 435
Field name Description Data type Key
Caution: Use of the MaximumQty setting 
typically results in more planned orders than 
would otherwise be generated. If the total 
demand quantity is large and the 
MaximumQty is set to a small number, then 
this can result in very large numbers of 
planned orders being generated which can 
potentially impact system performance. Note 
also that a global setting can be used to place 
a cap on the number of planned orders 
generated per part each day as discussed in 
Limiting the number of planned orders.
Note:  SupplierUOM applies to 
MaximumQty.
Table 6.70: PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 446 =====
436 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
MinimumQty The minimum order quantity (more than one 
planned order can be placed in a period).
If OrderPolicy.MinimumUsage is either Use 
or RoundMult then the quantity on any 
planned order generated from this part source 
will be greater than or equal to the value in this 
field (with this field being ignored when 
MinimumUsage is set to Ignore).
However, regardless of the MinimumUsage 
value, if the OrderPolicy.MaximumUsage 
option is set to CampaignPlanning, then this 
field represents the minimum planned order 
quantity across all supply batches (orders) in a 
production campaign. This means a given 
campaign using this part source must continue 
planning batches until this minimum supply 
amount has been built. In other words, the 
minimum number of batches that make up a 
valid production campaign is defined as 
MinimumQty/MultipleQty (and rounded up 
to the next integer where necessary).
Additionally, if OrderPolicy.MaximumUsage 
is set to Kanban, than this field is not used in 
determining planned order quantities.
Notes: 
 l Depending on the setting in 
PartSource.Type.ConstraintMinimumRul
e this minimum value may also be applied 
when splitting supplies for constraint 
consumption or incremental availability.
 l SupplierUOM applies to MinimumQty. 
Quantity  
Table 6.70: PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 447 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 437
Field name Description Data type Key
MinimumShelfLife
Threshold
This field can be used to extend the minimum 
shelf life requirements on dependent demands 
that are satisfied by supplies from the part 
source. Values provided in this field should be 
expressed in 
Part.PlanningCalendars.ExpiryCalendar 
intervals.
By default, the earliest expiry date on a 
planned order is taken from the dependent 
demand it is used to satisfy. However, if a 
positive value is provided in this field, then that 
number of intervals is added to the planned 
order's DueDate. If the resulting date is later 
than the earliest expiry date on the dependent 
demand, it is reported in the 
PlannedOrder.EarliestExpiryDate field 
(otherwise, the EarliestExpiryDate from the 
DependentDemand record is used).
Similarly, the earliest expiry date on a 
scheduled receipt is typically taken from the 
latest dependent demand it is used to satisfy. 
However, if a positive value is provided in this 
field, then that number of intervals is added to 
the scheduled receipt's EffDueDate (for 
reschedulable receipts the ReschedDate is 
used). If the resulting date is later than the 
earliest expiry date on the dependent demand 
(or later than the date that otherwise would be 
used as the earliest expiry date), then it is 
reported in the 
ScheduledReceipt.EarliestExpiryDate field 
instead.
Default value: -1
Integer  
Table 6.70: PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 448 =====
438 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
MLSPreTarget
Sequence
In multi-level search logic, this field can be 
used to specify an additional selection criterion 
that gets applied when distinguishing between 
eligible part sources of the same priority 
(where those eligible sources can satisfy the 
provide the supply on-time and the lowest 
level component supplies being used by each 
are of the same type). For example, this field 
might used to specify a preference between 
secondary part sources.
A lower value in this field indicates a higher 
sort sequence or priority. Thus, if two part 
sources of the same priority are otherwise 
equivalent and eligible to provide a given 
supply, then the one with lower value in the 
field would be used. If those part sources had 
the same value in this field, then 
PartSource.Target field is next looked at to 
determine supply sourcing.
Integer  
Table 6.70: PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 449 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 439
Field name Description Data type Key
MultipleQty The target lot size for planned orders.
Generally, if OrderPolicy.MultipleUsage is set 
to Use, then planned orders from the part 
source should be generated in multiples of this 
value (for other MultipleUsage settings, the 
value in this field is ignored). 
However, regardless of the MultipleUsage 
value, if the OrderPolicy.MaximumUsage 
option is set to CampaignPlanning, then the 
value in this field represents the intended 
batch size and all planned orders from the part 
source are generated for exactly this quantity. 
Note that if this field is set to 0, then campaign 
planning is disabled even if MaximumUsage is 
set to CampaignPlanning.
Additionally, if OrderPolicy.MaximumUsage 
is set to Kanban, than this field is not used in 
determining planned order quantities.
Notes: 
 l Depending on the setting in 
PartSource.Type.ConstraintMultipleRule
, this multiple may also be applied when 
splitting supplies for constraint 
consumption or incremental availability.
 l SupplierUOM applies to MultipleQty. 
Quantity  
Table 6.70: PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 450 =====
440 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
OrderThreshold Specifies a minimum quantity of demand that 
must be accumulated before a new planned 
order from this part source will be created to 
satisfy those demands.
This can be used in situations where a 
combination of small or intermittent demand 
quantities and large lot size requirements can 
cause  new planned orders to be created long 
before most of the supply is actually needed. 
For example, this can mitigate against supply 
being created that will start to expire before 
most of it is needed. This can also be used to 
defer planning of expensive supply that would 
otherwise result in inventory being held for 
long periods of time. 
However, because this setting delays planning 
of supply until the specified demand quantity 
is accumulated, it can be expected to have a 
negative impact on overall demand 
satisfaction.
Note: This field's usage is determined by the 
setting in the 
OrderPolicy.OrderThresholdUsage field. For 
more information, see Order generation 
threshold.
Quantity  
OverheadCost The standard overhead cost associated with 
one unit of this supply (in SupplierUOM). 
Negative values are treated as zero.
Note: The PartSourceTimePhasedAttributes 
table can be used to specify time-phased 
overhead cost values. Values provided on that 
table override those on PartSource.
Money  
Table 6.70: PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 451 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 441
Field name Description Data type Key
OverheadCost2 A second standard overhead cost associated 
with one unit of this supply. OverheadCost2 is 
used in the NewProcessCost calculation.  
Negative values are treated as zero.
Default value: 0
Note: The PartSourceTimePhasedAttributes 
table can be used to specify time-phased 
overhead cost values. Values provided on that 
table override those on PartSource.
Money  
PlanningTimeFence Used in determining the earliest a new order 
can be planned using this part source (based 
on the setting in the OrderPolicy.PTFRule 
field).
This field should be expressed in either the part 
or source's time units (based on the setting in 
the OrderPolicy.PTFUnit field).
Default value: 0
Note: Negative values are not recommended 
in this field as this can result in the calculated 
PTFDate being in the past. 
Quantity  
Table 6.70: PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 452 =====
442 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
PrePlanLimit The maximum number of 
Source.LeadTimeUnits to try to plan orders 
(when the current period has insufficient 
constraint)  before trying to plan orders 
through other and/or lower priority part 
sources.
If the demand cannot be satisfied across any of 
the part sources, in respect of their 
PrePlanLimit values, then  periods before the 
pre-plan limits are checked. This usage of this 
field is dependent upon its value as follows:
 l <0: If this selected source has insufficient 
constraint in the current period, use this 
source in earlier periods until it has no 
more constraint available (respecting 
planning time fence). If there is still 
insufficient constraint, then try same 
priority part sources in the current period 
and earlier until they have no more 
constraint available (respecting planning 
time fence). Finally, if there is still 
insufficient constraint available, then try 
lower priority part sources in the current 
period and earlier (respecting planning 
time fence). Note that all negative values 
are treated identically.
 l 0: If this selected source has insufficient 
constraint in the current period, then try 
same priority sources in the current period, 
and if there is still insufficient constraint 
available then try lower priority sources in 
the current period. Otherwise, try earlier 
periods. 
 l >0: If this selected source has insufficient 
constraint in the current period, then try 
this source in the number of earlier periods 
defined by PrePlanLimit. If there is still 
insufficient constraint, then try same 
Integer  
Table 6.70: PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 453 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 443
Field name Description Data type Key
priority sources in the current period (and 
respecting their PrePlanLimit in earlier 
periods). Finally, if there is still insufficient 
constraint available, then try lower priority 
part sources in the current period (and 
respecting their PrePlanLimit in earlier 
periods).
Default value: 0
For more information about this field, see Pre-
planning limits.
Priority A priority value to use when selecting a 
PartSource to supply a given part requirement 
(used when multiple active PartSources can 
either provide the supply on-time or else 
equally late). 
The interpretation of this value is controlled by 
the SourceRule.PriorityRule field (defines 
whether higher or lower values are assumed to 
indicate a higher effective priority).
Note: If pre-planning is used then, depending 
on the PrePlanLimit setting, a lower priority 
PartSource might be chosen to reduce short 
term excess (see Pre-planning limits.).
Quantity  
Table 6.70: PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 454 =====
444 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
SafetyLeadTime Number of days to offset the demand due date 
when determining when supply should be 
planned to cover that demand. This  is intended 
to protect against fluctuations in supply and 
demand and will impact planned order due 
dates as well as EffLeadTime and 
CumLeadTime calculations. This value should 
be expressed in either the part's 
PlanningCalendars.TimeUnits calendar or the 
Everyday/Gregorian calendar as determined by 
the part's 
PartType.SafetyLeadTimeCalendarRule 
setting. 
Note that usage of this field depends on the 
setting in the PartType.UseSafetyLeadTime 
field, with additional PartType settings 
available to specify how safety lead time is 
applied for specific types of demands.
Default value: 0
Note: SafetyLeadTime is ignored and not 
used to offset due dates for safety stock 
demands.
Caution: Safety lead time defined on the part 
source has limitations with certain other 
analytics (such as, multi-level search). 
Additionally, if it is used with a CommitLevel 
of High and there are different order priorities, 
this this can lead to unexpected results (for 
example, demand and planned order due 
dates becoming out of sync). Unless safety lead 
time values truly need to be part-source 
specific, it is typically recommended to define 
safety lead time at the part level (use 
Part.SafetyLeadTime instead).
Integer  
Table 6.70: PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 455 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 445
Field name Description Data type Key
SecondaryPriority If the part's SourceRule.AllotmentRule is set 
to ProportionalLotSize or OngoingToDate, 
this field is used to chose between eligible 
sources of the same Priority that also have the 
same Target value or the same Target value 
and calculated quota rating. 
This value is also used in multi-level search 
logic in cases where two eligible part sources 
are still considered equal after evaluating their 
Target values.
The interpretation of this value is controlled by 
the SourceRule.PriorityRule setting (defines 
whether higher or lower values are assumed to 
indicate a higher secondary priority).
Quantity  
ShippingCost The standard cost associated with shipping 
using this part source. Negative values are 
treated as zero. 
Note: This field is in the Solutions namespace.
Money  
SupplierPart Supplier's part number (used for reporting if 
the part source is not a Transfer). If the part has 
only one part source, leave this field empty. 
If the part has more than one part source, 
specify the supplier's part number.
String  
Table 6.70: PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 456 =====
446 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
TaktTime The number of time units (typically, work days) 
in between consecutive planned orders when 
the required quantity for the part exceeds the 
value in MaximumQty. The time unit is set by 
the value in 
Part.PlanningCalendars.TimeUnits.
If this field is < 0, then planned orders are 
spread back in time starting from the supply's 
original due date. If this field is > 0, then 
planned orders are spread ahead in time 
starting from the supply's original due date. If 
the spread planned orders reach PTFDate 
before the required quantity is created, then 
multiple planned orders are created on 
PTFDate.
If this field is set to 0, then multiple planned 
orders to satisfy a demand for the part can be 
created on the same date.
For more information, see Spreading planned 
orders with Takt Time.
Default value: 0
Note: If a given part source uses Takt Time 
(that is, TaktTime <> 0), then the part source is 
treated as unconstrained regardless of whether 
or not any constraints are assigned to the part 
source.
Integer  
Table 6.70: PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 457 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 447
Field name Description Data type Key
Target Target allotment value to use for this part 
source when there are multiple active part 
sources (of the same priority) eligible to satisfy 
planned requirements for the part. 
This value is used to determine the percentage 
split of planned requirements among the 
active part sources. Depending on the setting 
in the SourceRule.AllotmentRule field, this 
split among sources is either made 
proportionally between sources for each 
requirement or on ongoing basis with each 
requirement meant to be satisfied by only one 
source whenever possible.
If all active part sources have their Target set to 
0, this indicates demands should be evenly 
split across the eligible sources.
Default value: 0
Quantity  
ToDateQty The quantity that has been allocated to this 
part source to date. It's used as the initial value 
for ongoing allotments.
Note: SupplierUOM applies to ToDateQty.
Quantity  
UnitCost The cost of one unit (in terms of 
SupplierUOM) of this part from this part 
source.  This cost is assumed to include 
material, labor, and overhead costs combined. 
It is therefore similar to Part.StdUnitCost. For 
a part's primary part source, this field will 
typically equal Part.StdUnitCost, with 
appropriate UOM conversion.
Money  
Table 6.70: PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 458 =====
448 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
VarLeadTime The portion of lead time dependent upon 
order quantity (in units of SupplierUOM). 
If OrderPolicy.UseVarLeadTime is set to Y, 
the value in this field is then multiplied by the 
order quantity (PlannedOrder.Quantity or 
ScheduledReceipt.Quantity) to determine the 
variable portion of lead time. Otherwise, this 
field is ignored.
Note: VarLeadTime is also ignored if 
PartSourceType.ConstraintDateRule is set to 
either DuringLeadTime, Start, or 
StartExtend.
Quantity  
Yield Factor to reduce supply quantities to account 
for scrap. This value is affected by the 
OrderPolicy.YieldUsage control value. 
If this factor is expected to vary over time, then 
the TimePhasedYield table should be used to 
specify yield values for the part source.
Note: SupplierUOM applies to Yield (only if 
the OrderPolicy.YieldUsage control value is 
ScrapPiece).
Quantity  
Foundry The foundry where a component is produced.
Note: This field is in the Attributes 
namespace.
String  
Table 6.70: PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 459 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 449
Field name Description Data type Key
AllotmentOverride
 
A reference to the allotment override reserving 
component supply to dependent demands 
generated from supply using this part source. 
The reserved supply is allocated to these 
dependent demand(s) before it can be made 
available to dependent demands generated 
from supplies using a part source that does not 
reference the allotment override.
Referenced table: AllotmentOverride 
(Nullable)
Reference  
BOMAlternate A reference to the alternate BOM used by this 
part source. When Netting logic performs 
explosion calculations, only components that 
have BillOfMaterial.Alternate matched to 
either the value in this field or the first value in 
the BOMAlternate table (that is, common 
components) can potentially create dependent 
demands for supplies using this part source.
For more information about alternate BOMs, 
see Alternate BOM calculations.
Referenced table: BOMAlternate
Reference  
OrderPolicy Order policies for this part source.
Referenced table:OrderPolicy
Reference  
Table 6.70: PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 460 =====
450 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
Pool The pool associated with this part source.
For parts that have inventory pooling logic 
enabled (the MUEPoolNettingType.PoolRule 
field is set to either Net or Optional), the 
referenced pool can influence the part source 
selection process. This is dependent on the 
MUEPoolNettingType.PoolFlowToCommon 
setting as follows:
 l When PoolFlowToCommon is either 
NetOriginal, Ignore, or Forecast, 
planned orders can only be created on part 
sources where the Pool reference matches 
the pool being netted or is Null.
 l When PoolFlowCommon is NetCommon, 
planned orders can only be created on part 
sources where the Pool reference matches 
the "common" pool, or is Null. Note that 
this means planned orders cannot be 
created on part sources where the Pool 
reference is a match for the pool being 
netted.
Once the part source is chosen in 
consideration of pool, new planned orders are 
created on either the matching supply pool or 
common pool, and dependent demands 
generated on components inherit their pools 
from the parent supplies. Thus, if pooling is 
enabled on an end-item assembly, part source 
selection for the assembly can be pool-based 
and that pool information propagated down to 
its components.
 When pooling is disabled on an end-item 
assembly and this field is populated,  supplies 
are still planned in the common pool (as 
determined from 
PlanningCalendars.CommonPool) and the 
part source(s) that can be used for those 
supplies are not dependent on the referenced 
Reference  
Table 6.70: PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 461 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 451
Field name Description Data type Key
PartSource.Pool values. However, if pooling is  
enabled on a lower-level component, then the 
Pool referenced from that assembly can be 
injected onto the dependent demands and 
hence planned supplies for those components.
For more information, see Pools and part 
sources.
Referenced table:Pool (Nullable)
ProductionGroup A reference to the production group 
associated with this PartSource record.
Referenced table:ProductionGroup
Reference  
ProductionRecurrenceA reference to the production recurrence 
associated with this PartSource record.
Referenced table: ProductionRecurrence
Reference  
ProductionWheel A reference to the production wheel associated 
with this PartSource record.
Referenced table:ProductionWheel
Reference  
Routing The standard routing for supplies using this 
part source. This field is used by the Capacity 
Requirements Planning algorithm. This field 
must contain a value (it can be set to None if it 
is not being used.
Referenced table:Routing
Reference Syntax: Routing.Id
Reference  
Table 6.70: PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 462 =====
452 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
SupplierUOM The units used for ordering from this source. 
Dependent demands are created using this 
unit of measure (UOM).
SupplierUOM applies to all unit quantity fields 
in PartSource. These are:
 l MaximumQty
 l MinimumQty
 l MultipleQty
 l ToDateQuantity
 l Yield (only if the OrderPolicy.YieldUsage 
control value is ScrapPiece)
String
(Enum)
 
TransferPart Part to be supplied (only used for Transfer 
sourcing, includes site.) Typically set to the part 
itself for Make and Buy part sources.
Referenced table: Part
Reference Syntax: TransferPart.Name, 
TransferPart.Site
Reference  
Type A value that is associated with processing rules 
that defines how the sourcing data is 
interpreted.
Referenced table: PartSourceType

Table 6.70: PartSource (Mfg) fields
PartSource table:  calculated and set fields
The following describes the calculated and set fields in the PartSource table. For an introduction 
to this table, and descriptions of its input fields, see PartSource table.

===== PAGE 463 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 453
Field name Description Data type Key
EffFixedLeadTime Fixed lead time (based on FixedLeadTime 
and OrderPolicy values.)
Note: If using time-phased fixed lead times, 
then the lead time effective at the latter of the 
part's RunDate or 
PartSource.FirstEffectiveDate is used.
Quantity  
EffLeadTime The total lead time for this part source (sum 
of all lead time components defined on the 
part and part source). This includes effective 
safety, fixed, and variable lead times as well as 
lead time adjustments. For part sources where 
the Type.TransitRule is set to Cumulative, 
this also includes pre-ship lead time, transit 
time, and dock-to-stock lead time.
Notes: 
 l Fixed and variable lead times are ignored 
when calculating lead time for transfer 
sources (lead time calculations start from 
the built date).
 l If using time-phased lead time (for fixed, 
dock-to-stock, transit, or pre-ship lead 
times). then the lead times effective at the 
latter of the part's RunDate or 
PartSource.FirstEffectiveDate are used.
 l If using time-phased safety lead time for a 
part, any effective and applicable values 
defined in the 
SafetyLeadTimeProfileDetail table are 
ignored when calculating the value 
returned by this field. If populated, the 
Part.SafetyLeadTime field value is used 
instead.
Quantity  
Table 6.71: Calculated PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 464 =====
454 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
EffUnitCost The unit cost of one unit of this part source 
(reported in terms of stock units). 
The calculation used to populate this field 
depends on the setting in the 
PartSourceType.CostRule field as follows:
 l If set to MaterialLaborOverheadCosts 
then calculated based on the material, 
labor and overhead cost values from the 
applicable 
PartSourceTimePhasedAttributes 
record (if one exists) or the PartSource 
record. 
If there are multiple time-phased attribute 
records for  the part source, the earliest 
PartSourceTimePhasedAttributes 
record within the part source's effectivity 
is used (unless that record becomes 
effective later than the part source's 
EffectiveInDate in which case the values 
from the PartSource record are used). 
 l Otherwise if set to UnitCost then derived 
from the UnitCost value on the 
PartSource record.
 l And if the applicable calculation returns a 
value less than or equal to zero (<= 0), 
then derived from the Part.StdUnitCost 
value.
Currency conversions on this field use the 
FirstEffectiveDate field's conversion rate.
Money  
EffVarLeadTime Variable lead time (based on VarLeadTime 
and OrderPolicy rules).
Quantity  
Table 6.71: Calculated PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 465 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 455
Field name Description Data type Key
EffYield Effective yield on this part source. Calculated 
by combining (multiplying) the Yield and 
CoProductYield values (and respecting the 
OrderPolicy.YieldUsage value).
If OrderPolicy.YieldUsage is set to 
ScrapPiece this field reports -Yield, and 
the net quantity added to inventory is 
Quantity + EffYield. If 
OrderPolicy.YieldUsage is set to anything 
other than ScrapPiece this field reports a 
value between 0 and 1, and the net quantity 
added to inventory is Quantity * EffYield.
Note: If time-phased yield logic is applicable, 
the Yield and CoProductYield values used  
are taken from the TimePhasedYield table. 
For a given part source, the  
TimePhasedYield record that is effective on 
the latter of   PartSource.EffectiveInDate or  
Part.RunDate is used. 
Quantity  
FirstEffectiveDate Date when this PartSource record becomes 
effective (undefined if never effective). 
Planned orders from the part source cannot 
be earlier than this date (or later than 
LastEffectiveDate).
If a demand falls outside of the effective 
window for all potential part sources, then it 
can either be shifted into effectivity or left 
unsatisfied (based on the part's 
SourceRule.SourceEffectivityWindow 
setting).
Date  
Table 6.71: Calculated PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 466 =====
456 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
IsRecursive Set to Y when this PartSource record is 
involved in a recursive data structure. For 
example, this might occur when a part is a 
component of itself. Similarly, this might 
occur if there is a part at two different sites 
and each has a transfer part source meant to 
transfer supply from the other part and site.
Valid values are:
 l Y
 l N
For more information about recursive data, 
see Recursive data.
Default value: N
Boolean  
LastEffectiveDate Last date when this PartSource record 
remains effective (undefined if never 
effective). Planned orders from the part 
source cannot be later than this date (or 
earlier than FirstEffectiveDate).
If a demand falls outside of the effective 
window for all potential part sources, then it 
can either be shifted into effectivity or left 
unsatisfied (based on the part's 
SourceRule.SourceEffectivityWindow 
setting).
Date  
LeadTimeDate The lead time date for this part source. This 
date is calculated starting from the run date 
(Part.PlanningCalendars.RunDate.FirstDate
) and adding all lead time components (either 
inclusive or cumulative) while respecting the 
relevant planning calendars. This field is 
useful when looking for demand and supply 
due within a part source's lead time.
For more information about lead time, see 
Lead time calculations.
Date  
Table 6.71: Calculated PartSource (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 467 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 457
Field name Description Data type Key
PTFDate The date of the planning time fence as 
calculated based on the run date, 
PlanningTimeFence, and the setting in the 
OrderPolicy.PTFRule field. When 
OrderPolicy.OrderGenerationRule is set to 
AfterPTFDate, planned orders from the part 
source can only be generated after this 
calculated date.
For more information about how this date is 
calculated, see Determining the PTFDate. 
Notes: 
 l If time-phased lead times are used, then 
the lead times effective at the latter of the 
part's RunDate or 
PartSource.FirstEffectiveDate are used.
 l If period-based days of supply logic is 
used, the PTFDate must be on a 
PeriodSupplyDueCalendar interval and 
therefore this calculated date might be 
rounded ahead to the next calendar 
interval (for an example, see PTFDate and 
Days of Supply).
Date  
SourceKanban The SourceKanban selected for this part 
source. If this part source has no 
SourceKanbans, then this reference can be 
null.
Referenced table: SourceKanban
Reference  
BinaryDecisionSources Set of BinaryDecisionSource records that 
reference this part source. 
Set  
MEIOLeadTimes Set of MEIOLeadTime records that contain 
stochastic lead time parameters for this part 
source.
Set

===== PAGE 468 =====

safety stock parameters for parts from this 
part source.

Optimization

Set of records that contain a reference to a

source.

source.

allocations of supply to demand made where 
the supply uses this part source.

records for this part source.

Table 6.72: PartToProperty  (Scheduling) fields
PredecessorResourceEligibility table
The PredecessorResourceEligibility table defines the eligible or ineligible paths between 
primary resources used to produce predecessor and successor operations to define fixed 
routing. In addition, the EnableQualifications field must be set to True/Yes for the resource 
group that the primary resources belong to.
For example, if you have two operations where operation A is the predecessor operation and 
operation B is the successor operation. When you set the predecessor resource to IM-1000 and 
P-1000 as the resource, if operation A is scheduled to be produced on IM-1000, then operation B 
must be produced on P-1000. 
Only primary resources can be defined on this table.
Field name Description Data type Key
Value The name of this routing path. String Y
Qualification Identifies if the predecessor resource is 
qualified or disqualified to be used in fixed 
routing.
String (Enum)  
PredecessorResource The resource assigned to produce a 
predecessor operation.
Referenced table:Resource (Scheduling)
Reference  
Resource The resource assigned to produce a 
successor operation.
Referenced table: Resource (Scheduling)
Reference  
Table 6.73: PredecessorResourceEligibility (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 471 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 461

The Property table lists the property values and their associated property types. Every part has 
assigned properties that are used to determine weighted dispatch rules are applied to 
operations.

PropertyType The property type associated with this property. 
Referenced table: PropertyType
Reference Y
ColorCode The color for this property that will display on the 
operation bar in the Gantt chart. Colors must be 
defined as a hex code value.
String

Table 6.75: Property  (Scheduling) fields
ReplenishmentRule table
The ReplenishmentRule table defines how secondary resources can be replenished throughout 
the schedule by specifying properties for the rule. It determines when the resource is 
replenished, the quantity that is replenished, and what the replenishment interval is. To apply a 
replenishment rule to a secondary resource, the resource must have its IsHeld field set to True 
on the SecondaryResourceRequirement table. This makes sure that route operations are held  
until the secondary resource is available.
After defining this rule, it must them be mapped to a secondary resource using the values from 
the ResourceToReplenishmentRule table.

===== PAGE 473 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 463
Field name Description Data type Key
Name The name of the replenishment rule. String Y
MaxAccrual The maximum allowable quantity of the 
accumulated secondary resource. This limit is only 
applied when Mode is set to Replenish By.
Quantity  
Mode The type of replenishment mode applied to the 
resource. Valid values include:
 l Replenish To: increases the secondary resource 
quantity to the value defined in the Quantity 
field.
 l Replenish By: increases the secondary resource 
quantity by the value defined in the Quantity 
field. It can keep replenishing until it reaches the 
value specified for the MaxAccrual quantity.
String 
(Enum)
 
Quantity The quantity of secondary resource to replenish. Quantity  
RecurrenceFrequencyThe time unit assigned to the replenishment 
interval. The recurrence frequency and interval are 
used together to determine when the 
replenishment recurs. Valid values include:
 l Day: replenish every x day.
 l Hour: replenish every x hour.
 l Month: replenish every x month.
 l Shift: replenish every x shift.
 l Week: replenish every x week.
Where x is the value specified for 
RecurrenceInterval.
Quantity  
Table 6.76: ReplenishmentRule (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 474 =====
464 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
RecurrenceInterval The number of time units that, along with the 
RecurrenceFrequency value, determines the 
replenishment cycle.
For example, a RecurrenceInterval of 2 with a 
RecurrenceFrequency of Day sets the 
replenishment to occur every 2 days.
String 
(Enum)
 
StartupCondition The condition that determines the initial condition 
for the replenishment rule Valid values include:
 l ShiftAvailability: the initial quantity of the 
secondary resource is determined by the 
available quantity of the resource defined in the 
ShiftPatternResourceAvailability table.
Note: When a replenishment rule is applied to a 
resource, available quantity might eventually 
exceed the value specified for 
ShiftPatternResourceAvailability.AvailableQuan
tity.
String 
(Enum)

Resource (Scheduling) table
The Resource(Scheduling) table describes the resources available in the system. Operations are 
scheduled on primary resources and secondary resources (such as labor) are applied as 

===== PAGE 475 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 465
additional constraints to operation eligibility.
Field name Description Data type Key
Name The name of the resource. String Y
Area The area associated with this resource.
Referenced table: Area
Reference Y
Efficiency The efficiency of the resource expressed 
as a scaling factor applied to the 
resource. This can only be applied to 
primary resources and is measured as a 
percentage in decimal format.
Quantity  
Maximum
ConcurrentBatches
The maximum number of concurrent 
operations that can run on the resource.
Integer  
MaximumOperationSizeThe maximum size that an operation can 
be to be scheduled on this primary 
resource. This value must be a value > 0.
Quantity  
MaximumTotalSize The maximum total size of all operations 
that can be simultaneously processed on 
this primary resource. This value must be 
a value > 0.
Quantity  
Rate The production rate of the resource. Integer  
SortKey Used to determine the order that 
resources display in the Gantt chart.
Note: This field is not currently used but 
will be implemented in future releases.
Integer

Resources are grouped based on similar or overlapping capabilities.

this resource group.
Default value: N

ResourceToReplenishmentRule table
The ResourceToReplenishmentRule table is used to define the mapping between a secondary 
resource and a replenishment rule. This applies the rule to the resource based on the rule's 
defined properties.

Table 6.90: RouteOperation  (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 485 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 475
Field name Description Data type Key
PartSource
EffectivityType
Determines how part source compatibility and 
effectivity windows are applied when selecting a 
resource and start time for an operation
For the part source, the operation must be 
scheduled on a resource that is linked to the part 
source referenced by the operation 
(ScheduledReceiptOperation.Scheduled
Receipt.PartSourceor  
PlannedOrderOperation.Planned
Order.PartSource).
For the operation’s start time, it must fall within 
the effective date range 
(PartSource.FirstEffectiveDate to 
PartSource.LastEffectiveDate) for any part 
source linked to the resource’s constraint.
Valid values include:
 l Unconstrained: the part source assigned to 
the resource is not considered and the 
operation is scheduled based on other 
scheduling constraints. Operations can be 
scheduled on any eligible resource at any 
time.
 l EffectivityWindow: the operation can only 
be scheduled within the effectivity window 
for the part source assigned to the resource 
that the operation is scheduled on.
 l EffectivityWindowAndOrderPartSource: 
the operation can only be scheduled within 
the effectivity window for the part source 
assigned to the resource that the operation is 
scheduled on and it can only be scheduled 
on a resource that has the same part source 
as the scheduled receipt order or planned 
order associated with the operation.
String (Enum)  
Table 6.90: RouteOperation  (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 486 =====
476 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key

value is used to assess if this operation can be 
scheduled on a resource with a maximum 
operation or maximum total size constraint 
applied to it.
Quantity

operation.
Referenced table: Resource(Scheduling)

associated with this route operation.

Table 6.96: RouteOperationToProperty  (Scheduling) fields
ScheduledReceipt table       
The ScheduledReceipt table contains supply for a single part that has an assigned due date. 
Examples of supplies are: purchase orders and manufacturing orders.
Note: If a scheduled receipt has its DueDate, DockDate, and Shipment.ShipDateTime 
all set to Undefined or Future, its RecommendedDate is typically set to Future 
(though this is generally considered an invalid configuration). However, in some 
configurations of Service Update 2602 and later, a receipt with this combination of 
Undefined or Future input dates is now completely ignored and not processed by the 
Netting algorithm. As of Service Update 2505 (limited availability version), a scheduled 
receipt with this combination of Undefined or Future dates is now completely ignored 
by the Netting algorithm.
Field name Description Data type Key
Line A unique identifier associated with each 
ScheduledReceipt record with the same 
Order.Type and Order.Id. 
Default value: blank string
String Yes
Order A value that determines the processing 
policies of this scheduled receipt.
Referenced table:SupplyOrder
Reference Syntax: Order.Type, Order.Id, 
Order.Site
Reference Yes
Table 6.97: ScheduledReceipt (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 492 =====
482 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
ActionCode The action code for an order line brought in 
from an SAP IDOC process and included in a 
supplier collaboration process. This field is 
taken from the IDOC message and can 
contain the following values:
 l Added: Records defined by the message 
are added to the Maestro database. If the 
specified records already exist, the 
message fails.
 l Changed: Records defined by the 
message are modified, or inserted if they 
do not already exist in the Maestro 
database. 
 l Cancelled: Records defined by the 
message are cancelled, which modifies 
the status of the order records.
 l Deleted: Records defined by the 
message are deleted. 
 l NotChanged: Records defined by the 
message are not modified, so no actions 
are taken and the record is ignored.
 l Locked: Records defined by the message 
are locked, and cannot be modified.
Note: This field is in the SupCollab 
namespace.
This field maps to the ACTION field in the 
E1EDP01 segment of the IDOC message.
String  
ActualDockDate The actual date this supply arrived at the 
receiving dock, which is used in historical 
reporting.
Note: This field is in the Solutions 
namespace.
Date  
Table 6.97: ScheduledReceipt (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 493 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 483
Field name Description Data type Key
ActualShipDate The actual date this supply was shipped from 
the supplier, which is used in historical 
reporting.
Note: This field is in the Solutions 
namespace.
Date  
AnchorDate The anchor date associated with this supply. 
When the BOMType.DateRule is set to 
AnchorDate, anchor date logic is enabled 
and the date in this field is used instead of 
the order start or due date for bill effectivity. 
With bill effectivity based on anchor date, 
material allocations for an order are frozen 
despite subsequent rescheduling of the 
order. 
This field is only applicable when 
BOMType.DateRule is set to AnchorDate. 
If this field is left undefined when anchor 
date logic is enabled, the DateRule is 
treated as being set to CalcStartDate 
instead.
Default value: Undefined
Date  
BuildStatus Records whether an order was scheduled to 
completion. Valid values are:
 l No_Build: The order was not scheduled 
to start within the scheduling horizon.
 l Partial: The order was started, but not 
completely scheduled (finished) in the 
scheduling horizon.
 l Complete: The order was completely 
scheduled in the scheduling horizon.
Note: This field is obsolete.
Default value: No_Build
String (Enum)

collaboration process, if a closed loop 
process using SAP IDOCs has been 
established, whether the buyer has 
committed to the order. A buyer can commit

 l Y: The buyer has committed to the order.

order.

namespace.

For an order line committed to by a supplier, 
the date and time the supplier confirmed the 
order. This field provides data for an IDOC

namespace.

segment of the IDOC message. The Time 
component of this field maps to the EZEIT 
field of the E1EDP01.E1EDP20 segment of 
the IDOC message.

namespace.

Quantity  
CurrentOperation When a routing has been partially 
completed, this field is used to start the 
processing of the routing at an operation 
other than the first operation. When 
backward scheduling the order, all 
operations remaining will be scheduled 
relative to the due date of the order. When 
forward scheduling the order, all operations 
remaining will be scheduled relative to the 
MRP RunDate. 
This field is used by the Capacity 
Requirements Planning algorithm. If this 
algorithm is not being used, this field can be 
left blank.
Default value: 0
Note: This field is applicable to operations 
defined through the Operation table only 
(for operations configured through the 
CRPOperation table, the 
CRPOperationState table should be used to 
indicate the current operation).
Integer

486 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
CurrentOperation
CompleteQuantity
The number of units completed by the 
current operation. The difference between 
the scheduled receipt's Quantity and this 
field indicates what remains to be processed 
by the current operation.
This field is used by the Capacity 
Requirements Planning algorithm. If this 
algorithm is not being used, this field can be 
left blank.
Default value: 0
Note: This field is applicable to operations 
defined through the Operation table only 
(for operations configured through the 
CRPOperation table, the 
CRPOperationState.CompleteQty should 
be used to indicate the amount of the 
current operation that is complete).
Integer  
DockDate The date this supply is supposed to be 
delivered to the manufacturer's dock.  
Algorithms will calculate from DueDate if 
DockDate is Undefined.
Date  
DockToStock
LeadTime
The time needed to move materials from the 
receiving dock to available inventory.
This value is used in lead time calculations 
when the part source's lead time is 
overridden by the scheduled receipt (as set 
by the values on 
SupplyStatus.LeadTimeOverride and 
SupplyType.LeadTimeRule).
Quantity

Maestro.
Note: This field is obsolete.

===== PAGE 497 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 487
Field name Description Data type Key
DueDate The date the order is planned to be in stock.
Default value: Future
Date  
ExpiryDate The date on which the material in this supply 
expires (is no longer usable). If a value is 
provided in this field, it is also reported in 
the CalcExpiryDate field (as long as 
PartType.ExpiryRule is not set to Ignore). 
Otherwise, the CalcExpirydate field is 
calculated by the material expiry algorithm.
For guidelines about when to populate this 
field, see Providing an expiry date for 
scheduled receipts.
Default value: Undefined
Date  
ExpiryStartDate The date on which the material in this supply 
starts to expire. If a value is provided in this 
field, it is also reported in the 
CalcExpiryStartDate field (as long as 
PartType.ExpiryRule is not set to Ignore). 
Otherwise, if a value is not provided in this 
field, then CalcExpiryStartDate is either 
determined from an algorithm calculation or 
set to Future (depending on the configured 
expiry rules and types). This field is most 
typically used with expiry parts that are 
designated as limiting components.
Date  
FinishDateTime Displays the date and time this scheduled 
receipt has completed its last operation 
based on material and capacity.
Note: This field is in the ProdSched 
namespace.
DateTime

differently; just their statistics are reported

 l Y
 l N

is 1 and increase incrementally with each 
group order.

value is 0.

value is 0.

Integration Service. This field is also used to

Notes Notes field where additional information 
about the record can be added by a user.
Each time a note value is added to this field 
through a worksheet, a new record is created 
in the ScheduledReceipt_Notes table with a 
date and timestamp, user ID, note text, and a 
reference to the ScheduledReceipt record 
against which it was created.

Table 6.97: ScheduledReceipt (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 500 =====
490 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
OriginalDueDate The date on which the order was promised 
or contracted to be due. This is the DueDate 
that was originally on the ScheduledReceipt 
when the allocations were made. This may 
be different than the due date because the 
due date reflects the achievable target due 
date.
Default value: Undefined
Note:  The DueDate on the Allocation 
record is used with the OriginalDueDate on 
its parent scheduled receipt to determine 
effective lead time and hence in calculating 
Allocation.ReschedDate. Therefore, the 
DueDate on any Allocation records for the 
receipt should always be before this date. In 
cases where an unexpected 
Allocation.ReschedDate value is returned, 
setting this field to Undefined might 
resolve the problem.
Date  
Table 6.97: ScheduledReceipt (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 501 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 491
Field name Description Data type Key
OriginalQuantity This field should only be populated if it is 
used to calculate the 
Allocation.EffQuantity field (that is, if 
Maestro's mechanism of updating the 
Allocation every time there is a quantity 
change on the scheduled receipt is used).
If populated, this field must contain the 
same value as the Quantity field. Imported 
allocations will have the EffQuantity 
(quantity that is used in netting calculations) 
recalculated based on the 
ScheduledReceipt.OriginalQuantity * 
BillOfMaterial.QuantityPer; that is, the 
imported Allocation.RemainingQuantity is 
not used in netting calculations.
For more information about automatic 
allocation adjustments, see Automatic 
allocation adjustment).
Quantity  
Table 6.97: ScheduledReceipt (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 502 =====
492 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
OriginalRecordId Identifies records that have been sent to 
your enterprise data sources in a closed-
loop operation. This is a concatenation of 
the date the closed-loop operation was 
performed and the RecordId value for the 
record. When data is next updated, this 
value is compared to the new 
ScheduledReceipt records and ensures the 
records are not double-counted.
The RecordId value is a number 
representing the sequence a record was 
created in. Each additional record added to 
the table increments the RecordId by one, 
and RecordId values are not reused when 
records are deleted. For more information 
about RecordId, see the Maestro Resource 
Authoring Guide (Java client).
Note: This field is in the Solutions 
namespace.
String  
PreShipLT The time needed by the source to collect the 
inventory and prepare it for shipment. 
This value is used in lead time calculations 
when the source's lead time is overridden by 
the scheduled receipt (as set by the values 
on SupplyStatus.LeadTimeOverride and 
SupplyType.LeadTimeRule).
Quantity

confirmation has been sent to your 
enterprise data source.

accepted or committed to the order.

Table 6.97: ScheduledReceipt (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 506 =====
496 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
StartDate The date that the order is scheduled to be 
released to the supplier or shop floor. 
Notes: 
 l If this field is undefined, the start date is 
always calculated.
 l If this field is populated and 
SupplyStatus.IgnoreStartDate is set to 
Y, then this field is treated as if it were 
undefined. 
 l If this field is populated and 
SupplyStatus.IgnoreStartDate is set to 
N, the effective lead time for the receipt is 
determined as the difference between 
DueDate and StartDate.  Thus the 
AvailableDate for some non-
reschedulable receipt's can end up being 
earlier or later than might be expected 
based on start dates and input lead 
times.
 l When populated, the date provided is 
typically expected to fall on the lead time 
calendar 
(PartSource.Source.LeadTimeUnit).
 l In the case of constrained scheduled 
receipts, the following also applies when 
IgnoreStartDate is set to Y:
 l For Reschedulable scheduled 
receipts, this field is completely 
ignored (and also does not impact 
constraint consumption.
 l For NonReschedulable and 
RecommendOnly scheduled receipts, 
this field is not used in determining or 
overriding lead time (but does impact 
constraint consumption).
 l For Scheduled scheduled receipts, 
this field typically is used in 
Date

determining or overriding lead time 
(and also does impact constraint 
consumption). However, in some 
configurations of Service Update 
2602 or later, this field is no longer 
used in overriding lead time on 
constrained and Scheduled 
scheduled receipts but still impacts 
constraint consumption (which 
provides consistency with how 
NonReschedulable and 
RecommendOnly receipts are 
treated).

committed to the order. A supplier can

receipt includes (Quantity is the balance 
remaining, so the difference is received + 
scrap.)

Table 6.97: ScheduledReceipt (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 509 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 499
Field name Description Data type Key
Transaction
Sequence
The sequence of the scheduled receipt. This 
value is assigned to transaction sequence on 
resulting dependent demands. If dependent 
demands have the same order priority and 
the value of MaintainCommitments is set 
to Y for the dependent demands part, then 
supplies will be allocated by transaction 
sequence (smallest to largest).
Note: On startup, the server checks the 
maximum value for a transaction sequence 
number and if the value is greater than 
0x30000000 hex (or 805306368 decimal), it 
will reset the sequence number back to zero 
and re-sequence all the ScheduledReceipt 
and IndependentDemand records.
Integer  
TransitTime The number of days the order must be 
shipped prior to it being available for use in 
manufacturing.
This value is used in lead time calculations 
when the source's lead time is overridden by 
the scheduled receipt (as set by the values 
on SupplyStatus.LeadTimeOverride and 
SupplyType.LeadTimeRule).
Quantity  
Table 6.97: ScheduledReceipt (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 510 =====
500 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
Unconstrained
Quantity
The quantity of this supply that has had 
constraint applied to it and does not require 
any more constraint.  The constrained 
quantity is calculated, depending upon 
SupplyStatus.TargetAccum, as Quantity or 
TotalQuantity - UnconstrainedQuantity.  A 
result of 0 or a negative value results in no 
load on the constraint.
Values of UnconstrainedQuantity < 0 are 
ignored.
UnconstrainedQuantity is ignored unless 
the scheduled receipt is limited by 
constraint.
Note: If this field is set to a non-zero value, 
then incremental availability is automatically 
turned off this scheduled receipt.
Quantity  
UnitPrice The unit price to be used for this order. In 
cost calculations, UnitPrice is assumed to 
represent material costs only.  If UnitPrice is 
less than or equal to zero, use 
PartSource.EffUnitCost less labor and 
overhead costs for material costs instead.  
Labor and overhead costs are always 
calculated from PartSource fields.
Money  
Table 6.97: ScheduledReceipt (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 511 =====

Field name Description Data type Key

on order quantity (this value is multiplied by 
the scheduled receipt quantity).
This value is used in lead time calculations 
when the part source's lead time is 
overridden by the scheduled receipt (as set 
by the values on 
SupplyStatus.LeadTimeOverride and 
SupplyType.LeadTimeRule).
Note: In some configurations of Service 
Update 2602 and later, the usage of this field 
is further dependent on 
OrderPolicy.UseVarLeadTime being set to 
Y (otherwise, and all in previous service 
update, this field value is used as long as 
lead time overrides from scheduled receipts 
are enabled). 
Quantity

specified for the scheduled receipt in 
Enterprise Scheduling.
Note: This field is in the Scheduling 
namespace.

processed by the Scheduling algorithm. Log

(Nullable)
Reference

ScheduledReceipt record.  The batch field is

source.  It is only for reporting purposes and

Table 6.97: ScheduledReceipt (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 513 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 503
Field name Description Data type Key
BOMAlternate A reference to the alternate BOM used by 
this scheduled receipt. When Netting logic 
performs explosion calculations, only 
components that have 
BillOfMaterial.Alternate matched to either 
the value in this field or the first value in the 
BOMAlternate table (that is, common 
components) can potentially create new 
allocations for this scheduled receipt.
In cases where this value differs from the 
value in PartSource.BOMAlternate, the 
value in this field is used, and the part source 
value is ignored.
For more information about alternate BOMs, 
see Alternate BOM calculations.
Referenced table: BOMAlternate
Note: This field is ignored for a given 
scheduled receipt unless 
Order.Type.Source = Make, and 
Status.State is set to either NotReleased, 
Planned, or ReleasedButNotAllocated.
Reference  
InputModel The model that is associated with this 
scheduled receipt.
Based on the SupplyStatus.ModelUsage 
setting, either this model or the default 
model (the None model) is effective for the 
receipt. The actual usage of the receipt's 
effective model in algorithm calculations is 
determined by the setting in the 
Part.MUEPoolNettingType.ModelRule 
field. This also depends on the Model-Unit 
Effectivity capability having been enabled.
Referenced table: Model
Reference  
Table 6.97: ScheduledReceipt (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 514 =====
504 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
InputPool The pool to which this scheduled receipt 
belongs. This field supports inventory 
pooling, and is used only when   
Part.MUEPoolNettingType.PoolRule is set 
to either Net or Optional. 
If this field is left Null, then the pool from 
Order.Pool is used instead. Therefore, this 
field is typically used when the pools 
applicable to supplies more naturally 
correspond to individual line items rather 
supply orders, or when there is a need to 
inject pool information at the component 
level from scheduled assembly supply. 
Referenced table:Pool (Nullable)
Reference  
OrderPriority The priority associated with this order when 
SupplyStatus.PrioritySource is set to 
FromInput. Ignored for all other 
PrioritySource values (unless the Netting 
algorithm does not use this receipt to satisfy 
any demands). 
Referenced table: OrderPriority
Note: The effective order priority on 
scheduled receipts is reported in the 
EffOrderPriority field.
Reference  
Part Part for which this ScheduledReceipt exists. 
This field references the Part table. 
Referenced table: Part
Reference Syntax: Part.Name, Part.Site
Reference  
Table 6.97: ScheduledReceipt (Mfg) fields
