EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 505
Field name Description Data type Key
Routing The routing for the order. It is a reference to 
the Routing table through the Routing.Id 
field.
Note: This field is used by the Capacity 
Requirements Planning algorithm. If this 
algorithm is not being used, a value must 
still be entered in this field (in this case, it 
can be set to None).
Referenced table: Routing
Reference  
SavedPriority Duplicates OrderPriority, load with same 
data as OrderPriority. Not used by any 
algorithms, but might be used by 
applications, such as custom applications.
Referenced table: OrderPriority
Reference  
Shipment The shipment that contains (or is planned to 
contain) this supply.
Referenced table:Shipment (this reference 
is nullable in new Versions 10.1 and later).
Reference  
Status Processing rule modifiers on a per line basis 
used in conjunction with the Order.Type. A 
Firm status on a WorkOrder will generate 
NewAllocations, but Open ones already 
have Allocations.
Referenced tables: SupplyStatus
Reference  
Table 6.97: ScheduledReceipt (Mfg) fields
ScheduledReceipt table:  calculated and set fields
The following describes the calculated and set fields in the ScheduledReceipt table. For an 
introduction to this table, and descriptions of its input fields, see ScheduledReceipt table       .

===== PAGE 516 =====
506 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
AvailableDate The date this supply is likely to be available 
based on materials, order status, and constraint.
Note: For NonReschedulable scheduled 
receipts (assuming StartDate and DueDate are 
defined and IgnoreStartDate is set to N), this 
date is calculated differently depending on if 
the scheduled receipt uses constraint. If a non-
reschedulable receipt does not use constraint, 
then this date is calculated as the CalcStartDate 
plus the difference between the DueDate and 
StartDate. However, if a non-reschedulable 
receipt uses constraint then the part source's 
lead times are also involved in calculating the 
available date. That is, this field then depends 
on CalcStartDate plus applicable lead times, 
and also considering constraint availability. 
Thus, when constraints are used, if the sum of 
applicable lead times is greater than the 
difference between DueDate and StartDate 
then the AvailableDate will necessarily be later 
than DueDate (even if all constraint is available 
on time). 
Date  
Table 6.98: Calculated ScheduledReceipt (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 517 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 507
Field name Description Data type Key
AvailableStartDate The date of the earliest constraint consumed by 
this supply (ship date or start date) converted to 
a start date. If incremental availability 
calculations are turned off, then all component 
materials must be available before constraint 
can be used. If incremental availability 
calculations are turned on, then only some set 
of component materials must be available 
before constraint can be used.
Note:  For Scheduled scheduled receipts, this 
field will not typically reflect the earliest 
constraint consumption. These "scheduled" 
scheduled receipts, regardless of constraint 
availability, will always be seen as available on 
their due date. Therefore this field will reflect 
that due/available date less applicable lead 
time.
Date  
CalcDemandAttributesReports any demand attributes applicable to the 
scheduled receipt. 
The demand attributes reported in this field can 
come from the ScheduledReceipt record itself, 
from its PartSource record, or else from the 
driver IndependentDemand record.
NEW  This field was added in Service Update 
2511.
String  
Table 6.98: Calculated ScheduledReceipt (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 518 =====
508 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
CalcExpiryDate The calculated date on which the supply in this 
scheduled receipt will expire (is no longer 
usable). This field is calculated as follows:
 l If the ExpiryDate field is not Undefined, 
then it is to the ExpiryDate value .
 l If the ExpiryDate field is Undefined and 
PartType.ExpiryRule is Dependent, then it 
is set to the earliest of the receipt's own 
calculated expiry date or the calculated 
expiry date on any component supply 
allotted to the order (supplies from 
component parts that have an 
ExpiryType.RelationshipToAssembly 
setting of None are excluded from this 
calculation).
 l If the ExpiryDate field is Undefined and 
PartType.ExpiryRule is DependentLimit, 
then it is set to the earliest of either or the 
earliest calculated expiry date on any 
component supply allotted to the order 
(supplies from component parts that have 
an ExpiryType.RelationshipToAssembly 
setting of None are excluded from this 
calculation).
 l If the ExpiryDate field is Undefined and 
PartType.ExpiryRule is DependentStart, 
then it is calculated from 
CalcExpiryStartDate and adding the value 
in PartSource.IntervalsToExpiry (expressed 
in terms of the part's expiry calendar).
 l If ExpiryDate is Undefined and 
PartType.ExpiryRule is Self or 
SelfReset, then it is calculated by adding 
the value in PartSource.IntervalsToExpiry 
(expressed in terms of the part's expiry 
calendar) to either the supply's available 
date, dock date, or stock date (as 
determined by the setting in 
Date  
Table 6.98: Calculated ScheduledReceipt (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 519 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 509
Field name Description Data type Key
PartSourceType.ExpiryDateRule).
 l If PartType.ExpiryRule is Ignore, then it is 
always set to Future regardless of any other 
field settings.
CalcExpiryStart
Date
The calculated date on which this supply starts 
to expire. This date is rolled up the product 
structure through parts using a "dependent" 
expiry type. For assembly parts with a 
PartType.ExpiryRule setting of 
DependentStart, the earliest rolled up 
ExpiryStartDate from supply of a limiting 
component is then used to start expiry date 
calculations for the assembly supply. 
This field is populated as follows:
 l If an input value is found in 
ExpiryStartDate, then it is always used here 
(assuming the part's PartType.ExpiryRule is 
not set to Ignore).
 l If no input value is found and the part is 
designated as a limiting component (that is, 
the ExpiryType.ProcessingRule is set to 
LimitingComponent), then the component 
supply's due date, dock date, or start date is 
used (determined by 
PartSourceType.ExpiryDateRule)
 l If no input value is found and the part has a 
PartType.ExpiryRule of DependentStart 
with limiting components in its structure, 
then the earliest expiry start date rolled up 
from a limiting component supply is used.
 l Otherwise, set to Future.
Date  
Table 6.98: Calculated ScheduledReceipt (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 520 =====
510 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
CalcRequestStart
Date
The date on which work should start on this 
supply in order to meet the earliest request date 
on any driving independent demand it satisfies. 
For "make" and "buy" sources this returns an 
equivalent start date, whereas for "transfer" 
sources this returns an equivalent built date . 
Based on the original RequestDate exploded 
down from the driver IndependentDemand 
record and adjusted by lead time at each level in 
the product structure (if the supply directly 
satisfies an independent demand itself, then this 
is just the RequestDate adjusted by the part's 
lead time).
If the driver demand is not an independent 
demand, or has an undefined RequestDate, 
then this is the date on which work should start 
on this supply in order to meet the due date on 
the driver.
Date  
CalcStartDate The order start date as calculated by netting, 
including allowance for constraint.
If StartDate is not Undefined, then this is 
calculated as StartDate + (ReschedDate - 
EfDueDate PartSource.Source.LeadTimeUnit) 
PartSource.Source.LeadTimeUnit
Otherwise, if StartDate is Undefined, then this 
is calculated by subtracting lead time from 
ReschedDate.
Notes: 
 l  If pre-planning for constraints is enabled, 
this date can be earlier than expected (see 
Pre-planning limits and PrePlanRule).
 l If production wheels are used (production 
planning logic is enabled), this date is 
aligned to the start date of the production 
wheel cycle that the scheduled receipt is 
placed in. 
Date

===== PAGE 521 =====

Field name Description Data type Key
Constraint The Constraint that has insufficient remaining 
capacity to make this supply available for its 
MaterialAvailableDate. If this supply is not 
late, this field is empty.
Referenced table: Constraint
Reference  
CostOfGoodsSold The total rolled-up costs of goods sold from all 
component supplies used to fill this order. 
This field summarizes the date-specific cost 
values reported in the ScheduledReceiptCost 
table and described on page 
ScheduledReceiptCost table  .
Money  
Table 6.98: Calculated ScheduledReceipt (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 522 =====
512 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
DemandDate The date of the earliest demand that netting 
matches with this supply.  
DemandDate can differ from 
RecommendedDate for non-reschedulable 
supplies, if constraint limitations result in pre-
build recommendations, and for parts that use 
safety lead time.
Notes: 
 l The date reported in this field must typically 
belong to the part's 
PlanningCalendars.TimeUnits calendar. If 
this supply is matched to an independent 
demand with a due date that does not fall 
on the part's TimeUnits calendar, it is 
adjusted back to the previous TimeUnits 
interval before being reported in this field. 
However, in some configurations of Service 
Update 2602 and later, this rounding does 
not occur and the date from the demand is 
always reported (but supply due dates are 
adjusted as needed to be on the part's 
TimeUnits calendar). 
 l In some configurations of Service Update 
2602 and later, if a scheduled receipt is not 
used for a real demand in Netting, the 
DemandDate is left undefined. Otherwise, 
and in all previous versions, this field is still 
populated on receipts that aren't used for 
real demands. 
Date  
DockDateFrom
Start
The order dock date after adjustment based on 
any calendar rounding applied when 
determining the calculated start date. 
For example, parts that use "cumulative" lead 
time might end up with an earlier dock date 
from start based on rounding between time 
units, transit, ship, and lead time unit calendars. 
Date  
Table 6.98: Calculated ScheduledReceipt (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 523 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 513
Field name Description Data type Key
DueDateFromStart The order due date after adjusting for any 
calendar rounding applied when determining 
the calculated start date. 
For example, parts that use "cumulative" lead 
time might end up with an earlier due date from 
start based on rounding between time units, 
transit, ship, and lead time unit calendars. 
Date  
Table 6.98: Calculated ScheduledReceipt (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 524 =====
514 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
EarliestExpiryDate The earliest date that any supplies being used to 
satisfy dependent demands from this receipt 
may expire. 
Typically, this is determined from the latest 
EarliestExpiryDate found on any demand that 
Netting calculations determine this supply will 
satisfy. Otherwise, if there are no consuming 
demands, this value is calculated by adding the 
number of expiry calendar intervals defined in 
Part.MinimumShelfLife to the supply due 
date. 
In cases where expiry parts also use multi-level 
search, the EarliestExpiryDate is not rolled up 
from lower-level demands. Instead, this field is 
set to the value in the ExpiryDate field (if 
provided). Otherwise, if an ExpiryDate is not 
specified, this field is calculated by adding the 
number of expiry calendar intervals defined in 
Part.MinimumShelfLife to the supply due 
date.
Note that although scheduled receipts used to 
satisfy dependent demands typically inherit 
their EarliestExpiryDate from those dependent 
demands, this date can be extended if 
necessary. If a positive value is given in the 
MinimumShelfLifeThreshold field on the 
PartSource used for the supply, then those 
number of expiry calendar intervals are added 
to the scheduled receipt's EffDueDate (or 
ReschedDate for "reschedulable" scheduled 
receipts). If the resulting date is later than the 
earliest expiry date found on the dependent 
demand (or the date that otherwise would be 
used for earliest expiry), then that later date is 
reported in this field .
Notes: 
 l For non-expiry parts (where 
PartType.ExpiryRule is set to Ignore), this 
Date  
Table 6.98: Calculated ScheduledReceipt (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 525 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 515
Field name Description Data type Key
field always reports Past.
EffDockDate The date this supply is supposed to be delivered 
to the receiving dock. If the supply has a valid 
delivery date, then EffDockDate is calculated 
from that. Otherwise, if DockDate is not 
Undefined, then equals DockDate. Otherwise, 
is calculated from DueDate.
Date  
EffDueDate The date this supply is supposed to be available 
for use. If the supply has a valid delivery date, 
then EffDueDate is calculated from that. 
Otherwise, if DueDate is not Undefined, then 
equals DueDate.  Otherwise, is calculated from 
DockDate.
Date  
EffectiveUnitPrice The sum of all costs (material, labor and 
overhead) to produce this receipt, reported in 
stock units of measure.
If the UnitPrice > 0, this value is calculated as 
UOMConversion(UnitPrice + max
(LaborCost, 0) + max(OverheadCost, 0) 
+ max(OverheadCost2, 0). Otherwise, it is 
set to PartSource.EffUnitCost.
Currency conversions on this field use the 
EffDueDate field's conversion rate.
Money  
Table 6.98: Calculated ScheduledReceipt (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 526 =====
516 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
EffOrderPriority The effective order priority for this scheduled 
receipt. Depending on the setting in 
SupplyStatus.PrioritySource, this field either 
uses the OrderPriority value provided on the 
scheduled receipt record or is dynamically 
calculated based on the priorities of the 
demands satisfied by the receipt in netting 
calculations (these may not necessarily be the 
demands that capable-to-promise calculations 
later allocate to the scheduled receipt).
For parts where priority is relevant (for example, 
when PartType.CommitLevel is set High) , this 
value is used when allocating limited materials 
and constraint to an order, and is also 
propagated down to its dependent 
components.
For more information about how order priority 
on scheduled receipts is calculated and used, 
see Priorities on scheduled receipts.
Referenced table: OrderPriority
Reference  
EffQuantity The quantity of this receipt that remains to be 
received into inventory, in inventory units of 
measure. This value is calculated by taking the 
Quantity and subtracting 
InTransitConsumedQuantity (if applicable), 
then adjusting by yield/scrap, considering any 
co-product or by-product supply, and applying 
unit of measure.
If SupplyType.ProcessingRule is set to Ignore 
or SupplyStatus.State is set to Historical, or 
the part's PartType.ProcessingRule is set to 
Ignore, this field will always return 0 (zero). In 
such cases, none of the receipt can be used to 
cover demand by Netting.
Quantity  
EffShipDate The date this supply was or should be shipped 
based on EffDueDate.
Date  
Table 6.98: Calculated ScheduledReceipt (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 527 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 517
Field name Description Data type Key
EffStartDate The date the order was or should be started, 
calculated from DueDate, DockDate, and 
StartDate.
Date  
EstimatedExpiry
Date
The date that the Netting algorithm calculates 
this supply will expire. Used to determine which 
demands this receipt can potentially be used to 
satisfy (supply is only eligible for use if it is on or 
before the demand's EarliestExpiryDate). This 
date might be different than the actual expiry 
date reported in CalcExpiryDate which takes 
supply availabilities into account.
If a value is provided in the ExpiryDate field, 
then it is used here. Otherwise, calculated by 
adding the number of expiry calendar intervals 
defined in PartSource.IntervalsToExpiry to 
either the EffectiveDueDate, StartDate, or 
DockDate (as determined by the setting in 
PartSourceType.ExpiryDateRule).
Non-expiry parts, where PartType.ExpiryRule 
is set to Ignore, always return a value of 
Future.
Date  
ExpiringQuantity
 
The amount of this scheduled receipt that will 
not be used before it expires (reaches its 
CalcExpiryDate).
Quantity  
FirstAvailableDate The date the first portion of this supply should 
be available based on materials, order status, 
and constraint. This value is different than the 
AvailableDate field in cases where the order 
has been split due to incremental availability of 
component materials or constraint 
consumption in multiple periods.
Date  
Table 6.98: Calculated ScheduledReceipt (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 528 =====
518 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
FeatureBOMs Set of FeatureBOMForScheduledReceipt 
records associated with this ScheduledReceipt.
NEW  This field was added in Service Update 
2408.
Set  
FeatureList Set of ScheduledReceiptFeatureList records 
that reference this scheduled receipt.
Applicable to configurable, made to order end-
items (parts where PartType.FeatureBOMRule 
is set to a value other than Ignore). The set of 
string values in this field represents the list of all 
features required for the final assembly on this 
supply.
This feature list is then compared against values 
in the BillOfMaterial.FeatureRule field for each 
record that defines a component directly used 
in the end-item assembly that this order is for. If 
the FeatureRule field either matches a value 
defined in the feature list or is empty, then the 
component is required for this supply; 
otherwise, it is not.
Thus, this field ensures that the correct 
components by feature are selected for the 
supply and dependent demands placed 
correspondingly throughout the structure.
NEW  This field was added in Service Update 
2408.
VectorSet  
FeaturesList Set of ScheduledReceiptFeature records 
associated with this ScheduledReceipt.
  This field was added in Service Update 2408.
Set

Field name Description Data type Key
InTransit
ConsumedQuantity
The amount of the scheduled receipt that has 
been consumed (reduced) by related in-transit 
scheduled receipts. This value is reported in 
supplier units of measure, and is not adjusted 
for yield. 
For more information about in-transit 
scheduled receipts, see In-transit scheduled 
receipts.
Quantity  
IsEligibleForSchedulingIdentifies if this scheduled receipt is eligible to 
be scheduled.
Note: This field is in the Scheduling 
namespace.
 
Boolean  
MaterialAvailable
Date
The date this supply is available calculated from 
MaterialStartDate.
Date  
MaterialStartDate Date when all components are available and this 
order can be started.  For work orders, this is 
based only on component availability.  For 
purchase orders, this is the same as 
CalcStartDate.
Note: For assembly parts with non-zero values 
in the BillOfMaterial.LeadTimeOffset field, 
material start dates are further adjusted by 
subtracting the LeadTimeOffset value in units 
of the assembly's TimeUnits calendar.

Table 6.98: Calculated ScheduledReceipt (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 530 =====
520 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
Model The model used when netting this record. 
Either the Model value referenced in the 
InputModel field or the default Model value 
(None) is returned as determined by the settings 
in Part.Type.MUEPoolNettting.ModelRule 
and SupplyStatus.ModelUsage. The following 
criteria is used:
 l If ModelRule is set to Net or 
NetWithoutForecast and ModelUsage is 
set to Normal, then the InputModel value is 
returned.
 l If ModelRule is set to Net or 
NetWithoutForecast and ModelUsage is 
set to Ignore, then the default Model 
(None) is returned.
 l If ModelRule is set to ExplodeOnly or 
Ignore then the default Model (None) is 
returned.
 l If the optional Model-Unit Effectivity 
capability is not enabled, then the default 
model (None) is always returned regardless 
of field settings.
Referenced table: Model
Reference  
Table 6.98: Calculated ScheduledReceipt (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 531 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 521
Field name Description Data type Key
NeedQty Quantity of the supply actually needed to cover 
the demand. This field is expressed in stock 
units, and inflated to cover yield.
Note: In some configurations of Service Update 
2602 and later, this field only counts supply that 
is ultimately used to satisfy a real demand. Any 
scheduled receipt quantity that is used to satisfy 
a safety stock requirement but which does not 
ultimately satisfy a real demand (is pure excess), 
is not counted towards the NeedQty. 
Otherwise, and in all previous versions, this field 
includes quantities that are used to satisfy 
safety stock requirements but never satisfy any 
real demands.
Note: As of Service Update 2505 (limited 
availability version), this field only reflects 
supply that is ultimately used to satisfy a real 
demand. Any scheduled receipt quantity that is 
used to satisfy a safety stock requirement but 
which does not ultimately satisfy a real demand 
(is pure excess), is not counted towards the 
need quantity. 
Quantity  
NewProcessCost Standard labor and overhead cost of planned 
orders to complete this order.
Currency conversions on this field use the 
EffDueDate field's conversion rate.
Money  
NewPurchasesCost Cost of new purchases required in order to 
complete this order (through all levels of the 
bill).
Currency conversions on this field use the 
EffDueDate field's conversion rate.
Money  
Table 6.98: Calculated ScheduledReceipt (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 532 =====
522 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
OnTimeQuantity The quantity of this supply that could be 
completed on or before its ReschedDate.
Note: Previously, this field considered lead time 
and component material availability, but not 
constraint. However, in some configurations of 
Service Update 2602, and all configurations of 
Service Update 2603 and lter, constraint 
availability is now taken into account when 
calculating this field. 
Note: As of Service Update 2505 (limited 
availability version), constraint availability is also 
considered when calculating this field (not just 
component/material availability). 
Quantity  
Table 6.98: Calculated ScheduledReceipt (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 533 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 523
Field name Description Data type Key
ParameterDate The date that was used to determine which 
time-phased parameter values are applicable to 
this supply (compared against the 
EffectiveInDate values on certain applicable 
tables). 
For example, if using time-phased yield then  the 
TimePhasedYield record  effective for the part 
source on this date determines  the yield factor. 
Also, applies to time-phased SourceConstraint 
records as well as time-phased lead time logic 
(records in the 
TimePhasedPartSourceLeadTime and 
TimePhasedSourceLeadTime tables).
Depending on the status of the scheduled 
receipt, this date is set as follows:
 l For non-reschedulable receipts, the 
DueDate is used (set to Past if the  
DueDate is left undefined).
 l For reschedulable receipts, the 
ReschedDate is typically used; or else the 
date from the first attempt at rescheduling 
the supply. However, in some configurations 
of Service Update 2602 or later, the final 
ReschedDate from any subsequent 
attempts at rescheduling the supply is now 
used (previously, these subsequent attempts 
were ignored when calculating 
ParameterDate). 
For reschedulable receipts, the 
ReschedDate is typically used; or, prior to 
Service Update 2505 (limited availability 
version, the date from the first attempt at 
rescheduling the supply. As of Service 
Update 2505 (limited availability version), 
the final ReschedDate from any subsequent 
attempts at rescheduling the supply is now 
used. 
However, if SupplyStatus.AllowLatePlan is 
Date  
Table 6.98: Calculated ScheduledReceipt (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 534 =====
524 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
set to Y and the ReschedDate then moves 
past the demand's due date, that demand 
due date  is used instead. Additionally, if 
allotment overrides are used this is set to the 
receipt's effective stock date instead. And for 
part's that have PartType.CommitLevel set 
to High, this date will not always correspond 
to ReschedDate (but will always correspond 
to the period from which the effective 
quantity of the receipt is calculated).
PartSource The PartSource record that corresponds to this 
supply. Used by calculations for Transfer 
(explosion), Ongoing, and Yield processing.
For details about how this field is calculated, see 
Part sources for scheduled receipts.

as this ScheduledReceipt record (Part and

EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 525
Field name Description Data type Key
Pool The pool used when netting this supply. 
The value returned here is affected by the  
Part.MUEPoolNettingType.PoolRule setting 
as follows.
 l If PoolRule is set to Net or Optional and 
the InputPool reference on this line item is 
populated, then that referenced pool is 
used.
 l If PoolRule is set to Net or Optional and 
the InputPool reference on this line item is 
Null, then the pool referenced by 
Order.Pool is used (from the supply order).
 l If Part.MUEPoolNettingType is Ignore, 
Forecast, or ForecastOptional, the 
part's common pool, from the 
Part.PlanningCalendars.CommonPool 
reference, is always used.
Referenced table: Pool
Reference  
RecommendedDate This field considers rescheduling rules and 
expedite limits. It reports the date where 
Netting puts the scheduled receipt for the 
purpose of demand provision. If the supply is 
RecommendOnly, it will report the date where 
netting wanted it without actually moving it 
there. Note that dependent demands are not 
generated with reference to this date.
Date

DockDate
The dock date based on RecommendedDate. Date

===== PAGE 536 =====
526 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
ReschedDate This field considers rescheduling rules and 
expedite limits. It reports the date where 
Netting puts the scheduled receipt in order to 
generate dependent demands. If the supply is 
Reschedulable, then this field gives the same 
date as RecommendedDate; otherwise, it is the 
same as the DueDate.
Date  
ReschedDockDate The dock date based on ReschedDate. Date  
ShipDate The date this supply was or should be shipped 
based on ReschedDate.
Date  
ShipDateFromStart The order ship date after adjustment based on 
any calendar rounding applied when 
determining the calculated start date. 
For example, parts that use "cumulative" lead 
time might end up with an earlier ship date 
from start based on rounding between ship and 
lead time unit calendars. 
Date  
StockUnitQuantity Represents the supply quantity in inventory unit 
of measure (UOM), including any reduction for 
InTransitConsumedQuantity, and without 
applying yield or scrap factors. 
This field also does not account for the settings 
in either the SupplyStatus or SupplyType 
tables (and thus can return a positive value for 
orders that are ignored by Netting and have 
EffQuantity = 0). 
Note:  SupplierUOM applies to 
StockUnitQuantity.
Quantity

reported.

Zone
A reference to the entry in the 
ToleranceProfileZone table that applies to this 
ScheduledReceipt record. It is used in defining 
the acceptable levels of change when 
comparing scheduled receipts against baseline 
historical supply series.

referenced from the PartSupplier field, and the

Table 6.98: Calculated ScheduledReceipt (Mfg) fields
ScheduledReceiptOperation table
The ScheduledReceiptOperation table shows information for set of operations that must be 
performed to fulfill a scheduled receipt. Scheduled Receipt operations parallel route operations 
but may be modified manually or by your ERP system.
Field name Description Data type Key
Name Name of the scheduled receipt operation. String Y
ScheduledReceipt Name of the scheduled receipt associated with 
this scheduled receipt operation.
Referenced table: ScheduledReceipt
Reference Y
ActualEnd
DateTime
The date and time for the actual end of the 
scheduled receipt operation.
DateTime  
ActualStart
DateTime
The date and time for the actual start of the 
scheduled receipt operation.
DateTime  
Table 6.99: ScheduledReceiptOperation  (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 539 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 529
Field name Description Data type Key
InputBatchName The name of the batch for the scheduled 
receipt operation. Batches represent 
operations scheduled together on a resource. 
This value will always be unique.
String  
InputEnd
DateTime
The date and time that the operation is 
scheduled to end. This value is used as input 
for the Scheduling algorithm and is updated as 
operations are moved on the schedule.
DateTime  
InputStart
DateTime
The date and time that the operation is 
scheduled to start. This value is used as input 
for the Scheduling algorithm and is updated as 
operations are moved on the schedule.
DateTime  
IsLocked Identifies if the scheduled receipt operation is 
locked.
Boolean  
ProducedAt
Datetime
The date and time that the quantity for this 
operation was last updated.
DateTime  
Quantity The remaining quantity to be produced for this 
operation.
Quantity  
Sequence The sequence assigned to the scheduled 
receipt operation.
Quantity  
Size The size of the scheduled receipt operation. 
This value is used to assess if this operation can 
be scheduled on a resource with a maximum 
operation or maximum total size constraint 
applied to it.
Quantity  
TotalQuantity The total quantity to be produced for this 
operation.
Quantity  
Table 6.99: ScheduledReceiptOperation  (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 540 =====
530 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
UseRoute
Operation
Precedence
Identifies if the precedence relationships 
defined at the route operation level will be 
used for this scheduled receipt operation.
Default: False
Depending on which predecessor records are 
defined, the following is applied:
 l When only a route operation predecessor 
record exists and 
UseRouteOperationPrecedence = True: 
the scheduled receipt operation inherits its 
precedence relationships from its 
associated route operation.
 l When only a route operation predecessor 
record exists and 
UseRouteOperationPrecedence = False: 
no precedence is applied to the scheduled 
receipt operation.
 l When only a scheduled receipt operation 
predecessor record exists and 
UseRouteOperationPrecedence = True 
or False: the scheduled receipt operation 
uses the precedence defined in the 
scheduled receipt operation predecessor 
record.
 l When both route operation predecessor 
and scheduled receipt operation 
predecessor records exist and 
UseRouteOperationPrecedence = True: 
the scheduled receipt operation uses the 
precedence relationships defined by both 
predecessor records.
 l When both route operation predecessor 
and scheduled receipt operation 
predecessor records exist and 
UseRouteOperationPrecedence = False: 
the scheduled receipt operation only uses 
the precedence defined in the scheduled 
receipt operation predecessor record (route 
Boolean

Table 6.102: ScheduledReceiptOperationSegment  (Scheduling) fields
ScheduledReceiptOperationSegment
ResourceAssignment table
The ScheduledReceiptOperationSegmentResourceAssignment table defines a different 
secondary resource assignment to apply to a specified scheduled receipt operation than the 
default one assigned to it. The values from the secondary resource requirement specified in this 
record will override corresponding values in the default secondary resource requirement. 
To use the values from this specified secondary resource requirement, the scheduled receipt 
operation segment must have its ExcludeUnassignedSecondaryResourceRequirements field 
set to N. See ScheduledReceiptOperation table.
Field name Description Data type Key
Resource The secondary resource to assign to the 
scheduled receipt operation segment.
Referenced table: Resource
Reference Y
ResourceGroup The resource group that the secondary resource 
belongs to.
Referenced table: ResourceGroup
Reference Y

Table 6.106: SchedulingWhereConsumedInput  (Scheduling) fields
SearchAlgorithmRuleComponent table
The SearchAlgorithmRuleComponent tables defines the properties for components that make 
up a Search Algorithm Rule. A component determines how operations are modified to generate 
a candidate schedule. One or more components are combined into a rule to customize how 
exactly you want the algorithm to change operations as it searches for the best schedule.

===== PAGE 549 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 539
Field name Description Data type Key
SearchAlgorithmRule
ComponentType
The method used to generate a candidate 
schedule. 
Valid values are:
 l ExtensionPoint: An extension point is 
used to generate a candidate schedule.
 l GroupProperty: Two random 
operations with the same property type 
are selected and the first operation is 
moved to a position right before the 
second operation in the schedule. All of 
the operations adjacent to the first 
operation with the same property type 
are also moved with the first operation.
 l InsertionImprovement: A random 
operation is selected and inserted into 
each eligible position in the schedule. 
The position that results in the lowest 
score is selected as the final position for 
that operation.
 l Perturb: Three random operations are 
selected and exchange positions 
backwards in the schedule. For example, 
operation A, B, and C change positions 
to B, C, A. This exchange is repeated 
multiple times in each iteration to find 
the configuration with the lowest score.
 l SingleRandomRelocation: A random 
operation is selected and inserted into 
the schedule in a new randomly selected 
position.
 l SingleRandomSwap: Two random 
operations are selected and switch 
positions in the schedule.
 l SwapImprovement: A random 
operation is selected and exchanges 
position with each eligible operation in 
the schedule. The exchange with the 
lowest score is the one used.
String (Enum) Y
Table 6.107: SearchAlgorithmRuleComponent (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 550 =====
540 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
Note: The SingleRandomRelocation and 
SingleRandomSwap methods require a 
larger number of iterations to succeed at 
improving the schedule.
Sequence The sequence assigned to a component 
that determines when the component will 
be executed during an iteration.
Integer Y
SearchAlgorithmRule The Search Algorithm rule associated with 
this component.
Referenced table: SearchAlgorithmRule
Reference Y
EndIteration The end of when the component is eligible 
to be to be executed within a range of 
executions. This range is determined by the 
maximum runtime or maximum iterations. 
Start and EndIteration values of 0 imply 
that the component should be run through 
all iterations. 
Quantity  
Probability The chance that this component will be 
executed during an iteration.
Quantity  
StartIteration The start of when the component is eligible 
to be to be executed within a range of 
executions. This range is determined by the 
maximum runtime or maximum iterations
Start and EndIteration values of 0 imply 
that the component should be run through 
all iterations. 
Quantity  
Table 6.107: SearchAlgorithmRuleComponent (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 551 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 541
Field name Description Data type Key
PropertyType The property type associated with this 
component. For a large number of 
iterations, the algorithm groups together 
operations that have the same property 
type.
Referenced table: PropertyType

EnforceConcurrent
PrimaryResourceRuns
Determines if all resources associated with the

 l When set to True, all resources connected 
to the same secondary resource 
requirement must run operations 
concurrently.  
 l When set to False, 
ResourceType.AllowDispatchRuleGaps 
will be ignored.

Boolean

Table 6.108: SecondaryResourceRequirement  (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 553 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 543
Field name Description Data type Key
RequireAt Determines when a secondary resource is held 
or released during the segment to be 
scheduled on another segment. This is 
determined by the required quantity of the 
secondary resource.
Valid values are:
 l SegmentStart: 
If the required quantity is > 0, then the 
secondary resource is required from the 
start of the operation segment and held 
until released.
If the required quantity is < 0, then the 
secondary resource is released at the start 
of the operation segment.
 l SegmentEnd:
If the required quantity is > 0, then the 
secondary resource is required from the 
end of the operation segment and held 
until released.
If the required quantity is < 0, then the 
secondary resource is released at the end 
of the operation segment.
String 
(Enum)
 
Table 6.108: SecondaryResourceRequirement  (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 554 =====
544 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Key
SecondaryResource
SelectionRule
Determines which secondary resource to use 
for an operation on a primary resource when 
more than one eligible secondary resource is 
available.
Valid values are:
 l LeastGroups: the secondary resource 
mapped to the fewest resource groups is 
selected. Secondary resources that belong 
to multiple resource groups are 
considered to have higher flexibility and 
are therefore only used when necessary.
 l Priority: the secondary resource with the 
highest priority is selected.
 l NearestQtyAvailable: an average of 
eligible resource quantities over the 
expected time period is calculated and 
used to select the secondary resource that 
most closely meets the secondary 
resource requirement quantity.
String 
(Enum)

Table 6.116: ShiftPattern  (Scheduling) fields
ShiftPatternResourceAvailability table
The ShiftPatternResourceAvailability table contains information on when resources are 
available within a defined shift pattern. For resources with intervals that cross Daylight Savings 
Time (DST) new records for DST periods must be created. For example, for a shift pattern that 
crosses one DST period, one ShiftPatternResourceAvailability record should be defined with 
its ApplyEndDate at the start of the DST period and another record is defined with its 
ApplyStartDate and the start of the DST period to ensure that the resource is available at the 
right time when the time change occurs.
Field name Description Data type Key
ApplyStartDate The date that a resource is available to use a 
shift pattern.
Date Y
Resource The resource associated with shift pattern.
Referenced table: Resource(Scheduling)
Reference Y
ShiftPattern The shift pattern associated with this shift 
pattern resource availability record.
Referenced table: ShiftPattern
Reference Y
ApplyEndDate The date that a resource can no longer use the 
a shift pattern.
Date  
ApplyStartDate
OffsetDays
The number of offset days applied to the start 
date for the resource.
Integer  
Table 6.117: ShiftPatternResourceAvailability  (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 559 =====

Field name Description Data type Key
IsOverride Identifies if multiple records with an 
overlapping availability period can be 
scheduled. 
Boolean  
QuantityAvailable The quantity available at this resource for the 
shift pattern. 
Quantity values greater than 1 are only 
applicable to secondary resources.
For primary resources, any quantity value > 1 is 
assumed to be 1 by the Scheduling algorithm.
Quantity

Table 6.118: ShiftPatternResourceAvailability  (Scheduling) fields
Site table
The Site table contains valid site codes. An entry should be provided for each site value in the 
enterprise data.
This table is referenced by many other tables. The Site field in these other tables is usually a key 
field, which keeps planning data from different sites separate. For example, there might be 
different constraints affecting production at different locations, or different quantities of a part 
on hand at different locations.

===== PAGE 560 =====
550 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Types of sites
Maestro supports two types of sites: manufacturing/physical and inventory/logical. 
Manufacturing/Physical 
A manufacturing/physical site is an individual location within a company (or a company's 
subsidiary or a company's third-party manufacturing facilities) that segregates its supply and 
demand from other locations, for example, a plant, a specific geographic location, and so on. 
Typically, manufacturing, assembly of products, or both occur at this type of site. 
Data from manufacturing/physical sites is used to model supply and demand, and model the 
building of supply to support demand. The part data associated with manufacturing/physical 
sites includes a BOM structure. 
Enterprise data sources are often used as a site identifiers.
Inventory/Logical
Inventory/logical sites are used to identify units, such as business units and inventory hubs. An 
inventory/logical site is often an inventory location, and it segregates its supply and demand 
from other locations. Examples include retail site, hub, VMI, and order consolidation. 
These types of sites are primarily used to model a source of supply, demand, or both. 
The part data associated with the inventory/logical site type does not involve transformation of 
one part into another (for example, processing, assembly, and so on) and should not include any 
BOM structures. Maestro algorithms (including netting) must not be used in modeling exercises 
that explode BOM structures. 

===== PAGE 561 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Inputtables 551
Field name Description Data type Key
Value The key string used to reference a Site record (for 
example, from the Part table, or from the 
WorkCenter table if the Capacity Planning - 
Constraints application is enabled). 
Default value: blank string
Caution: Avoid including leading or trailing spaces 
in Site names. In some cases, this can lead to the Site 
not being visible in the Maestro interface (for 
example, from a workbook's Site control).
String  
Address The street address of the site. 
If this table is used to support maps, the address in 
this field determines the geocode that is used to 
mark the site's location on the map. To ensure that 
the correct geocode is used, follow the guidelines 
outlined in "Using address data to support maps" on 
page 1.
String  
Description Additional information about the site.
Default value: blank string
String  
LicenseType Indicates the type of site license. Valid values are:
 l Logical: This type of site is used to identify 
units, such as a specific business unit or a 
customer product line, within one physical site. In 
Maestro, this type of license is also referred to as 
Inventory/Logical.
 l Physical: This type of site is used to identify an 
individual location within a company that has 
separately defined enterprise data, such as a 
plant or a specific geographic location. In 
Maestro, this type of license is also referred to as 
Manufacturing/Physical.
For more information, see Types of sites.
String
(Enum)

SubRegion
 
The name of the sub-region that's associated with 
this site. This defines a geographic area that is 
smaller than a continent, but could include, for 
example, multiple countries or states.  For example, 
the sub-region of North West Europe could include 
Denmark, Finland, Norway, and Sweden.

===== PAGE 567 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Calculatedtables 557
Calculated tables
Calculated tables in Maestro hold records generated by algorithm calculations. after control 
settings are applied to input data. The data in calculated tables is not editable. 
The Enterprise Scheduling data model includes the following calculated tables:
BatchSelectionExplanation table
The BatchSelectionExplanation table contains logging results that explain which operations are 
selected to run in the schedule. Multiple potential operations are considered, but typically the 
operation with the lowest score is scheduled. Logging must be enabled in your system to 
generate results in this table.
Field name Description Data type Owner
IsSelected Identifies if the potential operation for 
this record was scheduled.
Boolean  
OperationScheduleId Unique identifier for the operation. String  
PotentialBatchName The potential operation name. Multiple 
operations are considered, but only the 
operation with Selected = Y is the 
scheduled.
String  
Score Numeric score that helps determine if a 
operation is scheduled or not.
Quantity  
AreaGroup The are group associated with the 
operation.
Referenced table: AreaGroup
Reference Yes
Table 6.121: BatchSelectionExplanation (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 568 =====
558 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Owner
PlannedOrder
Operation
The planned order operation associated 
with the operation selection explanation.
Referenced table: 
PlannedOrderOperation
Reference  
ScheduledReceipt
Operation
The scheduled receipt operation 
associated with the operation selection 
explanation.
Referenced table: 
ScheduledReceiptOperation
Reference  
Table 6.121: BatchSelectionExplanation (Scheduling) fields
ObjectiveFunctionExplanation table
When the Search Algorithm option is enabled in your system and a resource group has been set 
to use it, the ObjectiveFunctionExplanation contains the logging results of those algorithm 
runs. The Search Algorithm option runs the Scheduling algorithm multiple times to find a more 
effective schedule than the current schedule. A score, the objective function value, is used to 
assess the effectiveness of a candidate schedule generated by each run of the algorithm. The 
schedule iteration with the lowest score is set as the final schedule.
Field name Description Data type Owner
Detail More information on this log record. String  
Iteration The schedule iteration that the 
objective function score was 
calculated for.
Quantity  
ObjectiveFunctionValueThe calculated score used to assess 
the effectiveness of a candidate 
schedule. Lower scores indicate 
improved effectiveness.
Quantity  
Table 6.122: ObjectiveFunctionExplanation (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 569 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Calculatedtables 559
Field name Description Data type Owner
Runtime How long the algorithm ran to 
calculate that iteration. Measured in 
nanoseconds (ns).
Quantity  
AreaGroup The area group associated with this 
log record.
Referenced table: AreaGroup
Reference  
Table 6.122: ObjectiveFunctionExplanation (Scheduling) fields
OperationFeasibilityExplanation table
The OperationFeasibilityExplanation table contains logging results that explain the feasibility 
of scheduling for any scheduled operation. Failed operations are identified by failed resource or 
resource group. Logging must be enabled in your system to generate results in this table.
Field name Description Data type Owner
EventDateTime The date and time of the operation. DateTime  
IsFeasible Identifies if the operation was feasible. Boolean  
LogType The type of log recorded. String  
Message The message generated about the 
operation.
String  
PotentialBatchName For operations produced in batches, this 
value represents the batch ID and is 
used to identify which batch the 
operation belongs to. For single 
operations, this field has a blank value.
String  
AreaGroup The area group associated with the 
operation.
Referenced table: AreaGroup
Reference

OperationSchedule table
The OperationSchedule table displays calculated output from the scheduling algorithm that 
determines the scheduled start and end times for Planned Order or Scheduled Receipt 
operations and downtimes.

constraint.

OperationScheduleSegment table
The OperationScheduleSegment table describes the segments for each OperationSchedule 
record that has been calculated by the Scheduling algorithm. It contains the dates and times for 
the start and end of each individual segment in the schedule.

Table 6.127: OperationScheduleSegmentResourceAssignment  (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 575 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Calculatedtables 565
OperationSegmentTo
ChangeoverCondition table
The OperationSegmentToChangeoverCondition table describes the links between operation 
schedule segments generated by the Scheduling algorithm and changeover conditions that 
trigger a changeover on that operation.
A record is created for each operation segment and associated changeover condition. For 
example, if three changeover conditions trigger a changeover on one segment, three 
OperationSegmentToChangeoverCondition records are generated.
When only one changeover is triggered on a segment, the changeover condition acts as both 
the duration driver and the penalty driver. If there are multiple changeover conditions, then the 
highest duration and the highest penalty are flagged as the driving conditions.
Field name Description Data type Owner
IsDurationDriver Identifies if the associated 
changeover condition is the 
driver for the operation 
schedule segment's duration. 
Boolean  
IsPenaltyDriver Identifies if the associated 
changeover condition is the 
driver for the changeover 
penalty that contributes to the 
dispatch rule score.
Boolean  
OperationScheduleSegmentIdThe ID for the operation 
schedule segment that the 
changeover is triggered on.
String  
AreaGroup A reference to the Area Group 
table.
Referenced table: AreaGroup
Reference Yes
Table 6.128: OperationSegmentToChangeoverCondition  (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 576 =====
566 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Owner
ChangeoverCondition A reference to the changeover 
condition that triggers a 
changeover on the operation 
scheduled segment.
Referenced table: 
ChangeoverCondition
Reference  
Table 6.128: OperationSegmentToChangeoverCondition  (Scheduling) fields
PlannedOrder  table
The PlannedOrder table is a calculated table providing recommendations for new supply orders 
to be generated (receipts). The supply type is determined from the  
PartSourceType.PlannedOrderSupplyType control value referenced by the part source. There 
will never be more than one planned order generated for a particular part on the same 
TimeUnits interval (for example, work day). The order produced will cover all demand within the 
period. PartSource.OrderPolicy control values, such as those used when implementing 
parameters like days supply and lot sizing, can increase the effective coverage period and/or 
cause multiple planned orders to be generated on the same TimeUnits interval.
Notes: 
 l If attribute-based planning is used to impact the allotment of supply to demand, all 
relevant attributes can be reported on records in this table using the Attribute<*> 
syntax. For more information on the Attribute<*> syntax, see the Maestro Resource 
Authoring Guide (Java client). 
 l After upgrading to some configurations of Service Update 2602 (or later), the total 
number of planned orders might increase in environments that use safety stock and 
where the safety stock level changes over time (such as, with dynamic safety stock 
methods). In many cases, the total quantity from those planned orders will be the 
same but spread over more orders of differing priority levels. 

===== PAGE 577 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Calculatedtables 567
Field name Description Data type Owner
BatchIndex If the planned order is created as part of a 
production campaign, this represents the 
order's batch number (unique index value) 
within its campaign. For example, the first 
order scheduled in each campaign is given 
a value of 1, the second order a value 2, and 
so on.
Otherwise, for all orders from part sources 
that aren't enabled for campaign planning 
(have an OrderPolicy.MaximumUsage 
value other than CampaignPlanning), this 
field returns 0.
Integer  
Table 6.129: PlannedOrder (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 578 =====
568 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Owner
CalcRequest
StartDate
The date on which work should start on this 
supply in order to meet the earliest request 
date on any earliest driving independent 
demand it satisfies. For Make and Buy 
sources this returns an equivalent start date, 
whereas for Transfer sources this returns 
an equivalent built date . 
This field is based on the original 
RequestDate exploded down from the 
driver IndependentDemand record and 
adjusted by lead time at each level in the 
product structure (if the supply directly 
satisfies an independent demand itself, then 
this is just the RequestDate adjusted by the 
part's lead time). 
If the driver demand is not an independent 
demand, or has an undefined RequestDate, 
then this is the date on which work should 
start on this supply in order to meet the due 
date on the driver.
Note: The date associated with a given 
planned order might be subject to 
limitations when multi-level search logic is 
enabled and the 
MultiLevelSearchRule.ShortTermExcess 
is set to Reduce. For more information, see 
Reducing short term excess inventory.
Date  
Table 6.129: PlannedOrder (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 579 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Calculatedtables 569
Field name Description Data type Owner
CampaignIndex If the planned order is created as part of a 
production campaign, this gives the unique 
index value of the campaign it belongs to. 
Campaigns for a given part source are 
numbered starting with 1 for the earliest 
planned campaign and then incrementing 
for each subsequent campaign.
Otherwise, for all supplies from part sources 
that aren't enabled for campaign planning 
(have an OrderPolicy.MaximumUsage 
value other than CampaignPlanning), this 
field returns 0.
Note: This field supports the optional 
Campaign Planning capability.
Integer  
Table 6.129: PlannedOrder (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 580 =====
570 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Owner
DemandDate The date of the demand associated with this 
planned order. This field is calculated as one 
of the following:
 l For most parts, this reports the due date 
of the first demand satisfied by this 
supply. 
 l For order point parts (that is, parts with 
a SafetyStockPolicy.SafetyStockRule 
of OrderPointAverage, 
OrderPointOver, or 
OrderPointUnder), this is instead the 
date when inventory for the part 
dropped below the order point 
threshold.
Notes: 
 l The date reported in this field must 
typically belong to the part's 
PlanningCalendars.TimeUnits 
calendar. If the supply is created to 
cover an independent demand with a 
due date that does not fall on the part's 
TimeUnits calendar, it is adjusted back 
to the previous TimeUnits interval 
before being reported in this field. 
However, in some configurations of 
Service Update 2602 and later, this 
rounding does not occur and the date 
from the demand is always reported 
(but supply due dates are adjusted as 
needed to be on the part's TimeUnits 
calendar). 
 l In some configurations of Service 
Update 2602 and later, if the planned 
order is created to cover a safety stock 
requirement but does not ultimately 
satisfy any real demand (it is pure 
excess), the DemandDate is left 
Undefined. Otherwise, and in all 
Date  
Table 6.129: PlannedOrder (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 581 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Calculatedtables 571
Field name Description Data type Owner
previous versions, this field is still 
populated on planned orders that don't 
end up satisfying any real demands. 
 l As of Service Update 2505 (limited 
availability version), if the planned order 
is created to cover a safety stock 
requirement but does not ultimately 
satisfy any real demand (it is pure 
excess), this date is left Undefined. 
DockDate Date the supply is planned to be delivered 
to the receiving dock.
Date  
DockDateFrom
Start
The adjusted dock date calculated by 
applying lead time forward from the order 
start date. 
This is meant to account for any calendar 
rounding incurred when applying lead time 
backward from the due date to determine 
order start.
For example, parts that use "cumulative" 
lead time might end up with an earlier dock 
date from start based on rounding between 
time units, transit, ship, and lead time unit 
calendars. 
Note: This field's actual calculation is 
impacted by the applicable TransitRule 
setting. For more information, see Lead 
time calendar rounding.
Date  
DueDate The date the order is planned to be in stock. Date

Field name Description Data type Owner
DueDateFrom
Start
The adjusted due date calculated by 
applying lead time forward from the order 
start date. 
This is meant to account for any calendar 
rounding incurred when applying lead time 
backward from the due date to determine 
order start.
For example, parts that use "cumulative" 
lead time might end up with an earlier due 
date from start based on rounding between 
time units, transit, ship, and lead time unit 
calendars.

Table 6.129: PlannedOrder (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 583 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Calculatedtables 573
Field name Description Data type Owner
EarliestExpiry
Date
The earliest date that any supplies used to 
satisfy dependent demands generated from 
this order may expire. 
Typically, this is determined from the latest 
EarliestExpiryDate found on a demand 
that Netting calculations determine this 
supply will satisfy. In cases where the supply 
has been planned late (where the supply 
due date is later than the demand date), the 
EarliestExpiryDate from the demand is 
extended by the number of days late the 
supply is being planned. 
Otherwise, if there are no consuming 
demands, this value is calculated by adding 
the number of expiry calendar intervals 
defined in Part.MinimumShelfLife to the 
supply due date.
Note also that although planned orders 
used to satisfy dependent demands 
typically inherit their EarliestExpiryDate 
from those dependent demands, this date 
can be extended if necessary. If a positive 
value is given in the 
MinimumShelfLifeThreshold field on the 
PartSource used to supply the order, then 
those number of expiry calendar intervals 
are added to the order due date. If the 
resulting date is later than the earliest 
expiry date found on the dependent 
demand, it is reported in this field 
(otherwise, the date from the dependent 
demand is used instead).
For non-expiry parts (PartType.ExpiryRule 
is set to Ignore), this field always reports 
Past.
Date
 
 
Table 6.129: PlannedOrder (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 584 =====
574 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Owner
EffQuantity The quantity of this order that is expected 
to be received into inventory after applying 
yield/scrap, considering any co-product or 
by-product supply, and converting the unit 
of measure to inventory units.
Quantity  
Estimated
ExpiryDate
The date that the Netting algorithm 
calculates this supply will expire. Used to 
determine which demands this order can 
potentially be used to satisfy (supply is only 
eligible for use if it is on or before the 
demand's EarliestExpiryDate).
Calculated starting from DueDate and then 
adding the number of expiry calendar 
intervals defined in 
PartSource.IntervalsToExpiry. 
Note that this date might be different than 
the actual calculated expiry date reported in 
CTPPlannedOrder.ExpiryDate which takes 
supply availabilities into account.
Non-expiry parts (PartType.ExpiryRule is 
set to Ignore), always return a value of 
Future.
Date  
GroupLine The line number for the group order that 
this planned order belongs to. The first 
value is 1 and increase incrementally with 
each group order.
If PartSource.ProductionGroup is null, 
then this value is 0. 
Integer  
IsEligibleForSchedulingIdentifies if this planned order is eligible to 
be scheduled.
Note: This field is in the Scheduling 
namespace.
Boolean  
Table 6.129: PlannedOrder (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 585 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Calculatedtables 575
Field name Description Data type Owner
Line
 
The "line number" for this planned order. 
Orders planned for each part start at 1 and 
increment for each planned order.
Caution: The line number sequence for 
each part's planned orders is not 
guaranteed to align with due date sequence 
of those orders, and an increase in 
misaligned sequencing between Line and 
DueDate might be seen as of Service 
Update 2505 (limited availability version). 
Therefore it is not generally recommended 
to sort records from this table based on 
Line number (if date-based sequencing or 
records is required, it is recommended to 
sort by DueDate instead).
Integer  
Model The model that applies to this supply. It is 
set to None if either of the following 
conditions exist:
 l Model-Unit Effectivity is not enabled
 l Part.MUEPoolNettingType.ModelRul
e is Ignore
Referenced table:Model

Table 6.129: PlannedOrder (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 586 =====
576 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Owner
NeedQty Quantity actually needed to cover the 
demand. This field is expressed in stock 
units, and inflated to cover yield.
Note: In some configurations of Service 
Update 2602 and later, this field only counts 
supply that is ultimately used to satisfy a 
real demand. Any planned order quantity 
created to satisfy a safety stock requirement 
but which does not ultimately satisfy a real 
demand (is pure excess), is not counted 
towards the NeedQty. Otherwise, and in all 
previous versions, this field includes 
quantities created to satisfy safety stock but 
which are never used to satisfy a real 
demand.
Note: As of Service Update 2505 (limited 
availability version), this field only reflects 
supply that is ultimately used to satisfy a 
real demand. Any planned order quantity 
created to satisfy a safety stock requirement 
but which does not ultimately satisfy a real 
demand (is pure excess), is not counted 
towards the need quantity. 
Quantity  
Table 6.129: PlannedOrder (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 587 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Calculatedtables 577
Field name Description Data type Owner
OrderPriority The priority assigned to this planned order. 
OrderPriority is calculated as follows:
 l If Part.Type.CommitLevel is High or 
MediumExplodePriority, then this is 
set to the priority of the demand that 
drove the requirement for the planned 
order. 
 l If Part.Type.CommitLevel is Medium or 
Low, then this is set to the part's default 
priority (Part.Type.DefaultPriority).
 l If the planned order is generated to 
satisfy safety stock requirements, then 
the following applies:
 l Typically, the planned order priority 
is set to Part.Type.DefaultPriority 
regardless of CommitLevel.
 l In some configurations of Service 
Update 2602 and later then, 
dependent on CommitLevel, the 
planned order priority is set to the 
priority of the demand that is 
expected to consume the planned 
order, and any planned orders that 
are pure excess for safety stock is 
planned later in the horizon and set 
to the DefaultPriority. 
Additionally in these configurations, 
if a demand has a lower priority than 
the default priority, the 
corresponding planned order might 
reflect the default priority or the 
priority of the demand being 
satisfied.
 l  For parts using demand 
transformation logic (where 
DemandTransformationRule on 
the SafetyStockPolicy table is set 
FirstInFirstOut), planned orders 
Reference  
Table 6.129: PlannedOrder (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 588 =====
578 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Owner
for safety stock can take their priority 
from the eventual real demand they 
are assumed to satisfy. However, in 
some configurations of Service 
Update 2602 and later, demand 
transformation logic is no longer 
applicable as the standard safety 
stock logic now provides similar 
results.
 l  If the planned order is generated to 
satisfy safety stock requirements, then 
this is typically set to 
Part.Type.DefaultPriority regardless of 
CommitLevel. However for parts using 
demand transformation logic (where 
DemandTransformationRule on the 
SafetyStockPolicy table is set 
FirstInFirstOut), planned orders for 
safety stock can take their priority from 
the eventual real demand they are 
assumed to satisfy (and similar logic is 
always applied to safety stock 
requirements in the limited availability 
version of Service Update 2505 as 
described here). 
For planned orders generated for 
optimization parts, OrderPriority is 
calculated as follows:
 l For top-level optimization parts, if 
Part.Type.CommitLevel is High or 
MediumExplodePriority, then this is 
set based on the priority of the external 
demands that drove the requirement for 
the planned order. This calculation is 
performed as if the part source has 
SupplyStatus.PriorityStatus set to 
Highest. That is, the 
OrderPriority.Value associated with the 
lowest OrderPriority.PlanningPriority 
Table 6.129: PlannedOrder (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 589 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Calculatedtables 579
Field name Description Data type Owner
on any external demand satisfied by the 
supply is reported.
 l Otherwise, this is set to 
Part.Type.DefaultPriority.
Referenced table:OrderPriority
Note: For more information about how 
priorities are set on planned orders, see 
Priorities on planned orders
Origin Indicates the origin of this planned order 
record.
Valid values are:
 l Manual:  The planned order record was 
created to model a manual supply 
allocation record in the 
SupplyAllocation table.
 l Planned: The planned order record was 
created by Netting calculations.
String
(Enum)

Field name Description Data type Owner
ParameterDate The date used to determine which time-
phased parameter values are applicable to 
the supply (compared against the 
EffectiveInDate values on certain 
applicable tables). 
For example, if using time-phased yield 
then  the TimePhasedYield record  effective 
for the part source on this date determines  
the yield factor. Also, applies to time-
phased SourceConstraint records, as well 
as time-phased lead time logic (records in 
the TimePhasedPartSourceLeadTime and 
TimePhasedSourceLeadTime tables).
This is typically set to the triggering 
demand's DueDate and then adjusted by 
part-level safety lead time (as defined in 
Part.SafetyLeadTime or via the part's 
SafetyLeadTimeProfile reference).
Date  
Quantity The amount required (including any 
allowances for yield, as well any co-product 
or by-product supply considerations).
Note: Supplier UOM applies to Quantity.
Quantity

used to find PartSource.Supplier and

===== PAGE 591 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Calculatedtables 581
Field name Description Data type Owner
ShipDate The date the supply should be shipped 
from its supplier.
Date  
ShipDateFrom
Start
The adjusted ship date calculated by 
applying lead time forward from the order 
start date. 
This is meant to account for any calendar 
rounding incurred when applying lead time 
backward from due date to determine order 
start.
For example, parts that use "cumulative" 
lead time might end up with an earlier ship 
date from start based on rounding between 
ship and lead time unit calendars. 
Note: This field's actual calculation is 
impacted by the applicable TransitRule 
setting. For more information, see Lead 
time calendar rounding.
Date  
StartDate The date to start the order (now adjusted to 
compensate for constraint).
Date  
StartUnit The start unit number that applies to this 
supply. It is set to -1 if either of the 
following conditions exist:
 l Model-Unit Effectivity is not enabled
 l Part.MUEPoolNettingType.UnitRule is 
Ignore
Integer  
StockUnit
Quantity
Represents the supply quantity in inventory 
unit of measure (UOM) without applying 
yield or scrap factors.
Quantity  
Table 6.129: PlannedOrder (Mfg) fields

===== PAGE 592 =====
582 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Owner
Transaction
Sequence
The sequence of the planned order which is 
determined as follows: 
 l If CommitLevel is set to High or 
MediumExplodePriority, this value is 
copied from the demand that the order 
was generated to satisfy.
 l  If CommitLevel is set to Medium or 
Low, or if the supply satisfies no specific 
demand (that is, it is from safety stock), 
this value is set to 0.
The sequence is then assigned to 
transaction sequence on resulting 
dependent demands. If dependent 
demands have the same order priority and 
MaintainCommitments is set to Y for 
dependent demand's part, then supplies 
will be allocated by sequence (smallest to 
largest).
Note: In some configurations of Service 
Update 2602 and later, if a planned order 
satisfies multiple demands, then its 
TransactionSequence is taken from the 
highest priority of those demands 
(assuming a CommitLevel of High or 
MediumExplodePriority). And if the 
planned order satisfies multiple demands of 
that highest priority, the lowest sequence 
from those demands is taken. Otherwise, 
and in all previous service updates, the 
sequence is still taken from the demand it 
was created to satisfy.
Integer  
Table 6.129: PlannedOrder (Mfg) fields

Table 6.130: PlannedOrderOperation (Scheduling) fields
PlannedOrderWorkItem table
The PlannedOrderWorkItem table contains the work item records for planned orders divided 
into quantities that can be scheduled. The calculations are based on the lot size of the part for 
the order.

===== PAGE 594 =====
584 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
Field name Description Data type Owner
Name The name of the planned order work item. Quantity  
Quantity The quantity of the planned work order item. Quantity  
PlannedOrder The planned order associated with the planned 
work order item.
Referenced table: PlannedOrderOperation
String  
Route The route associated with the planned work 
order item.
Referenced table: Route
String  
Site The site associated with the planned work order 
item.
Referenced table: Site (MFG)
Reference  
Table 6.131: PlannedOrderWorkItem (Scheduling) fields
ResourceAvailability table
The ResourceAvailability table stores calculated results generated by the Resource Availability 
algorithm. The quantity of a resource that is available is stored by resource and date and time. 
User inputs from the Shift and Downtime tables are input into the algorithm to calculate when 
resources are available.

===== PAGE 595 =====
EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)Calculatedtables 585
Field name Description Data type Owner
EndDateTime The date and time availability for the resource 
ends.
DateTime  
QuantityAvailable The available quantity for the resource.
For primary resources only one of two values is 
accepted:
 l 1: the primary resource is available.
 l  0: the primary resource is unavailable. The 
number of operations than can run in 
parallel on it are determined by 
Resource.MaximumConcurrentBatches.
For secondary resources any number value is 
accepted.
Quantity  
StartDateTime The date and time availability for the resource 
starts.
DateTime  
Downtime The downtime associated with the resource 
availability.
Referenced table: Downtime
Reference  
Resource The resource associated with the resource 
availability.
Referenced table: Resource (Scheduling)
Reference  
ShiftInterval The shift interval associated with the resource 
availability.
Referenced table: ShiftInterval table
Reference  
Table 6.132: ResourceAvailability (Scheduling) fields
SchedulingWhereConsumed table
The SchedulingWhereConsumed table represents the calculated relationships (pegging) 
between operation relationships and between different order types such as scheduled receipts 
and planned orders.

===== PAGE 596 =====

scheduling where consumed 
record.

MaximumWait
Duration
The maximum time allowed 
between the completion time of 
the child operation and the start 
time of its parent operation.
Quantity  
MaximumWait
DurationExceeded
Determines if the maximum wait 
duration between a predecessor 
and successor operation can be 
exceeded or not.
Boolean

Duration

between the completion time of 
the child operation and the start 
time of its parent operation.

planned order.

type for this part is set to 
unconstrained.

SchedulingWhereConsumedExplanation table
The SchedulingWhereConsumedExplanation table tracks all orders that were considered to 
fulfill the Bill of Materials input requirements for an operation.

Table 6.134: SchedulingWhereConsumedExplanation (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 600 =====
590 EnterpriseSchedulingApplicationConfigurationGuide(ADG)CHAPTER6:EnterpriseSchedulingdatamodel
TraceOrderExplanation table
The TraceOrderExplanation table contains logging results that explain how selected orders are 
scheduled. The operations that make up a scheduled receipt order are traced sequentially 
through events in the schedule. 
Field name Description Data type Owner
EventTime The date and time when the event occurred. DateTime  
EventType The type of event executed on the 
operation as it was processed through the 
schedule. Valid values are:
 l Activity End: When the activity ends.
 l Activity Start: When the  activity starts.
 l Batch Selection: The batch associated 
with this operation.
 l Eligibility Check: Confirmation that the 
order meets the eligibility requirements 
to be scheduled.
 l Enter Queue: When multiple operations 
are competing to be scheduled on the 
same resource, the operation enters a 
queue on that resource. Operation 
priority is used to determine when the 
operation is scheduled.
 l Exit Queue: When the operation exits 
the resource's queue. The operation can 
now be scheduled on that resource.
 l Min Wait Time End: The end of the 
minimum wait time. The operation can 
now start.
 l Min Wait Time Start: The start of the 
minimum wait time.
String  
Message More information about the event, 
including successes and failures. 
String  
Table 6.135: TraceOrderExplanation (Scheduling) fields

===== PAGE 601 =====

operation traced for this order.
Referenced table: AreaGroup
 
Reference

this order is scheduled to run on.

ScheduledReceipt

The associated scheduled receipt operation 
for this order.
